This allows us to determine the gas content without ad hoc assumptions about gas mass scaling factors.
SPT0311 58 is extremely massive, with an intrinsic gas mass of M_ gas = 3.3 1.9 10^ 11 . Its large mass and intense star formation is very rare for a source well into the Epoch of Reionization.

sources.summary: The paper presents high-resolution ALMA observations of SPT0311-58, resolving a z=6.900 massive reionization-era system into turbulent, interacting, rapidly star-forming clumps.

quotes.quote: Here we present new 0.07'' resolution ALMA observations of the dust and 158 emission in SPT0311-58, improving on the spatial resolution of the previous observations from marrone18 by 16 in beam area. The data reveal a highly clumpy and turbulent structure in the rapidly star-forming central galaxies, among the most detailed views of any reionization-era system.

We have presented a detailed view of an extremely massive reionization-era system, using 0.07'' resolution observations of the dust and emission in the z=6.900 SPT0311-58 system to probe the structure of the central galaxies on scales down to 100 pc. The observations resolve the pair of central galaxies into at least a dozen clumps, each with turbulent velocity dispersion = 70-120 , size r_ circ, = 0.7-1.3 kpc, and mass / 10 . These galaxy-scale clumps are similar in mass to the population of currently-known reionization-era galaxies selected from rest-UV imaging surveys, but the SPT0311-58 clumps show SFRs elevated by about a factor of 3--5 compared to the UV-selected objects at similar mass. Globally, the chaotic and turbulent kinematics of SPT0311-58 present a striking contrast to recent claims of dynamically cold thin-disk rotation in some z 4 DSFGs.

sources.summary: The paper describes SPT0311-58 as a z=6.9 IR-luminous interacting system with HyLIRG and ULIRG components, hosted by an exceptionally massive dark-matter halo in a rare early density peak.

quotes.quote: Recently, reported high-resolution Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) observations of SPT0311-58, a IR-luminous system at z = 6.9 , originally identified in the 2500 deg ^2 South Pole Telescope (SPT) survey ( Carlstrom2011 ). ALMA reveals this source to be a pair of interacting extremely massive and IR-luminous star-bursting galaxies: SPT0311-58W, a HyLIRG with L_ IR = 3.3 0.7 10^ 13 L_ and a SFR 2900 M_ yr ^ 1 , and SPT0311-58E, a ULIRG with L_ IR = 4.6 1.2 10^ 12 L_ and SFR 540 M_ yr ^ 1 . Using different proxies, estimated the DM halo mass hosting this system to be M_ DM = (1.4 - 7) 10^ 12 M_ , showing that SPT0311-58 marks a rare peak in the cosmic density field at this early cosmic time and it lies close to the exclusion curve predicted by the current structure formation paradigm.

gmw_00005656.name: MACS J0744+3927
gmw_00005656.overview: MACS J0744+3927 is a galaxy cluster lensing system discovered by the Massive Cluster Survey [arXiv:1207.4489]. The lensed galaxy at redshift z=2.21 exhibits ordered rotational motion and a metallicity gradient typical of local disk galaxies [arXiv:1207.4489].

sources.summary: The paper treats MACS J0744+3927 as a lensed z=2.21 arc, modeling its source-plane properties and finding ordered rotation plus a local-disk-like metallicity gradient.

quotes.quote: MACS J0744+3927 (hereafter J0744) is a galaxy cluster lensing system discovered by the MAssive Cluster Survey Ebeling01 . The z=2.21 arc was identified in followup optical spectroscopy of the cluster and we secured the near-infrared spectrum as part of our screening program of bright z 2 arcs Richard11 . We previously observed the \ emission line with OSIRIS and described the source kinematics in .

Three galaxies (J0744, J1148, and J1206) show ordered rotational motion in both the 2-D maps and 1-D profiles with high local velocity dispersion 20 in all cases. J0744 has a gradient typical of local disk galaxies.

gmw_00005657.name: source A
gmw_00005657.overview: Source A is a compact galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.184 [arXiv:1406.2531]. It is dominated by an old stellar population with no ongoing star formation, and it hosts compact radio emission likely associated with an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1406.2531].

sources.summary: The paper identifies source A as one of five compact dry-merger galaxies, with an SDSS redshift near 0.184, old stellar population, no ongoing star formation, and compact AGN-like radio emission.

quotes.quote: We have designated the five compact galaxies as A, B, C, D and E (Fig. 1). The sources were all photometrically identified by the SDSS, with objects A, B, C and D classified as galaxies and object E misclassified as a star. The SDSS DR10 spectra of sources A and B (Fig. 2) place the targets at a redshift of z = 0.184 and z = 0.185 (Table 1), respectively.

The results show that 95 of the (fibre) stellar mass (Table 5) was formed more than 5 Gyr ago; sources A and B are dominated by old stellar populations. Galaxy A, the brightest (Table 2 and 3) and also the most massive (Table 5) of the system, is the only one of the five sources possessing a radio detection (Fig. 4). The recovery of approximately 70 of the radio flux at high resolution (Fig. 4), indicates that the radio emission is very compact and therefore, since there is no evidence for a putative centrally concentrated star-forming region, it is likely related to the presence of an Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN).

gmw_00005658.name: source B
gmw_00005658.overview: Source B is a compact early-type galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.185 [arXiv:1406.2531]. It is part of a dry-merger system and features an old stellar population with no ongoing star formation [arXiv:1406.2531].

sources.summary: The paper identifies source B as a compact galaxy in the dry-merger system, with an SDSS redshift near 0.185, absorption-line spectrum, no ongoing star formation, and old stellar population.

quotes.quote: We have designated the five compact galaxies as A, B, C, D and E (Fig. 1). The sources were all photometrically identified by the SDSS, with objects A, B, C and D classified as galaxies and object E misclassified as a star. The SDSS DR10 spectra of sources A and B (Fig. 2) place the targets at a redshift of z = 0.184 and z = 0.185 (Table 1), respectively.

The spectra also show a red continuum, no conspicuous emission lines and absorption features (Mg and NaD) characteristic of early-type galaxies (Sect. 2.2). e., the extracted Star Formation Histories (SFHs), confirm the lack of on-going star formation in sources A and B. The results show that 95 of the (fibre) stellar mass (Table 5) was formed more than 5 Gyr ago; sources A and B are dominated by old stellar populations.

gmw_00005659.name: KK258
gmw_00005659.overview: KK258 is a nearby transition-type dwarf galaxy located at a distance of approximately 2.23 Mpc [arXiv:1406.4479]. This gas-poor dwarf galaxy has an absolute magnitude of -10.3 mag and exhibits properties typical of transition dwarfs [arXiv:1406.4479].

sources.summary: The paper presents HST observations of KK258, measuring its TRGB distance, velocity, star-formation history, luminosity, surface brightness, H I limit, and unusual isolation.

quotes.quote: Here we present observations with the Advanced Camera for Surveys on the Hubble Space Telescope of the nearby, transition-type dwarf galaxy KK258 = ESO468-020. We measure a distance of 2.23 0.05 Mpc using the Tip of Red Giant Branch method. We also detect emission from this gas-poor dwarf transition galaxy at the velocity V_h = 92 5 or V_ LG = 150 . With this distance and velocity, KK258 lies near the local Hubble flow locus with a peculiar velocity 3 .

We discuss the star formation history of KK258 derived from its colour- magnitude diagram. The specific star formation rate is estimated to be log [ sSFR ] = 2.64 and 2.84 (Gyr ^ 1 ) from the FUV-flux and flux, respectively. KK258 has the absolute magnitude M_B = -10.3 mag, the average surface brightness of 26.0 mag arcsec ^ 2 and the hydrogen mass log (M_ HI ) < 5.75 M_ . We compare KK258 with 29 other dTr- galaxies situated within 5 Mpc from us, and conclude that its properties are typical for transition dwarfs.

gmw_00005660.name: top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal1
gmw_00005660.overview: The top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal1 is a merging system that features a diffuse plume and a long tidal tail [arXiv:2208.14572]. It also contains a clear central dust lane that runs perpendicular to its tidal tails [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.summary: The paper describes this GOODS-N galaxy as a merging or radio-galaxy/starburst system with a diffuse plume, long tidal tail, and central dust lane visible across optical and infrared images.

quotes.quote: In the band, there is a diffuse plume above the galaxy and a long tidal tail downward to the left in the image (top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal1 ). This galaxy is inside the FOV, but the tidal debris is not clearly visible without smoothing the data, and is most prominent in the F160W. It has a clear dust lane through the center perpendicular to the tidal tails visible in the optical wavelengths.

gmw_00005661.name: Galaxy 2
gmw_00005661.overview: Galaxy 2 is an interacting system located at a redshift of approximately z = 0.375 [arXiv:2208.14572]. It exhibits clear signs of a recent interaction with at least two, and possibly three, companion galaxies, featuring visible tidal tails that link the central galaxy to its neighbors [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.summary: Galaxy 2 is presented as a recent multi-galaxy interaction with visible tidal tails linking the central galaxy to companions at about z=0.375.

quotes.quote: Galaxy 2 shows a clear recent interaction with at least two galaxies, possibly a third, with measured redshifts, z 0.375 (bottom images in Fig. figure:gal1 ). There is a tidal tail between the two galaxies in the bottom right, which is also visible in the LBT band. Another tidal tail in the opposite direction probably links the central galaxy to a third spiral galaxy in the upper left.

gmw_00005662.name: top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal3
gmw_00005662.overview: The top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal3 is an elliptical galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of 0.530 [arXiv:2208.14572]. It features a diffuse debris tail and associated diffuse flux that are visible from the V through H bands [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.summary: The paper identifies this elliptical galaxy at z=0.530 as having a diffuse debris tail and associated diffuse flux seen from V through H bands.

quotes.quote: An elliptical galaxy with a diffuse debris tail visible in the and partly in the band mosaic (top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal3 ). It has a z_ spec = 0.530 Moran2007 . The diffuse flux above the main galaxy has a similar photometric redshift to the main galaxy, and can been seen in the V to H-bands.

gmw_00005663.name: bottom set of images in Fig. figure:gal3
aliases_local: bottom set of images in Fig. figure:gal3
gmw_00005663.overview: This system at a redshift of 0.299 consists of at least three spiral galaxies of similar size involved in a Stephan's-Quintet-like interaction [arXiv:2208.14572]. Two of these galaxies are already merging, while a third gravitationally influences one of the visible tidal tails [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.original_name: bottom set of images in Fig. figure:gal3
sources.summary: This system is described as a Stephan's-Quintet-like interaction of at least three similar spiral galaxies, with two merging and another influencing a tidal tail.

quotes.quote: Reminiscent of the nearby system, Stephan's Quintet, there are at least three similar size spiral galaxies involved with this interaction (bottom set of images in Fig. figure:gal3 ). Two of the galaxies are already merging, while a third appears to be gravitationally influencing one of the tidal tails. In the imaging, the tidal arms are visible in all bands, and the system has z_ spec = 0.299 Wirth2004 .

gmw_00005664.name: galaxy 5
gmw_00005664.overview: Galaxy 5 is an interacting system resembling M51 that features a diffuse tidal stream linking it to a smaller companion galaxy [arXiv:2208.14572]. The system contains a central dust lane, and neither the main galaxy nor its companion exhibits detectable ultraviolet flux in their cores [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.summary: Galaxy 5 is described as an M51-like interacting system with a diffuse stream to a smaller companion, a central dust lane, and no UV flux in either core.

quotes.quote: Similar to M51, galaxy 5 (top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal5 ) has a diffuse tidal stream on the bottom right linking it to a smaller companion galaxy and with a z_ spec = 0.253 Wirth2004 . The images show a dust lane through the center of the galaxy and parallel to the diffuse stream. In the centers of both the main and the companion galaxies, no detectable flux is present in both the F336W and F275W filters.

gmw_00005665.name: bottom galaxy in Fig. figure:gal5
gmw_00005665.overview: The bottom galaxy in Fig. figure:gal5 is a face-on disk galaxy featuring a very bright center and a possible bar [arXiv:2208.14572]. It is surrounded by smooth, diffuse tidal debris that appears to loop around the galaxy [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.summary: This face-on disk galaxy is described as having a bright center, possible bar, and smooth diffuse tidal debris looping around it, visible only after careful imaging treatment.

quotes.quote: There is smooth diffuse tidal debris visible in the LBT to the right of the galaxy that appears to make a loop around the galaxy, but is not detectable in the LBT (bottom galaxy in Fig. figure:gal5 ). From the imaging, it appears as a face on disk galaxy with a very bright center and possible bar. The tidal debris is only marginally seen without smoothing.

gmw_00005666.name: top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal7
gmw_00005666.overview: The top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal7 is an edge-on disk galaxy identified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2208.14572]. It exhibits extra diffuse flux upward and to the left, and it has a possible grazing companion of approximately equal size [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.summary: The paper describes this edge-on AGN disk galaxy as showing diffuse flux and a possible equal-sized grazing companion in the imaging.

quotes.quote: An edge-on disk galaxy, which has been identified as an AGN (top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal7 ). It has extra diffuse flux upward and to the left, which is seen in both and U band. In addition, it has a possible approximately equal size grazing companion.

gmw_00005667.name: bottom galaxy in Fig. figure:gal7
gmw_00005667.overview: The bottom galaxy in Fig. figure:gal7 is a lenticular or spheroidal radio galaxy at a redshift of 0.440 [arXiv:2208.14572]. It is classified as a high-excitation narrow-line radio galaxy and exhibits smooth, diffuse debris to its left [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.summary: This z=0.440 S0/spheroid radio galaxy is described as having smooth diffuse debris visible in LBT imaging and a high-excitation narrow-line radio classification.

quotes.quote: There is smooth, diffuse debris to the left of the central galaxy seen in the LBT , while the only shows background objects (bottom galaxy in Fig. figure:gal7 ). The galaxy has z_ spec = 0.440 Wirth2004 . It has also been categorized as a S0/spheroid galaxy, which is confirmed by the images, as well as a high-excitation narrow-line radio galaxy Wirth2004 .

gmw_00005668.name: top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal9
gmw_00005668.overview: This elliptical-looking galaxy features stellar rings with visible star-forming clumps [arXiv:2208.14572]. It appears to be tidally disrupting and absorbing a smaller companion galaxy located to its lower left at the same redshift [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.summary: This elliptical-looking galaxy is described as ringed, clumpy, and apparently absorbing a smaller galaxy at the same redshift.

quotes.quote: Appearing as an elliptical galaxy, but it clearly has rings with recent star forming clumps visible in the images (top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal9 ). The small bright object to its lower left has the same redshift z_ spec = 0.377 Wirth2004 . It appears to be a smaller galaxy being tidally disrupted and absorbed by the larger galaxy.

gmw_00005669.name: bottom galaxy in Fig. figure:gal9
gmw_00005669.overview: The bottom galaxy in Fig. figure:gal9 is a distorted merger of at least two galaxies [arXiv:2208.14572]. The central regions of both galaxies remain visible, and a tidal tail curves around the system, giving it a shrimp-type classification [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.summary: The paper describes this object as a distorted merger of at least two galaxies with visible central regions and a curving tail, classifiable as a shrimp-type system.

quotes.quote: At least two merging galaxies with the central regions of each galaxy still visible, but distorted (bottom galaxy in Fig. figure:gal9 ). From the LBT observations, there appears to be a tail coming from top and curving around to the right. From the Elmegreen2007 designations, this galaxy could be categorized as a shrimp type based on its shape and features visible in the different imaging.

gmw_00005670.name: top system of galaxies in Fig. figure:gal11
aliases_local: top system of galaxies in Fig. figure:gal11
gmw_00005670.overview: The top system of galaxies in Fig. figure:gal11 is a galaxy system located at a redshift of z = 0.798 [arXiv:2208.14572]. It contains three red elliptical galaxies and a long, low-surface-brightness diffuse stream that possibly links to a fourth galaxy [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.original_name: top system of galaxies in Fig. figure:gal11
sources.summary: This z=0.798 system is described as three red elliptical galaxies, possibly linked to a fourth, with a long low-surface-brightness diffuse stream.

quotes.quote: This system appears to have 3 elliptical galaxies with a long diffuse stream toward the upper left corner linking to a possible 4th galaxy in the system (top system of galaxies in Fig. figure:gal11 ). With redshifts of z = 0.798 Treu2005 , these red galaxies are almost not detectable in the LBT band. The redder filters show the same diffuse stream visible in the LBT band at low surface brightness levels.

gmw_00005671.name: bottom galaxy in Fig. figure:gal11
gmw_00005671.overview: The bottom galaxy in Fig. figure:gal11 is an elliptical galaxy surrounded by diffuse plumes [arXiv:2208.14572]. The largest of these plumes extends toward the lower left, and the galaxy's diffuse flux is visible in I-band imaging [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.summary: This elliptical galaxy is described as surrounded by diffuse plumes, especially toward the lower left, with its extended flux mainly visible in I-band imaging.

quotes.quote: An elliptical galaxy surrounded by diffuse plumes with the most prominent plume to the lower left (bottom galaxy in Fig. figure:gal11 ). It is on the edge of FOV, and does not have any redshift information. Only the I-band (F814) image shows the entire region around the galaxy and there does appear to be diffuse flux around the galaxy in this filter.

gmw_00005672.name: Fig. figure:gal13
gmw_00005672.overview: Fig. figure:gal13 is an interacting galaxy system located at a redshift of z = 0.937 [arXiv:2208.14572]. Imaging of the galaxy reveals a second possible distinct core or a starburst clump near its top [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.summary: The paper highlights this z=0.937 interacting galaxy as imaging-confirmed, with a possible second core or starburst clump near the top of the galaxy.

quotes.quote: This is a good example of where imaging was critical to confirm that it is an interacting system (Fig. figure:gal13 ). Also, it is the highest redshift galaxy in our sample with z_ spec = 0.937 Barger2008 . The image shows a second possible distinct core at the top of the galaxy, or a starburst clump.

gmw_00005673.name: top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal14
gmw_00005673.overview: Located at a redshift of z = 0.277, this galaxy features long tidal streams reminiscent of the Antennae galaxy [arXiv:2208.14572]. The weak near-ultraviolet flux along these diffuse streams indicates that there is little recent star formation occurring within the tidal tails [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.summary: This z=0.277 galaxy is described as Antennae-like, with long tidal streams whose weak NUV flux suggests little recent star formation in the tails.

quotes.quote: A galaxy with long tidal streams reminiscent of the Antennae galaxy, but the central region is unresolved (top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal14 ). The diffuse tidal streams are not seen seen in the band, except for a few faint spots along the tails. At a redshift of z_ spec = 0.277 Casey2012 , the corresponds to NUV flux, which means there is little evidence of recent star formation in these tidal streams.

gmw_00005674.name: middle system of galaxies in Fig. figure:gal14
aliases_local: middle system of galaxies in Fig. figure:gal14
gmw_00005674.overview: The middle system of galaxies in Fig. figure:gal14 consists of two galaxies with extended tidal tails detected by the Large Binocular Telescope [arXiv:2208.14572]. The bottom large galaxy is interacting with a smaller companion to its left, while the top galaxy is associated with a radio source [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.original_name: middle system of galaxies in Fig. figure:gal14
sources.summary: This system is described as two galaxies with LBT-detected tidal tails, infrared detections, a VLA association for the top galaxy, and interaction involving a smaller companion.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies with extended flux in tidal tails seen in the LBT , but only the brightest clumps are in the tails are detectable in the band (middle system of galaxies in Fig. figure:gal14 ). The system was detected in the infrared by Herschel and Spitzer , and the top galaxy is associated with a radio source (VLA; Morrison2010 ). The bottom large galaxy is interacting with the smaller galaxy to the left, while the top galaxy has z_ spec = 0.456 Casey2012 .

gmw_00005675.name: bottom galaxy in Fig. figure:gal14
gmw_00005675.overview: The bottom galaxy in Fig. figure:gal14 is a very red early-type galaxy, likely classified as an elliptical or S0 type [arXiv:2208.14572]. It has a photometric redshift of 0.48 and is surrounded by diffuse plumes and an upper-left stream [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.summary: This very red zphot=0.48 elliptical/S0 candidate is described as faint in U band and surrounded by diffuse plumes plus an upper-left stream.

quotes.quote: A very red galaxy, probably elliptical/S0 type galaxy, which is only barley visible in the LBT U band. This galaxy is surrounded by diffuse plumes and has a stream to the upper left (bottom galaxy in Fig. figure:gal14 ). From the literature, it has a photometric redshift of z_ phot = 0.48 Rafferty2011 .

gmw_00005676.name: top galaxy system in Fig. figure:gal17
aliases_local: top galaxy system in Fig. figure:gal17
gmw_00005676.overview: The top galaxy system in Fig. figure:gal17 consists of two large irregular galaxies [arXiv:2208.14572]. Multiple physically associated star-forming clumps surround these galaxies, and the larger galaxy displays an M51-like bridge to a smaller neighbor [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.original_name: top galaxy system in Fig. figure:gal17
sources.summary: This low-redshift system is described as two large irregular galaxies with associated star-forming clumps and an M51-like bridge to a smaller galaxy.

quotes.quote: Two large irregular galaxies just outside the FOV (top galaxy system in Fig. figure:gal17 ). There are multiple star forming clumps around the two galaxies, which are most likely physically associated based on their colors. The larger galaxy on the right side appears to have a M51 like bridge with the small galaxy to it's right.

gmw_00005677.name: middle galaxy in Fig. figure:gal17
gmw_00005677.overview: The middle galaxy in Fig. figure:gal17 is a galaxy at a spectroscopic redshift of 0.560 [arXiv:2208.14572]. It features a large extended shell of diffuse light that is absent in the U band, which may indicate a recent merger [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.summary: The paper describes this z=0.560 galaxy as having a large diffuse shell, absent in U band, that may indicate a recent merger.

quotes.quote: In the there is a large extended shell of diffuse light around the galaxy (middle galaxy in Fig. figure:gal17 ), which is not seen in the U band. This galaxy has z_ spec = 0.560 Albareti2017 . The diffuse shell could be a signature of a recent merger.

gmw_00005678.name: top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal20
gmw_00005678.overview: The top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal20 is surrounded by rings of diffuse light [arXiv:2208.14572]. Its outermost ring is visible only in the LBT band and may indicate a recent merger, though there is no sign of interaction with nearby galaxies [arXiv:2208.14572].

sources.summary: This galaxy is described as surrounded by diffuse rings, including an outer ring visible only in LBT imaging and possibly signaling a recent merger.

quotes.quote: Another galaxy with rings of diffuse light surrounding it (top galaxy in Fig. figure:gal20 ). The outer most ring is only seen in the LBT band, and could be a sign of a recent merger. There is no indication of interaction with any other galaxies nearby it, and no available redshift information.

gmw_00005679.name: SDSS J080304.74+220734.0
gmw_00005679.overview: SDSS J080304.74+220734.0 is a newborn active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z=0.1245 [arXiv:2402.19403]. While archival observations classified it as a star-forming galaxy, recent spectra reveal the emergence of broad Balmer lines and accretion disk emission [arXiv:2402.19403].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J080304.74+220734.0 as a newborn AGN whose spectra, optical rise, MIR colors, and variable X-rays indicate a transition from star-forming to active.

quotes.quote: The galaxy SDSS J080304.74+220734.0 (ZTF ID: ZTF20aaglfpy https://alerce.online/object/ZTF20aaglfpy , z=0.1245 ) showed the most significant change of activity and is presented in detail below. A spectrum of ZTF20aaglfpy taken in MJD 52943 (2003-10-31, plate 1584) with the SDSS spectrograph led to a Galaxy classification. This spectrum showed no evidence for broad emission lines, and the ratios of its narrow emission lines corresponded to the star forming portions of the BPT diagnostics diagrams.

The archival SDSS spectrum was well fitted with a combination of stellar populations and one template of narrow emission lines. For the SOAR spectrum, we took the stellar template fitted to the SDSS spectrum, with a variable normalization and allowed all other parameters to vary. The resulting fit requires the addition of broad Balmer lines and a powerlaw of negative slope indicating the relevance of the accretion disk emission. The middle panels in Fig. fig:ZTF20aaglfpy_spectrum show the spectral regions around H -O [ III ] and H -NII--SII, for the old SDSS spectrum (left) and the new SOAR spectrum (right), plotted in black, together with the best-fitting model in cyan, after subtraction of the continuum. The broad H and H lines, marked in green, are only required in the new spectrum.

gmw_00005680.name: LEDA 3080069
gmw_00005680.overview: LEDA 3080069 is a dwarf irregular galaxy located in the low-latitude Galactic neighborhood [arXiv:2405.04898]. It acts as a "bullet" galaxy that has collided with the spiral galaxy ESO 179-13, triggering intense, patchy star formation across its structure [arXiv:2405.04898].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LEDA 3080069 as Kathryn's Wheel's bullet dwarf irregular galaxy, with intense collision-triggered star formation and a limited contribution to the total gamma-ray/SFR budget.

quotes.quote: Located in the low-latitude Galactic neighborhood ( |b|=-8^ , distance 10 Mpc), the system includes a dwarf irregular galaxy, LEDA 3080069, acting as the `bullet' that has collided with an edge-on, late-type, spiral galaxy ESO 179 13 e.g., 1987ess..book.....L,2015MNRAS.452.3759P .

The continuum subtracted H image has revealed that the `bullet' galaxy, LEDA 3080069, is undergoing intense star formation, which is likely triggered due to collision with ESO 179 13 Figure fig:2 , see also, 2015MNRAS.452.3759P . In Figure fig:bullet , we show its high-resolution V band image taken with the VIsible MultiObject Spectrograph (VIMOS, now decommissioned) at Very Large Telescope (VLT, proposal id: 099.B-0215, PI: A. A. Zijlstra). Several patchy star-forming regions are visible, and the overall morphology is consistent, with this object being a dwarf irregular galaxy.

These results also rule out the possibility of the ray emission originating from the star-forming regions in the `bullet' galaxy LEDA 3080069 alone. This is because it has 24 contribution to the total SFR 2015MNRAS.452.3759P which would push the source even farther from the best-fit correlation (Figure fig:corr ).

gmw_00005681.name: PHL 6625
gmw_00005681.overview: PHL 6625 is a luminous quasi-stellar object located at a redshift of z = 0.3954 [arXiv:1707.02042]. It is currently undergoing an early-stage minor merger with a tidally distorted companion galaxy that appears as an arc structure [arXiv:1707.02042].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PHL 6625 as a luminous QSO whose host is interacting with an arc companion in a likely minor merger, with broad emission lines, a massive black hole, and quasar luminosity.

quotes.quote: PHL 6625 is a luminous quasi-stellar object (QSO) at z = 0.3954 located behind the nearby galaxy NGC 247 ( z = 0.0005 ). Hubble Space Telescope (HST) observations revealed an arc structure associated with it. We report on spectroscopic observations with the Very Large Telescope (VLT) and multiwavelength observations from the radio to the X-ray band for the system, suggesting that PHL 6625 and the arc are a close pair of merging galaxies, instead of a strong gravitational lens system. The QSO host galaxy is estimated to be (4-28) 10^ 10 M_ , and the mass of the companion galaxy of is estimated to be M_ = (6.8 2.4) 10^ 9 M_ , suggesting that this is a minor merger system.

The consistent redshifts of the two galaxies suggest that they are a merger event, between a luminous QSO (PHL 6625) and a tidally distorted companion galaxy, instead of a strong gravitational lens system. The stellar content of the two galaxies are not in contact yet, and the mass fraction of young stars is less than 1 (Figure fig:arc_sfh ), suggesting they are at the early stage of a merging process. Such a system seems to be an analogue of the nearby event Arp 142 (NGC 2936/37) in morphology e.g., Romano2008 , except that the central black hole in PHL 6625 is an active quasar.

gmw_00005682.name: Pegasus IV
gmw_00005682.overview: Pegasus IV is a compact, ultra-faint dwarf galaxy located at a heliocentric distance of approximately 90 kpc [arXiv:2203.11788]. It is a dark-matter-dominated system with an extremely low systemic metallicity, and it follows an elliptical, retrograde orbit [arXiv:2203.11788].

sources.summary: The paper announces Pegasus IV as a compact ultra-faint dwarf galaxy, measuring its distance, velocity dispersion, metallicity, mass-to-light ratio, proper motion, orbit, and possible distant RR Lyrae member.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of Pegasus IV, an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy found in archival data from the Dark Energy Camera processed by the DECam Local Volume Exploration Survey. Pegasus IV is a compact, ultra-faint stellar system ( r_ 1/2 = 41^ +8 _ 6 pc; M_V = -4.25 0.2 mag) located at a heliocentric distance of 90^ +4 _ 6 . Based on spectra of seven non-variable member stars observed with Magellan/IMACS, we confidently resolve Pegasus IV's velocity dispersion, measuring _ v = 3.3^ +1.7 _ 1.1   km s ^ 1 (after excluding three velocity outliers); this implies a mass-to-light ratio of M_ 1/2 /L_ V,1/2 = 167^ +224 _ 99 M_ /L_ for the system. From the five stars with the highest signal-to-noise spectra, we also measure a systemic metallicity of Fe/H = -2.67^ +0.25 _ 0.29 dex, making Pegasus IV one of the most metal-poor ultra-faint dwarfs.

These measurements provide strong evidence that Pegasus IV is a dark-matter-dominated dwarf galaxy, rather than a star cluster. We measure Pegasus IV's proper motion using data from Gaia Early Data Release 3, finding ( _ , _ ) = (0.33 0.07, -0.21 0.08) mas yr ^ 1 . When combined with our measured systemic velocity, this proper motion suggests that Pegasus IV is on an elliptical, retrograde orbit, and is currently near its orbital apocenter.

gmw_00005683.name: Leo A
gmw_00005683.overview: Leo A is an isolated dwarf irregular galaxy located in the Local Group [arXiv:1808.06027]. It is a low-mass system that exhibits recent star formation and possesses an extremely metal-poor interstellar medium [arXiv:1808.06027].

sources.summary: The paper presents Leo A as an isolated, low-mass Local Group dwarf irregular with recent star formation and very low ISM metallicity confirmed from H II-region and PN abundance modeling.

quotes.quote: Leo A, with coordinates (J2000.0) = 09:59:24.8, (J2000.0) = +30:44:57, is an isolated dIrr galaxy in the Local Group, located at about 800 kpc from the Milky Way and at 1200 kpc from M31, and it presents a radial velocity of 22.3 km s ^ 1 DolphinSaha2002,McConnachie2012 . Leo A is a low mass galaxy with M _ dyn 2.5 10 ^7 M _ , M _ stars = 6 10 ^6 M _ and M _ HI =1.1 10 ^7 M _ and references therein McConnachie2012 and shows recent star formation. Its stellar population contains old and young population components, although it conspicuously lacks a prominent ancient population, older than 10 Gyr TolstoyGallagher1998 .

Thus, it is confirmed that Leo A is one of the most metal poor galaxies in the Local Group, where its present ISM has 12+log(O/H) 7.4 0.2. Conclusively this galaxy has evolved very slowly in agreement with the star formation history derived by ColeSkillman2007 which includes initial 5 Gyr of quiescence. The log(N/O) = -1.6 is extremely low compared with the values for evolved galaxies as the Milky Way which presents log(N/O) of about -0.8 EstebanGarcia-Rojas2018 , but it is consistent with values found in irregular galaxies.

gmw_00005684.name: SDSS J125013.84+073444.5
gmw_00005684.overview: SDSS J125013.84+073444.5 is a starburst galaxy [arXiv:2303.13858, arXiv:1803.07207] that hosts a massive, clumpy rotating disc [arXiv:2303.13858]. The galaxy exhibits a disturbed morphology with a northeast tail [arXiv:1803.07207], driven by an extremely gravitationally unstable gas disc [arXiv:2303.13858].

sources.summary: The paper studies LARS 8 as a massive clumpy local galaxy whose unstable gas disc drives large-scale clump formation, short outer-disc depletion times, and suppressed central star formation.

quotes.quote: Using new high-resolution data of CO (2--1), H and H obtained with the Northern Extended Millimeter Array (NOEMA) and the Multi-Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) at the Very Large Telescope, we have performed a Toomre Q disc stability analysis and studied star formation, gas depletion times and other environmental parameters on sub-kpc scales within the z 0 galaxy SDSS J125013.84+073444.5 (LARS 8). The galaxy hosts a massive, clumpy disc and is a proto-typical analogue of main-sequence galaxies at z 1-2 . We show that the massive (molecular) clumps in LARS 8 are the result of an extremely gravitationally unstable gas disc, with large scale instabilities found across the whole extent of the rotating disc, with only the innermost 500 pc being stabilized by its bulgelike structure.

The disc of LARS 8 is highly unstable with Q<< 1 and has relatively short gas depletion times. The identified diffuse molecular structures, however, are virialized and thus similar to GMCs in the Milky Way or nearby galaxies. Hence, the short gas depletion times in the disc cannot be explained by local (sub-kpc) effects such as a higher local star formation efficiency, but must be triggered by large-scale processes that cause the formation of more massive and denser molecular clumps.

sources.summary: LARS08 is described from resampled HST and K-band data, showing a disturbed morphology, a northeast tail, and older-looking colors under low dust attenuation.

quotes.quote: LARS08 All optical data are HST images resampled to the K band. The HST data covers only the central regions of the galaxy. In panel D of Fig. fig:lars08 we therefore show the K image instead of a B band image in order to show the tail to the north-east. The three K band contours in panel D are at 20.8 , 22.8 , and 24.2 mag/arcsec ^2 . While the morphology is clearly disturbed, the radial color profiles, RGB image, and U-B color maps show a seemingly older stellar population, assuming that the dust attenuation is negligible.

gmw_00005685.name: J2255+0251
gmw_00005685.overview: J2255+0251 is a low-luminosity, high-redshift quasar host galaxy [arXiv:2401.08914]. Its stellar host was detected using the JWST NIRCam instrument, with half-light radius estimates ranging between approximately 0.5 and 3 kpc [arXiv:2401.08914].

sources.summary: The paper compares J2255+0251 high-redshift quasar host with BlueTides analogs, finding consistent host mass/radius/black-hole properties and predicting high star formation but difficult future detection.

quotes.quote: The hosts of two low-luminosity high-z quasars, J2255+0251 and J2236+0032, were recently detected using JWST's NIRCam instrument. These represent the first high-z quasar host galaxy stellar detections and open a new window into studying high-z quasars. We examine the implications of the measured properties of J2255+0251 and J2236+0032 within the context of the hydrodynamic simulation BlueTides at z = 6.5.

J2255+0251 is only detected in one filter (F356W) and has only one estimate for R_ 1/2 . These values are tabulated in Table tab:quasar_prob and Table tab:eiger and range between approximately 0.5 to 3 kpc. In Figure fig:rad_prop_bol , we compare the half light radii estimates of BlueTides galaxies from marshall_2022_dust against their bolometric luminosity.

gmw_00005686.name: J2236+0032
gmw_00005686.overview: J2236+0032 is a low-luminosity, high-redshift quasar host galaxy [arXiv:2401.08914]. Its central supermassive black hole has a mass that exceeds predictions for quasars of comparable bolometric luminosity [arXiv:2401.08914].

sources.summary: The paper compares J2236+0032 JWST-detected high-redshift quasar host with BlueTides, finding broadly consistent host properties and easier predicted detectability than J2255+0251.

quotes.quote: The hosts of two low-luminosity high-z quasars, J2255+0251 and J2236+0032, were recently detected using JWST's NIRCam instrument. These represent the first high-z quasar host galaxy stellar detections and open a new window into studying high-z quasars. We examine the implications of the measured properties of J2255+0251 and J2236+0032 within the context of the hydrodynamic simulation BlueTides at z = 6.5.

J2236+0032 has a black hole mass above that predicted by BlueTides for quasars with similar bolometric luminosities. The EIGER quasars J159-02 and J1120+0641 also fall within the expected black hole mass range, but the most massive quasar, J0148+0600, has a mass of 10^ 9.892 M_ which is above the mass limit of BlueTides. Stellar mass and SFR Figures fig:stellar_mass_sub_1 through fig:stellar_mass_sub_3 show the joint distribution of black hole masses and luminosity with stellar mass.

gmw_00005687.name: NGC 454
gmw_00005687.overview: NGC 454 is a strongly interacting physical pair of galaxies undergoing a mixed merger [arXiv:1708.02927, arXiv:1803.03967]. The eastern component is a distorted S0 lenticular galaxy with dust lanes and a Seyfert-like nucleus [arXiv:1708.02927, arXiv:2308.16152], while the western component is a highly distorted, starbursting disk galaxy remnant [arXiv:1708.02927, arXiv:2308.16152].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 454 E as the early-type member of the pair, likely an S0 with dust lanes, interaction-driven distortions, disk structure, and a Seyfert-like nucleus.

quotes.quote: The East part of the system, (labeled E in Figure figure1 , NGC 454 E hereafter), identifies the early-type member of the pair. NGC 454 E is crossed by dust lanes and it is distorted by the interaction. The U, B, V Johnson and Gunn I photometry by Johansson1988 presented the East member as a red elliptical with a luminosity profile that follows closely an ^ 1/4 law deVauc48 out to 15 \ from the galaxy center. The Stiavelli1998 high resolution HST imaging, in the F450W, F606W, and F814W filters, shows that NGC 454 E is likely an S0. Their luminosity profile, extending out to 30 , is much better fitted by two components: an ^ 1/4 law describing the bulge plus an exponential law Freeman1970 for a disk.

The nucleus of NGC 454 E, observed spectroscopically by Johansson1988 , revealed several emission lines and matched two of the empirical criteria proposed by Shuder1981 for a Seyfert galaxy: the line-width of H is larger than 300 km s ^ 1 and the [ OIII ] 5007 /H ratio is larger than 3. However, none of the high-excitation lines expected in this case, as HeII, were detected see also Donzelli2000,Tanvuia2003 . The AGN type of the nucleus has been recently detailed by Marchese2012 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 454 W as a distorted starburst/disk-galaxy debris component whose UV and H-alpha emission reveal connected star-forming complexes and interaction-driven structure.

quotes.quote: The West region of the pair labeled in Figure figure1 as W (NGC 454 W hereafter) has been considered by Johansson1988 as the debris of an irregular galaxy. However, the galaxy is so widely distorted by the on-going interaction that the classification is difficult. Stiavelli1998 suggested that it is the debris of a disk galaxy. NGC 454 W is a starburst galaxy, as shown by the H image of their Figure 6a and 6b Johansson1988 . The spectrum of NGC 454 W shows emission lines whose ratios, according to the above authors, are due to photo-ionization by star formation and shock heating.

Images show that the NGC 454 W emission dominates in the NUV bands. In NUV NGC 454 W1- W6 complexes appear included in a unique envelope which elongates up to NGC 454 SW and SE. In NUV two complexes, already revealed in H by Johansson1988 , are projected between NGC 454 SE and NGC 454 W.

sources.summary: NGC454 is presented as a strongly interacting two-component system, with distorted western debris, an early-type eastern companion, past star formation, and enhanced tidal-tail sSFR.

quotes.quote: NGC454 NGC454 is a strongly interacting system and is composed of two components. The western component NGC454W shows a huge amount of hot and massive star formation seen as bright knots in FUV and H . However, the eastern companion, NGC454E, is an early type galaxy and faint in H and FUV (Fig. fig:NGC454 ). NGC454NED02 (NGC454E) contains an old stellar population and dust lanes and is bright in NIR.

The western component NGC454W is highly distorted due to the interaction. It was classified as the debris of an irregular galaxy by Johansson1988A&A...191...29J while Stiavelli1998ApJ...492L.135S using high-resolution Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) images showed that it is the debris of a disk galaxy. Using smooth particle hydrodynamic simulations, Plana2017MNRAS.472.3074P suggested that the system is 12.4 Gyr old and is the result of interaction between two halos having equal mass and perpendicular spin. NGC454 shows previous episodes of star formation.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 454 as a nearby wet interacting pair, combining UV, optical, H-alpha kinematics, and simulations to understand the merger between its early- and late-type components.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage This paper focuses on NGC 454, a nearby interacting pair of galaxies (AM0112-554, RR23), composed of an early-type (NGC 454 E) and a star forming late-type companion (NGC 454 W). We aim at characterizing this wet merger candidate via a multi- analysis, from near-UV to optical using SWIFT-- UVOT , and mapping the H intensity ( I ) distribution, velocity ( V_r ), and velocity dispersion ( ) fields with SAM+Perot-Fabry@SOAR observations.

The subject of the present study is the NGC 454 system, a strongly peculiar, interacting AM 0112-554; AM1987 and isolated RR23; RR95 pair in the Southern Hemisphere. Johansson1988 described the system as ``a pair of emission-line galaxies in close interaction, or in the early-stage of a merger, consisting of a large elliptical and a blue irregular galaxy".

Table table1 summarizes some basic characteristics of NGC 454 E and W which appear as a prototype of an encounter/merger ( V _ hel =1 2 km s ^ 1 Tanvuia2003 ) between a late and an early-type galaxy, this latter having an active and peculiar Seyfert-like nucleus. Therefore, the study of this system can make progresses in our understanding of the effects of a wet interaction. Our contribution consists of two correlated pieces of observational information: (1) the investigation of the multi-wavelength structure of NGC 454 E via the optical and UV surface photometry and (2) the analysis of the kinematics of the ionized gas component.

sources.summary: The paper describes the NGC 454 system as a physical mixed-merger pair with distorted early- and late-type members, nearly null velocity separation, a disk, and S0 early-type component.

quotes.quote: We also analysed the NGC 454 system RR23 in the catalogue of Southern isolated pairs of RR95 which unlike NGC 3447/3447A system, is a physical pair. This is composed of a distorted early-type galaxy and a completely damaged late-type galaxy so that it is considered an example of on-going mixed merger.

The two members in the NGC 454 system, nearly superposed in projection, result to have a virtually null velocity separation. The velocity field of the late-type member is characterised by a small velocity gradient without any rotation pattern. We used UVOT observations to the SED of the system towards the near ultraviolet (see Fig. fig:5 ) and to demonstrate quantitatively that NGC 454 has a disk: S ersic indices run from n_ W2 =1.09 to n_ V =1.79 . The early-type member of the NGC 454 system is a bona fide S0. The global properties of this system, that is its total B-band absolute magnitude, multi-wavelength SED, and morphology, are well matched by an on-going major merger between halos with a mass ratio 1:1 and perpendicular spin.

gmw_00005688.name: MaNGA 1-166919
gmw_00005688.overview: MaNGA 1-166919 is a face-on green-valley galaxy at a redshift of approximately 0.07 [arXiv:1610.01602, arXiv:2105.07335]. It hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus that drives a kpc-scale outflow, which produces shocks in the interstellar medium and affects local star formation [arXiv:1610.01602, arXiv:2105.07335].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the Blob Source as a face-on MaNGA AGN host where GMOS reveals nuclear non-circular gas motions and radio/shock evidence for an AGN-driven outflow.

quotes.quote: Although originally not classified as an AGN in the SDSS classification based on the single-fibre spectrum, the spatially resolved MaNGA observations of MaNGA 1-166919 clearly show that a strongly ionizing source is residing in this galaxy. We hereafter dub the target the `Blob Source'. The galaxy stellar disk is observed close to face-on and its redshift is z = 0.0722 .

The MaNGA data of the Blob Source reveals a spherically symmetric region with a radius of 3 arcsec of AGN/LINER-dominated emission (Figure manga_data ). Since the [ OIII ] and H _ emission lines are not detected in the GMOS data due to a low signal-to-noise ratio, we use the H line to identify peculiarities in the gas velocity field. The MaNGA H velocity field shows ordered rotation of the gaseous disk of the host galaxy, which is the dominant effect even though the galaxy is viewed close to face-on (upper left panel in Figure GMOS_blob ).

The Blob galaxy was detected within the Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty-Centimeters FIRST, Becker_1995 survey at 1.4 GHz as an unresolved source with F _ 1.4 GHz = 2.6 mJy. If the dominant source of the shocks in the Blob galaxy was SF, then SF is also expected to explain the amount of radio emission observed in this source.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes MaNGA 1-166919 as a green-valley AGN host where a low-luminosity AGN drives a kpc-scale outflow that shocks gas and affects nearby star formation.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we do so by analyzing recent radio and optical integral field unit (IFU) spectroscopic observations of MaNGA 1-166919. Our results indicate that the bi-conical outflow in this galaxy is powered by a low-luminosity, low-Eddington ratio AGN ejecting material that drives 100-200 km s ^ 1 shocks into the surrounding interstellar medium (ISM) -- producing the hot, ionized gas and relativistic particles associated with the observed outflow. The energetics of the relativistic and ionized gas material produced at this shock are comparable, and both the mass outflow and kinetic power of the ionized gas in this outflow are higher than other AGN with similar bolometric luminosities. Lastly, while the host galaxy's total star formation rate is comparable to that of other star-forming galaxies with a similar stellar mass, there is evidence that the outflow both suppresses and enhances star formation in its immediate surroundings.

In this paper we present a detailed study of the radio and optical emission of MaNGA 1 166919 (Figure fig:desi_img ), a fairly nearby ( z 0.07 , Table tab:galprop ) galaxy whose optical colors suggest it lies within the ``green valley'' (see Figure fig:cmd ). Such galaxies are believed to be transitioning from the ``blue'' (star-forming) cloud to the ``red'' (quiescent) sequence, possibly as a result of a large scale outflow removing and/or reheating the gas needed to form additional generations of stars. A previous study of this galaxy by Wylezalek2017 , showed that it indeed hosts such an outflow. As demonstrated below, analyzing the multi-properties of the outflow and host galaxies provides important insight into how the outflow is produced by the central AGN and how it interacts with the surrounding galaxy.

gmw_00005689.name: NGC 1332
gmw_00005689.overview: NGC 1332 is an early-type lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1605.01346, arXiv:0906.0576] containing an intermediate-scale stellar disk [arXiv:1502.07024] and a possible end-on bar [arXiv:2303.01495]. It hosts a highly inclined circumnuclear dust disk [arXiv:1605.01346] with rotating molecular gas [arXiv:1707.08229] surrounding a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:1605.01346, arXiv:1906.06267].

sources.summary: ALMA detects bright CO in NGC 1332 coincident with its dust disk, clear nuclear rotation, and a central high-velocity PVD rise.

quotes.quote: The bright CO(2 1) emission seen in NGC 1332 , NGC 1380 , NGC 3258 , NGC 3268 , and NGC 6861 directly coincides with optically thick dust disks as seen in the structure maps of the corresponding optical HST images. Inspecting their image cubes reveals clear rotation about the nuclear continuum sources. To visualize their rotational properties, we integrated the flux densities in each channel over the elliptical regions coinciding with the optical dust disks. These CO velocity profiles (Figure  pvd_velchan ) exhibit nearly symmetric double-horned features characteristic of rotating disks with emission ranging between 300 and 500 km s ^ 1 from v_ sys .

We also constructed a position-velocity diagram (PVD; also shown in Figure  pvd_velchan ) for each galaxy by extracting a slice along the major axis as determined from the average disk position angle (measured in kin ). The extraction widths were set to the projected CLEAN beam FWHM along the disk major axis. A central rise in the PVD envelope to high velocity at small radius, which indicates a massive, compact object at the disk center, is unambiguously observed only in NGC 1332 and NGC 3258 , with a marginal detection in NGC 1380 within the central 0 1 . The PVD for NGC 6861 shows a 2 wide hole in CO emission.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1332 has butterfly-like higher-order kinematic patterns, suggests an end-on bar, and links this possibility to dynamical mass-estimate biases.

quotes.quote: NGC 1332 is a particularly interesting case: here, slope changes around |v_ rot / | 0.5 result in a transition from v_ rot h_3 correlation to anti-correlation, which then - via the oscillation of coefficients - reverberates to h_5 and to a lesser extend, h_7 . The region of v_ rot , h_3 correlation within |v_ rot / | 0.5 is also very noticeable in the h_3 map for the galaxy (see Fig. fig:FRkinmap ), forming a ``butterfly-shape'', that, as we will argue later on, could be indicative of the presence of a bar component.

One of the most striking kinematic patterns that we found for the kinematic maps which we produced for our sample are doubtlessly the ones for NGC 1332 (See Fig. fig:FRkinmap ): The h_3 map shows x-shaped or butterfly-like regions of v_ rot h_3 correlation at roughly 45 angles to the major axis of the galaxy, interrupting the usual v_ rot h_3 anti-correlation regions at smaller and larger radii. At the same time, we find two lobes of lowered h_4 for the central regions of the h_4 map, as well as extended handles or lobes of relatively heightened along the major axis in the map. These patterns look surprisingly similar to the 2D kinematic signatures of boxy/peanut bulges in simulated disk galaxies from Iannuzzi2015 : The x-shaped v_ rot h_3 correlation regions in particular seem to be a good match for maps of an end-on bar shown in Figure 29 of Iannuzzi2015 for a disk inclination of i = 80 ^ . Intriguingly, a similar inclination of i 85 ^ has been found by ALMA observations of the circumnuclear disk of NGC 1332 by Barth2015 . We therefore cautiously suggest the presence of an end on bar in NGC 1332.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA CO observations of NGC 1332's circumnuclear disk and models its regular gas rotation to measure a central black hole mass.

quotes.quote: We present Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) Cycle 3 observations of CO(2--1) emission from the circumnuclear disk in the E/S0 galaxy NGC 1332 at 0 044 resolution. The disk exhibits regular rotational kinematics and central high-velocity emission ( 500 ) consistent with the presence of a compact central mass. We construct models for a thin, dynamically cold disk in the gravitational potential of the host galaxy and black hole, and fit the beam-smeared model line profiles directly to the ALMA data cube. Model fits successfully reproduce the disk kinematics out to =200 pc.

NGC 1332 is a nearby S0 or E galaxy that contains a highly inclined circumnuclear dust disk. Its BH mass has been measured previously via stellar dynamics rusli2011 and by modeling the hydrostatic equilibrium of its X-ray emitting halo humphrey2009 , making it an excellent laboratory for direct comparison of independent measurement techniques.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1332 as a relaxed X-ray luminous lenticular galaxy and uses hydrostatic X-ray analysis to make its first direct SMBH mass measurement.

quotes.quote: NGC 1332 is an X-ray luminous lenticular galaxy with a relaxed X-ray morphology and a centrally peaked gas temperature profile buote96a,humphrey04b . Of the three galaxies, only NGC 1332 does not have a published \ measurement derived from dynamical methods.

For NGC 1332, our results constitute the first direct measurement of the SMBH mass, and so in Fig fig_mbh we compare it to the \ relation rather than a measurement derived from stellar dynamics. We find consistency; the X-ray determined \ clearly does not lie significantly above this relation.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1332 as an early-type galaxy with an intermediate-scale disk that leaves its galaxy size only slightly above 2 kpc; it uses bulge parameters for the compact spheroid.

quotes.quote: The intermediate-scale disk in NGC 1332, which does not dominate at large radii like large-scale disks do, is such that the size of this galaxy is only slightly larger than 2 kpc (Savorgnan et al. \ 2015, in preparation). However here we include the bulge rather than the galaxy parameters for NGC 1332.

As noted in Section Sec_data , NGC 1277, NGC 1332, NGC 5493 and NGC 5845 are examples of local early-type galaxies where the disk does not dominate the light at large radii, as in proto-typical lenticular galaxies. These disks are an order of magnitude larger than nuclear disks, and referred to as intermediate-sized disks.

sources.summary: The paper finds an almost fourfold central mass-to-light increase in NGC 1332 from combined disk and bulge components, with central values consistent with prior Barth models.

quotes.quote: NGC 1332 : We find a significant, almost factor-of-four increase towards the center of this galaxy from the superposition of the disk and bulge components. The central parts of this gradient ( 0.3 kpc ) have a slightly larger than our constant- models from R+11. Over most of the galaxy's spatial extent, however, our new models produce significantly lower . Our central is furthermore in agreement with the models by Barth2015 for the central 0.2 kpc .

sources.summary: The paper names NGC 1332 as a brightest early-type subgroup member and known X-ray source, with ROSAT imaging centered on it but no diffuse emission detected.

quotes.quote: The three sub-groups namely NGC 1407, NGC 1332, and NGC 1395 have their brightest members as an elliptical or an S0.

The optically bright early type galaxies NGC 1400, NGC 1407, NGC 1395, and NGC 1332 are known X-ray sources in this group. Trinchieri et al. (2000) reported the presence of diffuse X-ray emission around NGC 1407. The processed and calibrated X-ray images (0.1 keV - 2.0 keV) centered at NGC 1407, NGC 1395, and NGC 1332 were obtained from the ROSAT PSPC (Roentgen Satellite Position Sensitive Proportional Counter) archival data.
No diffuse emission is seen around NGC 1332 (not shown here).

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 1332 as an early-type ALMA black-hole target, noting central CO behavior and a CO-based mass consistent with X-ray gas modeling but below stellar dynamics.

quotes.quote: Their central CO properties can be divided into three regimes: (1) those with no line emission from within \ (i.e., due to large holes that may or may not be resolved); (2) those that show slight central upturns in emission-line velocities e.g., NGC 1332; bar16a , indicating the CO-bright gas does not populate very deep within ; and (3) those that exhibit very strong central velocity upturns, tracing quasi-Keplerian rotation.

For NGC 1332, our measurement of \ from high-resolution ALMA CO(2 1) data indicated a mass of = 6.6 10^8 \ with 10 model-fitting uncertainty bar16a , more than a factor of two smaller than the value of \ derived from stellar-dynamical modeling rus11 . The CO-based measurement was consistent, however, with an earlier determination of \ based on the hydrostatic equilibrium of the X-ray emitting gas in NGC 1332 hum09 .

gmw_00005690.name: GMP 3016
gmw_00005690.overview: GMP 3016 is a faint, extremely blue irregular dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1005.3874, arXiv:2209.05886] and a confirmed member of the Coma Cluster [arXiv:1005.3874, arXiv:2111.01140]. It features a detached northeastern ultraviolet emission tail [arXiv:2209.05886] with detected hydrogen-alpha emission [arXiv:2111.01140], while its nearly face-on main stellar disk lacks active star formation [arXiv:1006.4867, arXiv:2111.01140].

sources.summary: GMP3016 is a confirmed Coma member with an emission tail, no apparent parent-galaxy emission, faint blue-dwarf status, and fireball features.

quotes.quote: Two of the three galaxies, GMP3016 and GMP4232, are members, which is confirmed by Hectospec spectroscopy, and had \ tails as shown in Fig 4.

3 detached \ clouds: GMP2923, GMP3016, GMP4060, and GMP4232 have no apparent \ emission in the parent galaxy, only outside the galaxy. The color of the parent is very blue ( g-=0.27-0.30 ), and the magnitude is very faint ( > 16.8). The four galaxies are in fact the four least luminous galaxies among the parents. GMP3016 and GMP4060 show fireball features: \ emission following knots of young blue stars Yoshida2008 .

Their observed field overlapped with our field, and there were six galaxies in common (GMP2559, GMP2910, GMP3016, GMP3816, GMP4060, and GMP4232). It is striking that the UV asymmetric galaxies in our fields are only the six.

sources.summary: GMP 3016 is a faint irregular galaxy with an uncertain UV/u-band trail, a projected companion along the same axis, no clear H star formation, and possible chance alignment.

quotes.quote: GMP 3016 This is among the less convincing cases in the sample. GMP 3016 itself is a faint irregular galaxy. About 0.5 arcmin (15 kpc) to the north-east, there is an elongated UV source which points towards the galaxy which could be a trail similar to the case of GMP 2910. In the Adami u band imaging, the `trail' appears to extend up to 1 arcmin (30 kpc), with numerous clumps. A companion is seen in projection to the south-west, along the same axis as the `trail'.
There is no clear detection of star formation in H . It is possible that this system could be a chance alignment of unrelated galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes GMP 3016 as a blue irregular galaxy with UVIT evidence for a detached northeastern FUV trail, likely connected to stripping-related morphology.

quotes.quote: GMP 3016 This is an extremely blue, faint irregular galaxy as observed in the optical wavebands. yagi10 have reported `fireball' features i.e., extended emission following knots of blue stars yoshida08 from GMP 3016 (see the right panel of Fig. images (a)). On the other hand, smith10 found this to be a `less convincing case' of a stripping galaxy in the combined NUV + FUV image obtained with Galex . Our UVIT image (Fig. images (a)) however, shows a linear structure in the north-eastern part of the FUV image clearly extending towards GMP 3016. In FUV , this trail is detached from the galaxy and extends upto 26 kpc in the north-east direction All galaxies shown in Figs. images , images2 and images3 are oriented such that north is up and east is left of the image. , with the cluster centre < 200 h^ 1 kpc away parallel to the direction of the alignment of knots.

sources.summary: The paper adds GMP 3016 as a Coma member with H detected in its tail but not its main disk, younger bright-tail truncation ages, and a nearly face-on orientation.

quotes.quote: These include GMP 3016, the remaining known Coma cluster member with H detected in the tail but not in the main disc . GMP 3016 is 0.03 mag below the SDSS magnitude limit and would have been excluded by the original selection criteria.

The estimated truncation ages for the seven galaxies with bright tails (including GMP 3016) are younger ( 180 500 Myr) than for the remaining five, whose tails faded close to the limit of detectability.
All 11 galaxies from the primary sample have V_R/ >1 in their outer parts suggesting that they are rotationally supported systems; GMP 3016 has a nearly face-on orientation.

gmw_00005691.name: PGC 044679
gmw_00005691.overview: PGC 044679 is a barred S0 lenticular galaxy located in the Coma cluster [arXiv:2209.05886]. Far-ultraviolet imaging reveals two discrete components, including a nuclear component and a northeastern component that may be a satellite or background object [arXiv:2209.05886].

sources.summary: The paper reports PGC 044679 as a barred S0 Coma member with two FUV components, an uncertain northeastern component, and HI detection.

quotes.quote: PGC 044679 This is a barred S0 galaxy member of the Coma cluster lansbury14 . The FUV image (Fig. images (b)) shows two discrete components, one centred at the galaxy's nucleus and the other in the north-east. The nuclear component (C1 in Fig. images (b)) has an interesting morphology pointing in orthogonal directions, one of which points in the direction of the cluster centre which is at a distance of 256 h^ 1 kpc from the galaxy. The second component C2, however, remains to be further investigated to confirm whether it also corresponds to PGC 044679, is a satellite galaxy or a random background object. Unlike GMP 3016, this is therefore one of our less convincing cases of multiple component UV emission.

gmw_00005692.name: GMP 2787
gmw_00005692.overview: GMP 2787 is a faint irregular galaxy located near the Coma cluster [arXiv:2209.05886]. Its ultraviolet morphology is irregular, differs from its optical extent, and points away from the cluster center [arXiv:2209.05886].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes GMP 2787 as a faint irregular galaxy near the Coma velocity threshold, with UVIT morphology unlike its optical extent and directed away from the cluster center.

quotes.quote: GMP 2787 This is an irregular galaxy ( z=0.033; 9974 km s ^ 1 ), having radial velocity slightly above the maximum threshold considered here for selecting the members of the Coma cluster. It has a stellar mass of 9.39 M_ teimoorinia17 . This galaxy appears to be very faint in the Galex images. The UVIT image (Fig. images ()) of this galaxy shows an irregular morphology, very different from the optical extent of the galaxy, and pointing in a direction away from the cluster centre which is 236 h^ 1 kpc away.

gmw_00005693.name: GMP 3523
gmw_00005693.overview: GMP 3523 is a star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.194 [arXiv:2209.05886]. It features tail-like structures on its top and southwestern edges that are invisible in optical images and may represent tidal tails [arXiv:2209.05886].

sources.summary: The paper presents GMP 3523 as a star-forming galaxy with optically invisible FUV tail-like features, possibly tidal, and a brighter UVIT southwestern extension than in Galex.

quotes.quote: GMP 3523 This is a star-forming galaxy at z=0.194 with tail like features observed on the top and south-western edge of the galaxy (Fig. images2 (a)). All the tail like features are invisible in the optical image, and may be tidal tail(s) of this galaxy, although this needs to be confirmed in further investigation. The south-western extension was also seen in the Galex image e.g. donas95 , but appeared as a fainter feature relative to the UVIT image.

gmw_00005694.name: GMP 2910
gmw_00005694.overview: GMP 2910 is a post-starburst galaxy located in the Coma Cluster [arXiv:2209.05886]. It features a narrow, 60-kiloparsec-long tail rich in molecular gas [arXiv:1704.00824, arXiv:2010.11028] that is likely undergoing ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:2209.05886, arXiv:1704.00824] and exhibits core star formation [arXiv:1005.3874].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GMP 2910 as a well-studied post-starburst Coma galaxy with molecular and atomic gas, an FUV/H-alpha tail, likely ram-pressure stripping, and possible Seyfert activity.

quotes.quote: GMP 2910 This is the most well studied of our sources with unusual FUV morphology. Several authors have reported extended emission associated with this post-starburst galaxy commonly known as D100 z=0.0177 ; yagi07,smith10,yagi10,cramer19 . This source was also reported as KUG 1257+281 in the Kiso Survey for UV-excess galaxies takase93 . The FUV emission observed in the UVIT image (Fig. images2 (b)) coincides well with the extended H emission yagi07 . GMP 2910 is rich in molecular jachym17 and atomic gas teimoorinia17 , with a considerable HI-to-stellar mass fraction ( > 0.16 ; Table tab:HI ).

The extended FUV emission points in the northeast direction almost perpendicular to the vector pointing in the direction of the cluster centre. The spectacular narrow tail observed with the Subaru telescope's Suprime-Cam in the H narrow band filter see Fig. 2 of cramer19 is well aligned with the FUV feature observed with the UVIT. This galaxy is only 240 h^ 1 kpc away from the cluster centre, and hence likely a classic case of ram-pressure stripping.

sources.summary: The paper reports abundant molecular gas in D100's narrow ram-pressure-stripped tail, argues stripping is near peak pressure, and describes D100 as a rare molecular-tail case.

quotes.quote: We have discovered large amounts of molecular gas, as traced by CO emission, in the ram pressure stripped gas tail of the Coma cluster galaxy D100 (GMP 2910), out to large distances of about 50 kpc. D100 has a 60 kpc long, strikingly narrow tail which is bright in X-rays and H . Our observations with the IRAM 30m telescope reveal in total 10^9 M_ of H _2 (assuming the standard CO-to-H _2 conversion) in several regions along the tail, thus indicating that molecular gas may dominate its mass.

D100 is likely being stripped at a high orbital velocity 2200 km s ^ 1 by (nearly) peak ram pressure. Combined effects of ICM viscosity and magnetic fields may be important for the evolution of the stripped ISM. We propose D100 has reached a continuous mode of stripping of dense gas remaining in its nuclear region. D100 is the second known case of an abundant molecular stripped-gas tail, suggesting that conditions in the ICM at the centers of galaxy clusters may be favorable for molecularization.

sources.summary: GMP2910(D100) is treated as a parent of emission clouds; its clouds inform the flux model, and it shows core star formation with blobby extended emission.

quotes.quote: In this study, we used the spectroscopic result indicating that four clouds had similar redshifts to their respective parent galaxies (GMP2559, GMP2910, GMP2923, and GMP4060).
The \ total flux was calculated by assuming that the cloud had the same redshift as the parent galaxy, and its mix of [ NII ] 6548,6583 was similar to that of the GMP2910 clouds Yagi2007 .
2 connected \ clouds without disk star formation: GMP2910, GMP3071, and GMP3779 exhibit core star formation and extended \ emission connected to the core. The extended clouds have a blobby structure.

sources.summary: The paper cites D100 as a ram-pressure-stripped galaxy with an H2-rich, HI-deficient molecular tail that is massive, extends about 60 kpc, forms stars weakly, and likely formed in situ.

quotes.quote: It is also worth noting that the tails of the two ram pressure stripped galaxies, D100 in Coma cluster (Abell 1656) and EOS137-001 in Norma cluster (Abell 3627), are found to be H _2 rich but HI deficient jachym17,jachym19 . These molecular tails are estimated to be massive ( 10^9 M_ ) and extend to 60 kpc from the galaxy, but the level of star formation appears to be rather insignificant ( 10^ 3 10^ 2 M_ yr^ 1 ).

More interestingly, jachym17,jachym19 claim that the molecular tails are likely formed in-situ , rather than directly stripped from the galaxies because the age of the tails is estimated to be higher ( 200 Myr ) than the typical lifetime of dense molecular clouds ( 1-10 Myr).

gmw_00005695.name: NGC 4895
gmw_00005695.overview: NGC 4895 is a massive galaxy located in the Coma Cluster that hosts a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region [arXiv:2209.05886]. Its major axis is oriented orthogonally to the direction of the cluster center, and it contains multiple components in the far-ultraviolet [arXiv:2209.05886].

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4895 as a massive Coma galaxy with multiple FUV components, likely background extraplanar concentrations, an axis orthogonal to the cluster center, and LINER activity.

quotes.quote: NGC 4895 NGC 4895 ( z=0.0283 ) comprises multiple components in the FUV (Fig. images3 (a)). While the dominant component (C1) in centred at the galaxy's nucleus, the northwestern component C3 is a blue foreground star observed in the multi-band SDSS image. The relatively smaller, northeastern component C2 however, does not have an optical counterpart. The two extraplanar concentrations in southwest and southeast (C4 and C5, respectively) are likely background objects, with no optical counterparts available in the SDSS database. Hence, it will be interesting to explore the latter three in follow-up observations.

We also note that the major axis of this galaxy is orthogonal to the direction of the cluster centre. nisbet16 found this massive galaxy (log M*/M_ = 11.21 ) to be hosting a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region (LINER) type AGN.

gmw_00005696.name: GMP 2559
gmw_00005696.overview: GMP 2559 is a blue, HI-deficient spiral galaxy located in the Coma cluster [arXiv:2209.05886, arXiv:astro-ph/0109316] that exhibits an asymmetric distribution of gas and dust [arXiv:1006.4867, arXiv:2209.05886]. It features extended ionized-gas, radio, and ultraviolet tails projecting toward the southeast [arXiv:1202.1044, arXiv:2207.06624].

sources.summary: GMP 2559/IC 4040 is a dusty spiral with southeast and southwest UV features, diffuse emission, an H trail, secondary H peaks, and an HI offset aligned with the tail.

quotes.quote: GMP 2559 = IC 4040 This is a spiral galaxy with an irregular, patchy distribution of dust obscuration. It is one of only two galaxies in our sample that fall within the footprint of the Coma HST/ACS Treasury Survey The custom-reduced ACS imaging from this survey is publicly available from http://archive.stsci.edu/prepds/coma (Carter et al. 2008, Hammer et al. 2010b). The GALEX imaging reveals a plume of emission towards the south-east, and three compact sources to the south-west. At very low surface brightness levels, the region between these two features appears to be filled by faint diffuse UV emission. The UV plume to the south-east extends 1.8 arcmin ( 50 kpc) from the centre of GMP 2559, and can also be seen in the deep Adami u band image, though there is no clear counterpart in our shallower MegaCam data.

Co-located with the UV plume is an H trail extending at least 1.4 arcmin (40 kpc), with secondary H peaks at 12, 23 and 28 kpc from the nucleus. The ACS imaging appears to show compact, fairly red objects centrally located within these peaks, but these may simply be chance alignments. The galaxy is deficient in HI (Def _ HI =0.56 , Gavazzi et al. 2006), and Bravo-Alfaro et al. (2001) note that the HI distribution is offset towards the south-east (i.e. the same direction as the H tail).

sources.summary: IC 4040 is described as a spiral galaxy with a complicated expelled ionized-gas structure, major southeast and southern streams, and an EIG velocity field like RB199.

quotes.quote: IC 4040 Figure 7 presents a pseudo-color image (blue, green and red colors represent B , R_ C , and H images, respectively) of the spiral galaxy IC 4040 taken with Subaru Suprime-Cam (YY10, reproduced by permission of the American Astronomical Society; AAS). The figure clearly shows a very extended EIG expelled from the galaxy disk. The structure of the EIG is very complicated. Two major streams extend toward the southeast and south from the galaxy.

Figure 10 shows the velocity field of the EIG of IC 4040. The brightness of the EIG of IC 4040 allowed us to trace the velocity field along each slit. The IC 4040 EIG has a velocity field similar to that of the RB199 EIG; the relative radial velocities of the filaments increase almost monotonically with the distance from the nucleus (Figure 10). Within 5 kpc of the nucleus, the H gas along the galaxy major axis follows the galaxy rotation.

sources.summary: GMP2559 is one of the few parents with detected HI gas, has asymmetric emission, and is among the UV-asymmetric gaseous-stripping galaxies.

quotes.quote: Although the survey covered the entire field of this study, there were only three parents in which HI gas was detected (GMP2374, GMP2559, and GMP3779).
GMP2559 has negative \ on the northern side, but strong \ emission on the southern side. We also classified it as belonging to this type.

Their observed field overlapped with our field, and there were six galaxies in common (GMP2559, GMP2910, GMP3016, GMP3816, GMP4060, and GMP4232). It is striking that the UV asymmetric galaxies in our fields are only the six.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4040 as a spiral/irregular emission-line RPS galaxy with southeast asymmetric radio tails, 61.1 kpc extent, and a steep integrated spectrum.

quotes.quote: 20 (): IC 4040 (a.k.a. GMP 2559, KUG 1258 + 283, CGCG 160 252) The optical host shows emission lines hosted by a galaxy of spiral or irregular morphological type KentandGunn . The radio emission at both bands, 250--500 MHz and 550--850 MHz, is one-sided showing asymmetric tails toward the southeast, extending beyond the optical host galaxy, with the 550--850 MHz band image showing smaller extent than the 250--500 MHz band image. The source is also classified as an RPS galaxy see Sec. tab:tab3-rps Chenetal . The largest projected linear extent of the source in the 250--500 MHz band is 2 18 ( 61.1 kpc). The integrated spectral index is steep, (400--700 MHz) = 1.05 0.05, and it agrees with S_ 1.4 GHz = 16.31 0.10 mJy, within uncertainties Chenetal .

sources.summary: The paper describes GMP 2559 as a blue Sd spiral with HI deficiency, asymmetric gas, an HI tail matching extraplanar FUV emission, and star-forming knots also seen in H-alpha.

quotes.quote: GMP 2559 Also known as PGC 044789 and IC4040, this is a well studied blue, Sd type spiral galaxy in the Coma cluster. It is known to be an HI-deficient galaxy, having asymmetric HI distribution, such that most of the HI gas lies in the south-eastern part of the galaxy, while the north-western part appears depleted bravo00 . The major axis of this galaxy is almost orthogonal to the cluster centre. GMP 2559 exhibits an HI tail extending towards south-east miller09, chen20 , almost coincident with the extra-planar FUV emission as shown in Fig. images3 (). It is also noteworthy that the three ``knots" of star-forming region seen extending towards south-west in the UVIT image are also observed in the H emission see fig. 2 of chen20 , but not in the radio continuum.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4040 as a central Coma blue disk with compressed H I contours, a southeast gas-optical offset, and a confirmed double-peaked detection.

quotes.quote: IC 4040 This Sdm blue disk galaxy is projected near the very center of Coma, at only 15 (300 kpc) NE from NGC 4874. Its distribution (see Fig. 13) shows the contours compressed in the NW, and an offset in position of 9 ( 3 kpc), with the emission SE of the optical galaxy. Our detection confirms the double peak detection reported by Gavazzi (1989) at 7600 and 7850 . The velocity field for this galaxy is shown in Fig. 14.

gmw_00005697.name: GMP 2347
gmw_00005697.overview: GMP 2347 is an early-type spiral galaxy located 0.45 Mpc from the center of the Coma cluster [arXiv:2209.05886]. It features a central far-ultraviolet component and an off-center component that may represent a merging dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2209.05886].

sources.summary: The paper presents GMP 2347 as an early-type spiral with central and off-center FUV components, possible dwarf-galaxy merging, and an uncertain nearby faint blue galaxy.

quotes.quote: GMP 2347 This early-type spiral galaxy in the Coma cluster shows a central FUV component (C1), along with an off-centre component C2. The latter might be a dwarf galaxy merging with GMP 2347. This galaxy is 0.45 Mpc away from the cluster centre. Another faint galaxy GMP 2333 (component C3) can also be seen in the north-east direction relative to the galaxy in Fig. images4 (a).
