In the absence of redshift information, however, it is not possible to test if this faint blue galaxy is interacting with GMP 2347 or is a member of the Coma cluster.

gmw_00005698.name: NGC 4907
gmw_00005698.overview: NGC 4907 is a barred spiral galaxy in the Coma Cluster [arXiv:2209.05886] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2112.12244] classified as a LINER [arXiv:2209.05886]. This quenched late-type galaxy [arXiv:2112.12244] features a disturbed, highly deficient H I component [arXiv:2112.12244, arXiv:astro-ph/0109316] and asymmetric far-ultraviolet emission [arXiv:2209.05886].

sources.summary: The paper marks NGC 4907 as an AGN-bearing Coma source, identifies it with NGC 4921 as a massive quenched late-type galaxy, and notes its disturbed H I component.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig::sfms we marked 4 sources, NGC 4921, NGC 4907, NGC 4911, and NGC 4848, with red circles, indicating they have been found to harbour an AGN in previous studies mahajan10,gavazzi11,toba14 . The two most massive ones ( (M_ / M_ ) 11.0 -- 11.5 ), NGC 4921 and NGC 4907, are quenched late-type galaxies. NGC 4911 is of similar M_ , but resides on the SFMS, while NGC 4848 ( (M_ / M_ ) 9 ) has a sSFR consistent with that of a starburst galaxy. NGC 4921, NGC 4907, and NGC 4848 have disturbed H i components.

We note that, according to their MIR photometry, none of them have W1-W2 colours greater than 0.8, where most AGN lie stern12 . They are most likely Seyferts, whose star formation can be comparable with their AGN activity e.g. in Circinus for12 , and as a result can have MIR colours similar to regular SF galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4907 as a barred Coma spiral and LINER with asymmetric FUV emission, possible gas displacement toward the cluster center, and a central FUV cavity.

quotes.quote: NGC 4907 This face-on barred spiral galaxy in the Coma cluster, also known as GMP 2441, shows distinct FUV emission, unlike the symmetric spiral arms observed in the optical waveband. This is a LINER galaxy mahajan10, toba14 . Fig. images4 (b) shows that unlike the optical, the UVIT FUV image shows clear asymmetry between the northeast and southwestern regions of the galaxy. It is possible that gas is being pushed to the northeastern face as galaxy moves towards the cluster centre 0.44 Mpc away. Another interesting feature is the C-shaped hole seen in the FUV emission surrounding the bar at the centre.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4907 as a marginal H I detection with a shrunken, gas-deficient disk, attributing the likely cause to ICM-ISM interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC 4907 This Sb galaxy was only marginally detected in (Fig. 15). It is deficient by a factor of ten and presents a shrunken disk (not resolved by the VLA beam) which is smaller than the optical disk. Two facts make an ICM--ISM interaction the most likely explanation for this picture: the high radial velocity relative to the cluster (v _ rel =1179 ), and the projected location of NGC 4907, which puts it in a very high density region, 20 (400 kpc) NE from the cluster center.

gmw_00005699.name: GMP 2943
gmw_00005699.overview: GMP 2943 is a distorted spiral galaxy located in the Coma cluster [arXiv:2209.05886]. While it exhibits a spider-like optical morphology with a red nuclear region, its far-ultraviolet appearance reveals a face-on spiral structure [arXiv:2209.05886].

sources.summary: The paper describes GMP 2943 as a distorted Coma spiral whose optical spider-like morphology contrasts with a face-on FUV appearance, and notes separation from GMP 2989.

quotes.quote: GMP 2943 and 2989 GMP 2943 (C1 in Fig. images4 ()) is a distorted spiral galaxy in the Coma cluster, whose optically-red nuclear region is observed below the blue spiral arms which take on a spider-like morphology in the optical image as shown in Fig. g2943 . However, in the FUV image this galaxy appears to be a face-on spiral, much unlike it's optical facade. GMP 2989, which is also a Coma cluster member, is also seen alongside GMP 2943 in the image (Fig. images4 ()) as C2. This spiral galaxy has it's major axis pointed along the vector pointing in the direction of the cluster centre. GMP 2943 and 2989 are 1380 km s ^ 1 away in redshift, hence unlikely to be interacting with each other, but within 0.45 Mpc from the cluster centre.

gmw_00005700.name: GMP 2989
gmw_00005700.overview: GMP 2989 is a spiral galaxy located in the Coma Cluster [arXiv:2209.05886]. It is an active galactic nucleus host [arXiv:2209.05886] that retains a clear spiral structure despite having very low star formation rates and gas limits [arXiv:astro-ph/0109316].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GMP 2989 as a Coma spiral near GMP 2943, unlikely to be interacting with it, and the only bonafide AGN host among the unusual-FUV galaxies.

quotes.quote: GMP 2989, which is also a Coma cluster member, is also seen alongside GMP 2943 in the image (Fig. images4 ()) as C2. This spiral galaxy has it's major axis pointed along the vector pointing in the direction of the cluster centre. GMP 2943 and 2989 are 1380 km s ^ 1 away in redshift, hence unlikely to be interacting with each other, but within 0.45 Mpc from the cluster centre. GMP 2989 is also the only bonafide AGN host galaxy (see Fig. bpt ) amongst the galaxies with unusual FUV morphology discussed in this section.

sources.summary: The paper notes D216 retains clear spiral structure despite very low SFR and gas limits, implying gas removal occurred faster than morphological transformation.

quotes.quote: HST images (Caldwell et al. 1999) show that some of the PSB galaxies in Coma have retained their disk, even with a smooth spiral structure. D61 (Zw 160-104) shows two dust lanes forming an edge-on disk, and D216 (RB 160) shows clear spiral structure. Interestingly our limits to the SFR and content for these two galaxies are very low. Obviously the time scale for gas removal from these galaxies was shorter than for a morphological transformation.

gmw_00005701.name: GMP 3317
gmw_00005701.overview: GMP 3317 is a distant galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.2748 [arXiv:2209.05886]. While optical images show it as a small, faint, red spheroid, its far-ultraviolet emission reveals a much larger system with a possible hole surrounding the nuclear region [arXiv:2209.05886].

sources.summary: The paper treats GMP 3317 as a distant galaxy whose FUV extent suggests a larger system than the optical nucleus and shows a possible nuclear-region FUV hole.

quotes.quote: GMP 3317 GMP 3317 appears as a small, red, faint spheroid in the optical image, very close to a bright star. Not much is known about this distant galaxy ( z=0.2748 ). However, the FUV image inevitably shows that this is likely a much larger galaxy, only whose nucleus is observed in the optical images. Interestingly though, there appears to be a hole in the FUV emission surrounding the nuclear region as seen in Fig. images5 (a).

gmw_00005702.name: GMP 3170
gmw_00005702.overview: GMP 3170 is a Coma Cluster galaxy located approximately 0.04 megaparsecs from the cluster center [arXiv:2209.05886]. It features a central far-ultraviolet emission component and a tail of far-ultraviolet blobs extending perpendicular to the direction of the cluster center [arXiv:2209.05886].

sources.summary: The paper describes GMP 3170 as a Coma galaxy very near the cluster center, likely environmentally affected, with central FUV emission and a tail of FUV blobs.

quotes.quote: GMP 3170 This Coma cluster galaxy is only 0.04 Mpc from the cluster centre, and hence very likely experiencing the hostile environmental mechanisms. Besides the central component C1 in Fig. images5 (b), one can clearly see a tail of FUV blobs (components C2, C3 and C4) in the direction perpendicular to the vector pointing towards the cluster centre. Although follow-up spectroscopic observations are required to confirm our speculation that the blobs are connected to GMP 3170.

gmw_00005703.name: GMP 2956
gmw_00005703.overview: GMP 2956 is an S0 lenticular galaxy located in the Coma cluster [arXiv:2209.05886]. Its far-ultraviolet emission is divided into at least two components, including a core-centered component and a second component tilted toward the northeast with no obvious optical counterpart [arXiv:2209.05886].

sources.summary: The paper reports GMP 2956 as an S0 Coma galaxy with multiple FUV components, including a core-centered component and an optically unidentified component tilted northeast.

quotes.quote: GMP 2956 This S0 Coma cluster galaxy lies at a distance of 0.31 Mpc from the cluster centre. The FUV emission appears to be divided into at least two components: C1 is centered at the core of the galaxy, while the component C2 does not show any obvious optical counterpart. C2 also appears to be tilting in the north-east direction away from the direction of the cluster centre.

gmw_00005704.name: QSO2
gmw_00005704.overview: QSO2 is a faint companion quasar embedded within a Lyman-alpha nebula [arXiv:2111.15392]. It exhibits compact submillimeter continuum emission and has an estimated star formation rate of 183 solar masses per year [arXiv:2111.15392].

sources.summary: The paper identifies QSO2 as a faint companion quasar embedded in the ELAN, detects compact submillimeter continuum emission, and estimates a high star-formation rate with CIGALE.

quotes.quote: The detected sources are QSO, QSO2, AGN1, LAE1, and a newly discovered source SMG1. The positions, S/N and fluxes for the five detections are listed in Table tab:Interf , together with the deboosted fluxes estimated as explained in Appendix app:SMADeboosting . Summing up the deboosted fluxes of all detected sources, we find agreement within uncertainties with the detections in the single-dish datasets, 14.7 2.8 mJy. Therefore, all the continuum emission detected by LABOCA and SCUBA-2 is ascribed to compact sources.

Further, CIGALE determined the instantaneous SFR for each source, indicating SFR =521 74,183 49,104 21,50 11 M _ yr ^ 1 for QSO, QSO2, AGN1, LAE1, respectively. These SFRs are in line with values from the literature. In particular, the SFR in the QSO host agrees well with values usually found in z 2-3 quasars (e.g., Harris2016 ).

gmw_00005705.name: AGN1
gmw_00005705.overview: AGN1 is a faint, obscured type-II active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2111.15392]. It exhibits compact continuum emission and has an estimated star-formation rate of 104 solar masses per year [arXiv:2111.15392].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AGN1 as a faint obscured type-II AGN in the ELAN, detects compact continuum emission, and estimates its star-formation rate with CIGALE.

quotes.quote: The detected sources are QSO, QSO2, AGN1, LAE1, and a newly discovered source SMG1. The positions, S/N and fluxes for the five detections are listed in Table tab:Interf , together with the deboosted fluxes estimated as explained in Appendix app:SMADeboosting . Summing up the deboosted fluxes of all detected sources, we find agreement within uncertainties with the detections in the single-dish datasets, 14.7 2.8 mJy. Therefore, all the continuum emission detected by LABOCA and SCUBA-2 is ascribed to compact sources.

Further, CIGALE determined the instantaneous SFR for each source, indicating SFR =521 74,183 49,104 21,50 11 M _ yr ^ 1 for QSO, QSO2, AGN1, LAE1, respectively. These SFRs are in line with values from the literature. In particular, the SFR in the QSO host agrees well with values usually found in z 2-3 quasars (e.g., Harris2016 ).

gmw_00005706.name: J0025--0145
gmw_00005706.overview: J0025--0145 is an intermediately lensed quasar at a redshift of z=5.07 [arXiv:2303.04357]. A foreground lensing galaxy is located 0.6 arcseconds away from the quasar, which has a tentative rest-frame ultraviolet host galaxy detection [arXiv:2303.04357].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0025--0145 as a z=5.07 intermediately lensed quasar with a nearby foreground lensing galaxy, high inferred Eddington ratio, and tentative rest-UV host-galaxy detection.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report the discoveries of J0025--0145 ( z=5.07 ) which we identify as an intermediately-lensed quasar, and J2329--0522 ( z=4.85 ) which is a kpc-scale close quasar pair. The Hubble Space Telescope (HST) image of J0025--0145 shows a foreground lensing galaxy located 0 6 away from the quasar. However, J0025--0145 does not exhibit multiple lensed images of the quasar, and we identify J0025--0145 as an intermediate lensing system (a lensing system that is not multiply imaged but has a significant magnification). The spectrum of J0025--0145 implies an extreme Eddington ratio if the quasar luminosity is intrinsic, which could be explained by a large lensing magnification. The HST image of J0025--0145 also indicates a tentative detection of the quasar host galaxy in rest-frame UV, illustrating the power of lensing magnification and distortion in studies of high-redshift quasar host galaxies.

gmw_00005707.name: J2329--0522
gmw_00005707.overview: J2329--0522 is a close, kiloparsec-scale quasar pair located at a redshift of z=4.85 [arXiv:2303.04357]. The system consists of two resolved components with significantly different spectral properties and lacks any detected lensing galaxy [arXiv:2303.04357].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J2329--0522 as a z=4.85 kpc-scale close quasar pair, with two resolved components, different spectra, no detected lens galaxy, and record-high confirmed redshift.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report the discoveries of J0025--0145 ( z=5.07 ) which we identify as an intermediately-lensed quasar, and J2329--0522 ( z=4.85 ) which is a kpc-scale close quasar pair.

J2329--0522 consists of two resolved components with significantly different spectral properties, and a lack of lensing galaxy detection under sub-arcsecond seeing. We identify it as a close quasar pair, which is the highest confirmed kpc-scale quasar pair to date.

gmw_00005708.name: FIRST J121839.7+295325
gmw_00005708.overview: FIRST J121839.7+295325 is a radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0806.3781] that is likely hosted by a massive early-type galaxy [arXiv:0806.3781]. This system may be strongly lensing a background galaxy [arXiv:0806.3781], though the lensing galaxy itself remains optically undetected [arXiv:0806.3781].

sources.summary: The paper argues FIRST J121839.7+295325 may be an optically undetected massive early-type lens galaxy hosting a radio-loud AGN, with a high mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: We present evidence that the optically unidentified radio source, FIRST J121839.7+295325, may be strongly lensing a background galaxy. We estimate the redshift of the assumed gravitational arc, discovered in parallel imaging with HST, from MMT-Blue Channel spectroscopy to be z_ arc = . We present lens models with an Einstein radius of R_E = 1 3 which contains a mass of M_ dyn = 10^ 12 0.5 M _ , where the uncertainty reflects the range of possible lens redshifts. The putative lens is not detected to J_ lim = mag and H_ lim = mag in our MMT-SWIRC imaging. Using the flux limits from WFPC2 and SWIRC, we estimate that the dynamical mass-to-light ratio of J121839.7+295325 is M_ dyn /L_B 10 M _ L _ ^ 1 for A_V = 1 mag, and this lower limit could be as high as 30 M _ L _ ^ 1 for A_V = 0 mag. Since the radio source is optically unidentified ( V_ lim = 25.5 mag) and has a radio flux of S_ 1.4 GHz = 33 mJy, it is likely a massive early-type galaxy which hosts a radio-loud AGN at 0.8 z_ radio 1.5 . However, the present data cannot uniquely determine the mass-to-light ratio of the lensing galaxy, and hence the possibility that this system may be a reasonably dark lens is not ruled out.

gmw_00005709.name: Hercules dSph galaxy
gmw_00005709.overview: The Hercules dSph galaxy is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located at a distance of approximately 147 kiloparsecs [arXiv:0908.3489]. Its member stars are spatially confined to an elongated area that exhibits a slight distortion toward the Milky Way [arXiv:0908.3489].

sources.summary: The study builds a cleaned Hercules dSph member sample, finding low metallicity, abundance spread, systemic velocity, distance, and an elongated structure affected by foreground contamination.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: all observations were centered at RA= 16^h 31^m 05^s and Dec=+ 12^ 47' 18'' 2008ApJ...684.1075M .

quotes.quote: Our final sample consists of 28 red giant branch stars in the Hercules dSph galaxy. Based on these stars we find a mean photometric metallicity of 2.35 0.31 dex which is consistent with previous studies. We find evidence for an abundance spread. Using those stars for which we have determined radial velocities we find a systemic velocity of 45.20 1.09 km s^ 1 with a dispersion of 3.72 km s ^ 1 , this is lower than values found in the literature. Furthermore we identify the horizontal branch and estimate the mean magnitude of the horizontal branch of the Hercules dSph galaxy to be V_0=21.17 0.05 , which corresponds to a distance of 147^ +8 _ 7 kpc.

Through this search we have found in total 28 stars that are RGB members of the Hercules dSph galaxy. These stars are spatially confined to a fairly small, elongated area falling inside the core radius from 2007ApJ...668L..43C (see Fig. plot4_spec a). The RGB members show a slight distortion such that there appear to be a few stars trailing the ellipsoid in the direction of the Milky Way.

gmw_00005710.name: Fornax dwarf spheroidal galaxy
gmw_00005710.overview: The Fornax dwarf spheroidal galaxy is a dwarf spheroidal system [arXiv:2009.07858]. Kinematic data from its stellar tracers indicate positive excess kurtosis, which favors a cored dark matter density profile for the galaxy [arXiv:2009.07858].

sources.summary: The paper applies positive-definite velocity-distribution models to Fornax, finding positive excess kurtosis that favors a cored dark matter profile and constrains orbital anisotropy.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: We take the centre of Fornax as ( , )=(2 h 39 m 53 s ,-34^ 30'32'' ) , the position angle as 42.2 deg and the ellipticity of =0.31 from the King profile fit of .

quotes.quote: Data on the Fornax dwarf spheroidal galaxy indicate positive excess kurtosis and hence favour a cored dark matter profile.

We take the spectroscopic sample of 2633 stars presented in , who provide line-of-sight velocities with uncertainties for all stars and a metallicity indicator, a pseudo-equivalent width measurement of the Mg triplet, along with its uncertainty for 459 stars. We convert the on-sky positions into projected circularized radius R_c= 1- (x^2+y^2/(1- )^2)^ 1/2 where x/y are the positions along the major/minor axis of Fornax with respect to the dwarf centre. We take the centre of Fornax as ( , )=(2 h 39 m 53 s ,-34^ 30'32'') , the position angle as 42.2 deg and the ellipticity of =0.31 from the King profile fit of . Some targets were observed multiple times by from which an inverse-variance-weighted mean line-of-sight velocity and its error are estimated. For the remainder of the stars we use the single velocity measurement along with its error. discuss the effect of undetected binarity on the Fornax velocity distributions by comparing samples with and without binaries as determined by repeat observations. They find the distributions are very similar (less than 4 probability the distributions are drawn from different samples) so we conclude binarity is unimportant and do not attempt to correct for it. We correct for the velocity gradient across the dwarf produced by perspective rotation from the bulk proper motion using the equations in and the proper motion measurements from .

For the specific example of the Fornax dwarf spheroidal galaxy, we have shown using a chemo-kinematical mixture model that the data seem to favour positive excess kurtosis velocity distributions and hence indicate cored dark matter density profiles. Additionally, we have tested our modelling procedure on a series of dynamical dwarf spheroidal galaxy models with varying dark matter profiles, finding good recovery of the kinematic profiles and that a cored model well reproduces the observed data. Furthermore, we have investigated how the excess kurtosis measurement depends on the number and uncertainty distribution of stellar tracers. For a Fornax-like dSph. 2000 stars with uncertainties 2 km s ^ 1 are sufficient to measure the sign of the excess kurtosis and measure the mass density slope, although this assumes perfect knowledge of dwarf spheroidal membership.

gmw_00005711.name: NGC 5206
gmw_00005711.overview: NGC 5206 is a nucleated dwarf elliptical or lenticular galaxy located 3 Mpc away in the M 83 group [arXiv:1807.08765, arXiv:2109.12129]. Its central nuclear star cluster is relatively young, with most of its stars having formed less than 10 billion years ago [arXiv:1807.08765].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5206 as a nucleated dwarf elliptical whose nuclear star cluster is relatively young, mostly assembled less than 10 Gyr ago, and marginally SSP-like.

quotes.quote: NGC 5206 is a nucleated dwarf elliptical (dE/S0) galaxy that belongs to the M 83 group, and is located at a distance of 3 Mpc. nguyen+2018 measure similar to NGC 5102 flat velocity dispersion profile in the central region (from 0.7 to 0.4 ) at 33 , that peaks at 46 at the very centre. They measure a slow degree of rotation ( 10-15 ) and an overall roundness of the NSC.

NGC 5206  NGC 5206 is the other early-type galaxy in the sample. We combined the spectra from both periods of observation (P84 and P86) using a weighted average method. We measured an average velocity dispersion = 32.2 0.3 . At a difference with the nucleus of NGC 5102, this NSC seems to be compatible with continuous SFH and a gradual enrichment from 1.0 dex to +0.5 dex with a peak of the SFR 2 Gyr ago at a first glance (Figure fig:ngc5206 ). But NGC 5206's NSC also formed most of its stars less than 10 Gyr ago, similarly to the other early-type galaxy nucleus NGC 5102, with only 15 of the total mass being present >10 Gyr ago.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 5206’s nuclear star cluster with lower-quality data, finding an old integrated population and a tentative metallicity gradient similar to NGC 5102.

quotes.quote: The radial ranges explored in NGC 5206 and NGC 205 were much smaller due to poor data quality; in NGC 5206 we find a similar metallicity gradient to NGC 5102 (but with much lower significance),

From regularized CSP fits to the integrated light of the NSCs (Figure fig:205and5206results and Tables tab:NGC5206_Results_ALL tab:NGC205_Results_ALL ), we find that NGC 5206's NSC is the oldest of the clusters here (LW age 5.8 Gyr), with LW metallicity of 0.72. The best-fit LW age ( 4.2 Gyr) and metallicity ( 0.61) resulting from unregularized CSP fits for NGC 5206 (also shown in Figure fig:205and5206results and Table tab:NGC5206_Results_ALL ) are consistent with the regularized solutions. These integrated results are in rough agreement with the LW age of 2.91 Gyr and metallicity of -0.32 given by regularized fits in Kacharov2018 . SSP fits to the integrated light of NGC 5206 provide an age of 5.0 Gyr and [ M/H ] of 0.71. These SSP results are consistent with our CSP results but disagree with the SSP results of Kacharov2018 (age = 2.05 Gyr, [ Fe/H ] = -0.16); although these are likely similar fits based on the expected age-metallicity degeneracy. For the NGC 205 NSC, we find a LW age 0.73 Gyr and a LW metallicity of 0.78 from regularized CSP fits, while the unregularized fits provide a LW age of 1.1 Gyr and metallicity of 0.84 (Figure fig:205and5206results and Table tab:NGC205_Results_ALL ). This is the first full spectrum stellar population fit to NGC 205 presented in the literature. Previous information on the stellar population is available from the index measurements of Sharina2006 and photometry near the center from Butler2005 and Monaco2009 . Although these studies include light from larger radii in the galaxy, they generally find metallicities 0.9 for NGC 205, which is consistent with our results. Monaco2009 also find evidence for a population of stars that formed < 1 Gyr ago in the center of NGC 205 similar to our integrated age estimates. We note in both galaxies that the errors on our age and metallicity measurements are large due to the low S/N and a significant age-metallicity degeneracy. From the spatially resolved fits to the NGC 5206 NSC, we find a metallicity gradient that is similar to the one observed in NGC 5102 (see Figure fig:bestparams5206 ).

However, our results suggest that a mild metallicity gradient may be present in NGC 5206. We note this gradient is not very significant, and higher quality data would be needed to verify this trend. The narrow radial range explored for this galaxy is a direct result of poor data quality -- the data's S/N does not surpass our binning threshold of 10 at larger radii. Furthermore, the maximum regularization values determined for this data at each radius are comparable to the largest regularization values given for NGC 5102. These high regularization parameters result in excessively smoothed distributions of weights indicating that the stellar population synthesis results obtained from this data are not very constraining.

sources.summary: The paper represents NGC 5206 as a dE/S0 galaxy with two nuclear star clusters and a bulge, adopting mild triaxiality and finding stable modeled profiles.

quotes.quote: NGC5206 NGC 5206 is a dE/S0 type galaxy with an estimated stellar mass of 2.4 10^9 M_ . Its velocity dispersion ranges from 35-40 km/s and the average rotational velocity is about 10 km/s. ngu18 put an upper mass limit of 4.7 10^5 M_ on a central IMBH with an influence radius of roughly 1 pc. As in the observational decomposition, we represent the stellar distribution of NGC 5206 by two NCSs and a bulge; Table tab:ngc5206param gives NSC and bulge parameters for NGC 5206.

We adopt a mildly triaxial shape ( b/a 0.95 and /a 0.85 ) for NGC5206 galaxy model. Figure fig:ngc5206multi presents various key profiles as a function of distance and their stability in our run for the NGC 5206 galaxy model. Again, our initial model reproduces these key parameters reasonably well and they are fairly stable during the course of our stability run.

gmw_00005712.name: NGC 3411
gmw_00005712.overview: NGC 3411 is the dominant elliptical galaxy in the USGC S152 group [arXiv:astro-ph/0612287]. It hosts a weak, quiescent nuclear radio source [arXiv:astro-ph/0612287] surrounded by a steep-spectrum, core-halo-like diffuse radio component [arXiv:1103.1364].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3411 as the dominant elliptical in USGC S152, finding an unusual group-halo temperature structure, weak radio nucleus, and possible recent AGN heating.

quotes.quote: We present , \ and VLA observations of the USGC S152 group and its central elliptical NGC 3411. Imaging of the group X-ray halo suggests it is relaxed with little apparent structure. We investigate the temperature and metal abundance structure of the group halo, and find that while the abundance distribution is fairly typical, the temperature profile is highly unusual, showing a hot inner core surrounded by a cool shell of gas with a radius of 20-40 kpc, at the center of the larger group halo.

VLA observations at 1.4, 5 and 8 GHz reveal a relatively weak nuclear radio source, with a core radio luminosity L _ R =2.7 10^ 38 , and a diffuse component extended on scales of a few arcseconds (or more). A lack of evidence for activity at optical or X-ray wavelengths supports the conclusion that the central black hole is currently in a quiescent state. We discuss possible mechanisms for the formation of temperature features observed in the halo, including a previous period of AGN activity, and settling of material stripped from the halo of one of the other group member galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3411 as a steep-spectrum core-halo-like radio source in a relaxed X-ray group with evidence for a reheated cool core.

quotes.quote: NGC 3411 is characterized by a diffuse component, mostly extended in the north-south direction, which covers a much larger scale ( 80 kpc) than the high frequency images in O'Sullivan et al. (2007). Such emission appears as an irregular halo surrounding the central compact component. No jets connecting the point source and the extended emission are visible in our images. This morphology resembles the core-halo radio sources typically found at the centers of relaxed cool-core clusters (e.g., Mazzotta Giacintucci 2008 and references therein; see also Sect. sec:ngc1587 ), although the total extent of the source in NGC 3411 is smaller than the typical size of core-halos (from several tens to few hundreds of kpc in radius). The source has a steep spectral index ( = 1.44 ) -- one of the steepest among the sample (Table 4) -- as usually found for core-halo sources.

gmw_00005713.name: J163912.11+405236.5
gmw_00005713.overview: J163912.11+405236.5 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=4.88 [arXiv:0907.1447]. It hosts a narrow-line active galactic nucleus that displays a strong Lyman-alpha emission line [arXiv:0907.1447].

sources.summary: The paper reports J163912.11+405236.5 as a z=4.88 radio galaxy with non-ultra-steep spectrum, Ly emission, typical radio luminosity, and faint IRAC host emission.

quotes.quote: J163912.11+405236.5 is detected in the FIRST survey with a flux-density of 22.5 mJy and is unresolved at the 5 arcsec resolution of this survey. This source is also identified in the Northern VLA Sky Survey (NVSS; Condon et al. 1998) with S_ 1.4 GHz = 21.8 0.8  mJy and is therefore consistent with being point like. There is also a source detected in the 325 MHz Westerbork Northern Sky Survey (WENSS; Rengelink et al. 1997) at 16 39 12.17 +40 52 40.3 (J2000) which is 3.6 arcsec away from the FIRST centroid, therefore we associate this source with the FIRST source.

In Fig.  fig:spectrum we show the 1D-spectrum of the radio galaxy J163912.11+405236.5 which displays a strong emission line at 7149 , which we identify as Lyman- due to its blue cut-off and broader red wing, at a redshift of z=4.88 . The line has a flux of 1.85 10^ 19  W m ^ 2 , which at this redshift corresponds to a luminosity of L_ Ly = 4.7 10^ 36  W and a rest-frame full-width half maximum of 1040 km s ^ 1 , i.e. a narrow-line AGN. There are no other emission lines in our spectra which also suggest that the line is indeed Lyman- rather than other common emission lines in radio galaxies.

This is the second highest redshift radio galaxy known to date and demonstrates the promise of our new survey in finding high-redshift radio sources. Given the redshift, the radio luminosity of the source at 325 MHz is _ 10 (L_ 325 MHz /  W Hz ^ 1  sr ^ 1 ) = 26.94 and _ 10 (L_ 1400 MHz /  W Hz ^ 1  sr ^ 1 ) = 26.47 at 1.4 GHz.

gmw_00005714.name: UDG1137+16
gmw_00005714.overview: UDG1137+16 is a large ultra-diffuse galaxy located within the low-density UGC 6594 group [arXiv:2102.00598, arXiv:2405.09104]. It features a disturbed morphology and tidal signatures [arXiv:2309.15148, arXiv:2405.09104], consisting of a central stellar body surrounded by an outer stellar envelope undergoing tidal interaction [arXiv:2102.00598].

sources.summary: The paper studies UDG1137+16 as a large UDG in the UGC 6594 group with two stellar components, likely dark matter dominance, and weak evidence for strong tidal formation.

quotes.quote: Here, we combine new, deep imaging from the Jeanne Rich Telescope with deep integral field spectroscopy from the Keck II telescope to investigate the formation of UDG1137+16 (dw1137+16). Our new analyses confirm both its environmental association with the low density UGC 6594 group, along with its large size of 3.3 kpc and status as a UDG. The new imaging reveals two distinct stellar components for UDG1137+16, indicating that a central stellar body is surrounded by an outer stellar envelope undergoing tidal interaction. Both the components have approximately similar stellar masses.

From our integral field spectroscopy we measure a stellar velocity dispersion within the half-light radius (15 4 km \ s^ 1 ) and find that UDG1137+16 is similar to some other UDGs in that it is likely dark matter dominated. Incorporating literature measurements, we also examine the current state of UDG observational kinematics. Placing these data on the central stellar velocity dispersion -- stellar mass relation, we suggest there is little evidence for UDG1137+16 being created through a strong tidal interaction.

sources.summary: UDG1137+16 is a group UDG with tidal features, a slightly higher abundance ratio than cluster analogs, unusual metallicity, and a complicated formation history.

quotes.quote: Details about each observation can be found in the original papers presenting the dynamical and kinematic analysis of those UDGs: the Virgo cluster UDG VCC 1287 Gannon2020G , NGC 5846-UDG1 in the NGC 5846 group Forbes2021 , UDG1137+16 from the UGC 6594 group Gannon2021 , several UDGs in the Perseus cluster (i.e., PUDG-R15, PUDG-S74, PUDG-R84 and PUDG-R24; Gannon2022 ), and Yagi358 in the Coma cluster Gannon2022b . However, PUDG-R24 has been identified as a very recent infall into the Perseus cluster and UDG1137+16 presents tidal features, which can in both cases explain the young stellar ages. This is compatible for the two cluster UDGs, while the remaining one, the group UDG1137+16, presents a slightly higher abundance ratio than its cluster counterparts, despite sharing a similar SFH type.

UDG1137+16 is the only UDG that is clearly different to any of the comparison samples, with a metallicity that is more typical for older galaxies. UDG1137+16 has a complicated formation as shown by its tidal signatures Gannon2021 , but it has metallicity similar to the NIHAO UDGs of similar stellar mass.

sources.summary: UDG1137+16 is described as a group satellite of UGC 6594, also called dw1137+16, with disturbed morphology suggesting stripping.

quotes.quote: UDG1137+16 UDG1137+16 is a satellite of the galaxy UGC 6594 in a group environment. It is also known as dw1137+16 by Muller2018 . It has a disturbed morphology suggestive that it is undergoing stripping Gannon2021 . Any mass calculated with the values listed in the catalogue should be treated cautiously. M_r was transformed into V band using stated g- colour (0.65) and V=g-0.3 .

gmw_00005715.name: D500-2
gmw_00005715.overview: D500-2 is a dwarf galaxy classified as an Sm spiral galaxy [arXiv:0907.5533]. Its global profile shows a double-horned shape, and its velocity field is dominated by rotation with a flat rotation velocity of 68 km/s [arXiv:0907.5533].

sources.summary: The paper classifies D500-2 as an Sm galaxy, finds it rotation dominated, and derives a flat rotation velocity of 68 km/s from both tilted-ring and pV analyses.

quotes.quote: D500-2 is also known as UGC 5716 and is classified as an Sm galaxy. We assume a distance of 17.9 3.4 Mpc. The global \ profile of D500-2 (cf. Fig.  fig:btf:d500-2 ) shows the double-horned profile typical for spiral galaxies, and its velocity field indicates that D500-2 is clearly dominated by rotation.

After a gentle inner rise, the rotation curve of D500-2 reaches a flat part at a velocity of 68 . The rotation curve, as well as the radial distributions for PA and \ are shown in Fig.  fig:btf:d500-2-rc . The maximum rotation velocity from the pV diagram is 68 .

gmw_00005716.name: D500-3
gmw_00005716.overview: D500-3 is a low surface brightness [arXiv:1306.4278] dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:0907.5533] located approximately 18.5 Mpc away [arXiv:0907.5533]. It features a 5 kpc bar [arXiv:1306.4278], exhibits solid-body rotation [arXiv:0907.5533], and hosts two bright core H II regions surrounded by fainter star-forming regions [arXiv:1306.4278].

sources.summary: The paper describes D500-3 as a dwarf irregular with solid-body rotation, adopts the optical inclination, and estimates its maximum rotation velocity from the pV diagram.

quotes.quote: D500-3 is a dwarf irregular (dI) at a distance of 18.5 3.2 Mpc. Its velocity field (cf. Fig.  fig:btf:d500-3 ) indicates solid-body rotation throughout the entire disk, as confirmed by the pV diagram. We fit isophotes to different intensity levels in the zeroth moment map and find an inclination of the \ disk 42 .

Because of the large \ beam size, we consider the optical inclination superior to the \ inclination and use it in our further analysis. The solid-body rotation and the limited spatial resolution make it impossible to derive a well-determined rotation curve using tilted-ring models. We are, however, able to get an estimate for the maximum rotation velocity by using the position-velocity diagram and find 45 .

sources.summary: The paper describes D500-3 as an LSB galaxy with two bright core HII regions, surrounding fainter regions, and a 5 kpc bar with HII-region sizes from 25 to 150 pc.

quotes.quote: D500-3 (upper left) displays two bright regions near the galaxy core and a number of fainter regions surrounding the core. None of the HII regions are evident as higher continuum surface brightness regions from V frames. Even though the brightest two regions are relatively high in H luminosity (38.24 and 38.17 log L _ H , approximately 20 Orion complexes), their stellar populations have no effect on the optical structure of the nearby region of the galaxy. The 5 kpc bar is indicated in the upper right of the frame, where the larger HII regions are 100 to 150 pc in size, ranging down to 25 pc for the fainter regions.

gmw_00005717.name: D512-2
gmw_00005717.overview: D512-2 is a dwarf galaxy classified as Hubble type Sm [arXiv:0907.5533]. Located at an estimated distance of 14.1 Mpc, its velocity field exhibits solid-body rotation in its inner regions and a flat rotation curve in its outer parts [arXiv:0907.5533].

sources.summary: The paper presents D512-2 as an Sm galaxy with inner solid-body rotation, a flat outer rotation curve, and consistent pV and tilted-ring velocity estimates.

quotes.quote: D512-2 is a galaxy of Hubble type Sm. We assume a distance of 14.1 2.2 Mpc. The velocity field of D512-2 shows signs of solid-body rotation in its inner parts.

The rotation curve (cf. Fig.  fig:btf:d512-2-rc ) confirms what was already suggested by the velocity field: a solid-body rotation in the inner parts and a flat part in the outer regions. The rotation velocity of the flat part of the rotation curve is 35 . From the pV diagram, we estimate the (inclination corrected) maximum rotation velocity to be 37 .

gmw_00005718.name: D564-8
gmw_00005718.overview: D564-8 is a dwarf irregular galaxy located between 6.5 and 8.7 Mpc away [arXiv:0907.5533, arXiv:2006.01735]. It features an asymmetric global neutral hydrogen profile [arXiv:0907.5533], an extended neutral hydrogen disk containing eight detected gas holes [arXiv:2006.01735], and a maximum rotation velocity of 25 km/s [arXiv:0907.5533].

sources.summary: The paper identifies D564-8 as a dwarf irregular with an asymmetric H I profile, derives tilted-ring parameters, and measures a maximum rotation velocity of 25 km/s.

quotes.quote: For the dwarf irregular D564-8 (also known as F564-V3, see deblok-1996b ), we assume a distance of 6.5 2.6 Mpc. The global \ profile of D564-8 is asymmetric with its peak flux density towards the receding side of the galaxy. The asymmetry of the global \ profile can be traced also in the pV diagram.
Keeping all parameters except the rotation velocity fixed to their best estimates, we derive the rotation curve of D564-8. The maximum rotation velocity is 25 (see Fig.  fig:btf:d564-8-rc ).

sources.summary: The paper discusses LSB F564-V3 as a declining-curve candidate, noting an alternate name, non-detection of outer decline, low-SNR decline, and unphysical mass implications.

quotes.quote: We also reproduce in Fig. fig-rc three rotation curves from the 2015AJ....149..180O analysis of the LITTLE THINGS survey 2012AJ....144..134H : those of DDO 46, DDO 168 and LSB F564-V3.

Unfortunately neither DDO 46 nor LSB F564-V3 appear in the 2017MNRAS.466.4159I analysis: the former was excluded due to its low inclination (and thus large and uncertain correction to the rotation velocity), while the reason for the exclusion of the latter is unclear. A rotation curve for LSB F564-V3 was also determined by note that in that work the name LSB D564-8 is used 2009A&A...505..577T , who found no decline in the outer parts. Furthermore, inspection of figs. A.49 A.7 in 2015AJ....149..180O reveals that the declining part of the rotation curves of DDO 46 and LSB D564-8 occurs in regions of very low signal-to-noise ratio.

Finally, we note that the rotation curves of DDO 46, DDO 168 and LSB F564-V3 cannot be interpreted as tracers of the enclosed mass, as they fall more steeply than the Keplerian limit (illustrated by the slope of the thin grey lines in Fig. fig-rc ): if taken at face value, the declining rotation velocities in the outer parts of these galaxies imply a (nonsensical) declining cumulative dynamical mass profile.

sources.summary: The paper describes F564-V3 as a distant dwarf where eight H I holes were detected, mostly Type 3, with an extended H I disk.

quotes.quote: F564-V3 F564-V3 or LSBC D564-08 is situated at a distance of about 8.7 Mpc away from the Milky Way with V-band surface brightness 14 . We detected eight \ holes in this galaxy, five of which are Type 3 and the rest of which are Type 2. The scale height of the galaxy is 140 pc and the \ disk is extended to about 2.4 kpc.

gmw_00005719.name: D572-5
gmw_00005719.overview: D572-5 is an irregular [arXiv:0907.5533] low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:1306.4278] located at a distance of 14.6 Mpc [arXiv:0907.5533]. It features luminous core H II regions, fainter outer H II regions, and blue optical knots in its brighter areas [arXiv:1306.4278].

sources.summary: The paper describes D572-5 as a barely resolved irregular galaxy where low resolution prevents reliable rotation-curve or pV velocity measurements.

quotes.quote: D572-5 is an irregular galaxy at a distance of 14.6 3.2 Mpc. Our \ data of this galaxy are barely resolved (see, e.g., the moment maps in Fig.  fig:btf:d572-5 ), which makes it difficult to derive a reliable \ inclination. We therefore adopt the value of the I band inclination 50 as given by in further analysis.

Due to the low resolution, substantial off-axis \ emission enters the beam, causing the large velocity range in the minor axis pV diagram. Given these problems, a rotation curve or a \ from the major axis position-velocity diagram cannot be reliably derived. The only usable indicators for the maximum rotation velocity are 0 \ and 0.

sources.summary: The paper describes D572-5 as an LSB galaxy with comparatively luminous core HII regions, fainter outer regions, possible diffuse outer-disk H emission, and blue optical knots at its brighter regions.

quotes.quote: Galaxy D572-5 (upper right) exhibits a more luminous set of HII regions from other LSB galaxies, again several bright regions in the core and a few fainter HII regions in the outer regions. There is some indication of diffuse H emission in the outer disk, but insufficient to warrant inclusion by our selection algorithm. The brighter HII regions are just visible in the continuum V frames as distinct blue knots.

gmw_00005720.name: D575-1
gmw_00005720.overview: D575-1, also known as IC 3810, is classified as an Sm/Irr dwarf galaxy [arXiv:0907.5533]. Its global neutral hydrogen profile is well described by a Gaussian, but the galaxy exhibits too little velocity structure to estimate its rotation [arXiv:0907.5533].

sources.summary: The paper classifies D575-1/IC 3810 as an Sm/Irr galaxy with a Gaussian H I profile but too little velocity structure for resolved rotation estimates.

quotes.quote: D575-1, also known as IC 3810, is classified as an Sm/Irr galaxy. We assume a distance of 10.0 3.6 Mpc. Its global \ profile is well described by a Gaussian.

The first-moment map and the position-velocity diagrams show only a small velocity range and it proved impossible to estimate either \ or . The inclination of the \ disk (derived using ellipse fitting and corrected for the beam size) is 53 and in reasonable agreement with the optical inclination ( 61 ) from (though the observed small velocity range in the velocity field and pV diagram casts some doubt on the validity of these values).

gmw_00005721.name: D575-2
gmw_00005721.overview: D575-2, also known as UGC 8011, is a dwarf galaxy classified as Hubble type Im [arXiv:0907.5533]. It is kinematically lopsided, featuring a rotation curve that rises linearly in its inner regions before flattening at a velocity of 74 km/s [arXiv:0907.5533].

sources.summary: The paper treats D575-2/UGC 8011 as an Im galaxy with kinematic lopsidedness, a well-constrained center, and a rotation curve flattening at 74 km/s.

quotes.quote: D575-2, or UGC 8011, is a galaxy of the Hubble type Im, with an assumed distance of 12.2 4.5 Mpc. It was not possible to estimate \ from the position-velocity diagram. The galaxy is kinematically lopsided, which can be seen in the differences in the velocity contours between the approaching and receding side.

Fixing the PA to these values, we derive the inclination by averaging over all data points with >0 . The resulting inclination ( 57 5 ) is halfway between the optical inclination and the one from the \ disk. Keeping the inclination fixed to the kinematic estimate, we derive the rotation curve of D575-2 (see Fig.  fig:btf:d575-2-rc ). After a linear increase in the inner parts of the galaxy, the rotation velocity reaches a flat part at 74 .

gmw_00005722.name: D575-5
gmw_00005722.overview: D575-5 is a gas-rich, low surface brightness dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:2012.09911, arXiv:0907.5533]. Although it displays features of a star-forming dwarf, it lacks current H-alpha star formation [arXiv:2012.09911] and shows no clear signs of rotation [arXiv:0907.5533].

sources.summary: The paper presents F575-3 as a gas-rich LSB dwarf with no current H-alpha star formation, extremely blue colors, a very metal-poor RGB, and recent bursty star formation.

quotes.quote: We present new HST WFC3 near-IR observations of the CMD's in two LSB galaxies, F575-3 and F615-1, notable for having no current star formation based on a lack of H emission. F575-3 has the bluest RGB of any CMD in the literature, indicating an extremely low mean metallicity. The two galaxies chosen for near-IR HST imaging are F575-3 (also known as KDG215 and D575-3) and F615-1 (also known as KDG023).

Whereas, F615-1 has the colors typical to an early-type spiral (see Table 1 and Figure 2 from Schombert, McGaugh Lelli 2019), F575-3 is the bluest galaxy in the LSB catalog with a B-V color of 0.26, similar to the blue colors of a starburst irregular. F575-3 displays all of the features of a star-forming dwarf galaxy with a prominent blue and red helium branches (bHeB and rHeB), strong M-type AGB colony and a dominant blue main sequence (bMS).

F575-3 has similar recent SF features in its CMD when compared to other star-forming dwarf galaxies in the literature, yet has no current SF (i.e., H emission or obvious bright OB clusters). The shape and position of RGB in F575-3 indicates that a majority of the old stars in the RGB are an extremely metal-poor population.

sources.summary: The paper classifies D575-5 as a dwarf irregular with a narrow Gaussian H I profile and no clear resolved rotation signature.

quotes.quote: D575-5 is classified as a dwarf irregular at an assumed distance of 7.7 3.1 Mpc. Its global \ profile has a Gaussian shape and its 0 is the smallest of our sample ( 0 \ 19 for the Hanning smoothed data cube). estimate the optical inclination to be 66 , which is higher than the (beam corrected) inclination of the \ disk, for which we derive 50 .

However, neither the velocity field, nor the major axis pV diagram show clear signs of rotation. We were unable to derive a maximum rotation velocity from either the velocity field, or from the major-axis position-velocity diagram. The second-moment map shows values of 6--8 \ throughout the entire disk.

gmw_00005723.name: D631-7
gmw_00005723.overview: D631-7, also known as UGC 4115, is a dwarf irregular galaxy located at an estimated distance of 5.5 Mpc [arXiv:0907.5533]. It features a well-resolved rotating velocity field with a rotation curve that flattens at 58 km/s [arXiv:0907.5533], and its dark matter profile is better fit by an isothermal halo than an NFW halo [arXiv:1911.11877].

sources.summary: The paper identifies D631-7/UGC 4115 as a dwarf irregular with a well-resolved rotating velocity field and a rotation curve flattening at 58 km/s.

quotes.quote: D631-7 is also known as UGC 4115 and is classified as a dwarf irregular. estimate its distance to be 5.5 0.6 Mpc using the luminosity of the tip of the red giant branch. The global \ profile of D631-7 is single-peaked. Its velocity field is well-resolved and shows clear signs of rotation.

Keeping all parameters except \ fixed, we determine the rotation curve of D631-7 (see Fig.  fig:btf:d631-7-rc ). It shows the typical solid-body rotation in the inner parts, but reaches a flat part at 58 . Assuming the kinematical inclination we derive 53 from the pV diagram.

sources.summary: The paper says UGC4115 is one of two void dwarf galaxies better fit by an isothermal halo, while unconstrained NFW fitting failed and required fixing the concentration parameter.

quotes.quote: For 2 galaxies, viz. UGC4115 and UGC5288, the isothermal halo gave a better fit compared to the NFW halo.

For the galaxy UGC4115, we did not get physical results when both and R _ 200 were allowed to freely vary during the fitting process. For this galaxy we hence fix the value of to the average concentration parameter for other galaxies ( 5.5 ).

gmw_00005724.name: D640-13
gmw_00005724.overview: D640-13 is classified as an Im/Sm type dwarf galaxy located at an adopted distance of 13.4 Mpc [arXiv:0907.5533]. It is barely resolved in neutral hydrogen observations, showing no clear signs of rotation in its velocity maps [arXiv:0907.5533].

sources.summary: The paper describes D640-13 as an Im/Sm galaxy that is barely resolved in H I, so only corrected profile widths are used for its velocity proxy.

quotes.quote: D640-13 is an Im/Sm type galaxy with an adopted distance of 13.4 3.4 Mpc. Its global \ profile has a Gaussian shape. Comparing the size and the shape of the beam with that of the moment maps shows that the galaxy is barely resolved.

We therefore use the optical inclination for our further analysis. As neither the position-velocity diagrams, nor the first-moment map show clear signs of rotation, only the widths of the 0 and 0 profiles remain as a proxy for the maximum rotation velocity, which we correct using the optical inclination.

gmw_00005725.name: IKN
gmw_00005725.overview: IKN is a bright dwarf spheroidal galaxy located in the M81 Group [arXiv:1909.12997]. Its stellar population is dominated by metal-poor red giant branch stars, alongside intermediate-age asymptotic giant branch stars [arXiv:1909.12997].

sources.summary: The survey finds IKN is a bright M81 dwarf elliptical with larger luminosity than earlier estimates, metal-poor RGB stars, and intermediate-age AGB stars.

quotes.quote: Fields 1 to 4 include eight previously known dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxies of the M81 Group; IKN, KDG061, KDG064, BK5N, d0955+70, d1005+68, d1014+68, and d1015+69, as well as M81, M82 and NGC3077. In this section, we derive, in a homogeneous manner, the structural parameters and the photometric metallicity distributions of these dSphs based on the HSC survey.

Thanks to the photometric depth of our survey, we are able to resolve individual stars to at least 1.5 magnitude below the RGB tip (TRGB) of each dwarf galaxy. In Figure fig: cmds of old dwarfs , the dominant populations are seen to be metal-poor RGBs. The four brighter dwarfs, IKN, KDG061, KDG064 and BK5N, also show populations of asymptotic giant branch (AGB) stars brighter than the TRGB, indicating these systems contain intermediate age (0.5-5 Gyr) stars.
The radial profiles shown in Figures fig: map and profile 1 to fig: map and profile 3 are well described by the Sersic and King profiles.

gmw_00005726.name: KDG061
gmw_00005726.overview: KDG061 is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located within the M81 Group [arXiv:1909.12997]. Its stellar population is dominated by metal-poor red giant branch stars, alongside intermediate-age asymptotic giant branch stars [arXiv:1909.12997].

sources.summary: The survey derives KDG061 structural and metallicity properties, finds a metal-poor RGB plus AGB population, and notes possible M81/NGC3077 halo contamination.

quotes.quote: Fields 1 to 4 include eight previously known dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxies of the M81 Group; IKN, KDG061, KDG064, BK5N, d0955+70, d1005+68, d1014+68, and d1015+69, as well as M81, M82 and NGC3077. The four brighter dwarfs, IKN, KDG061, KDG064 and BK5N, also show populations of asymptotic giant branch (AGB) stars brighter than the TRGB, indicating these systems contain intermediate age (0.5-5 Gyr) stars.
The radial profiles shown in Figures fig: map and profile 1 to fig: map and profile 3 are well described by the Sersic and King profiles.

gmw_00005727.name: KDG064
gmw_00005727.overview: KDG064 is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located within the M81 Group [arXiv:1909.12997]. Its stellar population is dominated by metal-poor red giant branch stars, alongside intermediate-age asymptotic giant branch stars [arXiv:1909.12997].

sources.summary: The survey measures KDG064 structural and photometric metallicity properties and finds its RGB population is metal-poor with evidence for intermediate-age AGB stars.

quotes.quote: Fields 1 to 4 include eight previously known dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxies of the M81 Group; IKN, KDG061, KDG064, BK5N, d0955+70, d1005+68, d1014+68, and d1015+69, as well as M81, M82 and NGC3077. The radial profiles shown in Figures fig: map and profile 1 to fig: map and profile 3 are well described by the Sersic and King profiles.

gmw_00005728.name: BK5N
gmw_00005728.overview: BK5N is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located within the M81 Group [arXiv:1909.12997]. Its stellar population is dominated by a metal-poor red giant branch, but it also contains intermediate-age asymptotic giant branch stars [arXiv:1909.12997].

sources.summary: The survey derives BK5N structural parameters, finds a metal-poor RGB with intermediate-age AGB stars, and notes a slight outer excess likely from d1005+68.

quotes.quote: Fields 1 to 4 include eight previously known dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxies of the M81 Group; IKN, KDG061, KDG064, BK5N, d0955+70, d1005+68, d1014+68, and d1015+69, as well as M81, M82 and NGC3077. In this section, we derive, in a homogeneous manner, the structural parameters and the photometric metallicity distributions of these dSphs based on the HSC survey.

Thanks to the photometric depth of our survey, we are able to resolve individual stars to at least 1.5 magnitude below the RGB tip (TRGB) of each dwarf galaxy. In Figure fig: cmds of old dwarfs , the dominant populations are seen to be metal-poor RGBs. The four brighter dwarfs, IKN, KDG061, KDG064 and BK5N, also show populations of asymptotic giant branch (AGB) stars brighter than the TRGB, indicating these systems contain intermediate age (0.5-5 Gyr) stars.
The radial profiles shown in Figures fig: map and profile 1 to fig: map and profile 3 are well described by the Sersic and King profiles.

gmw_00005729.name: d0955+70
gmw_00005729.overview: The galaxy d0955+70 is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located within the M81 Group [arXiv:1909.12997]. Its stellar population is dominated by metal-poor red giant branch stars, and its radial profile is well described by Sersic and King profiles [arXiv:1909.12997].

sources.summary: The survey measures d0955+70 structural and metallicity properties, finding a larger Sersic radius than prior integrated-light estimates and a slight metal-poor MDF excess.

sources.summary: The survey analyzes d1005+68 as an M81 dwarf spheroidal, finding resolved RGB structure, consistent luminosity, and possible but uncertain outer overdensities.

sources.summary: The survey derives d1014+68 structural and metallicity properties, including a larger Sersic radius than earlier estimates and a slight metal-poor MDF excess.

gmw_00005732.overview: The galaxy d1015+69 is a faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy located within the M81 Group [arXiv:1909.12997]. Its stellar population is dominated by metal-poor red giant branch stars, and its radial profile is well described by Sersic and King profiles [arXiv:1909.12997].

sources.summary: The survey measures d1015+69 as a faint M81 dwarf spheroidal, finding resolved RGB populations, a larger Sersic radius, and possible noisy outer overdensities.

quotes.quote: Fields 1 to 4 include eight previously known dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxies of the M81 Group; IKN, KDG061, KDG064, BK5N, d0955+70, d1005+68, d1014+68, and d1015+69, as well as M81, M82 and NGC3077. gmw_00005733.name: d1006+69
gmw_00005733.overview: The object d1006+69 is a faint, metal-poor dwarf galaxy candidate [arXiv:1909.12997]. It is a satellite of the M81 group and has a distance of approximately 4.33 Mpc determined from its resolved red giant branch [arXiv:1909.12997].

sources.summary: The paper presents d1006+69 as a newly identified faint, metal-poor M81 dwarf satellite candidate with a resolved RGB and TRGB distance of 4.33 Mpc.

quotes.quote: However, two prominent overdensities, which we name d1006+69 and d1009+68, show convincing RGB sequences on the CMDs (see Figure fig: uko01 cmd and fig: uko02 cmd ). Figure fig: uko img shows portions of the HSC i band image showing these objects. It can be seen that d1006+69 projects close to the galaxy SDSSJ100655.25+695413.4, which has a photometric redshift of z 0.319 . The stars we identify as members of d1006+69 are too bright to be misidentified globular clusters associated with this background system and furthermore they do not distribute symmetrically around this galaxy.

Finally, combining these values with the observed i_ 0, TRGB , we calculate distance moduli as =28.18 0.10 for d1006+69 which corresponds to 4.33 0.20 Mpc, and =27.86 0.29 for d1009+68 which corresponds to 3.7 0.5 Mpc. The errors include both the photometric errors as derived from artificial star tests and the uncertainties in the TRGB calibration.

gmw_00005734.name: d1009+68
gmw_00005734.overview: The object d1009+68 is a dwarf satellite galaxy candidate of M81 [arXiv:1909.12997]. It has a resolved red giant branch stellar population and an estimated distance of 3.7 megaparsecs [arXiv:1909.12997].

sources.summary: The paper presents d1009+68 as a new M81 dwarf satellite candidate with a resolved RGB, TRGB distance estimate, structural parameters, and low luminosity.

quotes.quote: However, two prominent overdensities, which we name d1006+69 and d1009+68, show convincing RGB sequences on the CMDs (see Figure fig: uko01 cmd and fig: uko02 cmd ). Figure fig: uko img shows portions of the HSC i band image showing these objects.

Finally, combining these values with the observed i_ 0, TRGB , we calculate distance moduli as =28.18 0.10 for d1006+69 which corresponds to 4.33 0.20 Mpc, and =27.86 0.29 for d1009+68 which corresponds to 3.7 0.5 Mpc. The errors include both the photometric errors as derived from artificial star tests and the uncertainties in the TRGB calibration.

Figure fig: uko01 cmd and fig: uko02 cmd show the CMDs of galaxies and their nearby reference fields. The panels show stellar objects lying within the core radius (left), the Sersic half-light radius (middle), and a reference field of radius centerd 5 away from the galaxy center (right).

gmw_00005735.name: GS 4891
gmw_00005735.overview: GS 4891 is a massive star-forming galaxy at redshift z ~ 3.7 located within a galaxy group [arXiv:2309.14431]. The galaxy exhibits velocity gradients consistent with rotation, a clear gas metallicity gradient, and a compact ionized outflow extending 1.2 kiloparsecs from its nucleus [arXiv:2309.14431].

sources.summary: The paper studies GS 4891 as a z=3.7 star-forming group galaxy with rotating ionized gas, metallicity gradients, substructure, and a compact ionized outflow.

quotes.quote: We present NIRSpec IFS observations of a galaxy group around the massive GS 4891 galaxy at z 3.7 in GOODS-South that includes two other two systems, GS 4891 n to the north and GS 28356 to the east. These observations, obtained as part of the GTO Galaxy Assembly - NIRSpec IFS (GA-NIFS) program, allow for the first time to study the spatially resolved properties of the interstellar medium (ISM) and ionized gas kinematics of a galaxy at this redshift. Leveraging the wide wavelength range spanned with the high-dispersion grating (with resolving power R=2700) observations, covering from to , we explore the spatial distribution of star-formation rate, nebular attenuation and gas metallicity, together with the mechanisms responsible for the excitation of the ionized gas.

GS 4891 presents a clear gradient of gas metallicity (as traced by 12 + log(O/H)) by more than 0.2 dex from the south-east (where a star-forming clump is identified) to the north-west. The kinematic analysis shows that GS 4891 presents velocity gradients in the ionized gas consistent with rotation. Finally, GS 4891 hosts an ionized outflow that extends out to r_ out = 1.2  kpc from the nucleus and reaches maximum velocities r_ out of approximately 400 km/s.

gmw_00005736.name: GS 28356
gmw_00005736.overview: GS 28356 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 3.6 [arXiv:2309.14431]. It resides as the eastern member of the GS 4891 galaxy group, exhibiting spatially resolved emission lines with low velocity variations [arXiv:2309.14431].

sources.summary: The paper treats GS 28356 as the eastern member of the GS 4891 z=3.7 galaxy group, showing spatially resolved emission and low velocity variation.

quotes.quote: The main galaxy, GS 4891, has a very close companion to the north, which we refer to as GS 4891 n, and another galaxy to the east at a projected distance of 15  kpc identified in CANDELS as GS 28356 (photo- z =3.646).

The three galaxies display spatially-resolved emission in the two lines over several kiloparsecs. The similar observed wavelength of the emission lines in their spectra confirms that they reside at the same redshift and are part of a galaxy group. The three galaxies exhibit clear detections of strong emission lines such as the doublet, and the hydrogen Balmer recombination lines and .
GS 28356 displays small variation in velocities (<50 km/s) and low W80 ( <150  km/s).

gmw_00005737.name: borg 0440 5244 682
gmw_00005737.overview: The source borg 0440 5244 682 is a candidate high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy at z ~ 8 [arXiv:1303.6109]. It is believed to be strongly lensed by a foreground group, showing an elongated shape and a possible faint counterimage [arXiv:1303.6109].

sources.summary: The paper identifies borg 0440 5244 682 as a candidate z 8 galaxy whose brightness, elongation, possible counterimage, and lens modeling suggest magnification by a foreground z 1.7 group.

quotes.quote: Photometry of Y_ 098 dropout and foreground sources The candidate z 8 galaxy, borg 0440 5244 682 was identified in field borg 0440 5244 (following the notation of bradley2012brightest ). The photometry presented in bradley2012brightest gives a J_ 125 magnitude of 25.9 0.1 mag, a Y_ 098 J_ 125 residual of > 2.1 mag, and S/N after sky subtraction in V_ 600 , Y_ 098 , J_ 125 and H_ 160 of 0.8, -0.5, 9.1 and 5.7 , respectively. We find a J_ 125 ellipticity for the dropout of 0.4 in the direction of the suspected critical curve of the lens. The J_ 125 half-light radius of the dropout was measured, using SExtractor , to be 0 21 ( 1.1 kpc at z 8 ).

We see 4 indicators that provide circumstantial evidence that borg 0440 5244 682 is a z 8 LBG lensed by a foreground group. We observe a possible faint counter image on the opposite side of the deflector. Both the brighter image (the `primary image'), and the fainter image (the `counter image') are completely absent in the Y_ 098 and V_ 600 filters, consistent with the z 8 LBG selection criteria, and with the lensing hypothesis (lensing is achromatic).

gmw_00005738.name: NGC 2207/IC 2163
gmw_00005738.overview: NGC 2207/IC 2163 is an interacting pair of spiral galaxies [arXiv:1107.3280, arXiv:2201.02657] that underwent a close encounter approximately 40 million years ago [arXiv:1107.3280]. This interaction has produced tidal tails [arXiv:1107.3280, arXiv:2201.02657] and large-scale star-forming clumps within their density waves [arXiv:astro-ph/0610217].

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 2163 as the nearby star-forming host of two obscured transients, placing them in dusty spiral arms and noting its distance, foreground extinction, star formation rate, and high supernova activity.

quotes.quote: Here we report the discovery of two transients discovered in dusty spiral arms of the galaxy IC 2163, SPIRITS 15c and SPIRITS 14buu. The IR luminosity of SPIRITS 15c was brighter than 17 mag, one of the most luminous transients discovered by SPIRITS to date and more luminous than the new classes of IR-dominated transients discussed above. Additionally, the spectrum of SPIRITS 15c is dominated by a broad emission line of He i , suggesting this is an explosive event such as an SN, but with significant dust extinction that obscured the transient in the optical.

SPIRITS 15c was discovered at a right ascension and declination of 06^ h 16^ m 28 49, -21 22 42 2 (J2000), coincident with a spiral arm of the galaxy IC 2163. We assume a distance modulus to IC 2163 of = 32.75 0.4 mag ( 35.5 Mpc, theureau07 from NED The NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) is operated by the Jet propulsion Laboratory, California Institute of Technology, under contract with the National Aeronautics and Space Administration. ; z=0.00922 , v=2765 km s ^ 1 , elmegreen00 ). We note that IC 2163 was selected as part of the SPIRITS sample based on an incorrect distance estimate that placed it within the 20 Mpc cutoff for SPIRITS galaxies.

Combining these and following kennicutt09 give global star formation rates of 1.9 M_ yr ^ 1 for IC 2163 and 6.8 M_ yr ^ 1 for both galaxies combined. The rate of SNe associated with this galaxy pair is also high, with 5 known SNe hosted in IC 2163 and NGC 2207 since 1975.

sources.summary: The paper describes this spiral pair as recently interacting, with radio emission tracing IC 2163 tidal material, gas accretion onto NGC 2207, and ordered magnetic fields along tails.

quotes.quote: NGC 2207/IC 2163 In this pair of spiral galaxies, NGC 2207 appears in the foreground, obscuring the other one, IC 2163. According to the numerical simulations, 4 10^7 yr ago IC 2163 passed behind NGC 2207 from west to east at a distance of about 20 kpc (Elmegreen et al. ). Seventeen young stellar clusters were discovered in both galaxies (Elmegreen et al. ), but the interaction has not yet led to a prominent starburst.

The radio emission at 4.86 GHz (Fig. f:n2207tp ) covers the optical extent of the system but displays a hole in the central part of NGC 2207 like in the map at 1.4 GHz (Condon ). The emission traces the optical western tidal tail of IC 2163 and peaks at the eastern edge of NGC 2207, where a gravitational instability region is suspected, producing a maximum in the infrared emission (Elmegreen et al. ). In this region, the total and polarized emission also have maxima, but not that degree of polarization, so there is no enhanced production of ordered magnetic field here, and the found radio properties of this region could result from a supply of relativistic electrons coming from the western tidal tail of the IC 2163. This idea is supported by accretion of gas from the IC 2163 onto the NGC 2207, as suggested by Elmegreen et al. ( ).

sources.summary: The paper cites Spitzer results showing that the NGC 2207/IC 2163 density waves contain large-scale star-forming clumps likely produced by gravitational instabilities and agglomeration.

quotes.quote: The recent Spitzer study of the NGC 2207/IC 2163 system by provides evidence of an agglomeration scale much larger than the scale of individual star clusters in the density waves of these galaxies. Figure 2, taken from that paper, illustrates these points. The distribution of star cluster luminosities (from HST I-band observations) is a fairly typical power-law at the more complete high luminosity (mass) end.

As in other galaxies, this is probably the result of turbulent dynamics within large clouds and cloud assemblies. Clumps seen in the Spitzer 8 m image have a narrow distribution at high (summed I band) luminosity. These clumps tend to be quite uniform in each wave and are probably the result of large-scale gravitational instabilities and agglomeration.

sources.summary: The paper places SN 2010jp in remote outer-arm or tidal-tail material of the interacting NGC 2207/IC 2163 pair, noting star-forming arms, faint outskirts, and low extinction.

quotes.quote: The ground-based continuum image (Fig. fig:image a and fig:image ) shows low surface brightness emission from outer spiral arms of NGC 2207 or tidal tails from the interacting pair of galaxies. SN 2010jp appears to reside close to (within 1 kpc) of one such faint string of continuum emission running north to south. The HST images in F555W and F814W indicate that the stars seen in this tidal tail have a relatively blue colour, perhaps indicating a moderately young population. This suggests that SN 2010jp belongs to one of these outer tails of the merging spirals, rather than to a very faint satellite dwarf galaxy. While the continuum-subtracted H image (Fig. fig:image b and fig:image d) shows copious star formation via chains of H ii regions along spiral arms in IC 2163 and NGC 2207, there is little evidence of bright H emission in the far outer spiral arms more than 10 kpc from the galaxy centres.

sources.summary: The paper monitored the NGC 2207/IC 2163 system weekly with Swift for about six months, collecting XRT and UVOT epochs and finding no transient events.

quotes.quote: NGC 2207/IC 2163 The weekly monitoring of the system NGC 2207/IC 2163 ranged from the beginning of November 2013 to the end of April 2014. Good data have been collected for 27 epochs by the XRT, for 26 epochs by the UVOT (see Table observations_NGC2207 ). We found no transient events in the UVOT data nor in the XRT data.

gmw_00005739.name: NGC 2770
gmw_00005739.overview: NGC 2770 is a nearby spiral galaxy [arXiv:0805.1188] characterized by a high gas mass [arXiv:1011.2521], substantial dust content [arXiv:0805.1188], and counter-rotating CO gas [arXiv:1011.2521]. It is interacting with companion galaxies [arXiv:2008.08091], including a dwarf companion [arXiv:1011.2521], which has driven an increased star formation rate [arXiv:2008.08091].

sources.summary: The paper explains NGC 2770’s unusually high core-collapse supernova rate through recently enhanced star formation, high dust extinction, and interaction with nearby companions.

quotes.quote: NGC 2770 ( UGC 4806 ) was dubbed an SN Ib factory thone09 because it hosted three type Ib SNe in ten years. Its SFR was reported to not be enhanced and, therefore, not compatible with such a high SN rate thone09 . In 2015, a fourth SN (type IIn) exploded, bringing the current rate to four over the last 20 years.

We provide support for the hypothesis that the increased SFR in is due to the interaction with its companion galaxies. The bridge between and B is definitive proof that they are interacting. The atomic disc of is also asymmetric. The interaction is not obvious on the optical image, which does not reveal disturbances. We report the existence of a total of four companions within 100 kpc (one identified for the first time).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2770 as the nearby spiral host of SN 2008D, measuring its nuclear redshift and linking the supernova sightline to substantial host dust and extinction.

quotes.quote: The contribution from the underlying host galaxy light was always negligible, as also apparent from archival Sloan Digital Sky Survey images. Our photometric results are listed in Table tab:photo . One of our spectra covers the nucleus of NGC 2770, allowing a precise measurement of its redshift: z = 0.0070 0.0009 .

It follows from the detection of strong Na 1 D with an equivalent width (EW) of 1.3 that the extinction toward SN 2008D is substantial in NGC 2770. Our best estimate of the reddening comes from comparing the colors of SN 2008D with those of stripped-envelope SNe, which have V-R R-I 0.1 around maximum (e.g., Folatelli06,Richmond96,Galama98 ). The resulting reddening is E(B-V) = 0.8 mag, corresponding to an extinction A_V = 2.5 mag (using the extinction law by Cardelli89 with R_V = 3.1 ). A large dust content is supported by absorption features in our high-resolution spectrum (see also Soderberg08a ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2770 as a nearby SN host with high gas content, possible recent dwarf accretion, elevated SN activity, and counter-rotating CO gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 2770 does have a dwarf companion, NGC 2770B, and based upon recent work on the local group of galaxies e.g. mcconnachie2009nature , it is clear that collisions with dwarf galaxies are ongoing into the present epoch. We accordingly conclude that NGC 2770 has absorbed a dwarf galaxy at some point in the last 10^8 yr or so. Although the SNe have occurred in different parts of NGC 2770, studies of other galaxies have shown that collisions or mergers can affect the star formation in the entire galaxy see e.g. bureau2006mnras .

Although NGC 2770 has a rather high gas mass and HI surface density compared to other nearby spiral galaxies, indicating that there is a lot of material present to produce stars, the star formation rate does not seem to be exceptionally high, and the same is true for the local star formation rate at the sites of the SNe thone2009apj . Our CO observations with the ARO 12-m telescope of the host-galaxy show evidence for counter-rotating gas in NGC 2770, only the second galaxy for which this has been found.

sources.summary: The paper monitored NGC 2770 with Swift, collected 25 XRT and UVOT epochs, found no relevant UV transient, and found no X-ray transient or variability in or around the galaxy.

quotes.quote: NGC 2770 The weekly monitoring of the galaxy NGC 2770 ranged from the second week of December 2013 to the beginning of June 2014. Good data have been collected for 25 epochs by both the XRT and the UVOT (see Table observations_NGC2770 ). In the UVOT data we found no transient events that could be interesting to our purposes. However, we identified a sudden drop of the UV flux emitted by a star outside the target galaxy.
In the XRT data we found no transient events and no variability inside or outside the galaxy NGC 2770.

gmw_00005740.name: 7C1826+6510
gmw_00005740.overview: 7C1826+6510 is a low-luminosity radio galaxy host [arXiv:astro-ph/9809155] located at a redshift of 0.646 [arXiv:astro-ph/9903314]. It exhibits a multicomponent morphology aligned along its radio axis [arXiv:astro-ph/9809155], which includes an associated secondary component dominated by an old stellar population [arXiv:astro-ph/9903314, arXiv:astro-ph/9809155].

sources.summary: The paper identifies component b as the 7C 1826+6510 host galaxy, contrasts it with aligned component d, and measures redshifts, old-star spectra, and a large velocity difference.

quotes.quote: 7C 1826+6510 The radio central component detected in an A-array 8 GHz VLA observation (Lacy et al. \ 1998b) shows that component `b' of Lacy et al. \ (1998b) is the host galaxy (as claimed by Lacy et al. \ 1993), although only the aligned component `d' has detected emission lines. Both `b' and `d' have spectra dominated by old stars (Fig. \ 2). The diffuse component `' which lies between `b' and `d' also has a red SED, but is too faint for us to be able to tell if it contains stars, or to measure a redshift for. The redshifts of `b' and the stellar component of `d' were determined by the cross-correlation technique described above. We find that `b' has a redshift of 0.646 0.001 and `d' a redshift of 0.685 0.001 (agreeing with the emission line redshift).

The velocity difference between these two components is 7100 400 kms ^ 1 , which is high, but not implausible if they are members of a rich cluster. Although clearly well-aligned, `d' lies outside our largest aperture so is excluded from our analysis in the next section.

sources.summary: The paper describes 7C1826+6510 as a low-luminosity radio source host with multicomponent aligned morphology, an associated secondary component, stellar-continuum spectrum, and diffuse material along the radio axis.

quotes.quote: The morphologies of the aligned material differ: in two cases, the hosts are apparently ellipticals with the major axis within 30 \ of the radio PA, while others (e.g. 7C1748+6731 and 7C1826+6510) have multicomponent morphologies along the radio axis similar to that seen in the high luminosity 3C sources. However, in the ``elliptical'' host 7C1745+6415, the spectrum and consequently the morphology visible in the I-band image are dominated by emission lines rather than stellar continuum (Fig. 2). On the other hand, in 7C1826+6510, which has a secondary component d lying along the radio axis well-separated spatially from the host galaxy b , the spectrum of d confirms that it is probably associated. Its spectrum is dominated by stellar continuum and shows a 4000 \ break and stellar absorption features with a 8000 km/sec velocity relative to the central component b . Furthermore, visible in the image is diffuse material lying along the radio axis.

gmw_00005741.name: NGC 7489
gmw_00005741.overview: NGC 7489 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0301250]. Its continuum lightcurves at different wavelengths exhibit a high degree of correlation, with time delays that increase with wavelength [arXiv:astro-ph/0301250].

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 7489 as the best-observed AGN continuum-reverberation case, where wavelength-dependent delays and difference spectra suggest disk variability can isolate the accretion-disk component.

quotes.quote: AGN continuum lightcurves at different wavelengths exhibit a high degree of correlation, with time delays generally shorter than a few days. In the best-observed case , NGC 7489, the time delay does increase with wavelength, consistent with ^ 4/3 . Moreover, subtracting bright and faint spectra yields a difference spectrum close to f_ ^ 1/3 . These results suggest that variability may be an effective way to isolate the disk component of AGN spectra. The NGC 7489 results yield estimates for M M and D , leading to an encouragingly sensible estimate of the Hubble constant, H_0 i 42 9 /Mpc.

gmw_00005742.name: RGB J1629+4008
gmw_00005742.overview: RGB J1629+4008 is a broad-line radio galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0509718] that has also been classified as a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:0704.3836]. It exhibits rapid X-ray variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0208501] and remains unresolved in radio observations [arXiv:astro-ph/0509718, arXiv:0704.3836].
