This is our most extreme host galaxy on the Fundamental Plane, lying in the upper right corner.

sources.summary: The paper describes 2201 + 315/4C + 31.63 as a z=0.2974 quasar and X-ray AGN with post-merger optical signs and FR II radio morphology.

quotes.quote: 2201 + 315 (J2203 + 3145) 2201 + 315 (4C + 31.63) at a redshift of z = 0.2974 was identified as a low optical polarization quasar 1996ApJS..104...37M and an intermediate-spectral-peaked source 2010A&A...518A..10V . It was detected by the Fermi /LAT with a 5 significance 2015ApJS..218...23A . It is also a strong X-ray AGN observed by Swift with a flux of 3.22 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 at 0.1 2.4 keV 2010A&A...520A..47M . The optical image shows signs of post-merger with unusual distribution of dust 1984ApJS...55..319H . In the radio band, this quasar displays a typical FR II morphology with a total size of 90 with a bright core and double lobes at opposite sides of the core aligning in the northeast--southwest direction 1984AJ.....89.1658G,1984AJ.....89..195N,2007ApJS..171..376C .

sources.summary: The note describes 2201 + 315 as a quasar with prior X-ray imaging, fast VLBI components, a long VLA jet, a northeast lobe, and near-core Chandra detection.

quotes.quote: 2201 + 315 (4C + 31.63) The X-ray image was first published by 2011ApJ...730...92H . This quasar has a VLBI components with apparent velocities up to 8.3 along a PA of -145 \ 2013AJ....146..120L . In the VLA image, the jet is -37 long at a PA of -110 , terminating in a hotspot 2007ApJS..171..376C . To the northeast, there is a lobe about 45 from the core. The radio jet is detected in the Chandra data only out to about 4 from the core.

gmw_00005791.name: UGC7576
gmw_00005791.overview: UGC7576 is a kinematically confirmed polar-ring galaxy located at a distance of approximately 94 Mpc [arXiv:1104.2052]. It features a central body with elliptical isophotes, a narrow polar ring crossing the central region, and a faint outer envelope [arXiv:1104.2052].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC7576 as a polar-ring galaxy with a narrow ring, central elliptical body, faint envelope, asymmetric colors, warped disk, high mass-to-light ratio, low metallicity, and cold-accretion evidence.

quotes.quote: UGC7576 UGC7576 (Fig. 7576 ) is a kinematically confirmed Polar Ring Galaxy Whi90 and its main properties are listed in Tab. global . This object is at a distance of about 94 Mpc, based on H_ 0 = 75 \ km \ s^ 1 \ Mpc^ 1 and heliocentric radial velocity of V = 7022 \ km \ s^ 1 , which implies that 1 arcsec = 0.45 kpc. The kinematics shows that the polar structure of UGC7576 is more similar to a ring rather than a disk, given that the rotation curve does not show differential rotation, contrary to those of NGC4650A and UGC9796. studied the global morphology of UGC7576 and they were able to distinguish three components: i ) the main central body with elliptical isophotes, ii ) a narrow polar ring crossing the central region of the main galaxy, iii ) a faint outer envelope. The gradient of the surface brightness distribution of the central galaxy decrease at \ 20'' , while both B-V and V-R color indices slightly decrease with radius.

gmw_00005792.name: UGC9796
gmw_00005792.overview: UGC9796, also known as IIZw 73, is a polar-ring galaxy located at a distance of approximately 72 Mpc [arXiv:1104.2052]. Its central S0 galaxy is rotationally supported, while its asymmetric polar structure exhibits a rapid rate of differential precession [arXiv:1104.2052].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC9796, also called IIZw 73, as a polar-ring galaxy with an asymmetric polar structure, HI-rich differentially rotating disk, high dark mass, low metallicity, and possible accretion origin.

quotes.quote: UGC9796 UGC9796 (Fig. 9796 ), also known as IIZw 73, is at a distance of about 72 Mpc based on H_ 0 = 75 \ km \ s^ 1 \ Mpc^ 1 and heliocentric radial velocity of V = 5406 \ km \ s^ 1 , which implies that 1 arcsec = 0.35 kpc. It has one of the most non-polar PRG. Its apparent major axis in fact is only 65^ from the major axis of the central S0 rotationally supported galaxy and this implies a rapid rate of differential precession. The polar structure is less symmetric than in UGC7576 and the distribution of the colors is also asymmetric, with the NE side considerably redder than the SW side.

gmw_00005793.name: MARK938
gmw_00005793.overview: MARK938 is a galaxy that has undergone a recent merger, displaying distinct tidal tails [arXiv:1708.02683]. Its blue spectrum reveals a post-starburst galaxy dominated by an A-star continuum, while its red spectrum shows shock-excited emission [arXiv:1708.02683].

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe MARK938 as a recent merger with tidal tails, controversial excitation classification, and S7 spectra indicating a post-starburst galaxy with shock-excited red emission.

quotes.quote: MARK938: A well-studied recent merger, with distinct tidal tails. The nature of the excitation has been controversial - Veron-Cetty86 classify it as a Sy 2 but Thean00 argue that it should be classified as a starburst rather than a Seyfert. However, our data clearly show it to be a post-starburst galaxy with an A-star dominant continuum spectrum in the blue (very deep Balmer absorption), while the red spectrum is consistent with shock-excited emission. This is visible in all parts of the main galaxy.

gmw_00005794.name: ESO 420-G13
gmw_00005794.overview: ESO 420-G13 is a post-starburst galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1708.02683]. It features a one-sided extended narrow-line region, a reddened counter-cone, and a bright star-forming region near the nucleus that likely forms a confining ring [arXiv:1708.02683].

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe ESO 420-G13 as a post-starburst galaxy with one-sided and counter-cone extended narrow-line emission, a bright star-forming region near the nucleus, and an apparent confining ring.

quotes.quote: ESO 420-G13: In \ there is a one-side ENLR ( PA 30^ ) embedded in a galaxy with a strong post-starburst (A-star) signature. The ENLR can be traced to the edge of the WiFeS field. Close to the nucleus, and on the SW side, there is a bright region of star formation. This probably forms a confining ring to the ENLR. At \ + the reddened counter-cone of the ENLR is visible.

gmw_00005795.name: ESO 362-G08
gmw_00005795.overview: ESO 362-G08 is a post-starburst galaxy viewed at a high inclination [arXiv:1708.02683]. It hosts an obscured Seyfert 2 nucleus and exhibits very deep Na I D-line absorption, alongside a dynamically distinct, high-excitation emission region on its northeast side [arXiv:1708.02683].

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe ESO 362-G08 as an obscured Seyfert 2 in a high-inclination post-starburst galaxy, with deep Na D absorption and a dynamically distinct high-excitation region.

quotes.quote: ESO 362-G08: Obscured Sy 2 nucleus in post-starburst galaxy seen at high inclination. Very deep Na 1 D-line absorption is seen. An amazing high-excitation ``Green Bean" emission region is seen with Sy 2 emission signature on NE side of nucleus; looks to be a lower abundance minor galaxy blueshifted with respect to the main galaxy. The Green Bean displays a separate rotational signature, confirming that it is a dynamically distinct entity. This region had been first described by Fraquelli00 , but was interpreted in terms of the ejection of an ENLR from the main galaxy.

gmw_00005796.name: NGC 3100
gmw_00005796.overview: NGC 3100 is a lenticular [arXiv:2112.00755] low-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:2305.01075] that forms a pair with the barred spiral NGC 3095 [arXiv:2112.00755]. It hosts a young radio source [arXiv:2305.01075] whose jets interact with a disrupted molecular gas disc [arXiv:2010.04685], driving a mild outflow [arXiv:2112.00755, arXiv:2405.10683].

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA observations of NGC 3100, a late S0 radio-galaxy host, finding excited CO near radio lobes and mild jet-driven molecular-gas kinematic disturbances.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present follow-up Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) observations of one source, NGC 3100, targeting the ^ 12 CO(1-0), ^ 12 CO(3-2), HCO ^ + (4-3), SiO(3-2) and HNCO(6-5) molecular transitions. ^ 12 CO(1-0) and ^ 12 CO(3-2) lines are nicely detected and complement our previous ^ 12 CO(2-1) data. By comparing the relative strength of these three CO transitions, we find extreme gas excitation conditions (i.e. T_ ex 50 K) in regions that are spatially correlated with the radio lobes, supporting the case for a jet-ISM interaction. An accurate study of the CO kinematics demonstrates that, although the bulk of the gas is regularly rotating, two distinct non-rotational kinematic components can be identified in the inner gas regions: one can be associated to inflow/outflow streaming motions induced by a two-armed spiral perturbation; the second one is consistent with a jet-induced outflow with v_ max 200 km s ^ 1 and M 0.12 M _ yr ^ 1 . These values indicate that the jet-CO coupling ongoing in NGC 3100 is only mildly affecting the gas kinematics, as opposed to what expected from existing simulations and other observational studies of (sub-)kpc scale jet-cold gas interactions.

NGC 3100 is optically classified as a late S0 galaxy, characterised by a patchy dust distribution and a bright nuclear component ( Sandage79,Lau06 ; Figure fig:NGC3100_optical , left panel). It resides in a poor group and forms a pair with the barred spiral galaxy NGC 3095, located at a projected linear distance of 120 kpc ( Devac76 ). NGC 3100 is the host galaxy of the radio source PKS 0958-314 e.g. Ruffa19a,Ruffa20 Ekers89 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3100 as its most interesting CO detection, with a ring-like disc, spiral perturbation, warps, residual non-circular motions, and likely AGN-feeding inflow.

quotes.quote: NGC 3100 is the most interesting of our CO detections. The best-fitting gas distribution is consistent with a two-armed spiral embedded in an exponential disc. The distortions in the rotation pattern are best described by the combination of warps in both position angle and inclination, although high residuals ( 40 km s ^ 1 ) in the velocity maps suggest the presence of non-circular motions in the plane of the CO disc.

A harmonic decomposition of the line-of-sight velocities indicates that these high residuals are consistent with the presence of non-negligible radial motions ( 10 of the rotational velocity), that we link with the streaming motions associated to the spiral perturbations in the inner CO disc. The location of the disc along the line of sight suggests that the dominant radial motions are inwards towards the nucleus, supporting a scenario in which the observed cold gas is contributing to the fuelling of the AGN activity. A number of observational and theoretical constraints allow us to conclude that an external origin of the cold gas (i.e. via interaction with the companion galaxies) is strongly favoured in NGC 612 and NGC 3100.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3100 as a nearby LERG and group-center radio galaxy with newly detected central H I absorption, group H I asymmetries, and cold gas accretion sustaining young nuclear activity.

quotes.quote: We present Australia Compact Telescope Array (ATCA) 21-cm observations of the nearby low-excitation radio galaxy (LERG) NGC 3100 . This is the brightest galaxy of a loose group and hosts a young ( 2 Myr) radio source. The ATCA observations reveal for the first time the presence of neutral hydrogen ( H I ) gas in absorption in the centre of this radio galaxy, and in emission in two low-mass galaxies of the group and in a diffuse dark cloud in the proximity of NGC 3100 .

The analysis of the H I absorption complex in NGC 3100 indicates that the atomic phase of the hydrogen is distributed as its molecular phase (observed at arcsecond resolution through several carbon monoxide emission lines). We suggest that the interactions occurring within the group are causing turbulent cold gas clouds in the intra-group medium to be slowly accreted towards the centre of NGC 3100 . This caused the recent formation of the cold circum-nuclear disk which is likely sustaining the young nuclear activity.

sources.summary: The paper argues NGC 3100 hosts a jet-ISM interaction whose 10 GHz radio structure differs from typical FR I LERGs, with asymmetric jets bending near a disrupted CO disc.

quotes.quote: The case of NGC 3100 The presence of a jet-ISM interaction in NGC 3100 has been inferred from several observational signatures (see Papers I and II). The well-resolved radio structure observed in our newly-acquired 10 GHz JVLA data further strengthens this scenario.

The properties of the jet flow in NGC 3100 are clearly different from those observed in the other four objects (see Figure fig:VLAcont ) and in typical FR I LERGs (see Section sec:method ). The two jets are rather asymmetric, both in surface brightness and morphology: the northern jet is fainter and also undergoes an evident bend. It is clear from Figure fig:NGC3100_jetDisc that the distortion of the northern jet is morphologically coincident (at least in projection) with the disruption of the CO disc (at Declination offsets between 1 and 2 arcsec).

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 3100 as a lenticular radio-galaxy host where ALMA finds jet-molecular gas coupling, a CO-plane outflow, and high-excitation nuclear gas.

quotes.quote: Another interesting (although less extreme) example is the one of the radio galaxy PKS 0958–314, hosted by the local ( z=0.0088 ) lenticular galaxy NGC 3100 and detected at high-resolution with ALMA in multiple CO transitions (up to J_ upper =3 ; see Ruffa19b,Ruffa22 ). In this object, the ongoing jet-molecular gas coupling is inducing an outflow in the plane of the CO distribution with v_ max 200 km s ^ 1 and M 0.12 M _ yr ^ 1 . The interaction is also strongly altering the molecular gas physics by inducing very high CO line ratios and thus high gas excitation (with temperatures T_ ex 50 K) around the nuclear regions [ see Figure fig:CO_jet , right panel ] .
This would explain why in presence of a 2 Myr-old radio source like the one hosted in NGC 3100 the jet-induced perturbations in the gas kinematics are much less extreme than in objects like IC 5063.

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe NGC 3100 as an S0 galaxy with deep broad Na D absorption, a minor-axis bipolar LINER outflow, and similarity to NGC 1052.

quotes.quote: NGC 3100: This S0 galaxy displays deep and broad Na D absorption around the nucleus. A bipolar outflow of gas with a LINER spectrum is seen aligned with the minor axis of the galaxy. In this respect, it is like NGC 1052.

gmw_00005797.name: NGC 4845
gmw_00005797.overview: NGC 4845 is a nearby, steeply inclined Seyfert 2 spiral galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:1304.0397, arXiv:1708.02683]. Its nucleus hosted a tidal disruption event in 2010, which triggered a hard X-ray flare and subsequent variable radio emission [arXiv:1511.01206, arXiv:1903.11042].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4845 as a nearby Seyfert 2 spiral, gives its adopted distance and coordinates, and reviews multiwavelength observations before a hard X-ray flare.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Coordinates of the galaxy (i.e. _ J2000 =12^ h 58^ m 01 19 and _ J2000 =+01 34 33 02 , VV2010 )

quotes.quote: NGC 4845 is a nearby, high surface brightness spiral galaxy with morphological type SA(s)ab sp classified as a Seyfert 2 VV2006 . The distance to \ was determined as D=15.6 Mpc based on the Tully-Fisher relation TF88 . Corsini99 obtained D=13.1 Mpc by including the influence of a dark matter to the systematic velocity. We adopt in this paper D=14.5 Mpc ( H_0=75 ) after Shapley2001 who performed a thorough literature search to determine the most reliable distance of this galaxy. The galactic disk inclination is 76 \ Pizzella2005 .

Coordinates of the galaxy (i.e. _ J2000 =12^ h 58^ m 01 19 and _ J2000 =+01 34 33 02 , VV2010 ) set the Galactic neutral hydrogen column density into the direction of the source to N_H^ Gal = 1.67 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 DL90,Stark92 . \ has been observed, so far, in the optical (e.g. by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey -- SDSS, the Palomar Optical Sky Survey -- POSS and the Hubble Space Telescope -- HST), and in the infrared domain (by the Two Micron All Sky Survey -- 2MASS and the Infrared Astronomical Satellite -- IRAS) Moshir90,Spinoglio95,Djorgovski98,Sanders2003,Jarrett2003, Schneider2005 . The galaxy has also been observed by the Green Bank Telescope and the Very Large Array in the radio bands 1.4, 4.8, 8.4 GHz Condon98,Condon2002 .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4845 as a MAXI TDE candidate with a measured hard X-ray peak, possible variable-AGN interpretation, radio-supported outflow, peak delay, and decay fit.

quotes.quote: We also identify another black candidate that occurred in NGC 4845 Niko13 , which has been already listed in the 2nd MAXI /GSC catalog Hiroi13 . The 30-day averaged peak flux in the 4--10 keV band is 3.2 mCrab, with a significance of s_ D = 6.8 , which corresponds to a luminosity of 10 ^ 42.3 erg s ^ 1 at z= 0.004110 . black We note, however, that the X-ray flare of NGC 4845 may be attributed to a variable AGN, which is supported by the radio observation of the unresolved central core Irw15 .

From the time evolution of the radio spectrum, Irw15 also suggested the presence of an expanding outflow or a jet, which may be associated with the X-ray flare. It is not yet unclear whether the X-ray flare is due to a TDE or the AGN.

The second characteristic is that the decay light curves are consistent with a power-law profile of t^ 5/3 , where t is time since the onset time of each TDE. We note that the time of the flux peak, t_ p , is delayed from the TDE onset time by approximately 80 days for NGC 4845, 20 days for Swift J1644+57 and Swift J2058+05, and 5 days for Swift J1112-82. Hence, we set the central day of the bin showing the highest flux as t_ p , and estimate the TDE onset time by correcting for these offsets. We confirm that the 10-days light curves in the 3--10 keV band follow power-law profiles, as shown in Figure tde_lc . A power-law fit to the light curve over 90 days after the peak flux is found to be acceptable in terms of a ^2 test, yielding the best-fit index of 1.86^ +0.45 _ 1.55 , 1.94^ +0.39 _ 0.48 , 1.92^ +0.58 _ 0.73 , and 2.00^ +1.14 _ 1.25 for Swift J1112-82, Swift J1644+57, Swift J2058+05, and NGC 4845, respectively.

sources.summary: CHANG-ES reports a nearby Virgo TDE in NGC 4845, with variable radio emission and a fitted GPS-like AGN spectrum whose peak declined and shifted lower.

quotes.quote: Observations of NGC 4845 resulted in a serendipitous radio detection V of a Tidal Disruption event (TDE) approximately one year after its hard X-ray outburst nik13 . These authors attribute the X-ray variability to a super-Jupiter mass object being devoured by a 10^5 M_ black hole. CHANG-ES detected the radio emission as well as the variability of the radio emission from its five independent data sets spanning approximately 6 months. Remarkably, this is a very close TDE since the galaxy is in the Virgo Cluster. The power of in-band spectral fitting paid off for this strong source.

Because we had 3 total intensity data points at L-band, 2 total intensity points at C-band, as well as in-band spectral indices for each one of these data points, it was possible to fit a spectrum to the AGN. As shown in Figure  fig:N4845 a, the AGN is a GHz-peaked source (GPS) which is an order of magnitude closer than any GPS source seen before. The spectral peak both declines with time as well as shifts to lower frequencies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4845 as a nearby spiral galaxy whose nucleus hosted IGR J12580+0134, interpreted as a super-Jupiter tidal disruption event producing a bright transient and off-beam jet.

quotes.quote: IGR J12580+0134 in the nucleus of NGC 4845 --- a galaxy located at the distance of only 17 Mpc -- is likely such a case. IGR J12580+0134 is a flaring hard X-ray source initially observed by Integral (Walter et al. 2011). Follow-up X-ray observations with XMM-Newton , Swift , and MAXI , together with the Integral data suggested that the source resulted from a TDE of a super-Jupiter by the central SMBH in NGC 4845 (Nikolajuk Walter 2013).

IGR J12580+0134 was discovered by Integral (Walter et al. 2011) during January 2-11, 2011, with a position consistent with that of a nearby spiral galaxy NGC 4845. Swift /XRT and XMM-Newton observations started a few days later, confirmed the association of the transient with the nucleus of the galaxy. The peak flux of the transient is F_ 2-10 keV > 5.0 10^ 11 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 (Nikolajuk Walter 2013), corresponding to a brightening by a factor > 100 compared with the flux upper limit of the galaxy before the outburst (Fabbiano et al. 1992).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4845 as a strong circular-polarization AGN, observed after a tidal disruption event and showing a resolved conversion disk.

quotes.quote: The discovery of strong CP, at a level of 2 in a Virgo Cluster CHANG-ES galaxy, NGC 4845, however irw15 has opened up the possibility of exploring new nearby AGNs in detail, especially AGNs that are embedded in other emission. NGC 4845 was observed serendipitously about one year after a tidal disruption event ( TDE ) was detected in the hard X-ray regime; this event set the outburst time. The fact that CHANG-ES observations were broad-band yielding in-band spectral indices, and because multiple observations in different arrays were obtained, we were able to fit a simple AGN jet model with helical B fields to this source.

Four out of our detections are resolved , showing unique structures, including a jet in NGC 4388 and a CP `conversion disk' in NGC 4845. The conversion disk is inclined to the galactic disk but is perpendicular to a possible outflow direction. Such CP structures have never before been seen in any galaxy to our knowledge.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4845 as a nearby Seyfert 2 barred spiral with edge-on dust lanes and a central disc; its molecular gas is mostly regular rotation with weak extra kinematics.

quotes.quote: The galaxy NGC 4845 is a nearby Seyfert 2 with a clear starburst component Thomas , classified as an intermediate barred spiral galaxy (SABab), at z = 0.00411 located in the Virgo Southern Extension ( Irwin_2015 and references therein). We adopt the Tully-Fisher distance of 18 Mpc. The inclination on the sky is almost edge-on, revealing contrasted dust lanes on the near side, and a peanut-shape for the bulge. The galaxy contains a bright unresolved core with a surrounding weak central disc (1.8 kpc diameter).

Our kinematical analysis seems to confirm that unlike the one in NGC 4968 the bar in NGC4845 is unable to change significantly the regular rotation pattern of the molecular gas. This additional weak kinematics (see Fig. Barolo-4845 and Diskfit-4845 ) could be due to gas inflowing or outflowing the central region but with the present data there is not firm evidence of it.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4845 as the nearby Virgo-cluster host of TDE IGR J12580+0134, tracing its nuclear radio decay, plateau, and later flare.

quotes.quote: We discuss new observations of IGRJ12580+0134, a TDE observed in NGC 4845 ( d=17 Mpc) in November 2010, with the Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA The National Radio Astronomy Observatory is a facility of the National Science Foundation operated under cooperative agreement by Associated Universities, Inc. ). We also discuss a reanalysis of 2010-2011 Swift and XMM-Newton observations, as well as new, late-time Swift observations. Our JVLA observations show a decay of the nuclear radio flux until 2015, when a plateau was seen, and then a significant ( factor 3) radio flare during 2016.

IGR J12580+0134 is a TDE that occurred in 2010 in the nucleus of NGC 4845 -- a galaxy located in the Virgo cluster. Its distance of just 17 Mpc, gave us a rare chance to scrutinize a TDE and its aftermath with the highest possible resolution. The source was initially detected in 2010 November by Integral Walter11 .

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe NGC 4845 as a steeply inclined Seyfert 2 with a large ionization cone, faint counter-cone, circumnuclear HII disk, and disk-rotation-dominated dynamics.

quotes.quote: NGC 4845: This steeply-inclined Sy 2 galaxy has a magnificent ionization cone with an opening angle of 120^ emerging perpendicularly from a circum-nuclear disk of \ regions with its axis at PA = 350^ . The counter ionization cone is faintly visible, mainly in \ because of the high extinction. Together, both ionization cones fill the WiFeS field of view. The dynamics of the ENLR are dominated by the underlying rotation of the galaxy disk.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 4845 as an Sa galaxy whose triaxial bulge has constrained axis ratios, supported by photometric and kinematic measurements and non-circular gas motions.

quotes.quote: In 1989, two independent works of bertola89 and gerhard89 reached the same conclusion about the triaxial bulge of the Sa galaxy NGC 4845. Using a combination of photometric and kinematic measurements they restrict the intrinsic axis ratio of its bulge to Q=0.74 and F=0.6 . Their works were mainly supported by the presence of non-circular gas-motions in the galaxy centre.

gmw_00005798.name: ESO 323-G77
gmw_00005798.overview: ESO 323-G77 is a partially obscured Seyfert 1 galaxy that exhibits polarized emission close to its nucleus [arXiv:1708.02683]. The galaxy features a narrow, two-sided ionization cone extending north-south, alongside active star formation occurring near the nucleus [arXiv:1708.02683].

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe ESO 323-G77 as a partially obscured Seyfert 1 with polarised nuclear emission, a narrow two-sided ionization cone, HST-mapped inner structure, and nuclear star formation.

quotes.quote: ESO 323-G77: This is a partially obscured Sy 1 with polarised emission close to the nucleus Schmid03 . We find a two-sided ionization cone with a remarkably narrow opening angle ( 20^ ) extending N-S across the field. The inner region of this galaxy was mapped with HST by Mulchaey96 . There is evidence of active star formation activity close to the nucleus (strong \ emission and deep absorption).

gmw_00005799.name: NGC 4128
gmw_00005799.overview: NGC 4128 is an S0 early-type galaxy located approximately 36 Mpc away [arXiv:astro-ph/0409061]. It features a red nucleus, a boxy structure on a 1 kpc scale, and a disky structure on both smaller and larger scales [arXiv:astro-ph/0409061].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4128 as an S0 with a nuclear stellar disk, red nucleus, boxy inner structure, old populations, and tentative kinematically distinct core.

quotes.quote: NGC 4128 The most distant galaxy in the sample is NGC 4128 (36 Mpc). It is an S0 galaxy and it has not been detected in radio nor in IR. The isophotal parameters show that it is disky between 35 and 530 pc. On 1 kpc scale it has boxy parameters and on larger scales it becomes disky again. The color image shows a red nucleus.

It is probable that the difference in the color between the nucleus and the rest of the galaxy, as well as the higher metallicity detected in the nucleus is connected with the unusual spatially resolved kinematic profiles (Fig.  f:ngc4128 ). The velocity dispersion is flat in the center. The velocity curve also shows an unusual flattening in the central 0 2, measurements being positive on the both sides of the galaxy nucleus.

gmw_00005800.name: NGC 5308
gmw_00005800.overview: NGC 5308 is an early-type galaxy that hosts a very thin and bright nuclear stellar disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0409061]. The stars within this nuclear disk rotate rapidly, reaching speeds of 100 kilometers per second within 15 parsecs of the galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0409061].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5308 has the sample’s largest, very thin and bright nuclear stellar disk, fast rotation, distinct bulge/nucleus colors, and uncertain formation history.

quotes.quote: NGC 5308 In many aspects NGC 5308 is different from the other galaxies in this study. Our photometry reveals the largest nuclear stellar disk in the sample of galaxies in this study. Unlike in the other galaxies, the nuclear disk of NGC 5308 is very thin and bright.

The stars in the disk of NGC 5308 rotate fast, reaching 100 km s ^ 1 within 15 pc from the nucleus (Fig. f:ngc5308 ). The velocity dispersion has a peak of about 300 km s ^ 1 in the center and is relatively flat in the inner 15 pc. This trend is also visible in the kinematics measured excluding the Mg b line from the fit, although the right hand side of the plot shows considerably lower velocity dispersion values. This is reflected in the panel with Mg b values, which are slightly higher on the right hand of the plot. The Mg b and Fe5270 index values have opposite trends.

gmw_00005801.name: SDSS J150904.22+043441.8
gmw_00005801.overview: SDSS J150904.22+043441.8 is a nearby type-2 quasar and luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1905.06288]. The galaxy exhibits a disrupted morphology indicative of a past merger, alongside barely resolved ionized and molecular outflows [arXiv:1905.06288].

sources.summary: The paper presents EMIR spectroscopy of J1509, describing it as a luminous IR type-2 quasar with disrupted morphology and ionized plus molecular outflows that may affect its host galaxy.

quotes.quote: We report high spectral resolution (R 4000) K-band spectroscopy of the QSO2 SDSS J150904.22+043441.8 (also IRAS F15065+0446 and hereafter J1509) obtained with the instrument Espectr ografo Multiobjeto Infra-Rojo (EMIR) on the 10.4 m Gran Telescopio Canarias (GTC). This is a nearby QSO2 (z = 0.1118) from the catalogue of 2008AJ....136.2373R , having an [ O III ] luminosity of 10 ^ 8.56 L_ (L _ bol =4.9 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 using the bolometric correction of 2004ApJ...613..109H ). It is a luminous IR galaxy (LIRG) according to its total IR luminosity log(L _ IR /L_ )=11.6 2011ApJ...730...19S and the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) broad-band optical images show a disrupted galaxy morphology that could be indicative of a past galaxy interaction/merger.

Using the red wing of the Pa line we find that the ionized outflow in J1509 is barely resolved, and we measure FWHM _ out =1.34 0.18 kpc. For the molecular outflow we used the S(5) and S(1) lines, which have the highest S/N in our nuclear spectrum. In the case of S(1) the noisy continuum prevents a good determination of the continuum-free spatial profiles, but using the continuum-subtracted red and blue wings of the S(5) line (only red continuum available; see Fig. fig2 ) we also find the molecular outflow to be barely resolved, with FWHM _ out =1.46 0.20 kpc.

gmw_00005802.name: LEDA 1712304
gmw_00005802.overview: LEDA 1712304 is a low-mass galaxy with an estimated stellar mass of 2.7 billion solar masses [arXiv:1905.01831]. It hosts a heavily obscured active galactic nucleus characterized by a deep silicate dust absorption feature [arXiv:1905.01831].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LEDA 1712304 as a heavily obscured AGN with deep silicate absorption, modeling its circumnuclear dust and global infrared-to-submillimeter spectral properties.

quotes.quote: Using AKARI/IRC spectroscopy, we discover a new sample of a heavily obscured AGN in LEDA 1712304 which shows a deep spectral absorption feature due to silicate dust. We study the infrared (IR) spectral properties of circumnuclear silicate dust in LEDA 1712304. We perform IR spectral fitting, considering silicate dust properties such as composition, porosity, size and crystallinity. Spectral energy distribution (SED) fitting is also performed to the flux densities in the UV to sub-millimeter range to investigate the global spectral properties.

The best-fit model indicates 0.1 m sized porous amorphous olivine ( Mg_ 2x Fe_ 2-2x SiO_4 ; x=0.4 ) with 4 crystalline pyroxene. The optical depth is _ sil 2.3 , while the total IR luminosity and stellar mass are estimated to be L_ IR =(5 1) 10^ 10 L_ and M_ star =(2.7 0.8) 10^ 9 M_ , respectively. In such low L_ IR and M_ star ranges, there are few galaxies which show that large _ sil .

gmw_00005803.name: 2MASX J13084201-2422581
gmw_00005803.overview: 2MASX J13084201-2422581 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy that hosts a compact, flat-spectrum radio source at its center [arXiv:1402.3530]. Observations have detected a 21 cm absorption line near the systemic redshift, indicating the presence of cold neutral hydrogen gas that is not rapidly infalling or outflowing [arXiv:1402.3530].

sources.summary: The paper reports a previously unknown 21 cm absorption line in this Seyfert 2 galaxy, confirmed with ATCA and consistent with compact nuclear H I absorption near the systemic redshift.

quotes.quote: Of these detections, three were previously known: Centaurus A Roberts:1970 , NGC 5793 Jenkins:1983 and Arp 220 Mirabel:1982 , while the fourth, 2MASX J13084201-2422581, was not previously reported in the literature. It should be noted that while the first three galaxies are common to both samples, 2MASX J13084201-2422581 is only listed in Sample 1, since its 2MASS K_ s band magnitude ( K_ s = 11.8 ) was not bright enough to be included in the catalogue of . We examined further the 21 cm absorption seen in 2MASX J13084201-2422581 and NGC 5793 by re-observing these galaxies with the Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA) Broadband Backend CABB; Wilson:2011 in 2013 February 13--16.

We detect a previously unknown 21 cm absorption line against the compact flat-spectrum radio source at the centre of the Seyfert 2 galaxy 2MASX J13084201-2422581. By comparing the marginal likelihoods for increasingly complex models, we find that the HIPASS data warrant only a single-component Gaussian model, while a four-component model is favoured by the CABB data. The redshift of the peak absorption is consistent with the systemic redshift of the host galaxy, implying that the bulk of the cold H i gas is not rapidly infalling or outflowing with respect to ionized gas in the nucleus

gmw_00005804.name: SPT0125-47
gmw_00005804.overview: SPT0125-47 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy located at a redshift of z = 2.5148 [arXiv:1810.04695, arXiv:1906.05469]. Observations of the galaxy have detected a redshifted water line [arXiv:1906.05469], as well as carbon monoxide, HCN, and HCO+ emissions that indicate a relatively homogeneous interstellar medium [arXiv:1810.04695].

sources.summary: The paper reports strong dense-gas and carbon-line detections in SPT0125-47, finding similar dense- and lower-density gas velocity profiles and a relatively homogeneous interstellar-medium maturity.

quotes.quote: Detected lines We detected three > 5 lines in SPT0125-47. ^ 13 CO(4-3), HCN(5-4), and HCO ^+ (5-4) are detected at 7.2, 5.3, and 5.6 , respectively.

Similarity of the line profiles in SPT0125-47 and differential lensing In SPT0125-47, dense-gas tracers are detected at more than 5 and it is thus possible to compare the line profile of HCN and HCO ^ + with [ CI ] and CO (see Fig. fig:profile_comp ). Our high-S/N [ CI ] detection and the ancillary CO(3-2) line detection from the redshift search have a similar asymmetric profile with a much broader redshifted tail. A similar asymmetry is found for HCN(5-4) and HCO ^ + (5-4). This suggests that molecular regions from the lowest to the highest density are distributed in the same way across this object.
In SPT0125-47, however, we found very similar ^ 12 CO and ^ 13 CO line profiles, suggesting the abundance ratio of the two molecules remains roughly constant throughout.

sources.summary: The appendix reports APEX observations of SPT0125-47's redshifted water line, with the line clearly detected, measured at 21.8 Jy km/s and unresolved in the 280 GHz beam.

quotes.quote: htp APEX observations of \ in SPT0125-47 We observed \ ( _ rest =987.927 GHz) line in SPT0125-47 at z =2.5148 using the APEX-2 receiver of the Swedish Heterodyne Facility Instrument SHFI; vassilev08 on the Atacama Pathfinder Experiment (APEX). The observations in the shared ESO+Swedish project 092.A-0467 (PI M. Aravena) were done between July and November 2013 in excellent conditions with Precipitatable Water Vapor 0.25 < PWV < 0.5 mm, and a total on-source integration time of 3 hours. We reduced the data using the standard procedures in the IRAM CLASS software. The line is clearly detected (Figure spt0125_spectrum ) with a line flux of 21.8 3.7 Jykm s ^ 1 and a line width of 117 km s ^ 1 . Note that the source is unresolved in the 280 GHz APEX beam of 22 3.

gmw_00005805.name: SPT0551-50
gmw_00005805.overview: SPT0551-50 is a gravitationally lensed, high-redshift starburst galaxy located at a redshift of z=3.16 [arXiv:1810.04695]. It is a dusty star-forming system that exhibits particularly broad dense-gas spectral lines [arXiv:1810.04695].

sources.summary: The paper treats SPT0551-50 as a lensed high-redshift dusty star-forming galaxy with tentative dense-gas-line detections, broad lines, and no dedicated lens model.

quotes.quote: We detected two of these lines (S/N > 5) in SPT-125-47 at z=2.51 and tentatively detected all three (S/N 3) in SPT0551-50 at z=3.16.
We have no good lens model for SPT0551-50 and we thus assume the median magnification of the SPT sample of 6.3 Spilker2016 .

This is caused by the presence of several lines in this side band, which can be particularly broad, especially in SPT0551-50. The line-versus-continuum flux density ratio is of the order of unity and the slope of the continuum versus frequency inside a spectral window is non-negligible. This makes this task even more difficult. We performed various tests and realized that the continuum subtraction was very sensitive to our parameter choices, creating important systematics. For instance, with SPT0551-50, the recovered flux in the moment-zero map varies by more than 1- if we remove a flat background measured between HCO ^ + (5-4) and HNC(5-4) or if we use a first-order baseline using in addition the other side band to constrain the slope.

gmw_00005806.name: SG1202
gmw_00005806.overview: SG1202 is a face-on, open-armed spiral galaxy with three spiral arms [arXiv:2310.20073]. The galaxy exhibits enhanced star formation and younger stellar populations within its arms compared to its inter-arm regions [arXiv:2310.20073].

sources.summary: The paper describes SG1202 as an open-armed three-armed spiral whose downstream star-formation and metallicity offsets support density-wave behavior, with younger stellar populations in its arms.

quotes.quote: Fig fig:FOV shows the coloured images of our pilot sample including two three-armed face-one spiral galaxies: MAGPI1202197197 (left of Fig fig:FOV , hereafter SG1202) and MAGPI1204198199 (right of Fig fig:FOV , hereafter SG1204). The pilot sample has the largest \ and the highest signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) among the 12 non-merging spiral galaxies, which allows for a detailed study and comparison between arm and interarm regions without the need for binning.

2 To summarise, the open-armed spiral galaxy SG1202 shows higher \ (left panel of Fig fig:sfr_updown ) and lower gas-phase metallicity (left panel of Fig fig:z_updown ) in the downstream ( \ < 0 ; orange) than the upstream ( \ > 0 ; purple). As a proxy for the stellar age within SG1202, \ does not show a distinguishable offset between both sides of the arm regions (left panel of Fig fig:d4k_updown ).

In SG1202, we observe higher \ (Fig fig:sfr ), systematically lower metallicity (Fig fig:z ), and younger stellar age (Fig fig:d4k ) in the spiral arms than the inter-arms. In SG1204, we do not find a statistically significant difference in metallicity (Fig fig:z ) or \ (Fig fig:d4k ) between the arms and inter-arms. We do, however, recover higher \ (Fig fig:sfr ) in the arms of SG1204. These results support the enhancement of star formation within the arm regions. The diversity in the metallicity and stellar age azimuthal trends between SG1202 and SG1204 may be a result of a combination of beam-smearing effects and/or shorter mixing timescale, likely due to the tighter pitch angles in SG1204 compared to SG1202. We find higher \ in the leading edge of spiral arms ( \ < 0 ; downstream) in SG1202 (Fig fig:sfr_updown ). This matches the expectation of the density wave theory. The observed higher metallicity in the trailing edge of the spiral arms of SG1202 (Fig fig:z_updown ) is consistent with the expectations of radial gas flows driven by spiral arms Khoperskov_2023 .

gmw_00005807.name: SG1204
gmw_00005807.overview: SG1204 is a tightly wound, three-armed spiral galaxy viewed face-on [arXiv:2310.20073]. It exhibits no differences in star formation rate, metallicity, or stellar age between its downstream and upstream regions, which is consistent with dynamic spiral theory [arXiv:2310.20073].

sources.summary: The paper describes SG1204 as a tighter three-armed spiral with enhanced arm star formation but no significant azimuthal metallicity or age offsets, consistent with dynamic spirals.

quotes.quote: Fig fig:FOV shows the coloured images of our pilot sample including two three-armed face-one spiral galaxies: MAGPI1202197197 (left of Fig fig:FOV , hereafter SG1202) and MAGPI1204198199 (right of Fig fig:FOV , hereafter SG1204). The pilot sample has the largest \ and the highest signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) among the 12 non-merging spiral galaxies, which allows for a detailed study and comparison between arm and interarm regions without the need for binning.

The tightly wounded spiral galaxy SG1204 presents no significant differences in the \ (right panel of Fig fig:sfr_updown ), 12 + log(O/H) (right panel of Fig fig:z_updown ) or \ (right panel of Fig fig:d4k_updown ) between the downstream and the upstream.

Our findings for SG1204 are consistent with the dynamic spiral theory. This could also be impacted by radial gas flows, which is consistent with the simulated spiral galaxies with no radial gradients in Khoperskov_2023 .

gmw_00005808.name: SDSS J1607+3319
gmw_00005808.overview: SDSS J1607+3319 is a Type-1.9 active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of 0.063 [arXiv:2301.01957]. It is characterized as a double-peaked broad-line system with a central dust torus half-opening angle estimated to be around 46 degrees [arXiv:2301.01957].

sources.summary: The paper selects SDSS J1607+3319 as a unique Type-1.9 double-peaked broad-line AGN and estimates its dust-torus half-opening angle from accretion-disk inclination arguments.

quotes.quote: For the unique Type-1.9 DPAGN, accretion disk originations of double-peaked broad H can be applied to determine the inclination angle of the central accretion disk, which is well accepted as substitute of the half opening angle of the central dust torus. Then, among low redshift Type-1.9 DPAGN in SDSS, SDSS J1607+3319 at redshift 0.063 is collected, and the half opening angle of the central dust torus is determined to be around 46 4 , after considering disfavoured BBH system to explain the double-peaked broad H through long-term none variabilities and disfavoured local physical conditions to explain disappearance of broad H through virial BH mass properties. The results indicate that more detailed studying on dust torus of AGN can be appropriately done through Type-1.9 DPAGN in the near future.

Based on the main hypothesis and corresponding criteria above, Type-1.9 DPAGN SDSS J160714.40+331909.12 (= ) at redshift 0.063 is collected as the unique target of the manuscript, based on two main unique features through properties of its spectroscopic and long-term variabilities, well discussed in the next section.

gmw_00005809.name: The Mice
gmw_00005809.overview: The Mice is an interacting galaxy pair that recently underwent its first passage [arXiv:1305.5865]. Its northern component, NGC 4676A, is an edge-on barred disk [arXiv:1405.7814] featuring a dust-obscured LINER nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0309377], shock-driven bicones from a galactic outflow [arXiv:1405.7814], and a straight northern tail with active star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0402248].

sources.summary: For NGC 4676A, the authors describe a dust-obscured LINER-type nucleus, weak elongated H emission, red V-shaped structure, and blue scattered light from young stars.

quotes.quote: NGC 4676 (The Mice) The Mice is a pair of spiral galaxies with only moderately active nuclei (classified as LINER-type by keel85 ). The nucleus of NGC 4676A or Nuc 1 (the northern component of the pair) is covered by dust in our optical V and I images (Fig. f:n4676n1 ). This dust is likely associated with the dense edge-on molecular disk imaged in CO(1-0) by yun01 . Adopting standard conversion factors, the observed peak CO flux density suggests that the nucleus of NGC 4676A is hidden beneath A_V 60 magnitudes of extinction.

The H emission from NGC 4676A is weak. In general, the emission is elongated in the north--south direction of the main body. The V-shaped structure has very red V-I colors above 2.2. Perhaps even more surprisingly, the color of the heavily extinguished dust patch near the base of the tail is actually bluer than its surroundings, having an average V-I value less than 1.

sources.summary: The paper describes The Mice as a well-studied interacting pair with prominent prograde tidal tails, then presents an Identikit model placing first passage about 175 Myr earlier.

quotes.quote: The Mice is a well known interacting galaxy pair. The system received its name from the prominent tails which result in the system resembling two ``playing mice'' Vorontsov-Vel'Iaminov1958 . These prominent tidal tails suggest a prograde interaction. The straightness of the northern tail is consistent with the spin vector of the northern galaxy being near the plane of the sky.

Our best match to this system has two prograde disks, both with small tilts relative to the orbital plane ( i=15^ and 25^ for NGC 4676A and NGC 4676B, respectively; Figure fig:idkMice ). The initial passage was r_ peri =15 kpc. The two galaxies have just passed for the first time and are still moving towards apocenter.

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies NGC 4676A as an edge-on barred disk, finds little evidence for a classical bulge, and identifies shock-powered bicones from a galactic outflow.

quotes.quote: While NGC 4676A has previously been classified as an S0, the CALIFA maps of the stellar kinematics show no evidence for a classical bulge, with low nuclear velocity dispersion and constant rotation above with height above the major axis. A young, thin, disk is visible in the stellar population maps, with older populations extending into the ``boxy'' shaped bulge. The high dust and gas content of the galaxy is also inconsistent with its classification as an S0. It is possible that the boxy morphology evident in the imaging (See Figure fig:hst ) comes from a strong bar e.g. Kuijken:1995,Bureau:1999p9430,Bureau:2005p8704, MartinezValpuesta:2006p7585,Williams:2011 ; further detailed investigation of higher order moments in the velocity map may help to confirm this.

Spectacular bicones driven by fast shocks in NGC 4676A. The extreme line ratios seen at the outer edge of the bicones are consistent with being caused by fast shocks ( v_s 350 ) driven by a superwind. The emission of NGC 4676A in soft X-rays is also found to be elongated along the minor axis of the galaxy, coincident with the ionised gas bicones seen here Read:2003p7304 , again implying a strong galactic outflow. In the nearby Universe observational studies of galactic superwinds have focussed primarily on LIRGs and ULIRGs, where a clear driving source is present see for a review Veilleux:2005p7318 . As NGC 4676A is less luminous than a LIRG, and has a moderate star formation rate despite its very large mass, it would not have been an obvious candidate for superwind investigations.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4676a as the northern member, links it to the straight northern tail, discusses unusual tail kinematics, and notes observed tail star formation missing from the simulations.

quotes.quote: The individual galaxies are NGC 4676a, to the north, and NGC 4676b, to the southeast. The length and straightness of the bright northern tail imply that we are viewing the aftermath of a nearly-direct passage roughly edge-on to the orbit and disk planes, while the curve of the fainter southern tail is consistent with a more face-on view of an inclined disk.

On the other hand, H spectroscopy by SR98 indicated a remarkably large difference of 300 km s^ 1 between the nucleus of NGC 4676a and its associated tail, and a large velocity dispersion within the tail itself; it's tempting to interpret this as measurement error, but TST72 reported somewhat similar results, so the situation is not entirely clear.

Outside of the centers of the galaxies, star-forming regions are observed in the straight tail extending to the north of NGC 4676a. None of the simulations presented here reproduce this phenomenon, although the shock-induced models come closer inasmuch as they show enhanced star formation in their tails until t 0.4 ; the density-dependent model seems less promising in this regard since star formation in its tails declines after first passage.

gmw_00005810.name: AUDFs01
gmw_00005810.overview: AUDFs01 is a distant galaxy at a redshift of z=1.42 [arXiv:2008.11394]. It features four connected clumps at consistent redshifts and exhibits high emission-line equivalent widths, indicating the production of a copious amount of ionizing photons [arXiv:2008.11394].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AUDFs01 as a z=1.42 LyC-leaking galaxy detected by AstroSat, with high emission-line equivalent widths, rest-frame EUV emission, and four connected clumps at consistent redshifts.

quotes.quote: The galaxy AUDFs01 (RA: 53.1444, Dec: -27.7911) at z=1.42 has been selected from the Hubble Extreme Deep field having one of the highest H 6563 , and [ O iii ] 5007 fluxes, measured by the HST Grism G141 under the 3DHST program . Both emission lines have high equivalent widths, EW(H )= 1210 , EW( [ O iii ] ) = 1517 , indicating the production of a copious amount of ionizing photons in the galaxy with relatively few old stars.

The Lyman break of the galaxy AUDFs01 is redshifted to an observed wavelength _ lim =2188.8 \ (LyC limit for this galaxy) and the FUV filter probes the galaxy in the rest-frame wavelength range 537 - 723 . This is the first detection of Extreme Ultra-Violet (EUV, 600 \ rest-frame) photons from a distant galaxy.

As marked in Fig. fig:fig1 , the galaxy has 3 bright clumps (C1, C2, C3) and a fainter one (C4) - all appear to be connected by 12 surface brightness contour. They are connected in redshift too. In Fig. fig:EDFig1 , we show the HST grism G141 image of AUDFs01 and its full spectrum. The redshift of the galaxy is derived using the H line.

gmw_00005811.name: GN17
gmw_00005811.overview: GN17 is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0605573] that satisfies the criteria for classification as a massive galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0605573]. It is optically faint due to dust obscuration [arXiv:1006.0746] and is identified as the dominant submillimeter emitter in its system [arXiv:astro-ph/0605573].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GN17 with the brighter radio component, arguing it dominates the submillimeter emission and satisfies BzK high-redshift galaxy criteria.

quotes.quote: GN17: There are two radio sources within the search radius, separated by 5.5 arcsec. One of them is three times brighter then the other. Both sources have an IRAC/MIPS counterpart, although the fluxes of the IRAC and MIPS counterparts are different by factors of 5 and 10, respectively. The brighter radio source is much fainter optically and has a photometric redshift of 1.72 (Paper III) which would indicate a separation of 45 kpc if both sources are at the same redshift. This is perhaps a little too large to be considered one system and, in fact, the fainter radio source has a photometric redshift of 1.2. By fitting the MIPS flux to a range of templates we find that the brighter radio source is more likely to be the dominant submm emitter, and therefore assign this as the counterpart. Cohen et al. (2000) list a spectroscopic redshift for this galaxy of 0.884, but given its optical and IR colours, such a redshift seems very unlikely. This counterpart satisfies the `BzK' criteria for classification as a massive galaxy at z>1.4 (see Daddi et al. 2005 for more details), and therefore we use our photometric redshift rather than the apparently discrepant spectroscopic redshift.

sources.summary: The paper treats source 317 as an i775 dropout with unreliable photometric redshift, high 24 micron flux, and a flux ratio suggesting optical faintness from dust obscuration rather than very high redshift.

quotes.quote: The i_ 775 dropout of Lehmer2005 is source 317. Its photometric redshift (3.25) is based in lower significance B_ 435 , V_ 606 , and i_ 775 measurements and it is not reliable. This the source with the highest 24 m flux in our sample ( f_ 24 m =664 Jy ), which would yield a mid-infrared luminosity in the order of 10^ 26 W Hz ^ 1 in the 3.5 m rest-frame band if the source was lying at z 6 . Its low f_ 3.6 m /f_ 24 m ratio however ( 10^ 1.1 ) implies dust obscuration according to Fig. ir_plot , making it more likely to be optically faint as a result of obscurarion rather than high redshift.

gmw_00005812.name: GN28
gmw_00005812.overview: GN28 is a high-redshift galaxy located within 8 arcseconds of a massive radio jet [arXiv:astro-ph/0605573]. The galaxy exhibits a disturbed optical detection and submillimeter emission near the end of this jet, which suggests the presence of jet-induced star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0605573].

sources.summary: The paper treats GN28 as a tentative counterpart near a massive radio jet, with disturbed optical emission suggesting possible jet-induced star formation.

quotes.quote: GN28: This source is unique in that it is within 8 arcseconds of a massive radio jet (Borys et al. 2002). Within the search radius, there is faint MIPS emission, elongated in the NE-SW direction, which connects several IRAC sources. One of these IRAC sources is 0.6 arcseconds from the end of a radio jet and so the usual assumption that radio flux is indicative of the submm counterpart is more difficult here. The 15 arcsec long radio feature is the most prominent in the entire GOODS-N field (Richards et al. 1998). There is a disturbed optical detection coincident with the end of the radio jet, which is confirmed to be at a similar redshift ( z_ spec =1.020 , Phillips et al. 1997) to that of the central AGN ( z_ spec =1.013 , Richards et al. 1998). The fact that the submm emission seems coincident with the end of the jet is suggestive of jet-induced star formation.

gmw_00005813.overview: The active galaxy 0500+019 is a variable radio source associated with a host galaxy at a redshift of 0.583 [arXiv:astro-ph/0108185, arXiv:1909.00813]. It is classified as a gigahertz-peaked spectrum radio galaxy with an S-shaped structure and a detected 21-cm H I absorption feature [arXiv:1106.0578, arXiv:astro-ph/0108185].

sources.summary: The paper includes 0500+019 as a reddened quasar target with known 21-cm absorption, reports millimetre observations, and argues that existing scans do not rule out reddening at another redshift.

quotes.quote: Target selection As per cwm+06 , our sources were selected from the Parkes Half-Jansky Flat-spectrum Sample (PHFS, dwf+97 ) With the addition of 0500+019, included since it has been detected in 21-cm absorption cmr+98 . We prioritised the three targets according to the 3-mm flux densities estimated from an interpolation of the decimetre and near-infrared values: 12.8 hours of integration on 0500+019 ( S_ est 0.32 Jy), 13.6 hours on 1430--155 ( S_ est 0.08 Jy) and 7.2 hours on 1504--166 ( S_ est 0.8 Jy), which, upon comparison with the observed values (Table sum ), were reasonable estimates.

In any case, of all of our targets, only one (0500+019) has been detected in 21-cm absorption (see table 6 of cww+08 and Sect. lotag ), and so we would have no information on what the width of each of the undetected lines should be.

Spectral scans have, however, been attempted mcw02,cwmk03 , with 0500+019 being one of the targets common to ours. We summarise the results in Table scans , from which we see that <30 of the redshift space towards 0500+019 has been scanned for HCO ^+ and only 12 for CO, with some overlap in these ranges. We therefore cannot rule out that the cause of the reddening may be at some other redshift towards 0500+019 and indeed for the rest of the sample.

sources.summary: The paper treats 0500+019 as a GPS radio galaxy with an S-shaped source, weak polarization, no clear flat-spectrum region, and a possible CSO classification.

quotes.quote: 0500+019 : we consider the radio source to be associated with a galaxy at z=0.583 rather than a background quasar (cf. Stickel et al. 1996a, and see also paper I for references on individual objects). The radio source is S-shaped as is sometimes found among GPS radio galaxies, with the northern part brighter than the southern part. Polarized emission is detected at a low fractional level ( 0.3 at the total intensity peak position in p.a. +101 ) only in the brightest component.

The spectral index image shows that a clear flat-spectrum region is not present, the flattest spectral index being close to the brightest component B. The low resolution image does not permit us to separate the contribution of the subcomponents B1 and B2, but we see slightly darker shades just south of the peak intensity on the low resolution image, and we suspect the flattest component is component B2. Therefore, if the core is located in component B2 or close to it, this radio source would be a genuine CSO.

sources.summary: PKS 0500+019 is a variable radio source with a re-detected H I absorber; the paper treats the absorption as associated with its host radio galaxy.

quotes.quote: PKS 0500+019 had an ASKAP-12 observation taken in February 2017, motivated due to a correlator block failure at the redshift of the known H i absorption feature during the ASKAP-BETA observation.
PKS 0500+019 This well-studied variable radio source is know to have H i absorption, which was originally detected by .

However, this feature was stated by to be possibly spurious, and the conclusion that the H i absorption system and radio source are indeed associated, rather than PKS 0500+019 being a background quasar, has since been supported .

gmw_00005814.overview: The active galactic nucleus 0738+313 is a core-dominated, radio-loud quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/9911142, arXiv:astro-ph/0603591] featuring a core-jet radio structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0108185]. Its radio jet bends toward the east [arXiv:1410.6653, arXiv:astro-ph/0108185] and is accompanied by an X-ray jet [arXiv:0804.1564, arXiv:astro-ph/0603591] and weak, extended radio lobes [arXiv:0804.1564, arXiv:astro-ph/9911142].

sources.summary: The paper maps 0738+318 with changing jet EVPAs, offset polarization, and a core-inner-jet transverse RM gradient significant from about 15 to 3.5.

quotes.quote: 0738+318 Figure fig:pmaps1 b shows our 7.9 GHz polarization map for 0738+318. The EVPAs in the inner part of the jet lie along the jet, which is oriented toward the south, and become roughly transverse to the jet beyond a bend toward the east a few mas from the core. The polarization beyond this bend is offset toward the northern edge of the jet.

This RM map shows the presence of an RM gradient roughly transverse to the jet direction in the core/inner jet region. Figure fig:0738RM shows two slices taken roughly perpendicular to the jet direction plotted together with their errors. In both cases, the transverse RM gradients are in the same direction and monotonic. The significance of the transverse RM gradient in the inner of the two regions considered is approximately 15 , dropping to 3.5 further from the core.

sources.summary: The paper reports an X-ray and radio jet in B2 0738+313, interprets the large-scale jet as possible relic activity, and notes no X-rays from its radio lobes.

quotes.quote: Large scale X-ray jets were discovered in two sources in our sample: PKS1127-145 siem2002,siemi2007 and B2 0738+313 siem2003 . A re-analysis of the radio data confirmed the presence of the radio jets. Both jets have similar morphologies in X-ray and radio.

A large scale jet in B2 0738+313 could represent such old structure siem2003 . A faint radio lobe emission has been detected on both sides of the core, while the X-ray jet is propagating towards one of the two lobes. However, we do not detect any X-rays associated with the radio lobes in this source.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 0738+313 as a core-jet radio source whose polarized components, bending jet, and extended lobes suggest boosted compact activity plus older emission.

quotes.quote: 0738+313 : our 15 GHz VLBA image is dominated by two strong components. The diffuse emission originating from the southern one and leading towards the SE seen in the 5 GHz image is still present in the 15 GHz image, but is less significant. Component A is weakly polarized (0.5 at the peak position) and has an inverted spectrum, thus it is (or it harbors) the core of the radio source.

The identification of the core makes this object a core-jet radio source. This is also confirmed by the MEM image, with a jet leading to the South and then suddenly bending to the East. This radio source is known to have an arc-second scale double (or rather triple) morphology, with two weak and diffuse lobes on each side of the compact much stronger component (Stanghellini et al 1998).

sources.summary: The paper uses OI 363 as a compact background quasar for two damped absorbers, emphasizing its core-dominated radio structure, small VLBI core, and near-unity covering factor.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report the detection of redshifted absorption in two low redshift ( z= 0.2212 , z=0.0912 ) damped systems seen towards the quasar OI 363 (0738+313, z_ em = 0.630 ) .

OI 363 is a core dominated Gigahertz peaked source whose total flux decreases from 2.2 Jy at 1.64 GHz to 1.59 Jy at 408 MHz. VLA maps at 1.64 GHz and 1.4 GHz show that the source is highly core dominated, with about 97 of the total flux in an unresolved core component. VLBI measurements at 5 GHz show that the core size is 10 milli arcseconds ( 16 pc and 31 pc at redshifts of 0.0912 and 0.2212 respectively, for H_0 = 75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 and q_0 =0.5 ).

At both redshifts, the depth of the line (see Figures fig:lz fig:hz ) considerably exceeds the flux in the weak lobes, this implies that the absorbers must cover the central core. Given the small size of this central core, the covering factor is likely to be close to unity.

sources.summary: The paper describes QSO B0738+313 as a radio quasar sightline with two low-redshift DLA absorbers, a core-dominated radio structure, weak extended lobes, and spectra extracted across the QSO.

quotes.quote: The two DLA absorption systems identified in the UV spectrum of the z_ em = 0.630 QSO B0738+313 (OI 363), are both examples of low-redshift absorbers with no corresponding luminous disk galaxy (Rao Turnshek 1998).

B0738+313 is a core dominated quasar, with only 2 of its total flux found in weak lobes which extend 30 north and south of the core at this frequency (W. Lane, in preparation; Murphy et al. 1993). New WSRT observations (W. Lane, in preparation) find no evidence for the presence of the main component line against the extended lobes, and any other absorption is ruled out at 3 optical depths of _ north 0.03 and _ south 0.08 , for velocity resolution v = 4.5 km s ^ 1 ) over velocities in a range of several hundred km s ^ 1 to either side of the main component redshift.

At 1302 MHz the QSO center is lightly resolved into a core and an extended component, as shown in Figure 3, and all of the ``core'' flux from unresolved observations is recovered. The symmetric, weak, extended lobes, which lie at roughly 30 '' to the north and south of the quasar (Murphy, Brown, Perley 1993), have been resolved away. Spectra from three locations across the QSO have been extracted.

sources.summary: The paper reports that OI 363 is a core-dominated radio-loud quasar with an X-ray jet but no detected extended [O II] emission in the IFS data.

quotes.quote: OI 363 This is a core-dominated, flat spectrum RLQ stanghellini97 . Chandra X-ray observations have revealed an X-ray jet with several knots extending to the south-east of the quasar siemiginowska03 . The end of the X-ray jet at 200 kpc is spatially coincident with the weaker extended radio emission. The [ O ii ] emission line is within the wavelength range of the IFS observations, but a subtraction of the QSO emission from the IFS data cubes reveals no extended emission in either data sets. A narrow-band image created from the INTEGRAL data cube at the wavelength region around [ O ii ] at z=0.63 with a width of 20 is shown in Fig. fig:3c_0738_map .

gmw_00005815.overview: The radio source 0742+103 is identified with a compact host galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0611600] and is classified as a peaked-spectrum source [arXiv:0909.5100]. It exhibits a compact, bent jet morphology [arXiv:astro-ph/0108185] and is considered a potential candidate for a two-sided jet structure [arXiv:0909.5100].

sources.summary: The paper identifies B0742+103 with a compact host galaxy, reports faint spectra with inconsistent possible lines, and cannot independently confirm the literature redshift.

quotes.quote: B0742+103 : Neither Stickel96 nor Vries95 were able to identify the host of this relatively bright radio source. Our UT1 image ( Figure maps ), which unambiguously identifies the source with a compact host galaxy, indicates that the tentative identifications by Fugmann88 and Vries00b were correct: we establish R =23.1. We obtained long slit spectra of this source both in the January and the December run. Both observations show a faint spectrum and features which could be lines. However, none of these features are present in both spectra.

We detect a faint (slightly brighter than noise) emission at 6922 which could be the Best03 C iii ] . Having only one emission feature, we cannot get an independent redshift measurement. If their redshift is correct, using the 5 GHz flux density from O'Dea98 , the source would have a radio power of 2.1 10^ 28 W/Hz.

sources.summary: The paper says 0742+103 has uncertain identification, possibly a quasar, with a compact bent jet morphology, no detected flat-spectrum core, and weak northern polarization.

quotes.quote: 0742+103 : the identification of this radio source is uncertain -- Stickel et al. (1996b) suggest it is a quasar of magnitude m_R 23.6 . The 15 GHz image suggests a structure reminiscent of 3C48 although the scale is about 100 times smaller. The MEM image shows a jet leading to NW, then bending to NE and finally widening and bending to the East as confirmed by the 5 GHz image. The spectral index image fails to reveal any flat region which would allow us to locate the core. Faint polarized emission is detected only on the northern tip, where the jet broadens.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 0742+103 as a high-redshift peaked-spectrum MOJAVE source and potential two-sided jet candidate, fitting a weak southeastern component but assigning the brightest feature as the core.

quotes.quote: 0742+103: This is the only bona fide peaked-spectrum source in the flux-density-limited MOJAVE sample TTT05 , and given the known characteristics of this class, it is a potential candidate for two-sided jet structure. It is among the highest-redshift objects in the MOJAVE sample ( z = 2.624). We have fit a slightly weaker component to the southeast of the brightest feature at all epochs, but have arbitrarily assigned the core position to the brightest feature in the images. We were not able to find any published shorter wavelength VLBI images that could be used to clarify the structure near the core.

gmw_00005816.overview: The radio source 0743-006 is a polarized quasar that exhibits a polarization percentage of 1 at its peak total intensity [arXiv:astro-ph/0108185]. It features an elongated candidate core and a knotty, expanding jet with a changing magnetic-field orientation [arXiv:astro-ph/0108185].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0743-006 as a polarized radio source with an elongated candidate core, a possible knotty expanding jet, and changing magnetic-field orientation.

quotes.quote: 0743-006 : the candidate core component visible in the 5 GHz image is seen as an elongated structure in the 15 GHz VLBA image, with just a hint of the 5 GHz NE jet. The MEM image reveals the elongated component as a triple structure (a knotty jet?) and a least-squares fit to the "cleaned and restored'' image provides a good fit with 3 components, with slightly increasing sizes from the southwestern to the northeastern one, suggesting (taking into consideration the morphology seen in the 5 GHz image) the presence of an expanding jet. The low resolution of the 5 GHz image does not allow us to disentangle the contribution to the flux density of the distinct components, therefore it is not possible to determine if any of the 3 components in the 15 GHz image has a flat spectral index.

The radio source is polarized with a polarization percentage of 1 at the peak position in the total intensity image with a transverse magnetic field. The fractional polarization increases towards the NE, also changing its position angle, with an inferred projected magnetic field which becomes parallel to the local source axis.

gmw_00005817.overview: The radio source 1143-245 exhibits a resolved north-south structure extending about 5 milliarcseconds [arXiv:astro-ph/0108185]. This structure consists of a weak, wiggling jet-like emission leading to the south and a strong, compact component with polarized emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0108185].

sources.summary: The paper reports that 1143-245 has a resolved north-south VLBI structure, a compact component plus wiggling jet-like emission, no flat-spectrum region, and significant polarization.

quotes.quote: 1143-245 : the VLBI image at 15 GHz shows a structure roughly in the NS direction extending about 5 mas; both components are resolved by the present observations. The MEM image reveals a number of further details, with a strong quite compact component and a weak wiggling jet-like structure leading to the South. The spectral index is not flat in any region of the source, despite its core-jet morphology. As in other similar cases, a possible explanation is that the source experienced significant flux density variability between the 2 epochs, or that the true core is embedded in the brightest component, or that there are errors in the image registration.

The brightest region has significant polarized emission. At the peak position in the total intensity image the fractional polarization is 1 in p.a. 14^ . The VLA data show a total fractional polarization of 1.4 at 8.4 GHz.

gmw_00005818.name: NGC 309
gmw_00005818.overview: NGC 309 is a galaxy with imaging data spanning 12 photometric bands from the far-ultraviolet to the W2 band [arXiv:2101.09717]. It hosts solar-neighborhood analog regions that are grouped in a ring-like structure [arXiv:2204.13725].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 309 to demonstrate piXedfit imaging, spectrophotometric cubes, pixel binning, and resolved SED fitting of its center and spiral arms.

quotes.quote: The target galaxy for this example is NGC 309. Imaging data over 12 bands ranging from the FUV to W2 are used to obtain the spatially resolved SED data cube. As can be seen from the fluxes maps, the 2MASS bands are the shallowest among the photometric bands used in the analysis.

Figure  fig:demo_pixbin shows two pixel binning results for the NGC 309 obtained with binning requirements that only differ in S / N thresholds for the three 2MASS bands. The pixel binning results in the top and bottom panels use 2MASS S / N thresholds of 1 and 3 , respectively. The S / N threshold for the rest of the photometric bands is set to 10 (see Figure  fig:maps_multiband_fluxes for the set of the photometric bands).

Figure  fig:demo_pixedfit_ngc309 shows fitting results using MCMC for two spatial bins in the NGC 309, one located around the center and the other located in the spiral arms. For centrally-located bin, the fitting is done to both spectrum and photometric SED simultaneously.

sources.summary: NGC 309 is one of three CALIFA example hosts of solar-neighborhood analog regions; its SNA spectrum matches the reference, and likely SNA spaxels appear ring-like.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:sna-showcase summarizes our main results for three galaxies hosting SNA regions, as an illustrative example: NGC 5947 , NGC 5000 and NGC 309 . For each galaxy we show the contour of the likelihood map ( L _ ij ^2 _ ij /2 ) on top of the RGB composite image, highlighting the location of the most likely SNA. In addition we show the corresponding spectra of those regions together with the reference SNA spectrum. We observe that the SNA spectrum of the three example galaxies is well within the uncertainty band, _ (shaded region), for most of the wavelength range. A higher L _ SNA indicates that the likelihood of finding a SNA within the target galaxy is more concentrated around a specific region.

In top panels of Fig. fig:sna-showcase , NGC 5000 shows the most concentrated likelihood of hosting SNA spaxels, with an apparent azimuthal symmetry. At first sight, this pattern is also followed by NGC 5947 and NGC 309 , whereby likely SNA spaxels group in a ring-like structure.

gmw_00005819.name: 8C 1909 + 722
gmw_00005819.overview: The high-redshift radio galaxy 8C 1909 + 722 is a system of two interacting, star-forming galaxies superimposed along the line of sight [arXiv:1001.2018, arXiv:1010.1385]. The system exhibits extended dust emission on scales of tens of kiloparsecs [arXiv:astro-ph/0509364], with its most active component hosting an accreting supermassive black hole [arXiv:1001.2018].

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C +72.26 as a z~3.5 radio-galaxy system of two interacting star-forming galaxies, with the more active component hosting the black hole powering the radio source.

quotes.quote: We present a range of new observations of the `broad absorption line radio galaxy' \ ( z 3.5 ), including sensitive rest-frame ultraviolet integral field spectroscopy using the Gemini/GMOS-N instrument and Subaru/CISCO K band imaging and spectroscopy. We show that \ is a system of two vigorously star-forming galaxies superimposed along the line of sight separated by 1300 200 km s ^ 1 in velocity, with each demonstrating spectroscopically resolved absorption lines. The most active star-forming galaxy also hosts the accreting supermassive black hole which powers the extended radio source. We conclude that the star formation is unlikely to have been induced by a shock caused by the passage of the radio jet, and instead propose that a collision is a more probable trigger for the star formation. Despite the massive starburst, the UV-mid-infrared spectral energy distribution suggests that the pre-existing stellar population comprises 10^ 12 \ of stellar mass, with the current burst only contributing a further 2 , suggesting that \ has already assembled most of its final stellar mass.

In this paper we discuss our observations of the z 3.5 source , also known as 6C 1909+72, or TX J1908+7220. Although originially classified as a `broad absorption line radio galaxy' from its optical spectrum spectrum (Dey 1999; de Breuck et al. \ 2001), we demonstrate that the absorption features are P Cygni profiles characteristic of a massive starburst which provides the entire rest-frame ultraviolet continuum.

sources.summary: The paper reports a strong central 350 micron source for 8C 1909 + 722, supporting extended dust emission on tens-of-kiloparsec scales.

quotes.quote: 8C 1909 + 722 The 350- m sharc-ii map shows a strong ( 5 ) central source ( S_ 350 m =90 15 mJy), and a second significant ( 4 ) source about 38 south-eastwards ( S_ 350 m 148 32 mJy). The former is offset from the RG-position by 6 , while the latter lies within 8 of a strong scuba source detected about 46 south-east of the RG (Stevens et al. \ 2003).

These offsets can easily be accounted for considering that a) we have not corrected for any potential astrometrical offsets between the sharc-ii and scuba maps, 2) the beam widths are large ( fwhm 15 in the case of scuba ), and that ) the two sources are clearly extended at 850- m. The RG is marginally resolved at 350- m, and exhibit the same general shape as seen at 850- m, thus apparently confirming the claim by Stevens et al. \ (2003) that the (850- m) dust emission in this object (and its brightest companion) is extended on tens of kpc scales.
This seems to be backed up by the detection of a weak ( 4 , S_ 350 m =70 17 mJy) source only 19 north-east of the RG, and thus within the north-east extended 850- m emission from the RG.

sources.summary: The paper describes TXS J1908+7220 as two superimposed star-forming galaxies whose transmitted quasar continuum prevents a stellar-mass measurement.

quotes.quote: TXS J1908+7220 ( z=3.530 ): Using integral field spectroscopy, smi10 argued that this broad line radio galaxy is a system of two vigorously star-forming galaxies superimposed along the line of sight and separated by 1300 km s ^ 1 in velocity. The detailed SED decomposition of smi10 also showed that the near-IR emission of this HzRG is dominated by transmitted quasar continuum. While the hot dust continuum does not contribute substantially at _ rest =1.6 m, this transmitted quasar continuum prevents us from measuring the old stellar population, and we provide only an upper limit to the stellar mass.

gmw_00005820.name: ESO 418-8
gmw_00005820.overview: ESO 418-8 is a bulgeless galaxy characterized by a fairly featureless disk [arXiv:1205.1936]. The galaxy lacks recent large-scale star formation, hosting only localized, knotty star-forming regions and weak, extended radio emission across its disk [arXiv:1205.1936].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 418-8 as a featureless-disk bulgeless galaxy with only localized star-forming regions and weak extended radio emission from its disk.

quotes.quote: ESO 418-8, NGC 4299, NGC 4540 and UGC 4499 : All four galaxies have fairly featureless disks that do not support recent large scale star formation or spiral structure. Only knotty or very localised star forming regions are seen in the optical and UV maps gildepaz.etal.2007 . The NVSS maps reveal extended but relatively weak radio emission from the disks and is probably associated with the localized star forming regions.

gmw_00005821.name: NGC 4299
gmw_00005821.overview: NGC 4299 is a bulgeless spiral galaxy [arXiv:1304.1279, arXiv:1205.1936] that shares a common neutral hydrogen envelope with its close companion, NGC 4294 [arXiv:1304.1279, arXiv:astro-ph/0406243]. It features a truncated disk with localized star-forming regions [arXiv:astro-ph/0406243, arXiv:1205.1936] and symmetric radio continuum emission [arXiv:1304.1279].
