sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4299 shares an H I envelope with NGC 4294, has symmetric radio continuum, shallow disk spectral index, weak 6 cm polarization, and possible outer magnetic arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 4299 Together with NGC 4294, NGC 4299 shares a common H i envelope (Chung et al. 2009). The radio continuum emission of this face-on galaxy is symmetric and more extended at 6 cm than at 20 cm. The spectral index of the galactic disk is quite shallow ( 0.6 ). Polarized emission is only detected at 6 cm at a low level. The magnetic field structure seems to be that of a symmetric spiral where detected.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4299 as a truncated Virgo spiral with unusually high H emission, bright HII regions, an irregular outer ridge suggesting ICM pressure, and close companion NGC 4294.

quotes.quote: The only sample galaxy with both enhanced star formation and a truncated H disk is NGC 4299. Its global H equivalent width of 84 \ is one of the 3 highest values in the Virgo sample, and its H image (Figure enhanced3 ) shows numerous bright HII regions throughout a disk truncated beyond 0.7 r_ 24 . The outer extent of the H distribution is irregular, except in the southwest where it forms a well-defined ridge extending over 90 , suggesting ongoing ICM pressure. The most luminous HII complex in the galaxy is located at the southern end of this ridge. NGC 4299 is in an apparent pair with NGC 4294, which is projected 5.8 \ (27 kpc) away, and has a similar line-of-sight velocity.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4299 as a featureless-disk bulgeless galaxy with only localized star-forming regions and weak extended radio emission from its disk.

quotes.quote: ESO 418-8, NGC 4299, NGC 4540 and UGC 4499 : All four galaxies have fairly featureless disks that do not support recent large scale star formation or spiral structure. Only knotty or very localised star forming regions are seen in the optical and UV maps gildepaz.etal.2007 . The NVSS maps reveal extended but relatively weak radio emission from the disks and is probably associated with the localized star forming regions.

gmw_00005822.name: NGC 4540
gmw_00005822.overview: NGC 4540 is a bulgeless galaxy characterized by a fairly featureless disk [arXiv:1205.1936]. The disk does not support recent large-scale star formation, showing only localized, knotty star-forming regions and weak extended radio emission [arXiv:1205.1936].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4540 as a featureless-disk bulgeless galaxy with only localized star-forming regions and weak extended radio emission from its disk.

quotes.quote: ESO 418-8, NGC 4299, NGC 4540 and UGC 4499 : All four galaxies have fairly featureless disks that do not support recent large scale star formation or spiral structure. Only knotty or very localised star forming regions are seen in the optical and UV maps gildepaz.etal.2007 . The NVSS maps reveal extended but relatively weak radio emission from the disks and is probably associated with the localized star forming regions.

gmw_00005823.name: UGC 4499
gmw_00005823.overview: UGC 4499 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0210152] characterized by a bulgeless, featureless disk [arXiv:1205.1936] and a strong central bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0210152]. The galaxy lacks large-scale star formation, hosting only localized star-forming regions and weak extended radio emission [arXiv:1205.1936].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 4499 as a featureless-disk bulgeless galaxy with only localized star-forming regions and weak extended radio emission from its disk.

quotes.quote: ESO 418-8, NGC 4299, NGC 4540 and UGC 4499 : All four galaxies have fairly featureless disks that do not support recent large scale star formation or spiral structure. Only knotty or very localised star forming regions are seen in the optical and UV maps gildepaz.etal.2007 . The NVSS maps reveal extended but relatively weak radio emission from the disks and is probably associated with the localized star forming regions.

sources.summary: The paper says NFW models overpredict UGC 4499's inner slope, constant-core models fit better, and its strong bar may contribute non-circular motions.

quotes.quote: However, in a number of cases, the NFW model overpredicts the inner slope of the rotation curve: UGC 731, UGC 4325, UGC 4499, and F563-V2, and in two cases, UGC 5721 and UGC 11707, the NFW model seems to fare better than the pseudo-isothermal model.
Only for four galaxies in our sample do models with =0 yield significantly better fits than for =1 independent of \ (UGC 4325, UGC 4499, F563-V2 and F568-3).

Interestingly, 3 out of the 4 galaxies which are poorly fit by an NFW halo have strong bars (UGC 4499, F563-V2 and F568-3), suggesting that the non-circular motions associated with bars may have contributed to the poor fits.

gmw_00005824.name: NGC 3346
gmw_00005824.overview: NGC 3346 is a nearby, nearly face-on bulgeless galaxy [arXiv:1205.1936]. It features a small central bar associated with a fairly strong spiral structure, as well as diffuse radio emission distributed over its inner disk [arXiv:1205.1936].

sources.summary: NGC 3346 is described as a nearby, nearly face-on bulgeless galaxy with a small central bar, fairly strong spiral structure, and diffuse inner-disk radio emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 3346 and NGC 5668 : Both galaxies are relatively nearby and nearly face-on in orientation. NGC 3346 has a small bar in the center that is associated with a fairly strong spiral structure. NGC 5668 has less prominent spiral structure. The NVSS maps shows diffuse and extended radio emission distributed over the inner disks of both galaxies.

gmw_00005825.name: NGC 3445
gmw_00005825.overview: NGC 3445 is a lopsided, one-armed spiral galaxy that forms a triplet with NGC 3440 and NGC 3458 [arXiv:1205.1936]. It features a small central oval bar, a nuclear star cluster, and extended disk star formation that may be tidally triggered by a neighboring galaxy [arXiv:1205.1936].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3445 as a lopsided one-armed galaxy in a triplet, with tidally triggered disk star formation, radio emission, CO gas, and a small central oval.

quotes.quote: NGC3445 : Optical images suggest that this is a one armed spiral galaxy. The prominence of the southern spiral arm gives the galaxy a lopsided appearance; only a closer inspection of the 2MASS K band image reveals the smaller northern arm. NGC 3445 forms part of a triplet of galaxies with two other galaxies, NGC 3440 and NGC 3458. Close to the southern arm and lying east of the nucleus of NGC 3445, there is a prominent star forming region that may have formed from a retrograde encounter with a neighbouring galaxy cao.etal.2007 . GALEX observations of the galaxy show that the star formation is extended over the disk smith.etal.2010 though much of it is associated with the spiral arms and the nucleus. The galaxy has a bright nucleus that hosts a nuclear star cluster seth.etal.2008 but no AGN.

The radio flux in the NVSS maps is large compared to other galaxies in our sample but due to the poor resolution of NVSS, not much can be said about the radio morphology. This is one of the few galaxies in our sample that has been detected in CO emission Boker.etal.2003AA ; the molecular gas mass is relatively low and centrally concentrated. The galaxy has a small oval bar in the center but bulge-disk separation was not possible in this galaxy, perhaps because the `bulge' is too small Baggett.etal.1998 .

gmw_00005826.name: NGC 3782
gmw_00005826.overview: NGC 3782 is an inclined, bulgeless disk galaxy with a small bar in its center [arXiv:1205.1936]. The galaxy lacks a strong spiral structure, featuring diffuse radio emission and knots of star formation distributed across its disk [arXiv:1205.1936].

sources.summary: NGC 3782 is described as an inclined bulgeless galaxy with a small central bar, disk star-formation knots, and diffuse extended radio emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 3782 and NGC 3906 : Both galaxies have small bars in their centers but no strong spiral structure. Knots of star formation are distributed over their disks. The radio emission is diffuse and extended over the galaxy disks. NGC 3782 is fairly inclined ( i=60^ ) but NGC 3906 is close to face-on in morphology.

gmw_00005827.name: NGC 3906
gmw_00005827.overview: NGC 3906 is a nearly face-on, bulgeless Magellanic spiral galaxy [arXiv:1205.1936, arXiv:1602.07689]. It features a small central stellar bar that is offset from the photometric center of its disk [arXiv:1205.1936, arXiv:1602.07689], while knots of star formation and diffuse radio emission are distributed across the disk [arXiv:1205.1936].

sources.summary: NGC 3906 is described as a nearly face-on bulgeless galaxy with a small central bar, disk star-formation knots, and diffuse extended radio emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 3782 and NGC 3906 : Both galaxies have small bars in their centers but no strong spiral structure. Knots of star formation are distributed over their disks. The radio emission is diffuse and extended over the galaxy disks. NGC 3782 is fairly inclined ( i=60^ ) but NGC 3906 is close to face-on in morphology.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3906 as an observed Magellanic spiral with a 1.2 kpc bar-photometric-center offset and matches simulations to its disk/bar-center measurement setup.

quotes.quote: This explains the nature of the offset bar found in many Magellanic-type galaxies, including the Large Magellanic Cloud (LMC) and NGC 3906.

A good example of these systems is offered by NGC 3906 that shows evidence of the bar offset from the photometric center of the galaxy by 1.2 kpc (see, e.g., Figure 1 of deSwardt:2015fk ), or NGC 4618 1991AJ....101..829O .

An observationally motivated approach has been used to define disk and bar centers as in the study of the Magellanic spiral NGC 3906 by deSwardt:2015fk . The stellar or gaseous disk of the primary galaxy is projected to a face-on density map with 256 square bins in each dimension. Each bin has a size of 78 pc, corresponding to 1 arcsecond resolution at the distance of the observed galaxy NGC 3906.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC3906 as a local offset-bar example: its bar is offset from the disc, coincides with the HI dynamical centre, and may reflect dark subhalo interaction or an unseen companion.

quotes.quote: More recently, Swardt2015 measured an offset of 0.9 kpc between the centre of the stellar bar and the centre of the disc in NGC3906. In this case the bar centre coincides with the dynamical centre determined through HI observations. In contrast with the LMC, NGC3906 is observed to be isolated, thus a possible explanation for the observed offset is an interaction with the dark matter subhalo, or an unidentified fast moving companion.

gmw_00005828.name: NGC 4701
gmw_00005828.overview: NGC 4701 is a non-barred, bulgeless spiral galaxy featuring a small, bright nucleus and a flocculent spiral structure [arXiv:2203.00694, arXiv:1205.1936]. Star formation is associated with its spiral pattern, and CO gas is detected asymmetrically, primarily on the blueshifted side of the galaxy [arXiv:2203.00694, arXiv:1205.1936].

sources.summary: NGC 4701 is a non-barred spiral with strong central arms and asymmetric CO detection; maximal-disk assumptions affect its fitted dark-matter slope only within uncertainties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4701 --- This spiral galaxy shows no evidence for a bar, though the spiral arms are prominent and strong towards the centre. CO is mainly detected on the blueshifted side of the galaxy. This is not due to a tuning error, but rather due to less signal on the redshifted side of the galaxy.

For NGC 4701 (and the innermost point of NGC 5692), we fix the stellar velocity curve to a maximal disk where the observed stellar velocity is larger than the CO rotation curve (see Section ssec:stellarrotcurves ). As a test, we relax the maximal disk assumption for NGC 4701 and re-fit . Though we find a slightly shallower slope ( = 0.1^ +0.6 _ 0.5 ), it is consistent with the slope measured for a maximal disk ( = 0.4^ +0.8 _ 0.8 ) well within the uncertainties.

sources.summary: NGC 4701 is described as a flocculent bulgeless galaxy with a small bright nucleus, spiral-pattern star formation, diffuse disk radio emission, and FIRST emission in the inner disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 4701 and NGC 5584 : Both galaxies have a small bright nucleus and a flocculent spiral structure. Star formation is associated with the spiral pattern. The NVSS images show extended but diffuse radio emission from the disks and is probably associated with star forming regions over the spiral arms. NGC 4701 also shows some emission in the higher resolution FIRST radio map. The emission is concentrated about the nucleus and the inner disk.

gmw_00005829.name: PSO J055.4244--00.8035
gmw_00005829.overview: PSO J055.4244--00.8035 is a high-redshift, radio-loud quasar [arXiv:1503.04214]. Optical spectra of the object reveal a sharp Lyman-alpha break, indicating a redshift estimated at z=5.68 [arXiv:1503.04214].

sources.summary: The paper confirms PSO J055.4244--00.8035 as a newly discovered high-redshift, radio-loud quasar and estimates its redshift as z=5.68 from optical spectra.

quotes.quote: The other two spectroscopically followed up objects -- PSO J055.4244--00.8035 and PSO J135.3860+16.2518 -- were confirmed to be high-redshift quasars.

PSO J055.4244--00.8035 ( z=5.68 0.05 ) The discovery spectrum was taken on 2014 February 22 using the DBSP spectrograph with a total exposure time of 2400 s. These observations were carried out in 1 seeing using the 1 5 wide longslit. This spectrum shows a sharp \ break indicating that the object is unambiguously a quasar at z > 5.5 but the S/N does not allow us to determine an accurate redshift.

We estimate the redshift instead by comparing the observed quasar spectrum with the composite SDSS z 6 quasar spectrum from . We assume the redshift that minimizes the ^2 between the observed spectrum and the template (the wavelength range where the minimization is performed is _ rest = 1240 -- 1450 ). The estimated redshift is z=5.68 .

gmw_00005830.name: PSO J135.3860+16.2518
gmw_00005830.overview: PSO J135.3860+16.2518 is a high-redshift, radio-loud quasar [arXiv:1503.04214]. It has an estimated redshift of approximately 5.63, although this measurement remains highly uncertain [arXiv:1503.04214].

sources.summary: The paper confirms PSO J135.3860+16.2518 as a newly discovered high-redshift, radio-loud quasar, estimating z=5.63 while emphasizing redshift uncertainty.

quotes.quote: The other two spectroscopically followed up objects -- PSO J055.4244--00.8035 and PSO J135.3860+16.2518 -- were confirmed to be high-redshift quasars.

PSO J135.3860+16.2518 ( z=5.63 0.05 ) The discovery spectrum was taken with EFOSC2 at the NTT on 2014 March 3. The observations were carried out with the Gr 16 grism, 1 5 slit width, 1 3 seeing, and a total exposure time of 3600 s. The spectrum is very noisy but it resembles the shape of a high-redshift quasar with a tentative \ line at 8100 .

The combined MODS-FORS2 spectrum is shown in Figure fig:spectra . The estimated redshift by matching to the z 6 quasar composite spectrum from is z=5.63 0.05 . The redshift estimate for PSO J135.3860+16.2518 is quite uncertain as represented by its error bar. There might be a tentative \ line which would place this quasar at a slightly higher redshift.

gmw_00005831.name: the z=3.99 galaxy
gmw_00005831.overview: The z=3.99 galaxy is a massive quiescent galaxy that experienced a starburst episode approximately 500 Myr before the observed epoch, followed by rapid quenching [arXiv:2311.11569]. It features an evolved stellar population and is surrounded by four massive quiescent galaxies at a similar redshift [arXiv:2311.11569].

sources.summary: The paper confirms a massive quiescent galaxy at z=3.99, finding a post-starburst history, rapid quenching, consistent dynamical and stellar masses, and a surrounding concentration of quiescent galaxies.

quotes.quote: Follow-up near-infrared spectroscopic observations with MOSFIRE on Keck confirm a massive ( 10^ 11 \ M_ ) quiescent galaxy at z=3.99 . Our spectral energy distribution (SED) analyses reveal that the galaxy experienced an episode of starburst about 500 Myr prior to the observed epoch, followed by rapid quenching. As its spectrum is sufficiently good to measure the stellar velocity dispersion, we infer its dynamical mass and find that it is consistent with its stellar mass. The galaxy is surrounded by 4 massive ( >10^ 10 \ M_ ) quiescent galaxies on a 1 physical Mpc scale, all of which are consistent with being located at the same redshift based on high-accuracy spectro-photometric redshifts.

Only one of them is sufficiently bright to measure its redshift from absorption features and its spectrum is shown in Fig. 3. The spectrum is rather noisy (the median S/N per pixel is 1.8), but we can still identify multiple Balmer absorption lines, which yield z_ spec =3.987 0.001 . The continuum is relatively flat overall, but there is a hint of the 4000 \ break on the blue end, which indicates an evolved stellar population.

gmw_00005832.name: BGG1
gmw_00005832.overview: BGG1 is the northern brightest group galaxy in the foreground of MACS0329 [arXiv:2301.12173]. It features a disk component with visible spiral arms on its northeastern side and exhibits star formation indicated by [OII] emission [arXiv:2301.12173].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BGG1 as the northern brightest group galaxy, obtains spectroscopy, and finds a disk component, [OII] emission, star formation, and visible spiral arms.

quotes.quote: Since these two galaxies will prove to be the two brightest galaxies of a group in the foreground of MACS0329, we refer to them as BGGs (BGG1 to the north and BGG2 to the south). New spectroscopic TNG data Long-slit spectroscopic observations of galaxies in the field of GrG J0330-0218 were performed in November and December 2015 and in January 2016 at the TNG. As instrument, we used the Device Optimized for LOw REsolution Spectroscopy (DOLoRes) with the LR-B grism. With LR-B Grm1 all the relevant spectral features can be observed at the group redshift , while the resolution is enough for the measurement of accurate galaxy redshifts. In total, we acquired spectra for 18 galaxies with exposure times between 900 s and 1800 s. These observations focused on the two galaxies BGG1 and BGG2 and on bright galaxies in their vicinity.

The most interesting result of the decomposition is that BGG1 clearly has a disk component. Disks are fragile structures, but they are not uncommon in groups where low relative velocity encounters occur. Confirmation of the presence of a disk is found in the spectrum of this BGG1, which is shown in Fig. figspectra . The [ OII ] emission line is clearly visible, suggesting that some star formation is underway. In addition, some spiral arms are visible in the northeastern side of BGG1, again confirming the presence of a disk in this galaxy.

gmw_00005833.name: BGG2
gmw_00005833.overview: BGG2 is a southern brightest group galaxy located in the foreground of the MACS0329 cluster [arXiv:2301.12173]. It is classified as a simple early-type galaxy with a surface brightness profile that fits a single Sersic component [arXiv:2301.12173].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BGG2 as the southern brightest group galaxy, targets it spectroscopically with BGG1, and describes its surface-brightness profile as a simple early-type galaxy.

quotes.quote: Since these two galaxies will prove to be the two brightest galaxies of a group in the foreground of MACS0329, we refer to them as BGGs (BGG1 to the north and BGG2 to the south). New spectroscopic TNG data Long-slit spectroscopic observations of galaxies in the field of GrG J0330-0218 were performed in November and December 2015 and in January 2016 at the TNG. As instrument, we used the Device Optimized for LOw REsolution Spectroscopy (DOLoRes) with the LR-B grism. With LR-B Grm1 all the relevant spectral features can be observed at the group redshift , while the resolution is enough for the measurement of accurate galaxy redshifts. In total, we acquired spectra for 18 galaxies with exposure times between 900 s and 1800 s. These observations focused on the two galaxies BGG1 and BGG2 and on bright galaxies in their vicinity.

On the other hand, BGG2 appears to be a simple early-type galaxy. The surface brightness profile can be fitted using a single S ersic component with n=4 . ICL computation To estimate the amount of ICL in GrG J0330-0218 we used the SB cut method.

gmw_00005834.name: SMM J16359+6612
gmw_00005834.overview: SMM J16359+6612 is a starburst submillimeter galaxy at a redshift of z=2.517 [arXiv:0907.1087]. It is gravitationally lensed into three components by the A2218 cluster, contains warm molecular gas, and shows no evidence of an obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0907.1087].

sources.summary: The paper treats SMM J16359+6612 as a lensed z=2.517 starburst submillimeter galaxy with CO-detected molecular gas, no obscured AGN evidence, and no detected water megamaser emission.

quotes.quote: Both SMM J14011+0252 and SMM J16359+6612 have multi-wavelength characteristics consistent with ongoing starburst activity, as well as CO line emission indicating the presence of warm molecular gas. Our observations do not reveal any evidence for H _2 O megamaser emission in either target, while the lensing allows us to obtain deep limits to the H _2 O line luminosities, L_ H_2O < 7470 L _ (3- ) in the case of SMM J14011+0252, and L_ H_2O < 1893 L _ for SMM J16359+6612, assuming linewidths of 80 km s ^ 1 .

Our second target, \ is a z=2.517 SMG lensed into three components by the A2218 cluster (Kneib et al. \ 2004), with the brightest component lensed by a factor of 22. All three components have been detected in CO line emission (Sheth et al. \ 2004; Kneib et al. \ 2005), revealing a mass in molecular gas of only 3 10 ^9 M _ . Non-detections of \ in both the 0.5--2 and 2--8 keV Chandra X-ray bands imply that no obscured AGN is present, a conclusion supported by the mid-IR spectrum of SMM J16359+6612, which is similar to a starburst galaxy template derived from z 0 galaxies (Rigby et al. \ 2008).

gmw_00005835.name: MACS0647--JD
gmw_00005835.overview: MACS0647--JD is a triply lensed galaxy at a spectroscopic redshift of z=10.17 [arXiv:2305.03042]. It is spatially resolved into two small components, designated A and B, which may represent an ongoing galaxy merger [arXiv:2305.03042].

sources.summary: The paper presents JWST spectroscopy of the triply lensed z=10.17 galaxy MACS0647--JD, finding emission lines, low metallicity, and two resolved components.

quotes.quote: MACS0647 JD We present /NIRSpec prism spectroscopy of , the triply-lensed z 11 candidate discovered in \ imaging and spatially resolved by \ imaging into two components A and B. Spectroscopy of component A yields a spectroscopic redshift z=10.17 based on 7 detected emission lines: , , , , , , and . These are the second-most distant detections of these emission lines to date, in a galaxy observed just 460 million years after the Big Bang. Based on observed and extrapolated line flux ratios we derive a gas-phase metallicity Z = \ = 7.5 -- 8.0, or (0.06 -- 0.2) , ionization parameter \ \ 1.9 0.2 , and an ionizing photon production efficiency log ( _ ion )=25.2 0.2 erg \ Hz.

MACS0647--JD is triply-lensed by a massive foreground galaxy cluster, MACSJ0647.7+7015 z=0.591 ; Ebeling2007 . Despite lensing magnifications of 8, 5, and 2 Meena2023 , the three lensed images JD1, JD2, and JD3 were spatially unresolved in \ imaging. /NIRCam imaging resolved \ as having two small components in a possible galaxy merger at z=10.6 0.3 Hsiao2022 . The brighter component A has an effective radius 70 24 pc and appears bluer, likely due to its young stellar population ( 50 Myr old) and no dust.

gmw_00005836.name: NGC2683-DGSAT-1
gmw_00005836.overview: NGC2683-DGSAT-1 is a low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy candidate located near NGC 2683 [arXiv:1511.04446]. The galaxy exhibits an irregular shape, which suggests it may be undergoing tidal disruption [arXiv:1511.04446].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC2683-DGSAT-1 as a low-surface-brightness dwarf candidate near NGC 2683, notes its irregular shape, models it with GALFIT, and suggests possible tidal disruption.

quotes.quote: We refer to these galaxies as NGC2683-DGSAT-1 and 2 in our catalogue. The former has an irregular shape, while the latter is almost spheroidal. Similar to the other LSB galaxies in this paper, we fit a Sersic model to these two galaxies. The results are shown in Figure fig:dgsats and Table t:results . In the case of NGC2683-DGSAT-1, because of its irregular shape and many foreground stars on top of its image, it is very difficult to obtain an accurate model.

NGC5457-DF-7 is very faint and its measured properties have large uncertainties, but NGC2683-DGSAT-1 is bright enough to show its irregular shape, which suggests that it might be undergoing tidal disruption.

gmw_00005837.name: NGC5457-DGSAT-1
gmw_00005837.overview: NGC5457-DGSAT-1 is a low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy candidate located in the M101 field [arXiv:1511.04446]. It was likely missed by the Dragonfly project because its structure was not resolved from a nearby bright star [arXiv:1511.04446].

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC5457-DGSAT-1 as a newly found low-surface-brightness dwarf candidate in the M101 field, modeled after masking a bright neighboring star and missed by Dragonfly because of resolution.

quotes.quote: With our search strategy explained in Section subsec:searching , we could not only detect DF1 , DF3 , DF4 , DF6 and DF7 , but we also find yet another low surface brightness galaxy (the NGC5457-DGSAT-1 ) which was not reported by the Dragonfly project. This dwarf galaxy candidate was also independently discovered by karachentsev15 The image of M101 used in our study, taken by the astrophotographer F. Neyer (see Table t:telescopes ), is also used in that paper. The Dragonfly LSB galaxies and our obtained models for them are shown in Figure fig:m101_galfit .

In the case of NGC5457-DGSAT-1 , the bright neighbouring star was masked before fitting a Sersic model to the galaxy. In the resulting residual image, the bright star ( USNO-A2.0 1425-08068454 , indicated with a red arrow) and the uncatalogued point source (identified by a yellow arrow) can be easily compared with those in the SDSS image. The results of the modelling for NGC5457-DGSAT-1 are also shown in Table t:results . The reason that Dragonfly could not detect this object is most likely because their FWHM is larger and, therefore, this LSB galaxy and its neighbouring stars were not resolved properly.

gmw_00005838.name: SDSS J0304+0022
gmw_00005838.overview: SDSS J0304+0022 is a quasar that exhibits an extreme kinematic contrast between its narrow-line region and its extended narrow-line region [arXiv:1512.05595]. The galaxy's extended narrow-line region gas is almost completely quiescent, while its unresolved emission features an exceptionally broad, blue-shifted component [arXiv:1512.05595].

sources.summary: The paper finds SDSS J0304+0022 has an extreme unresolved-versus-extended kinematic contrast, with quiescent ENLR gas after deblending and an exceptional unresolved blue-shifted component.

quotes.quote: The most extreme difference between NLR and ENLR kinematics is observed for the QSO SDSS J0304+0022 with W_ 80 1500 km s ^ 1 for the NLR and almost completely quiescent kinematics for the ENLR with W_ 80 400 km s ^ 1 .

Another special case is SDSS J0304 + 0022 for which we detect a huge offset of >500 km s ^ 1 in the radial velocity close to the QSO position after subtracting the unresolved emission. The [ ] line in this QSO has an exceptional broad blue-shifted component, but only the narrow [ ] component is actually spatially resolved.

In particular the cases where the [ ] line appears broader than 1000 km s ^ 1 (FWHM) on kpc scales reduces by several 100 km s ^ 1 down to 400 km s ^ 1 in the most extreme case of SDSS J0304+0022 when the spatially unresolved component is removed.

gmw_00005839.name: SDSS J0924+0642
gmw_00005839.overview: SDSS J0924+0642 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy [arXiv:1512.05595]. It features a symmetric velocity gradient across its nucleus with an amplitude of 230 km/s and a relatively small extended narrow-line region [arXiv:1512.05595].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J0924+0642 as a low-contrast case with a much smaller deblended ENLR, a symmetric velocity gradient, and the largest beam-smearing size correction.

quotes.quote: At the lowest contrast ratio, SDSS J0924 + 0624 stands out again, because the size of the ENLR is much smaller than 2.5'' after subtracting the unresolved emission.
An extreme case is SDSS J0924 + 0642 ( C<0.2 ) where we see a symmetric velocity gradient across the nucleus with an amplitude of 230 km s ^ 1 .

Since the maximum ENLR size drops to R_ max =6 kpc after subtracting the unresolved emission for SDSS J0924+0642 and no clear break radius in the line ratios is detected for any of the objects, we adopt a fixed radius of D=5 kpc for which we compute the energetics. This is also consistent with the results of our analysis except for SDSS J0924+0642 where the difference reaches even 0.3 dex due to a very low contrast ratio.

gmw_00005840.name: SDSS J2214+2115
gmw_00005840.overview: SDSS J2214+2115 is a quasar that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1512.05595]. The galaxy exhibits an extended outflow indicated by an increase in line width on kiloparsec scales, and it shows a systematic decrease in its line-ratio distribution at low surface brightness [arXiv:1512.05595].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J2214+2115 as the one QSO where deblending increases kpc-scale line width, indicating a powerful extended outflow and unusual line-ratio behavior.

quotes.quote: The opposite happens for QSO SDSS J2214+2115 for which we detect significantly broader lines in the ENLR after removing the NLR contribution.
Exceptions from the flat distributions are SDSS J0233-0743 and SDSS J2214+2115, which show a systematic decrease of [ ] /H at low surface brightness.
There is only one case, SDSS J2214+2115, where we find an increase in the line width on kpc scales which indicates a very powerful extended outflow.

gmw_00005841.name: MADCASH-1
gmw_00005841.overview: MADCASH-1 is an old, metal-poor dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy located 35 kpc in projection from its host, NGC 2403 [arXiv:2012.09174]. Photometry reveals that the dwarf consists solely of stellar populations older than 10 Gyr [arXiv:2012.09174].

sources.summary: The paper confirms MADCASH-1 as an old, metal-poor dwarf satellite of NGC 2403, measuring its distance, luminosity, stellar population, and structural properties.

quotes.quote: MADCASH-1 (aka MADCASH J074238+652501-dw; Carlin2016 ) is the first dwarf we reported from our survey. It is located 35 kpc in projection from LMC analog NGC 2403, and, with a luminosity near the dividing line between UFDs and classical dwarfs, is the faintest known dwarf satellite of a MC-mass host.

The superior resolution of HST clearly resolves MADCASH-1 into individual stars, yielding a well-defined RGB of an old, metal-poor population in the CMD. We use the HST data to confirm that MADCASH-1 is a dwarf galaxy at roughly the same distance as NGC 2403, and to measure its structural parameters and assess its stellar populations.

HST photometry reaching >3.5 mag below the TRGB reveals that MADCASH-1 (officially designated MADCASH J074238+652501-dw) consists solely of old ( >10 Gyr), metal-poor ( [ M/H ] 2.0 ) stellar populations.

gmw_00005842.name: MADCASH-2
gmw_00005842.overview: MADCASH-2 is a classical dwarf spheroidal galaxy located near NGC 4214 [arXiv:2012.09174]. Although its stellar population consists mostly of ancient, metal-poor stars, it has experienced some recent star formation despite having no detected neutral hydrogen [arXiv:2012.09174].

sources.summary: The paper confirms MADCASH-2 as a dwarf spheroidal near NGC 4214, with mostly ancient metal-poor stars, some recent star formation, and no detected H I.

quotes.quote: The second dwarf discovered as part of our survey is tentatively called ``MADCASH-2'' for simplicity, but is officially named MADCASH J121007+352635-dw (following IAU naming conventions). It was first seen by eye in examination of deep Subaru+HSC images around NGC 4214.
MADCASH-2 easily resolves into its individual stars in the HST image, yielding a deep, detailed CMD consistent with its being a classical dwarf spheroidal (dSph) at roughly the distance of NGC 4214.

Modelling of MADCASH-2's CMD suggests that it contains mostly ancient, metal-poor stars (age 13.5 Gyr, [ M/H ] 2.0 ), but that 10 of its stellar mass was formed 1.1--1.5 Gyr ago, and 1 was formed 400--500 Myr ago. Given its recent star formation, we search MADCASH-2 for neutral hydrogen using the Green Bank Telescope, but find no emission and estimate an upper limit on the H I mass of < 4.8 10^4 \ M_ .

gmw_00005843.name: COBRA104254.8+290719
gmw_00005843.overview: COBRA104254.8+290719 is a radio galaxy that exhibits an additional radio feature located to the left of its upper lobe [arXiv:2308.16238]. This extra component is faintly visible in FIRST imaging, but it is excluded when measuring the galaxy's main projected physical size and area [arXiv:2308.16238].

sources.summary: The paper identifies extra radio emission near COBRA104254.8+290719, notes it is faintly visible in FIRST, and excludes associated components from size and area measurements.

quotes.quote: However, a few sources have greatest physical sizes that are impacted by small features in the 10 contour creating rays that do not trace the structure as determined by eye (e.g., COBRA104254.8+290719, COBRA154638+364420). For example, COBRA104254.8+290719 has an additional radio feature to the left of the upper lobe, which was initially identified as the location of the greatest radio extent. While these features are real (and the feature for COBRA104254.8+290719 appears, though much fainter, in the FIRST image as well), we ignore these features when measuring the projected physical size and use the greatest extent from the main lobe as shown in Figure Fig:AllClusters .

Unlike the projected physical size, for the projected physical area, we only measure the extent within the 10 contours of the main lobes (for some of the radio sources, such as COBRA104254.8+290719, there are additional associated components, which we do not include).

gmw_00005844.name: COBRA104541+282028
gmw_00005844.overview: COBRA104541+282028 is a radio galaxy characterized by two separate radio lobes [arXiv:2308.16238]. Because it lacks an identified core, its physical extent is estimated using the location of its host galaxy [arXiv:2308.16238].

sources.summary: The paper describes COBRA104541+282028 as having two radio lobes with no identified core, so its physical extent is estimated from the host-galaxy core location.

quotes.quote: Additionally, for COBRA104541+282028 (see Figure Fig:AllNonClusters ), the radio contours trace two separate lobes with no identified core. For this source, we estimate the size as the position from the core, which is the location of the host galaxy identified in Paterno-Mahler2017 , to the farthest extent of the radio lobes. Thus, the physical extent extends beyond the actual size traced by the radio lobes, which may add an additional source of error.

gmw_00005845.name: Fornax
gmw_00005845.overview: The Fornax dwarf spheroidal galaxy exhibits a mildly declining velocity dispersion profile [arXiv:1204.5181]. This profile shows systematic differences between the major and minor axis regions, suggesting some degree of tangential anisotropy outside the central regions of the galaxy [arXiv:1204.5181].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Fornax dSph stellar velocities, finding radial evolution from peaked to flat-topped line profiles, tangential anisotropy outside the center, and possible weak intrinsic rotation.

quotes.quote: Fornax The kinematic sample presented by Wa09data consists of 2409 measurements for stars with a probability of membership higher than 0.9. We include this information in our likelihood ( lik ), and we discard measurements with a smaller membership probability. As already found in Section attenuation , this kinematic sample comes with a (normalized) level of uncertainty of _m/ 0.22 , which is the smallest in the currently available selection of dSphs.

The symmetric deviations display a clear evolution from positive values of s (and h_4 ) in the center of Fornax, to negative values of s (and h_4 ) at larger radii, with a tentative return towards a more Gaussian profile at the end of the sample`s radial coverage ( 3 half-light radii).

We confirm a mildly declining dispersion profile in Fornax and also note a systematic difference between the major axis and minor axis regions, with the minor axis showing a lower line of sight velocity dispersion. If we were to interpret the result by following the suggestions of both Ge93 and vdMF93 , we would conclude that Fornax shows some degree of tangential anisotropy, at least outside its central regions.

gmw_00005846.name: J0055+0146
gmw_00005846.overview: J0055+0146 is a high-redshift quasar host galaxy [arXiv:1501.07538]. Observations with ALMA have resolved both its continuum and [C II] line emission, revealing a spatial extent of approximately 2.9 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1501.07538].

sources.summary: The paper’s ALMA data detect J0055+0146 in line and continuum, resolve both components, infer its star-formation properties, and derive a host-galaxy dynamical measure from CII.

quotes.quote: CFHQS J005502+014618 (hereafter J0055+0146) and CFHQS J222901+145709 (hereafter J2229+1457) were observed with ALMA on the 28, 29 and 30 November 2013 for Cycle 1 project 2012.1.00676.S. Between 22 and 26 12 m diameter antennae were used. The typical long baselines were 400 m providing similar spatial resolution to our Cycle 0 observations. Observations of the science targets were interleaved with nearby phase calibrators, J0108+0135 and J2232+1143. The amplitude calibrator was Neptune and the bandpass calibrators J2258-2758 and J2148+0657. Total on-source integration times were 4610 s for J0055+0146 and 5490 s for J2229+1457.

Figure fig:linespecj0055 shows the reduced spectrum of J0055+0146 from the two adjacent basebands. The final gap between the bands is only 150 km s ^ 1 and crucially the peak of the \ line is contained within the higher frequency band. The lower frequency band contains only a small amount of the line flux but provides an important constraint on the wings and hence the peak and width for a symmetric line. A single Gaussian plus flat continuum model was fit to the available data using a Markov-Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC). This process shows a good fit for a single Gaussian with FWHM = 359 27 km s ^ 1 .

For J0055 both the continuum and line are resolved with deconvolved source sizes of 0 51 0 13 by 0 35 0 26 at position angle 87 degrees and 0 50 0 14 by 0 18 0 27 at position angle 62 degrees, respectively. At the distance to this quasar the spatial extent of 0.5 is equal to a linear size of 2.9 kpc. We note that the missing data in the red wing of the \ line may cause a bias in the size and inclination if the emission comes from a rotating disk, but there is no evidence for this based on the similarity of the line and continuum sizes.

gmw_00005847.name: KKs57
gmw_00005847.overview: KKs57 is an early-type dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1002.0341]. It is characterized by a relatively metal-poor stellar population and an absence of recent star formation [arXiv:1002.0341].

sources.summary: The paper studies KKs57 as a dwarf spheroidal in the Centaurus A subgroup, finding no recent star formation, little HI, metal-poor populations, and differing stellar-population properties among the target dwarfs.

quotes.quote: The six early--type dwarfs of our sample have a quite wide range of luminosities (see Table infogen ), and their morphological types vary from dwarf spheroidals (KKs57, KKs55, CenN and KK189), to dwarf ellipticals (KK197 and ESO269-66, the latter being a nucleated galaxy). What they have in common is the absence of any recent star formation (as traced by H , or young populations from CMD), nor is there any significant amount of HI detected that would allow for ongoing or future star formation. For example, bouchard07 investigated the gas content in three of our target dwarfs (KK189, KK197 and ESO269-66) and found only upper limits for their HI masses ( <1.6 , <2.9 and <1.0 10^ 5 M _ , respectively).

Just as the complex and different star formation histories seen in our neighbourghood, we see that the metallicity and stellar population properties in our target dwarfs differ from each other (e.g., median metallicity, metallicity spread, fraction of intermediate--age stars). However, it looks like in our sample there are no purely old dwarfs such as Ursa Minor or Draco in the Local Group. Coming to our results, all of the studied dwarfs are relatively metal--poor, as is expected for galaxies of such low luminosities.

gmw_00005848.name: CenN
gmw_00005848.overview: CenN is an early-type dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1002.0341]. It is characterized by a relatively metal-poor stellar population, an absence of recent star formation, and no detected neutral hydrogen [arXiv:1002.0341].

sources.summary: The paper studies CenN as a dwarf spheroidal in the Centaurus A subgroup, finding no recent star formation, little HI, metal-poor populations, and differing stellar-population properties among the target dwarfs.

quotes.quote: The six early--type dwarfs of our sample have a quite wide range of luminosities (see Table infogen ), and their morphological types vary from dwarf spheroidals (KKs57, KKs55, CenN and KK189), to dwarf ellipticals (KK197 and ESO269-66, the latter being a nucleated galaxy). However, it looks like in our sample there are no purely old dwarfs such as Ursa Minor or Draco in the Local Group. Coming to our results, all of the studied dwarfs are relatively metal--poor, as is expected for galaxies of such low luminosities.

gmw_00005849.name: KK189
gmw_00005849.overview: KK189 is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located in the Centaurus A subgroup [arXiv:1002.0341]. It is a metal-poor system that shows no signs of recent star formation and has only upper limits detected for its neutral hydrogen mass [arXiv:1002.0341].

sources.summary: The paper studies KK189 as a dwarf spheroidal in the Centaurus A subgroup, finding no recent star formation, little HI, metal-poor populations, and differing stellar-population properties among the target dwarfs.

quotes.quote: The six early--type dwarfs of our sample have a quite wide range of luminosities (see Table infogen ), and their morphological types vary from dwarf spheroidals (KKs57, KKs55, CenN and KK189), to dwarf ellipticals (KK197 and ESO269-66, the latter being a nucleated galaxy). For example, bouchard07 investigated the gas content in three of our target dwarfs (KK189, KK197 and ESO269-66) and found only upper limits for their HI masses ( <1.6 , <2.9 and <1.0 10^ 5 M _ , respectively).

However, it looks like in our sample there are no purely old dwarfs such as Ursa Minor or Draco in the Local Group. gmw_00005850.name: Hydra 2
gmw_00005850.overview: Hydra 2 is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1510.05539, arXiv:2108.04271] with a radial velocity similar to the leading arm of the Magellanic Stream [arXiv:1506.01021]. It completed at least 80% of its stellar-mass assembly early in its history, with star formation quenching more than 11.5 billion years ago [arXiv:2108.04271].

sources.summary: The paper uses Keck/DEIMOS spectroscopy to confirm Hydra II as a dwarf galaxy, citing unresolved velocity dispersion, marginal metallicity dispersion, the luminosity-metallicity relation, and a radial velocity similar to the Magellanic Stream.

quotes.quote: We present Keck/DEIMOS spectroscopy of stars in the recently discovered Milky Way satellites Hydra II, Pisces II, and Laevens 1 . We measured a velocity dispersion of _ ^ + km s ^ 1 for Pisces II, but we did not resolve the velocity dispersions of Hydra II or Laevens 1 . We marginally resolved the metallicity dispersions of Hydra II and Pisces II but not Laevens 1 . Furthermore, Hydra II and Pisces II obey the luminosity--metallicity relation for Milky Way dwarf galaxies ( Fe/H = and , respectively), whereas Laevens 1 does not ( Fe/H = ). The kinematic and chemical properties suggest that Hydra II and Pisces II are dwarf galaxies, and Laevens 1 is a globular cluster.
Hydra II has a radial velocity v_ helio = km s ^ 1 , similar to the leading arm of the Magellanic stream.

sources.summary: The paper derives the HST-based color-magnitude diagram and star formation history of Hydra 2, finding early stellar-mass assembly and quenching older than 11.5 Gyr within a seven-UFD comparison.

quotes.quote: We present the color-magnitude diagrams and star formation histories (SFHs) of seven ultra-faint dwarf galaxies: Horologium 1, Hydra 2, Phoenix 2, Reticulum 2, Sagittarius 2, Triangulum 2, and Tucana 2, derived from high-precision Hubble Space Telescope photometry. We find that the SFH of each galaxy is consistent with them having created at least 80 of the stellar mass by z 6 . For all galaxies, we find quenching times older than 11.5 Gyr ago, compatible with the scenario in which reionization suppresses the star formation of small dark matter halos. However, our analysis also reveals some differences in the SFHs of candidate Magellanic Cloud satellites, i.e., galaxies that are likely satellites of the Large Magellanic Cloud and that entered the Milky Way potential only recently.

On average, we find _ 90 = 12.06 0.72 Gyr ago for Magellanic satellites and _ 90 = 12.68 0.23 Gyr ago for non-Magellanic satellites (this becomes 12.67 0.41 if we exclude Sag 2); even though they are comparable within the errors, there is a marginal difference between these values (a two-sample KS test of the average distributions gives a p-value of 5.7 10^ 10 ). Indeed, the candidate Magellanic satellites appear to have more prolonged SFHs compared to non-Magellanic ones, possibly due to the fact that they should have resided in low-density environments for most of their lifetimes; however, the uncertainties are too large to reach a definitive conclusion.

sources.summary: The paper measures Hydra II with HST, compares its half-light radii and center to prior DECam and SMASH data, and notes metallicity evidence supporting a dwarf-galaxy nature.

quotes.quote: Hydra II discovered Hya II serendipitously in data from the Survey of the Magellanic Stellar History SMASH; nidever2017 , which used the Dark Energy Camera (DECam) on the Blanco 4m telescope. They reported an a_ h, exp =1 7 ( r_h in their paper), with the caveat that the chip gaps and a bright foreground star near the center of the galaxy added uncertainty to the measurement. The measurement from the DECam data gave an a_ h, exp 1 7 and an a_ h, p 1 5 . Our best-fit a_ h, exp is 2 70^ +0.41 _ 0.38 , and our a_ h, p is 2 13^ +0.42 _ 0.35 , which are within 2 and 1.4 of the values, respectively.

This dispersion paired with the large physical radius more strongly supports the dwarf galaxy nature of Hya II, although does not list Hya II among the spectroscopically confirmed UFDs. More recently, measured the MDF of Hya II from HST imaging and reported an average [ Fe/H ] of 3.08^ +0.11 _ 0.12 dex and a _ Fe/H =0.33^ +0.12 _ 0.13 dex based on 30 stars.

sources.summary: The paper reports an RR Lyrae associated with Hydra II, revising its distance, half-light radius, and absolute magnitude while keeping it in the dwarf-galaxy regime.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of one RR Lyrae star in the ultra--faint satellite galaxy Hydra II based on time series photometry in the g , and i bands obtained with the Dark Energy Camera at Cerro Tololo Inter-American Observatory, Chile. The association of the RR Lyrae star discovered here with Hydra II is clear because is located at 42 from the center of the dwarf, well within its half-light radius of 102 . The RR Lyrae star has a mean magnitude of i = 21.30 0.04 which is too faint to be a field halo star. This magnitude translates to a heliocentric distance of 151 8 kpc for Hydra II; this value is 13 larger than the estimate from the discovery paper based on the average magnitude of several blue horizontal branch star candidates. The new distance implies a slightly larger half-light radius of 76^ +12 _ 10 pc and a brighter absolute magnitude of M_V = -5.1 0.3 , which keeps this object within the realm of the dwarf galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats Hya2 as a possible LMC associate: its orbital pole and velocities agree with predictions, especially for a leading-arm or second-pericenter interpretation.

quotes.quote: Hya2 and Dra2 could be associated with the LMC and merit further attention.

Dra2, Tuc2, Hya2 and Gru1 are also consistent within their measured 1 errors, where the errors in the poles are calculated as standard deviation of 1000 Monte Carlo drawings over the measurement errors in Galactocentric X , Y , Z and V_X , V_Y and V_Z .

Notice that Hya2 is an interesting case. In a first pericenter passage scenario, Hya2 occupies the foremost tip of the leading arm of the stream. Only a few particles are expected in that area of the sky (see Fig. fig:aitoff ), but despite this seemingly low chance of association, their velocities agree well with the observed ones for Hya2, suggesting that association of Hya2 to the LMC might not be quickly ruled out.

sources.summary: The paper uses Kirby classifications for Hydra II, retains six members with mutually consistent motions, and reports close agreement with Kallivayalil proper motion.

quotes.quote: Hydra II We use the catalog of Kirby_15 , which contains binary membership classification. After our cuts we have 6 members, whose motion agree well with each other. Our systemic PM agrees also very well with the determination by Kallivayalil_18 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Hyd2 as a possible LMC association needing follow-up, with low density and partially known orbital parameters making it promising.

quotes.quote: The authors suggest follow up measurements for the confirmation of Dra2 and Hyd2, which have incomplete proper motion information.
In particular some of the brighter `possible' associations already identified, Hyd2 with 1.4 4 and Dra2 with 2.5 3 , might deserve a closer follow up to confirm or rule out their Magellanic origin.

From that perspective, the likely associations for Hyd1 with = 4.7 km/s and Car2 with = 5.9 km/s, both within 100 pc, or the possibly associated Hyd2 with = 6.2 km/s, seem favored compared to the more dense Car3, Hor1, or Dra2 with 8 km/s at 50 pc data from simon2019UFDs .

gmw_00005851.name: Phoenix 2
gmw_00005851.overview: Phoenix 2 is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1909.06308, arXiv:2108.04271] located approximately 100 kiloparsecs away [arXiv:1909.06308] that likely accreted as a satellite of the Large Magellanic Cloud [arXiv:1907.09484, arXiv:1805.07350]. The galaxy completed at least 80 percent of its star formation by redshift z ~ 6 and was quenched more than 11.5 billion years ago [arXiv:2108.04271].

sources.summary: The paper compares Phoenix II half-light radii and magnitude to prior DES and Megacam studies, and cites spectroscopy, metallicity, and mass-segregation evidence supporting a galaxy classification.

quotes.quote: Phoenix II From the DES data for Phe II, reported an azimuthally-averaged r_h of 1 3 ( a_ h, exp 1 7 ), and published an a_ h, p value of 1 2 , although were unable to constrain a position angle or ellipticity. Using the same DES data, measured similar values, with a_ h, exp 1 6 and a_ h, p 1 5 . took follow-up data with Megacam at the Magellan Clay telescope, reaching 2-3 magnitudes deeper, and reported an exponential a_ h, exp 1 5 .

Our a_ h, exp =1 58^ +0.26 _ 0.23 is consistent with the three past literature measurements. Our a_ h, p =1 5^ +0.20 _ 0.17 is also consistent with the past Plummer profile fits. Broadly, our and values are consistent with past measurements, with the exception of .

VLT/FLAMES spectroscopy for Phe II was presented in , who measured a velocity dispersion of 11.0 9.4 km s ^ 1 and an intrinsic metallicity spread of 0.33 dex for the five stars that the authors identified as members. Their assessment of Phe II as a galaxy agreed with the previous suggestion by , who based their classification on its ellipticity and position in the size-luminosity plane.

sources.summary: The paper repeatedly confirms Phe 2 as an LMC satellite, comparing its classification with earlier candidates, proper-motion arguments, and a proposed satellite plane.

quotes.quote: We study the orbits of ultra-faint dwarf galaxies in the combined presence of the Milky Way and LMC and we find 6 dwarfs which were likely accreted with the LMC (Car 2, Car 3, Hor 1, Hyi 1, Phe 2, Ret 2), in addition to the SMC, representing strong evidence of dwarf galaxy group infall. Of their 7 highest confidence candidates ( p_ LMC > 0.7 ), we confirm that Hor 1, Ret 2, and Phe 2 are likely LMC satellites. Of these, we confirm Phe 2 is likely an LMC satellite.

Finally, they also argued that Phe 2 could be an LMC satellite based on their prediction of its proper motion and the existence of an overdensity of stars with that proper motion in Gaia DR2. Our analysis which makes use of the observed proper motion and radial velocity from finds that Phe 2 is a likely LMC satellite. Of the remaining satellites, we confirm that Phe 2 is an LMC satellite, that Tuc 4 has a chance of being an LMC satellite. In addition to the SMC, we identify six dwarf galaxies as being highly likely bound to the Cloud previously: Car 2, Car 3, Hor 1, Hyi 1, Phe 2, and Ret 2.

sources.summary: The paper presents Phoenix II as a strong dwarf-galaxy candidate, finds one RR Lyrae near its center, and derives a distance modulus of 20.01 mag at about 100 kpc.

quotes.quote: Phoenix II is an ultra-faint satellite (M _V -2.7 mag, D _ 84 kpc, _0 19.6 mag; MutluPakdil2018 ) discovered in DES Year 1 by two independent groups Bechtol2015,Koposov2015 . A more recent study by MutluPakdil2018 solved discrepancies in the structural parameters from the previous studies by using deeper photometry from Magellan/MegaCam. The location of this system in the luminosity-half light radius plane makes it a strong candidate to be a dwarf galaxy, supported by spectroscopic measurements.

The ( g- , g ) CMD of Phoenix II from our Goodman data (Fig. fig:phe2_cmd ) shows few HB stars. Of these one is a RRL located at a distance of 2 45 from the center of Phoenix II (see Fig. fig:phe2_rrl_field ).
Thus, the distance modulus of Phoenix II is _0 =20.01 0.08 mag (D _ =100 3 kpc).

sources.summary: The paper derives the HST-based color-magnitude diagram and star formation history of Phoenix 2, finding early stellar-mass assembly and quenching older than 11.5 Gyr within a seven-UFD comparison.

quotes.quote: We present the color-magnitude diagrams and star formation histories (SFHs) of seven ultra-faint dwarf galaxies: Horologium 1, Hydra 2, Phoenix 2, Reticulum 2, Sagittarius 2, Triangulum 2, and Tucana 2, derived from high-precision Hubble Space Telescope photometry. We find that the SFH of each galaxy is consistent with them having created at least 80 of the stellar mass by z 6 . For all galaxies, we find quenching times older than 11.5 Gyr ago, compatible with the scenario in which reionization suppresses the star formation of small dark matter halos. However, our analysis also reveals some differences in the SFHs of candidate Magellanic Cloud satellites, i.e., galaxies that are likely satellites of the Large Magellanic Cloud and that entered the Milky Way potential only recently.

On average, we find _ 90 = 12.06 0.72 Gyr ago for Magellanic satellites and _ 90 = 12.68 0.23 Gyr ago for non-Magellanic satellites (this becomes 12.67 0.41 if we exclude Sag 2); even though they are comparable within the errors, there is a marginal difference between these values (a two-sample KS test of the average distributions gives a p-value of 5.7 10^ 10 ). Indeed, the candidate Magellanic satellites appear to have more prolonged SFHs compared to non-Magellanic ones, possibly due to the fact that they should have resided in low-density environments for most of their lifetimes; however, the uncertainties are too large to reach a definitive conclusion.

sources.summary: The paper predicts proper motions for Phx2, finds a Gaia DR2 overdensity consistent with that prediction, and recommends radial-velocity follow-up.

quotes.quote: For the remaining sample, we use the simulation to predict PMs and radial velocities, finding that Phx2 has an overdensity of stars in DR2 consistent with this PM prediction.
For the case of Phx2 we find a pretty convincing clump of stars that are consistent with its PM prediction, see Figure fig:Phx2 . This would be an interesting target for radial velocity follow-up.

For the remaining sample, we use the simulation to predict proper motions and radial velocities, finding that Phx2 has an overdensity of stars in DR2 consistent with this PM prediction. It would be a good candidate for radial velocity follow up.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Phoenix II high-probability members, finds its proper motion consistent with LMC-satellite accretion models, and agrees with spectroscopy-based measurements.

quotes.quote: Phoenix II has 9 members with high probability, 2 of which are BHBs, and the remaining 7 RGB members are all above the empirical stellar locus and are therefore likely to be metal-poor members. The systemic proper motion of Phoenix II agrees with the accretion models of Sales2017MNRAS.465.1879S, Kallivayalil2018ApJ...867...19K and is candidate for a LMC satellite. Kallivayalil2018ApJ...867...19K starting from their model predictions, used a clustering algorithm to identify 4 stars near Phoenix II with a similar systemic proper motion of ( _ , _ ) = (0.54 0.10, -1.17 0.12) . Our model identifies additional members but it is consistent with their result. Fritz2018arXiv180507350F find six members from their combination of DR2 and VLT/FLAMES spectroscopy (one is considered a possible member).

sources.summary: Phoenix II is the clearest dwarf galaxy in the sample, with resolved velocity dispersion, metallicity spread, and an orbital pole close to the LMC.

quotes.quote: Among the four objects, the only one that we can place firmly in the category of dwarf galaxies is Phoenix II given its resolved large velocity dispersion ( 9.5_ 4.4 ^ +6.8 km/s) and intrinsic metallicity spread (0.33 dex). Also for Columba I we measure a clear metallicity spread. The orbital pole of Phoenix II is well constrained and close to that of the LMC, suggesting a prior association.

The system with the most constrained properties is Phx II; for this object, we resolve its l.o.s. velocity dispersion ( 11.0_ 5.3 ^ +9.4 km/s), for the preferred membership selection; we also find an intrinsic metallicity spread of at least 0.33 _ 0.16 ^ +0.29 dex; these properties suggest its nature as a dwarf galaxy.
Therefore Phx II appears as a promising system for having been a former LMC satellite.

sources.summary: The paper groups Phoenix II with Pictoris I, noting DES discovery, unavailable velocity or metallicity data, large dwarf-like structural parameters, and no significant mass segregation.

quotes.quote: Phoenix II and Pictoris I were discovered by bechtoletal2015 and koposovetal2015 in Dark Energy Survey (DES) data. No velocity or metallicity information is available for these systems. The structural parameters that have been determined for both systems by mutlupakdiletal2018 and munozetal2018 make them larger than normal star clusters and are more typical for dwarf galaxies. Since we also do not detect significant mass segregation in both systems we conclude that they are most likely dwarf galaxies, although given the large relaxation times, a star cluster nature is not completely ruled out either by our data.

gmw_00005852.name: Sagittarius 2
gmw_00005852.overview: Sagittarius 2 is a Milky Way satellite whose classification is debated, with studies identifying it as either an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1805.01839, arXiv:2108.04271] or an old, metal-poor globular cluster [arXiv:2005.05976]. It is a very metal-poor system [arXiv:2005.05976] that completed at least 80 percent of its star formation by redshift z ~ 6 [arXiv:2108.04271].

sources.summary: Sagittarius II lacks public spectroscopy in this study; the paper estimates its proper motion from Gaia astrometry and notes an extended asymmetric VPD distribution and possible outer HB members.

quotes.quote: We present mean absolute proper motion measurements for seven ultra-faint dwarf galaxies orbiting the Milky Way, namely Bo o tes III, Carina II, Grus II, Reticulum II, Sagittarius II, Segue 2 and Tucana IV. For four of these dwarfs our proper motion estimate is the first ever provided. The adopted astrometric data come from the second data release of the Gaia mission. We determine the mean proper motion for each galaxy starting from an initial guess of likely members, based either on radial velocity measurements or using stars on the Horizontal Branch identified in the Gaia ( G_ BP G_ RP , G) colour-magnitude diagram in the field of view towards the UFD.

The UFDs Bo o tes III, Grus II, Sagittarius II and Tucana IV do not have publicly available spectroscopic information. As mentioned in Sect. method we therefore use Horizontal Branch (HB) candidates located within 15 from the centre of each UFD (from mc12 ) to obtain an initial guess on the mean astrometric parameters. HB stars have been chosen because they are easy to detect in the CMD, being bluer than the typical sequences described by the majority of stars located in the respective fields of view. This is clearly demonstrated in Fig. sel_hb , where the CMD regions adopted to select HB stars are marked with a cyan box. To demonstrate the effectiveness of the selection procedure described in Sect. method , Fig. sel_hb shows a snapshot of the last iteration step from the point of view of the CMD. The red filled circles are stars that survived the 2.5 clipping astrometric selection and items 4 ii ) and 4 iii ) of the procedure. However, only those located inside the blue delimited box will be used for the final mean proper motion estimate. In general, the selected members are located on the typical evolutionary sequences characteristic of the stellar populations seen in dwarf galaxies. These sequences are quite well-defined despite the meagre numbers ( 40 50 stars). The fact that the poorly populated red giant branches are also quite faint (the brightest HB is at G 19 ) contributes in spreading them out in G_ BP G_ RP colour. The colour uncertainty at these magnitudes is 0.1 , whereas the measured spread amounts to 0.15 . Therefore we cannot exclude the presence of multiple, possibly chemically heterogeneous sub-populations (in fact UFDs typically show spread in their metallicity distribution, see tolstoy09 ). The distribution in the VPD of the final members of the four UFD galaxies is shown in Fig. vpd_hb with red filled circles. Like for the dwarfs analysed in Sec. wspec , they describe a reasonably well concentrated clump of stars, clearly separated from the bulk of the field population (black dots). It is interesting to note that in the case of Bo o tes III, a third concentration of stars clearly stands out at ( _ , _ ) (-5, -1) : it is made up of stars belonging to the Galactic globular cluster NGC 5466, located very close to the galaxy also on the sky. Their quite different distances from the Sun (with the UFD 30 kpc farther away, ferraro99 ) and kinematics, with Bo o tes III having ( _ , _ ) = ( 1.21 0.13 , 0.92 0.17 ) , corresponding to a difference in velocity of about 100 km s ^ 1 , implies that the two systems are not associated to each other. The spreads in the distributions of members in the VPDs of the four UFDs are consistent with the typical proper motion uncertainties of their stars. This is true even for the case of Sagittarius II, whose distribution seems significantly more extended and asymmetric than for the other systems. This is because for its stars the errors on _ are a factor of 1.5 larger than those on _ . The cyan filled circles in Fig. vpd_hb mark the initial HB candidates, most of them being located well within the region of the final members. Finally, Fig. sky_hb shows the distribution of the selected members on the sky. As in Sect. wspec , only stars represented as black dots and within the red circle have been considered for the final mean proper motion estimate. All the four galaxies show a peak in the concentration of their members within the projected distance cut given by the radius of the red circle. Interestingly however, for some of these (namely Bo o tes III and Tucana IV), the peak of the density distribution seems somewhat offset with respect to the nominal centre (given by the centre of the red circle, and taken from grillmair09 and dw15 , respectively). Moreover all the systems, with maybe the exception of Sagittarius II (which has been proposed to be a globular cluster rather than a UFD, see mp18 ), appear asymmetric and possibly depict an elongated shape. It is beyond the aim of this paper to make a quantitative analysis on these aspects, yet our findings already highlight how powerful can be to better assess the density profiles and centres of systems like the UFDs, which suffer from large amounts of contamination by field stars. The location of all the initially selected candidates HB stars are marked in Fig. sky_hb with cyan empty circles. Few of these HB stars have been lost during the procedure, but this was to be expected given that the initial selection is astrometrically blind. The fact that the actual members all lie well within the adopted projected distance cut from the nominal centre is a further check on the robustness of our procedure. Sagittarius II might show hints of the presence of few HB members outside this distance cut.

sources.summary: The paper reassesses Sagittarius II as a possible dwarf galaxy: earlier data favored a low-mass dark-matter halo, while new spectroscopy finds low velocity and unresolved metallicity dispersions and concludes it is an old metal-poor globular cluster.

quotes.quote: In that context, we present a new spectroscopic study of the faint MW satellite Sagittarius II (Sgr II) discovered by laevens15 , and studied in depth by L20 longeard20 . L20 used deep photometry, multi-object spectroscopy and the metallicity-sensitive, narrow-band photometry provided by the Pristine survey starkenburg17 to refine the structural properties of Sgr II and infer its dynamical and metallicity properties for the first time. Its velocity dispersion ( ^ L20 _ vr = 2.7^ +1.3 _ 1.0 km s ^ 1 ) favoured the existence of a low-mass DM halo and therefore a dwarf galaxy scenario.

We find a low velocity dispersion of _ v ^ SgrII = 1.7 0.5 km s ^ 1 , in agreement with the dispersion of Milky Way globular clusters of similar luminosity. We confirm the very metal-poor nature of the satellite ( [ Fe/H ] _ spectro ^ SgrII = -2.23 0.07 ) and find that the metallicity dispersion of Sgr II is not resolved, reaching only 0.20 at the 95 confidence limit. No star with a metallicity below 2.5 is confidently detected. Therefore, despite the unusually large size of the system ( _h = 35.5 ^ +1.4 _ 1.2 pc), we conclude that Sgr II is an old and metal-poor globular cluster of the Milky Way.

sources.summary: The paper derives the HST-based color-magnitude diagram and star formation history of Sagittarius 2, finding early stellar-mass assembly and quenching older than 11.5 Gyr within a seven-UFD comparison.

quotes.quote: We present the color-magnitude diagrams and star formation histories (SFHs) of seven ultra-faint dwarf galaxies: Horologium 1, Hydra 2, Phoenix 2, Reticulum 2, Sagittarius 2, Triangulum 2, and Tucana 2, derived from high-precision Hubble Space Telescope photometry. We find that the SFH of each galaxy is consistent with them having created at least 80 of the stellar mass by z 6 . For all galaxies, we find quenching times older than 11.5 Gyr ago, compatible with the scenario in which reionization suppresses the star formation of small dark matter halos. However, our analysis also reveals some differences in the SFHs of candidate Magellanic Cloud satellites, i.e., galaxies that are likely satellites of the Large Magellanic Cloud and that entered the Milky Way potential only recently.

On average, we find _ 90 = 12.06 0.72 Gyr ago for Magellanic satellites and _ 90 = 12.68 0.23 Gyr ago for non-Magellanic satellites (this becomes 12.67 0.41 if we exclude Sag 2); even though they are comparable within the errors, there is a marginal difference between these values (a two-sample KS test of the average distributions gives a p-value of 5.7 10^ 10 ). Indeed, the candidate Magellanic satellites appear to have more prolonged SFHs compared to non-Magellanic ones, possibly due to the fact that they should have resided in low-density environments for most of their lifetimes; however, the uncertainties are too large to reach a definitive conclusion.

sources.summary: The paper treats Sagittarius II as a debated dSph candidate and presents its first J-factor analysis for velocity-dependent dark-matter annihilation models.

quotes.quote: We also apply the same analysis to three additional dSphs: Crater II , Hydrus I , and Sagittarius II We note that the identification of Sagittarius II as a dSph versus a star cluster is not yet definite. It has a very compact size and high luminosity compared to what is expected for a dSph . The velocity dispersion is resolved, but the mass-to-light ratio is much lower than other ultrafaint dSphs, and the metallicity dispersion is possibly resolved .
This is the first J factor analysis of Sagittarius II.

gmw_00005853.name: VV 191
gmw_00005853.overview: VV 191 is an occulting galaxy pair consisting of a background elliptical galaxy, VV 191a, and a foreground spiral galaxy, VV 191b [arXiv:2208.14475, arXiv:2403.15619]. The spiral galaxy has a multiple-armed, flocculent dust structure, and the background elliptical galaxy strongly lenses a distant background galaxy [arXiv:2208.14475].

sources.summary: The paper presents VV 191 as a nearly ideal overlapping galaxy pair for dust attenuation studies, also revealing strong lensing by one component in JWST imaging.

quotes.quote: To assess this issue, one of the PEARLS targets is a pair of galaxies with a fortuitous chance alignment on the sky: a face-on spiral galaxy partially backlit by an elliptical galaxy. In such a configuration, the properties of the dust in the foreground spiral galaxy can be measured in an absolute sense, and to very low dust column densities, and in both arm and interarm regions. This target, VV 191, serves as a GTO pilot program to demonstrate the power of \ for future GO surveys that target a larger and more diverse sample of galaxies.

In addition, unknown to us when selecting the target and clearly revealed by the JWST data, one of the components of the galaxy system observed here strongly lenses a distant background galaxy, illustrating multiple configurations and outcomes of overlapping galaxy images at once. Previous work on dust in backlit galaxies has concentrated on grand-design spirals, often 2-armed systems, where application of symmetry to model the foreground galaxy is manageable. VV 191 projects the outer disk against such a bright part of the background galaxy that we can complement earlier work by studying the disk attenuation of a multiple-armed spiral, where the dust lanes have flocculent structure.

VV 191 was initially recognized as an occulting, rather than merging, system during the Galaxy Zoo survey for such pairs GZcatalog . It nearly matches the schematic ideal for analysis of such a pair, with a face-on spiral roughly half-projected against a background E or S0 galaxy (itself nearly circular in projection), and the spiral nearly face-on (photometric axial ratio within 0.15 of circular at all radii). The backlighting near the edge of the disk is so strong that uncertainties in correcting for the spiral's own light barely contribute to the error budget in retrieving the attenuation.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes VV 191 as an ideal overlapping galaxy pair, combining HST/JWST dust maps and GCMS Balmer-line data to compare attenuation and Balmer decrement measurements.

quotes.quote: Here we measure independently both the attenuation and \ of an occulting galaxy pair: VV 191. Attenuation measurements in the visible spectrum (A _ V,stars ) from dust maps derived from the F606W filter of HST and the F090W filter of JWST are matched with spaxel-by-spaxel \ observations from the George and Cynthia Mitchell Spectrograph (GCMS) of the McDonald Observatory. The 0.5 to 0.7 micron bandpass covers the Balmer lines for VV 191.

STARSMOG determined that VV 191 has the ideal configuration: a partially overlapping, bona fide occulting pair, i.e., overlapping on the sky but physically well-separated in distance. According to the VV atlas vorontsov-velyaminov_atlas_1959 , the VV 191 galaxy pair consists of VV 191a, the elliptical background galaxy, and VV 191b, the spiral galaxy located northeast to VV 191a.

gmw_00005854.name: VV 191b
gmw_00005854.overview: VV 191b is a face-on spiral galaxy characterized by an exponential disk profile [arXiv:2403.15619]. In its overlap region with its companion, the galaxy's dust lanes closer to the spiral center dominate the attenuation of ionized gas [arXiv:2403.15619].

sources.summary: The paper treats VV 191b as the foreground face-on spiral whose dust lanes, Balmer decrement, attenuation, redshift, and scale lengths are mapped across the overlap region.

quotes.quote: We present maps and plots of the Balmer lines for the VV 191 galaxy pair and for a specific region highlighting dust lanes for VV 191b in the overlap region. We compute A _ V, HII from \ and plot both quanities against A _ V, stars . Our results show that regions with higher dust content, residing closer to the spiral center, dominate ionized gas attenuation, leading to an overestimation of A _ V, HII by a factor or 2.

VV 191b is a face-on spiral disk, which qualifies as n = 1 S e rsic profile, the exponential profile. This simplifies Equation eq mu scale to just: (R) = _ 0 + 1.086 ( R h The scale length of a galaxy disk is an effective parameter in galaxy morphology in general.

gmw_00005855.name: VV 191a
gmw_00005855.overview: VV 191a is an elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0513 [arXiv:2310.16923]. It acts as a background galaxy in the VV 191 pair, backlighting the dust of a neighboring foreground spiral [arXiv:2403.15619] and hosting a population of massive globular clusters [arXiv:2310.16923].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes VV 191a as a z=0.0513 elliptical galaxy whose JWST/NIRCam data reveal 443 globular cluster candidates with a fairly uniform, unimodal color distribution and massive detected clusters.

quotes.quote: James Webb Space Telescope NIRCam images have revealed 443 globular cluster (GC) candidates around the z=0.0513 elliptical galaxy VV 191a. NIRCam broadband observations are made at 0.9--4.5 m using filters F090W, F150W, F356W, and F444W. Using photometry, the data is analyzed to present color-magnitude diagrams (CMDs) that suggest a fairly uniform population of GCs. Color histograms show a unimodal color distribution that is well fit by a single Gaussian, using color to primarily trace the metallicity.

The model shows that the VV 191a GCs (above the completeness limit) tend to be more massive, reaching upward of 10^7 M_ . It is likely that we are detecting the brightest and most massive members of the GC population while the fainter and less massive GCs are left undetected or below our formal completeness limits.

sources.summary: The paper describes VV 191a as the background elliptical in the VV 191 overlap, extending behind the foreground spiral and backlighting its dust while lacking Balmer-line emission.

quotes.quote: According to the VV atlas vorontsov-velyaminov_atlas_1959 , the VV 191 galaxy pair consists of VV 191a, the elliptical background galaxy, and VV 191b, the spiral galaxy located northeast to VV 191a. Both galaxies are large in the sky. determined the Petrosian radius of the spiral foreground as 12.5" = 12 kpc in the F606W filter. The background elliptical galaxy extends to 1.7 Petrosian radii away from the foreground spiral's center, backlighting dust roughly 20 kpc in radius.

gmw_00005856.name: POX 52
gmw_00005856.overview: POX 52 is a dwarf elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0801.3663, arXiv:0807.1535] that hosts a Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0807.1535, arXiv:astro-ph/0402110] powered by an intermediate-mass black hole [arXiv:2310.20137]. The galaxy features a classical bulge with no detected disk component [arXiv:2310.20137, arXiv:0807.1535] and exhibits strong, rapid X-ray variability [arXiv:0801.3663, arXiv:0807.1535].

sources.summary: The paper describes POX 52 as a dwarf elliptical IMBH AGN with an AGN spectrum, strong X-ray variability, rapid flaring, and optical-to-X-ray properties similar to NLS1 galaxies.

quotes.quote: Another recently discovered AGN with an IMBH is POX 52, a dwarf elliptical galaxy at z=0.021 . It was discovered in an objective-prism search for emission line objects by who also noted its star-like appearance in the Palomar Sky Survey images. Follow-up imaging and spectroscopic observations by revealed a dwarf galaxy with an AGN spectrum. The object was classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy by 1981A&AS...44..229K based on the flux-ratios of narrow emission lines although a weak broad component of the H line was seen with a full width at half maximum, FWHM 840 .

X-ray emission from Pox 52 is also highly variable. The 0.2-10 band light curve shows a trough-to-peak variations by a factor of 10 . There are several rapid variability events e.g., roughly one event in every 10000 , throughout the observations. The count rate is found to increase by a factor 2 in 2500 in these events.

sources.summary: The paper studies POX 52 as a nearby dwarf AGN host with a rapidly growing intermediate-mass black hole, decomposing its SED/images and linking bulge formation to merger history.

quotes.quote: POX 52 is in the nearby Universe of z = 0.021, or D = 93 Mpc, adopting a CDM cosmology with H_0 = 70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _ m = 0.3 , and _ = 0.7 , and is known to host a rapidly growing type-1 IMBH AGN with a BH mass of M_ BH 2 - 4 10^ 5 M_ and an Eddington ratio of _ Edd 0.2--0.5 Tho08 . The mass was derived by the single-epoch method, and the bolometric luminosity necessary for the Eddington ratio was derived by integrating an SED roughly fitted to observed data from the radio to the X-ray. The galaxy, thus, provides us with an invaluable opportunity to reveal both AGN and host-galaxy properties precisely.
