This single-object study is complementary to studies using large samples, given that we reveal the nature of the IMBH AGN in quite detail. We collected multi-wavelength data, including newly obtained broadband X-ray data by NuSTAR and XMM-Newton quasi-simultaneous observations.

Our SED analysis suggests that the SF activity peaked possibly around 7.5 Gyrs ago, corresponding to z 1. As expected from this inference, the old stellar population ( > 10 Myr) with a mass of M^ old _ star 8.3 10^ 8 M_ dominates the entire stellar mass (i.e., M_ star M^ old _ star ), while the mass of young stars is M^ young _ star 10^6 M_ . Related to this, the HST imaging analysis has given us an important insight into the host galaxy by finding that the classical bulge is the important structure.

sources.summary: The paper presents multiwavelength observations of POX 52, confirming a dwarf elliptical Seyfert 1 host, rapid X-ray variability, a radio-quiet-quasar-like SED, and a black hole mass near 2-4e5 solar masses.

quotes.quote: We present new multi-wavelength observations of the dwarf Seyfert 1 galaxy POX 52 in order to investigate the properties of the host galaxy and the active nucleus, and to examine the mass of its black hole, previously estimated to be 10^ 5 . Hubble Space Telescope ACS/HRC images show that the host galaxy has a dwarf elliptical morphology ( M_I= -18.4 mag, \ index n=4.3 ) with no detected disk component or spiral structure, confirming previous results from ground-based imaging. X-ray observations from both \ and \ show strong (factor of 2) variability over timescales as short as 500 s, as well as a dramatic decrease in the absorbing column density over a 9 month period. We attribute this change to a partial covering absorber, with a 94 covering fraction and N_ H = 58^ +8.4 _ 9.2 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 , that moved out of the line of sight in between the \ and \ observations. Combining these data with observations from the VLA, Spitzer , and archival data from 2MASS and GALEX , we examine the spectral energy distribution (SED) of the active nucleus.

Including data from the VLA, Spitzer , GALEX , and 2MASS, we compile an extensive SED that shows POX 52 to be consistent with the radio-quiet template of more massive quasars and estimate a bolometric luminosity of \ = 1.3 10^ 43   \ by incorporating all of the available data. Using a variety of mass estimators, we find a range of black hole mass   (1.8-4.2) 10^ 5 .

sources.summary: The paper describes POX 52 as a dwarf elliptical low-mass AGN with short- and long-timescale X-ray variability and Seyfert-like spectral characteristics.

quotes.quote: Two well-known low-mass AGN are the spiral galaxy NGC 4395 and the dwarf elliptical galaxy POX 52. Both are seen to be highly variable. Thornton08 looked at the spectral and variability properties from observations of POX 52 from XMM-Newton and Chandra , finding variability both on short, 500s, timescales and the 9 month timescale between the two observations. Another study by dewangan08 performed extensive analysis on both NGC 4395 and POX 52. Short timescale variability was seen in both NGC 4395 and POX 52.

Both NGC 4395 and POX 52 exhibited broad spectral characteristics of Seyfert 1 galaxies, but NGC 4395 lacked soft excess emission that is a known characteristic to narrow-line Seyfert 1s (NLS1s). From their examination of the data from XMM-Newton 's OM, were again able to confirm that NGC 4395 and POX 52 resemble NLS1s from their optical-to-X-ray spectral energy distributions. miniutti09 followed with a study of four IMBHs from the original sample of 19 IMBHs by greene04 : GH 1, GH 8, GH12, and GH 14 note that when using the ID from these are GH 11, GH 126, GH 171 and GH 181 Greene07 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies POX 52 as a dwarf Seyfert 1 galaxy with a dwarf elliptical host, broad emission lines, compact morphology, and an inferred intermediate-mass black hole.

quotes.quote: We describe new optical images and spectra of POX 52, a dwarf galaxy with an active nucleus that was originally detected in the POX objective-prism survey. While POX 52 was originally thought to be a Seyfert 2 galaxy, the new data reveal an emission-line spectrum very similar to that of the dwarf Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 4395, with broad components to the permitted line profiles, and we classify POX 52 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy. The host galaxy appears to be a dwarf elliptical, and its brightness profile is best fit by a S ersic model with an index of 3.6 0.2 and a total magnitude of M_V = -17.6 . Applying mass-luminosity-linewidth scaling relations to estimate the black hole mass from the broad H linewidth and nonstellar continuum luminosity, we find M_ BH 1.6 10^5 M_ . The stellar velocity dispersion in the host galaxy, measured from the Ca 2 8498, 8542 \ lines, is 36 5 km s ^ 1 , also suggestive of a black hole mass of order 10^5 M_ .

However, the new images reveal a very different morphology for POX 52. There is no obvious spiral or disklike structure in the images, and none of the images shows any obvious clumpiness such as might be expected from star-forming regions in the host galaxy. As shown in Figure galfitfig , the optical structure of POX 52 is most suggestive of a small elliptical galaxy with a bright nuclear point source.

sources.summary: The paper presents POX 52 as a low-luminosity host with a Seyfert-like nucleus, NGC 4395-like spectrum, low virial black-hole mass, and dwarf-elliptical morphology.

quotes.quote: As first noted by Kunth, Sargent, Bothun (1987), the presence of a Seyfert-like nucleus in POX 52 is unusual because of the low luminosity of the host galaxy. Barth et al. (2004) show that POX 52 bears a close spectroscopic resemblance to NGC 4395. Based on the broad profile of H , these authors derive a virial BH mass of 1.6 5 \ for POX 52, again remarkably close to the value of 1.3 5 \ predicted from the M_ relation given the measured central stellar velocity dispersion of 35 . Deep images reveal POX 52 to be most akin to a dwarf elliptical galaxy, to date an unprecedented morphology for an AGN host galaxy.

gmw_00005857.name: 1FHL J1410.4+7408 B
gmw_00005857.overview: 1FHL J1410.4+7408 B is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1608.05429]. It is probably radio quiet and is the likely counterpart to a Fermi gamma-ray source [arXiv:1608.05429].

sources.summary: The paper classifies the likely counterpart 1FHL J1410.4+7408 B as a BL Lac object, probably radio quiet, and more likely than the alternate NLS1 to explain the gamma-ray source.

quotes.quote: Likewise, the BL Lac object probably associated with 1FHL J1410.4+7408 B also does not show radio emission. It is important to note that, if confirmed, the BL Lac object 1FHL J1410.4+7408 B would be one of the very few radio quiet -ray emitting BL Lac objects identified to date. Similar recent cases can be found in Paggi14 and in Ricci15 . To be conservative, it is thus safe to say that it is still not clear which of the two sources is the actual -ray emitter, or that the two objects are possibly contributing to the total -ray flux detected with Fermi. However, given the above considerations, it is more likely that the counterpart to this 1FHL -ray source is the BL Lac object associated with 1FHL J1410.4+7408 B.

gmw_00005858.name: U0825-05946383
gmw_00005858.overview: U0825-05946383 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z=2.929 [arXiv:1608.05429]. Its optical spectrum displays strong, luminous emission lines, and it hosts a central black hole with an estimated mass of 4.6 billion solar masses [arXiv:1608.05429].

sources.summary: The paper identifies U0825-05946383 as the non-stellar optical source in the 1FHL J1115.0-0701 field, classifying it as a high-redshift quasar with luminous emission lines.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of the other optical source shows strong, luminous emission lines for Ly , NV, SIV, and CIV, at the high redshift value of z=2.929 0.003 ; these characteristics are typical of a high--redshift quasar. However, its potential association with the 1FHL source is not ironclad (see Section 5.3).

We classified the optical counterpart of the X--ray source found within the 1FHL J1115.0-0701 positional uncertainty ellipse as a high--redshift quasar, with z=2.929 0.003 . This value, in turn, allows us to estimate a luminosity distance of 24.7 Gpc, assuming H_0 =70.0 , _ m =0.3 and _ =0.7 . Following Park13 , we measured the flux and width of the CIV emission line together with the flux level of the continuum at 135 nm (rest--frame), both corrected for foreground galactic absorption, to obtain an estimate for the mass of its central black hole. This resulted in 4.6 10^ 9 M _ , which is within the range of expected black hole masses for this kind of AGN Vestergaard06 .

gmw_00005859.name: 2MASX J02051994 0233055
gmw_00005859.overview: The galaxy 2MASX J02051994 0233055 hosts an unobscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2210.11506]. This active nucleus is highly flux-variable and has exhibited luminosity variation over the last decade [arXiv:2210.11506].

sources.summary: The paper finds 2MASX J02051994 0233055 to be an unobscured, strongly flux-variable AGN rather than the heavily obscured candidate selected by the survey criteria.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J02051994 0233055 was determined to be a highly flux-variable, unobscured AGN. This is the first source studied using our criteria to select heavily obscured AGN that was instead discovered to be unobscured due to its strong flux variability. This highlights the importance of simultaneous soft and hard X-ray observations to accurately classify and characterize the heavily obscured AGN population.

Furthermore, half of the sources in our sample (2MASX J02051994 0233055 and 2MASX J04075215 6116126) exhibited significant luminosity variation in the last decade, suggesting that this might be a common feature of AGN.

gmw_00005860.name: 2MASX J04075215 6116126
gmw_00005860.overview: 2MASX J04075215 6116126 is classified as a Compton-thin active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2210.11506]. The source exhibits flux variability over timescales of days and years, while its line-of-sight column density remains constant [arXiv:2210.11506].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 2MASX J04075215 6116126 as a Compton-thin AGN with significant flux variability while its line-of-sight column density remained constant.

quotes.quote: Of the four candidates analyzed, 2MASX J02051994 0233055 was found to be an unobscured (N _ H < 10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) AGN, 2MASX J04075215 6116126 and IC 2227 to be Compton-thin (10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 < N _ H < 10 ^ 24 cm ^ 2 ) AGN, and one, ESO 362 8, was confirmed to be a Compton-thick AGN. Additionally, every source was found to have a statistically significant difference between their line-of-sight and average torus hydrogen column density, further supporting the idea that the obscuring material in AGN is inhomogeneous. Furthermore, half of the sources in our sample (2MASX J02051994 0233055 and 2MASX J04075215 6116126) exhibited significant luminosity variation in the last decade, suggesting that this might be a common feature of AGN.

2MASX J04075215 6116126 displayed significant flux variation ( 50 ) in only five days separating observations. Moreover, the flux varied by a factor of 3 when compared with observations taken 2 years prior. It was confirmed that N _ H,los remained constant during these periods, thus providing another example of an AGN with significant luminosity variation.

gmw_00005861.name: ESO 362 8
gmw_00005861.overview: ESO 362 8 is a confirmed Compton-thick active galactic nucleus located in the local universe [arXiv:2210.11506]. Its extranuclear structure is dominated by a bright emission-line cloud to the northeast and a dimmer cloud to the west-northwest, which may form a pair of ionization cones [arXiv:2112.07084].

sources.summary: The paper confirms ESO 362 8 as a bona fide Compton-thick AGN and uses it with the sample to support clumpy obscuring material around accreting SMBHs.

quotes.quote: Of the four candidates analyzed, 2MASX J02051994 0233055 was found to be an unobscured (N _ H < 10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) AGN, 2MASX J04075215 6116126 and IC 2227 to be Compton-thin (10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 < N _ H < 10 ^ 24 cm ^ 2 ) AGN, and one, ESO 362 8, was confirmed to be a Compton-thick AGN. Additionally, every source was found to have a statistically significant difference between their line-of-sight and average torus hydrogen column density, further supporting the idea that the obscuring material in AGN is inhomogeneous.
Of the 4 sources analyzed, one, ESO 362 8, is confirmed to be a bona-fide CT-AGN. This increases the sample of BAT-detected CT-AGN in the local Universe (z < 0.1) to 35.

sources.summary: ESO 362-G08 is described as having bright off-nuclear emission-line clouds, including a NE cloud with AGN-like BPT ratios and a possible WNW counterpart forming ionization cones.

quotes.quote: Due to its declination, we obtained no new spectroscopic data for ESO 362-G08, but long-slit data crossing the bright off-nuclear clouds were analyzed by , and integral-field spectroscopic data were released as part of the S7 survey S7 . The extranuclear emission-line structure is dominated by a bright cloud to the NE, with internal velocity structure. The S7 data also show a dimmer cloud to the WNW; the two clouds might form parts of a set of ionization cones if their boundaries are defined by the location of otherwise neutral gas. The NE cloud has line ratios putting it firmly in the AGN region of the usual BPT diagrams, as well as He II/H = 0.47 .

gmw_00005862.name: IC 2227
gmw_00005862.overview: IC 2227 is a nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:2210.11506]. It hosts a Compton-thin active galactic nucleus with an inhomogeneous obscuring torus [arXiv:2210.11506].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 2227 as a nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy and Compton-thin AGN whose torus column density differs from the line-of-sight value.

quotes.quote: Of the four candidates analyzed, 2MASX J02051994 0233055 was found to be an unobscured (N _ H < 10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) AGN, 2MASX J04075215 6116126 and IC 2227 to be Compton-thin (10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 < N _ H < 10 ^ 24 cm ^ 2 ) AGN, and one, ESO 362 8, was confirmed to be a Compton-thick AGN. Additionally, every source was found to have a statistically significant difference between their line-of-sight and average torus hydrogen column density, further supporting the idea that the obscuring material in AGN is inhomogeneous.
Both 2MASX J02051994 0233055 and 2MASX J04075215 6116126 are listed as galaxies. Meanwhile, ESO 362 8 and IC 2227 are Seyfert 2 (Sy2) galaxies.

gmw_00005863.name: MD157
gmw_00005863.overview: MD157 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.293 [arXiv:1005.1833]. It is a spectroscopically confirmed member of a protocluster and is classified as an X-ray luminous, emission-line active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1005.1833].

sources.summary: MD157 is described as a z=2.293 protocluster member, an X-ray luminous emission-line AGN, and one of the confirmed protocluster LAE counterparts.

quotes.quote: One of these X-ray detected MD galaxies is a spectroscopically confirmed protocluster member (MD157; z= 2.293); this object has an unobscured 2--10 keV X-ray luminosity of L_ 2-10 keV = 4.03 ^ +0.67 _ 0.58 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 , and is also an emission-line AGN (see Shapley et al. 2005).

Regarding the overlap between the protocluster AGN detected via their X-ray emission and those classified via emission lines in their optical/near-IR spectra, we find that the two X-ray detected BX/MD protocluster galaxies (MD157 and BX116) are both also identified as AGN from their spectral emission lines (Table tab:properties ).

As 88 per cent of the spectroscopic LAEs were found to lie at the protocluster redshift and both MD157 (=BNB10) and BX116 (=BNB3) are confirmed protocluster members, we assume that the third LAE (BNB110) is also a protocluster member.

gmw_00005864.name: BX116
gmw_00005864.overview: BX116 is a bright broad-line quasar located within a high-redshift galaxy overdensity at a redshift of z=2.269 [arXiv:1005.1833]. The galaxy is classified as an active galactic nucleus based on its optical and near-infrared spectral emission lines, and it is detected in X-rays [arXiv:1005.1833].

sources.summary: BX116 is presented as a bright broad-line QSO in the z=2.30 overdensity, with X-ray luminosity, emission-line AGN classification, and LAE association.

quotes.quote: One of the 5 X-ray detected BX galaxies (BX116) is a bright ( R =21.65 ), broad-line QSO (due to broad Ly , N V , Si IV and C IV emission lines in it's spectra) which also has a spectroscopic redshift that places it within the overdensity ( z=2.269 ). This object has an unobscured X-ray luminosity of L_ 2-10 keV = 9.98 ^ +7.12 _ 5.17 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 .

Regarding the overlap between the protocluster AGN detected via their X-ray emission and those classified via emission lines in their optical/near-IR spectra, we find that the two X-ray detected BX/MD protocluster galaxies (MD157 and BX116) are both also identified as AGN from their spectral emission lines (Table tab:properties ).

As 88 per cent of the spectroscopic LAEs were found to lie at the protocluster redshift and both MD157 (=BNB10) and BX116 (=BNB3) are confirmed protocluster members, we assume that the third LAE (BNB110) is also a protocluster member.

gmw_00005865.name: ARC1
gmw_00005865.overview: ARC1 is a strongly lensed Lyman Break Galaxy located at a redshift of 2.911 [arXiv:astro-ph/0404473]. It exhibits a fold-arc morphology with mirror-symmetric knots and an absorption spectrum that lacks active galactic nucleus signatures [arXiv:astro-ph/0404473].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ARC1 as a strongly lensed Lyman Break Galaxy at z=2.911, with fold-arc morphology, an absorbed spectrum, and a possible contribution to sub-millimeter emission.

quotes.quote: ARC1 appears to contain knots surrounded by diffuse emission which were not previously resolved from ground-based imaging. These knots display a mirror symmetry, arguing that ARC1 corresponds to a fold-arc (two images merging together).

The derived redshift for ARC1 (Fig. fig:spectra ) is 2.911 0.003 based on the strongest interstellar absorption lines: Si ii 1260.42 , O i 1302.17 \ and C ii 1334.5 \ as well as C iv 1548.19 and 1550.77 in absorption. There is also a hint of the triple Si vi lines at 1397 \ and the Si ii 1526.70 \ line. The ARC1 spectrum lacks AGN signatures sometimes found in the spectra of radio selected sub-mm galaxies. Moreover, it appears to show Ly in absorption, a feature seen in only about 20 of the spectroscopic survey of 100 sub-mm galaxies conducted by Chapman et al. (2004).

Given that the unmagnified sub-mm flux would be 0.1-0.2 mJy if the LBG is the host, then it is entirely possible that we have simply detected an ordinary LBG producing stars at a rate of a few solar masses per year.

gmw_00005866.name: KiDS 1042+0023
gmw_00005866.overview: KiDS 1042+0023 is a confirmed gravitationally lensed quasar system [arXiv:1805.12436]. It features two quasar spectra at the same source redshift positioned on either side of a central red lens galaxy [arXiv:1805.12436].

sources.summary: The paper reports KiDS 1042+0023 as the first confirmed KiDS lensed quasar, with two quasar spectra at the same redshift and a lens between the images.

quotes.quote: Of these, KiDS 1042+0023 is, to our knowledge, the first confirmed lensed quasar from KiDS, exhibiting two quasar spectra at the same source redshift at either sides of a red galaxy, with uniform flux-ratio f 1.25 over the wavelength range 0.45 m < <0.75 m .

The spectra of KiDS1042+0023(R.A.=10:42:37.255, Dec.=00:23:02.064), although of very low signal-to-noise (S/N 3-5 per pixel) show features typical of lensed quasars, confirming the very first lensed QSO discovered in the KiDS survey. We extracted a 1D spectrum at the position of the two multiple images as well as a spectrum in the middle, where the lens lies The separation of traces, based on direct modeling of the spatial profiles, fully accounts for cross-contamination and performs well even in very unfavourable seeing conditions (see e.g. Agnello18 ). . Despite the very poor S/N, the presence of two quasar spectra at the same source redshift is clear, with uniform flux-ratio f 1.25 over 0.45 m < <0.75 m .
we have confirmed the first of these high-graded new KiDS candidates (KiDS 1042+0023) in a spectroscopic run.

gmw_00005867.name: Mrk1089
gmw_00005867.overview: Mrk1089 is a dwarf galaxy that exhibits evidence of merging activity [arXiv:2306.10433]. Its global [CII] line profile is best described by a three-component model consisting of two narrow components and one broad component [arXiv:2306.10433].

sources.summary: The paper gives Mrk1089 a three-component [CII] model, linking the better fit to merger evidence and showing it as an example of complex line profiles.

quotes.quote: We found that, for three galaxies (i.e., Haro3, Mrk1089, and UM448), a three-Gaussian fit (with two narrow and one broad components) provided better residuals than obtained from the two-Gaussian fit, improving the modeling of their global [ CII ] profiles.

As the goodness of this fit was better than the double-Gaussian one for all the three sources and given the evidence of mergers, for the rest of the paper we use the parameters from the three-component modeling for Haro3, Mrk1089, and UM448.

Finally, the right panel shows the case of one of the three galaxies of our sample (Mrk1089) for which we found that a three-component modeling was a better description of their [ CII ] line profiles. As stated in Sect. subsec:individual_out , previous studies found evidence for possible merging activity in these objects. Following them, we fitted their spectra with two narrow and one broad components, resulting in better residuals than obtained with a double Gaussian fit only.

gmw_00005868.name: NGC 1032
gmw_00005868.overview: NGC 1032 is an isolated, edge-on early-type spiral or S0/a galaxy [arXiv:0906.2003, arXiv:2202.03449] that shows no signs of interaction [arXiv:0906.2003]. It is a gas-poor system [arXiv:1709.00457] with a diffuse thin disc [arXiv:1709.00457], low star-formation activity [arXiv:0906.2003], and a large bulge [arXiv:2202.03449].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1032 as a gas-poor outlier with an unusually large thin-disc scale height, diffuse thin disc, and likely merger-driven heating because it is isolated.

quotes.quote: The two largest scale-heights for a thin disc correspond to gas-poor galaxies, namely NGC 1032 and NGC 4370. The scale-heights of these two galaxies are far too large to be moved close to the linear fit by accounting for v_ uncertainties. This suggests that part of the large scatter seen for gas-poor galaxies in the plots in Sect.  masses and in this subsection might have a physical origin. Visually, the thin disc of NGC 1032 is very diffuse, whereas that of NGC 4370 is barely distinguishable from the thick disc (although the distinction is much clearer in the surface-brightness profile).

This and the fact that the seven most extreme outliers are on the positive side in Fig.  residual suggests that some discs of gas-poor galaxies may have been dynamically heated, a process which is more efficient in gas-poor galaxies MOS10 . NGC 4370 is in the Virgo cluster, so harassment is a viable heating mechanism there. NGC 1032 is isolated TU15 so heating would have been caused by the merger with a companion.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1032 as SN 2005E’s isolated edge-on early-type spiral host, emphasizing the supernova’s remote halo location and the galaxy’s old, low-star-formation environment.

quotes.quote: The SN position is 22.9 kpc (projected) from the centre and 11.3 kpc above the disk of its edge-on host galaxy, NGC 1032 (Fig. 1), which is itself at a distance of 34 Mpc. NGC 1032 is an isolated galaxy showing no signs of interaction, with the closest small satellite galaxy found at a distance >120 kpc in projection.

Supplementary Information accompanies the paper on www.nature.com/nature Figure 1 : The environment of SN 2005E (technical details about the observations can be found in SI, Section 1). (a) NGC 1032, the host galaxy of SN 2005E, as observed by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS), prior to the SN explosion. The galaxy is an isolated, edge-on, early-type spiral galaxy, showing no signs of star-formation activity, warping, or interaction. Its luminosity is dominated by the cumulative contribution of a multitude of low-mass old stars (yellow light in this image).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1032 as an S0/a Ca-rich transient host with large bulge light, low specific star formation, and a red-spiral-like early star-formation history.

quotes.quote: NGC 1032 (SN2005E) is an S0/a galaxy that is in the transition period between E and Sa galaxies on the stage sequence, while NGC 2639 (PTF10hcw) is an SAa galaxy. These two galaxies produce large bulge light and are considered early-type systems along the Hubble sequence with strongly decreasing SFR, suggesting a dearth of new stars within the galaxies, resulting in a small sSFR Sandage1986 . also found that massive red spirals such as NGC 2639 and NGC 1032 and massive elliptical galaxies have very similar SFHs. This means that they have only experienced one major star formation episode early on (and possibly fast quenching).

gmw_00005869.name: SDSS J1427+2930
gmw_00005869.overview: SDSS J1427+2930 is a quasar and active galactic nucleus that is classified as a possible changing-look AGN [arXiv:2404.10245]. It transitioned from a type 1.2 to a type 1 state and exhibited a flare that may be a tidal disruption event [arXiv:2404.10245].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J1427+2930 as a possible CL-AGN whose optical flare may be an AGN flare or TDE-like event, with complex light-curve and spectral evidence.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:specType indicates the quasar SDSS J1427+2930 is a possible CL-AGN that appears to have transitioned from type 1.2 to type 1 between MJD 53917 and MJD 59054. Years ago, it was reported as an SMBH-related flare (AT2019brs) by Folded Low Order whYte-pupil Double-dispersed Spectrograph North (FLOYDS-N) follow-up 2019TNSTR.366....1D, 2019TNSAN...4....1A . Later, 2021ApJ...920...56F further studied its multi-epoch spectra and estimated its SMBH mass of 10^ 8.2 M_ from the V band absolute magnitude of its host galaxy.

The MIR echos are seen in some TDE candidates 2016ApJ...828L..14J, 2021ARA&A..59...21G which indicates that SDSS J1427+2930 might be a TDE in AGN. Besides, 2021ApJ...920...56F observed a broad He ii emission ( FWHM 6000 km s^ 1 ) at 133 days after the peak luminosity. As shown in Figure fig:1427spec , the He ii emission also exists although the GTC spectra are observed at around 600 days after the peak, which indicates a possible TDE scenario.

gmw_00005870.name: SDSS J1420+3757
gmw_00005870.overview: SDSS J1420+3757 is a quasar and active galactic nucleus candidate that has undergone a brightness change of over one magnitude [arXiv:2404.10245]. It is classified as a changing-look candidate, transitioning from a Type 1 to a Type 1.2 spectral classification [arXiv:2404.10245].

sources.summary: The paper classifies SDSS J1420+3757 as a CL-AGN candidate whose luminosity, broad-line, Eddington-ratio, and Mg II changes favor accretion-rate variability.

quotes.quote: As shown in Figure fig:specType , we recognize SDSS J1420+3757 as a CL-AGN candidate by its spectral type change. Under the classification criterion in 1992MNRAS.257..677W , it changes from Type 1 to Type 1.2. Figure fig:J1420_results shows the two epochs' spectra and the band light curve. This quasar has been undergoing a dramatic luminosity change in the recent few years which dims its brightness over 1 magnitude and then re-bright.

A direct feature is that the Eddington ratio decreases by about 0.5 dex during the two epochs. Another property is the changes in Mg ii emission line, whose flux also decreases with the decreasing continuum brightness. Besides, Figure fig:baldwineffect (b) shows that the change in Mg ii EW follows the expectation by the Baldwin effect nicely. These features are seen in the CL-AGNs that are selected by the Mg ii variability 2019ApJ...883L..44G, 2020ApJ...888...58G .

gmw_00005871.name: UGCA 105
gmw_00005871.overview: UGCA 105 is an isolated dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:1311.4392]. It features a fragmented neutral hydrogen disk that is more extended than its optical disk, exhibiting ordered rotation, a moderate warp, and kinematically anomalous gas [arXiv:1311.4392].

sources.summary: The paper presents WSRT H I observations of the isolated dwarf irregular UGCA 105, finding an extended fragmented disk, ordered rotation, a warp, anomalous gas, and inflow compatible with accretion or fountain backflow.

quotes.quote: We present 21 cm line observations of the isolated nearby dwarf irregular galaxy UGCA 105, taken with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope (WSRT), and analyse the geometry of its neutral hydrogen ( H i ) disk and its kinematics. The galaxy shows a fragmented H i distribution. It is more extended than the optical disk, and hence allows one to determine its kinematics out to very large galacto-centric distances. The H i kinematics and morphology are well-ordered and symmetric for an irregular galaxy. The H i is sufficiently extended to observe a substantial amount of differential rotation.

Moreover, UGCA 105 shows strong signatures for the presence of a kinematically anomalous gas component. Performing tilted-ring modelling by use of the least-squares fitting routine TiRiFiC , we found that the H i disk of UGCA 105 has a moderately warped and diffuse outermost part. Probing a wide range of parameter combinations, we succeeded in modelling the data cube as a disk with a strong vertical gradient in rotation velocity ( 60 km s^ 1 kpc^ 1 ), as well as vertically increasing inwards motion ( 70 km s^ 1 kpc^ 1 ) within the radius of the stellar disk.

gmw_00005872.name: AGC198606
gmw_00005872.overview: AGC198606 is a neutral hydrogen cloud located near the dwarf galaxy Leo T [arXiv:1412.0286]. It exhibits ordered motion with a smooth morphology [arXiv:1412.0286], and it may possess an extremely low-mass optical stellar counterpart [arXiv:1508.02730].

sources.summary: The paper studies AGC198606 as an HI cloud or possible gas-bearing minihalo near Leo T, with ordered HI motion and no detected optical counterpart.

quotes.quote: We present neutral hydrogen (HI) imaging observations with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope of AGC198606, an HI cloud discovered in the ALFALFA survey. This object is of particular note as it is located 16 \ and 1^ .2 from the gas-bearing ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Leo T while having a similar HI linewidth and approximately twice the flux density. The HI imaging observations reveal a smooth, undisturbed HI morphology with a full extent of 23 16 \ at the 5 10^ 18 atoms cm ^ 2 level. The velocity field of AGC198606 shows ordered motion with a gradient of 25 \ across 20 . The global velocity dispersion is 9.3 \ with no evidence for a narrow spectral component.

No optical counterpart to AGC198606 is detected. The distance to AGC198606 is unknown, and we consider several different scenarios: physical association with Leo T, a minihalo at a distance of 150 kpc based on the models of 2013ApJ...777..119F , and a cloud in the Galactic halo. At a distance of 420 kpc, AGC198606 would have an HI mass of 6.2 10^5 , an HI radius of 1.4 kpc, and a dynamical mass within the HI extent of 1.5 10^8 .

sources.summary: The paper reports a possible optical counterpart to AGC198606, estimates its distance and luminosity, and argues it may be an extremely low-mass dwarf galaxy associated with an H I cloud.

quotes.quote: Applying our method to the AGC198606 imaging yields a possible detection (92 confidence) of an optical counterpart located 2.5 arc minutes away from the centroid of AGC198606's HI distribution but still within the HI disk. We use the optical data to estimate a distance to the stellar counterpart between 373 and 393 kpc, with an absolute magnitude of M_i = -4.67 0.09 . Combining the WIYN data with our previous estimate of the HI mass of AGC198606 from WSRT imaging yields an HI-to-stellar mass ratio of 45 110.

The stellar mass estimates for AGC 198606 are at the lower end of this range (10 ^ 3.7 10 ^ 4.1 M _ ). Simulations by br11 predict the existence of isolated ultra-faint dwarfs with similar properties. It is possible that AGC 198606 represents exactly such a system as predicted by these simulations. To put the putative optical counterpart of AGC 198606 into context, we compare its properties with several other dwarf galaxies in Table DGprops . We include two gas-rich dwarfs (Leo T and Leo P) as well as three ultra-faint dwarfs (UFDs) that are gas-free companions to the Milky Way (Segue 1, Willman 1, and Segue 2). The characteristics for Leo P come from rhode13 and leop4 , while those for the other galaxies are taken from mcconnachie12 . lrrrrrr Properties of Dwarf Galaxies Galaxy & Distance & M_V & r_e & M_ HI & M_ & M_ HI /M_ ``Friend of Leo T'' (AGC198606) & 378 kpc & -4.7 to -3.7 & & 5.0 10^5 & 4.6 - 12.0 10^3 & 42 to 110 Leo T & 409 to 420 kpc & -8.0 & 120 pc & 2.8 10^5 & 2 10^5 & 1.4 Leo P & 1720 kpc & -9.4 & & 9.3 10^5 & 5.7 10^5 & 1.6 Willman 1 & 38 kpc & -2.7 & 25 pc & & & Segue 1 & 23 kpc & -1.5 & 29 pc & & & Segue 2 & 35 kpc & -2.5 & 35 pc & & & Fill in appropriate citations here If it is indeed real, the optical counterpart of AGC 198606 moves the detection of dwarf galaxies associated with UCHVCs into a new regime. The first discovery of an optical counterpart associated with a bona fide UCHVC was that of Leo P leop1,rhode13,leop3,leop4 , but Leo P is both substantially further away (1.73 Mpc) and larger (M _V = 9.4, M _ HI = 9.3 10 ^5 M ) than AGC 198606. While the properties of Leo P are extreme (e.g., the lowest mass galaxy with current star formation, an abundance measurement consistent with the lowest metallicity systems known), AGC 198606 is even more extreme.

gmw_00005873.name: 2MASS J13260399+7023462
gmw_00005873.overview: 2MASS J13260399+7023462 is a highly luminous quasi-stellar object located at a redshift of z=2.89 [arXiv:2006.11915]. Follow-up observations with the Hubble Space Telescope confirm that this bright source is not gravitationally lensed [arXiv:2006.11915].

sources.summary: The paper reports a very bright z=2.89 QSO selected from Gaia and WISE, estimates extreme luminosity and black-hole properties, and finds no Hubble evidence for lensing.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery and confirmation of a bright quasi-stellar object (QSO), , at z= . This QSO is the first spectroscopically confirmed candidate from an ongoing search using the combination of Gaia and WISE photometry to identify bright QSOs at z>2 , the redshift regime for which the Lyman- forest is accessible with ground-based facilities. With a Gaia apparent magnitude G=16.07 , is one of the brightest QSOs known at z>2 , with only 15 currently known brighter QSOs. Given its inferred M_ 1450,AB magnitude and redshift, it is among the most luminous objects in the Universe; the inferred black hole mass and corresponding Eddington ratio are and , respectively. Follow-up Hubble observations confirm it is not gravitationally lensed.

gmw_00005874.name: MKN 573
gmw_00005874.overview: MKN 573 is an SAB0 type galaxy located approximately 72 Mpc away [arXiv:2101.11625]. It hosts a double radio source and exhibits extended, biconical soft X-ray emission on kiloparsec scales [arXiv:2101.11625].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MKN 573 as an SAB0 Compton-thick AGN with a double radio source, previously known biconical soft X-ray emission, and measured cone/cross-cone X-ray excesses.

quotes.quote: MKN 573 MKN 573 is an SAB0 type galaxy at RA=01:43:57.80 (25.991 ), Dec=+02:20:59.65 (2.350 ), and z=0.0172 (D _ lum 72 Mpc), with a double radio source ( Nag99 ).
MKN 573 has been previously shown to have extended, biconical soft X-ray emission on kpc scales ( Gon10,Pag12 ).
The total excess fraction for MKN 573 in 0.3-8.0 keV is 55.3 1.2 in the cone region, and 24.2 0.9 in the cross-cone region, which is consistent with the observed azimuthal dependence.

gmw_00005875.name: NGC 4789
gmw_00005875.overview: NGC 4789 is an elliptical [arXiv:1305.7228] or lenticular [arXiv:2207.06624] radio galaxy located on the periphery of the Coma Cluster [arXiv:2011.08856]. It features a narrow-angle-tail structure [arXiv:1305.7228, arXiv:2207.06624] with bent, collimated radio jets [arXiv:1305.7228] that connect to diffuse relic emission [arXiv:2203.01958].

sources.summary: The paper images NGC4789 as a NAT radio galaxy whose bent jets connect to Coma relic emission, show substructure and spectral ageing, and likely provide seed electrons for shock reacceleration.

quotes.quote: The diffuse emission connecting the relic to the NAT radio galaxy NGC4789 is imaged here with unprecedented resolution, that allows us to detect substructures in its surface brightness distribution. In particular, the bent jets of NGC4789 do not blend smoothly into the diffuse emission, as observed in other cases of radio galaxies nearby relics Bonafede14b, vanWeeren17_Nature, Stuardi19 , and two discontinuities between the endpoint of the jets and the diffuse emissions are detected. At the centre of the diffuse source region, there is a bright transverse bar. Such a bar has been detected in other bent tails (e.g. in Abell 2443, Clarke13 , and recently in the Shapley supercluster Venturi22 ) and are predicted by simulations of interacting AGN tails and shocks Nolting19 . The length of this source, measured from the relic's edge down to the endpoints of the jets, is 10 arcmin, corresponding to 280 kpc at the Coma redshift.

Overall, the spectral trend detected along the tail of NCG4789 and in the relic-NAT connection is consistent with AGN particle ageing. NGC4789 would be moving towards the SW of the cluster injecting particles into the ICM. Thus more recently injected particles are closer to the AGN core than the older ones that have been left behind.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4789 as an elliptical Coma-region radio galaxy with a narrow-angle-tail structure, collimated jets, and bending radio emission southwest of the relic.

quotes.quote: The radio emission of NGC 4789 is associated with an elliptical galaxy with an apparent optical diameter of 1 ' .7 located at redshift z 0.028 (De Vacoulers et al. 1976). It lies at 1.5^ from the cluster centre, South-West of the Coma relic. The radio source is characterised by a Narrow Angle Tail (NAT) structure. In our high-resolution images the source shows two symmetric and collimated jets that propagate linearly from the centre for 35'' in the SE- NW direction (see Fig. fig:20_RM ). Then, the jets start bending toward North-East up to a linear distance of 130'' from the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4789 as a narrow-angle-tailed radio galaxy with a long low-frequency tail and a 206.5 kpc projected radio extent.

quotes.quote: 2: NGC 4789 The optical host is type SA0 KentandGunn . The radio morphologies at both bands show the source to be a narrow-angle-tailed radio galaxy. We barely detect the extended source, 1253 + 275 in our high angular resolution image (see Fig. fig:f1-b3 ). Instead, the tailed radio source at the southwest is seen clearly in our low-resolution image (Fig. fig:n4789-xray ). The largest projected linear extent of the NGC 4789 galaxy in the 250--500 MHz band image

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4789 as a NAT radio source in Coma whose velocity and bent-jet morphology complicate its proposed connection to the radio relic.

quotes.quote: This poses a problem for the association between the radio relic and the NAT radio source NGC 4789, which is a possible source of seed relativistic electrons (En lin et al. 1998). Given its radial velocity of 8365 km/s and the morphology of the bent jet, NGC 4789 is apparently on the back side of the cluster (moving away), while the wall of galaxies, if associated with the NGC 4839 group, must be in front of the cluster (moving in). If the relic is indeed associated with the infalling wall of galaxies, then it would explain why the models of En lin et al. (1998) underestimated its fractional polarization; we are viewing the relic almost edge on.

sources.summary: The paper places radio galaxy NGC 4789 on Coma's periphery and links its tails and southwestward motion to diffuse bridge, relic, and stream-like radio emission.

quotes.quote: The group NGC 4839 and the radio-galaxy NGC 4789 are located at the periphery of Coma, towards A1367, and close to the filament of galaxies and groups that connects the two clusters.

The model components corresponding to NGC 48im39 and NGC 4789 have been excluded from the subtraction, as the emission from those tails merges with the diffuse emission of the bridge and of the relic.
These streams could mark regions of enhanced magnetic field or non-thermal plasma filaments resulting from the motion of the source NGC 4789 towards South-West.

gmw_00005876.overview: The galaxy 8084-3702 is a starburst-selected post-merger system [arXiv:2208.06426]. It exhibits an excess of molecular gas across its surface and experiences fuel-driven enhancements in star formation [arXiv:2208.06426].

sources.summary: The paper describes 8084-3702 as a starburst-selected post-merger with excess molecular gas across its surface and fuel-driven enhanced star formation.

quotes.quote: We note that three post-mergers (8084-3702, 8156-3701, 8615-3703) and one pair (8450-6102) were selected from the starburst sample as part of the original ALMaQUEST (ALMA program 2018.1.00541.S, PI: Ellison), which could bias our merger sample towards strongly star-forming galaxies.

Post-merger galaxies 8615-3703 and 8084-3702 have an excess of molecular gas across the galaxy's surface, rather than a central concentration. Yet that is not a universal scenario; within our sample, 8156-3701 has one of the strongest enhancements in SFR, but has suppressed across the galaxy.
Despite the diversity demonstrated in Table 2, it is interesting to note that 2 of the 3 post-mergers with a global SFR >0.5 are fuel driven enhancements (9194-3702, 8084-3702).

gmw_00005877.overview: The galaxy 8085-12701 is a merger system that exhibits elevated scatter in its resolved Kennicutt-Schmidt relation [arXiv:2208.06426]. It features many spaxels located above the star-forming main sequence, indicating an efficiency-driven enhancement of star formation [arXiv:2208.06426].

sources.summary: The paper presents 8085-12701 as a merger with many spaxels above the resolved star-forming main sequence, elevated rKS scatter, and efficiency-driven enhancement.

quotes.quote: Many mergers show a large population of spaxels above the star-forming main sequence, as is expected if mergers trigger star formation, such as 8156-3701 (1st row, 5th column), 8615-3703 (2nd row, 2nd column), and 8085-12701 (5th row, 1st column).

Many post-mergers and pairs have overall greater scatter in the rKS than the non-interacting sample (see 8615-3703 (2nd row, 2nd column), 8085-12701 (5th row, 1st column), 8083-12703 (5th row, 2nd column)), looking ``puffier''; a similar effect as was seen in .

There are cases of both post-merger and pair galaxies that are indisputably driven by a single mechanism, such as 9194-3702 (fuel driven) or 8085-12701 (efficiency driven), but for the majority the dominant star formation driver is less clear.

gmw_00005878.overview: The galaxy 8156-3701 is a starburst-selected post-merger system [arXiv:2208.06426]. It exhibits a strong enhancement in star formation rate despite having suppressed molecular gas across the galaxy [arXiv:2208.06426].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 8156-3701 as a starburst-selected post-merger with strong SFR enhancement, suppressed molecular gas, and SFE-dominated behavior out to two effective radii.

quotes.quote: Many mergers show a large population of spaxels above the star-forming main sequence, as is expected if mergers trigger star formation, such as 8156-3701 (1st row, 5th column), 8615-3703 (2nd row, 2nd column), and 8085-12701 (5th row, 1st column). We note that three post-mergers (8084-3702, 8156-3701, 8615-3703) and one pair (8450-6102) were selected from the starburst sample as part of the original ALMaQUEST (ALMA program 2018.1.00541.S, PI: Ellison), which could bias our merger sample towards strongly star-forming galaxies.
Yet that is not a universal scenario; within our sample, 8156-3701 has one of the strongest enhancements in SFR, but has suppressed across the galaxy.
8156-3701 (a post-merger with fuel fraction=0.04) has >0.5 out to two effective radii, powered by predominantly SFE.

gmw_00005879.overview: The galaxy 8615-3703 is a starburst-selected post-merger system [arXiv:2208.06426]. It exhibits widespread excess molecular gas across its surface and contains a large population of spaxels with enhanced star formation [arXiv:2208.06426].

sources.summary: The paper describes 8615-3703 as a starburst-selected post-merger with enhanced star-forming spaxels, greater rKS scatter, and widespread excess molecular gas.

quotes.quote: Many mergers show a large population of spaxels above the star-forming main sequence, as is expected if mergers trigger star formation, such as 8156-3701 (1st row, 5th column), 8615-3703 (2nd row, 2nd column), and 8085-12701 (5th row, 1st column). We note that three post-mergers (8084-3702, 8156-3701, 8615-3703) and one pair (8450-6102) were selected from the starburst sample as part of the original ALMaQUEST (ALMA program 2018.1.00541.S, PI: Ellison), which could bias our merger sample towards strongly star-forming galaxies.

Many post-mergers and pairs have overall greater scatter in the rKS than the non-interacting sample (see 8615-3703 (2nd row, 2nd column), 8085-12701 (5th row, 1st column), 8083-12703 (5th row, 2nd column)), looking ``puffier''; a similar effect as was seen in .
Post-merger galaxies 8615-3703 and 8084-3702 have an excess of molecular gas across the galaxy's surface, rather than a central concentration.

gmw_00005880.overview: The galaxy 9194-3702 is a merger [arXiv:2208.06426] that exhibits a disturbed optical morphology [arXiv:2210.12199] and a central post-starburst structure [arXiv:2210.12199]. Its central region is dominated by active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2210.12199], with off-center spaxels that are consistent with AGN-driven shocks [arXiv:2210.12199].

sources.summary: 9194-3702 is treated as a central post-starburst by PCA maps, with disturbed optical morphology, central AGN/composite ionization, and off-center shock-like AGN spaxels.

quotes.quote: We compare our spaxel classification maps to the ring and central PSB classifications made by chen_post-starburst_2019 , and find consistent determinations by visual inspection with the exceptions of 8080-3704 and 9194-3702, where the PCA classification shows a central post-starburst morphology, but they are classified as rPSBs in chen_post-starburst_2019 . At least four of the PSBs with extended gas appear to be tidally disturbed in the optical SDSS imaging: 8083-12703, 8655-3701, 9194-3702, and 9088-9102. Finally, the remaining five galaxies have central regions dominated by BPT AGN or composite spaxels and WHAN strong AGN spaxels: 8655-3701, 8982-6104, 8080-3704, 9088-9102, and 9194-3702.

Three of these central AGN galaxies also have isolated islands of off-center WHAN strong AGN spaxels: 8982-6104, 9088-9102, and 9194-3702, which again may be ionized from shocks. Three other galaxies have central AGN with separated off-center regions of AGN spaxels, consistent with AGN driven shocks: 8982-6104, 9088-9102, and 9194-3702.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes 9194-3702 as a merger with suppressed rSFMS behavior and AGN spaxels, enhanced central molecular gas, and fuel-driven star formation.

quotes.quote: Yet many spaxels and at times entire mergers are also below the star-forming main sequence, such as 9194-3702 (1st row, 1st column), 7975-6104 (3rd row, 3rd column), and 8078-12703 (4th row, 4th column). In particular, both galaxies with concentrations of ``AGN'' spaxels as defined by the BPT diagram tend to lie below the non-interacting rSFMS (see 9194-3702 (1st row, 1st column) and 9195-3703 (3rd row, 2nd column)), as has been observed previously with global quantities Ellison2016TheGalaxies,Leslie2016QuenchingSequence,Mcpartland2019DissectingUniverse .
Rather the AGN spaxels in the merger sample are comparable to star-forming isolated spaxels, or even enhanced in the case of 9194-3702 (1st row, 1st column) and 9195-3703 (3rd row, 2nd column).

There are cases of both post-merger and pair galaxies that are indisputably driven by a single mechanism, such as 9194-3702 (fuel driven) or 8085-12701 (efficiency driven), but for the majority the dominant star formation driver is less clear.

gmw_00005881.name: Spock
gmw_00005881.overview: Spock is a lensed galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1 [arXiv:2312.11603]. It is currently crossing a cluster caustic, resulting in numerous detected microlensing events of highly magnified stars [arXiv:2312.11603].

sources.summary: Spock is described as a z ~ 1 caustic-crossing lensed galaxy with numerous HST and JWST microlensing events from highly magnified stars.

quotes.quote: These events are mostly due to the existence of two galaxies (dubbed Warhol and Spock) at z 1 that are crossing the cluster caustic. One of these events was studied in detail by and corresponds to a double star being magnified by a millilens with mass smaller than 2.5 10^6 M _ , making it the smallest structure whose mass has been estimated through gravitational lensing above z=0.3 , and with implications for dark matter (DM) models. Earlier HST observations have revealed at least nine additional transient events in the Spock and Warhol galaxies Rodney2018,Chen2019,Kaurov2019,Kelly2023 , all of them consistent with being microlensing events, bringing the total number to a record 19 alleged microlensing events in a single cluster field. Because of this wealth of flickering events, MACS0416 is referred to as the Christmas tree galaxy cluster and is so far the most remarkable cluster in terms of the number of transient events, allegedly due to microlensing. The low redshift of the two aforementioned caustic crossing galaxies, Warhol and Spock, correlates with the large number of microlensing transient events of distant stars detected in these galaxies. Without lensing, stars in these galaxies would have apparent magnitudes in the range 36--40.

gmw_00005882.name: System 12
gmw_00005882.overview: System 12, also known as the Mothra galaxy, is a caustic-crossing lensed galaxy [arXiv:2312.11603]. It is crossing a portion of the caustic that yields high predicted macro-model magnifications, which has allowed the detection of one EMO-star [arXiv:2312.11603].

sources.summary: System 12 is identified as the Mothra galaxy, a caustic-crossing lensed galaxy with one detected EMO-star and high predicted macro-model magnifications.

quotes.quote: System 12 is the Mothra galaxy. This galaxy (panel C of Fig.  Fig_CritCurves ) is crossing one of the most powerful portions of the caustic, making it a great candidate for detecting EMO-stars. So far, though, only one EMO-star (Mothra) has been detected.

At that distance, our lens model gives macro-model = 972 and 716 for the counterimages with negative (the observed image) and positive parity, respectively, before accounting for milli- or micro-lensing.

gmw_00005883.name: System 34
gmw_00005883.overview: System 34 is a high-redshift galaxy that is currently crossing a gravitational lensing caustic [arXiv:2312.11603]. Because only its outer portion touches this caustic region, the predicted time delay for any of its variable sources is small [arXiv:2312.11603].

sources.summary: System 34 is a high-redshift caustic-crossing galaxy whose outer portion touches the strongest caustic region, giving a small predicted time delay for variable sources.

quotes.quote: System 34 (panel A of Fig.  Fig_CritCurves ) is in principle a challenging target for EMO-stars because of its high redshift (Table  Tab_1 ). However, this system is crossing the most powerful portion of the caustic among all galaxies listed in Table  Tab_1 , making it an interesting target. On the negative side, only the outer portion of the galaxy is touching the caustic, thus reducing the probability of seeing an EMO-star in this arc. The time delay for this arc is small (although with large uncertainty): an intrinsically variable source located in the middle of one of the two blue images near the CC would have its counterimage arrive just 11 days later (ignoring micro-lensing, which could be different in each counterimage).

gmw_00005884.name: System 8
gmw_00005884.overview: System 8 is a red, caustic-crossing gravitational lensing arc [arXiv:2312.11603]. The system contains two candidate extremely magnified open stars located very close to the critical curve, which may alternatively be globular clusters [arXiv:2312.11603].

sources.summary: System 8 is a red caustic-crossing arc with candidate EMO-stars near the critical curve, making it a strong target for future microlensing searches.

quotes.quote: System 8 is a colorful and very red arc containing two candidate EMO-stars (panel E in Fig.  Fig_CritCurves ). The two candidates are very close to the expected position of the critical curve. An alternative is that these two objects could be GCs in the cluster. Further evidence, for instance flux variability, is needed to confirm these candidates: GCs are not expected to vary, but EMO-stars do so because of both the ubiquitous microlenses and intrinsic variability. System 8 has a redshift similar to that of system 6, but it is crossing a more powerful caustic. This makes it a better candidate to search for EMO-stars.

gmw_00005885.name: Warhol
gmw_00005885.overview: Warhol is a galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1 [arXiv:2312.11603]. It is currently crossing a cluster caustic, which enables the detection of multiple microlensing transient events of its stars [arXiv:2312.11603].

sources.summary: Warhol is described as a z ~ 1 caustic-crossing lensed galaxy whose low redshift and high magnification enable multiple microlensing transient detections.

quotes.quote: These events are mostly due to the existence of two galaxies (dubbed Warhol and Spock) at z 1 that are crossing the cluster caustic. One of these events was studied in detail by and corresponds to a double star being magnified by a millilens with mass smaller than 2.5 10^6 M _ , making it the smallest structure whose mass has been estimated through gravitational lensing above z=0.3 , and with implications for dark matter (DM) models. Earlier HST observations have revealed at least nine additional transient events in the Spock and Warhol galaxies Rodney2018,Chen2019,Kaurov2019,Kelly2023 , all of them consistent with being microlensing events, bringing the total number to a record 19 alleged microlensing events in a single cluster field. Because of this wealth of flickering events, MACS0416 is referred to as the Christmas tree galaxy cluster and is so far the most remarkable cluster in terms of the number of transient events, allegedly due to microlensing. The low redshift of the two aforementioned caustic crossing galaxies, Warhol and Spock, correlates with the large number of microlensing transient events of distant stars detected in these galaxies. Without lensing, stars in these galaxies would have apparent magnitudes in the range 36--40.

gmw_00005886.name: MCG+08-11-002
gmw_00005886.overview: MCG+08-11-002 is a dusty luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1602.01097] in a late-stage merger phase, featuring two distinct nuclei separated by approximately 110 to 133 parsecs [arXiv:1602.01097, arXiv:1811.09281]. The system exhibits strong, clumpy nuclear starburst activity [arXiv:1602.01097, arXiv:1811.09281] but shows no evidence of shocked or outflowing molecular gas [arXiv:1811.09281].

sources.summary: The paper presents MCG+08-11-002 as a dusty LIRG late-stage merger whose young star clusters trace current coalescence-driven starburst activity and possible earlier nuclear passage.

quotes.quote: We present near infrared imaging and integral field spectroscopy of the centre of the dusty luminous infrared galaxy merger MCG+08-11-002 , taken using the Near InfraRed Camera 2 (NIRC2) and the OH-Suppressing InfraRed Imaging Spectrograph (OSIRIS) on Keck II. We achieve a spatial resolution of 25 pc in the K band, allowing us to resolve 41 star clusters in the NIRC2 images. We calculate the ages of 22/25 star clusters within the OSIRIS field using the equivalent widths of the CO 2.3 m absorption feature and the \ nebular emission line. The star cluster age distribution has a clear peak at ages 20 Myr , indicative of current starburst activity associated with the final coalescence of the progenitor galaxies. There is a possible second peak at 65 Myr which may be a product of the previous close passage of the galaxy nuclei.

MCG+08-11-002 (hereafter MCG08; z = 0.0198 , d = 86 Mpc , 1 arcsec = 417 pc ) is a LIRG ( L_ IR = 10^ 11.46 L_ ; Armus09 ) and a late stage merger, with two distinct nuclei separated by 0.32 ( 133 pc ). The galaxy is drawn from the Great Observatories All-sky LIRG Survey (GOALS), a flux-limited survey of the brightest infrared galaxies at z 0.1.

sources.summary: MCG +08 11 002 is a late-stage merging LIRG with two close nuclei; OSIRIS resolves clumpy nuclear star formation but finds no shocked or outflowing molecular gas.

quotes.quote: MCG +08 11 002 MCG +08 11 002 is a late-stage merging LIRG with two distinct nuclei separated by 133 pc clumps , with the ages of its stellar clusters thoroughly analyzed in our previous work . The extended emission relative to that of the K band continuum illustrates the strong and clumpy nuclear star formation (Paper I). In the finer resolution 35mas data taken since Paper I, the two continuum peaks are better resolved to a separation of 110 pc apart. The emission traces the bright but more extended continuum peak nucleus in the northeastern direction, leaving the compact continuum peak, likely a star cluster , void of ionized gas. The gas seems to concentrate on the compact nucleus star cluster but we see no evidence for shocked or outflowing molecular gas from the OSIRIS data.

gmw_00005887.name: UGC 11041
gmw_00005887.overview: UGC 11041 is a starburst galaxy that exhibits a variable gamma-ray excess [arXiv:1906.06720]. It displays relatively weak infrared and radio emission, suggesting it may harbor an obscured active galactic nucleus that drives this gamma-ray activity [arXiv:1906.06720].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 11041 as a starburst galaxy with variable gamma-ray excess and weak star-formation tracers, favoring an AGN contribution while noting a possible nearby radio-source association.

quotes.quote: Recently, gamma-ray excesses are reported to be spatially coincident with two starburst galaxies NGC 3424 and UGC 11041 in the fourth Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT) source catalog (4FGL). Different from other starburst galaxies detected by Fermi LAT, we find that the gamma-ray emission associated with NGC 3424 and UGC 11041 show significant flux variability. With relatively weak infrared and radio emission, NGC 3424 and UGC 11041 appear as outliers of the L_ L_ IR and L_ L_ 1.4 \ GHz relations of starburst galaxies. These results suggest that NGC 3424 and UGC 11041 may harbor obscured active galactic nuclei (AGNs) and the AGN activities provide the dominant contribution to the gamma-ray emission as compared to that provided by the starburst activities.

Although no AGN candidate is found, a flat-spectrum radio source MG2 J175448+3442 is very close to UGC 11041 and could be an alternative candidate associated with the gamma-ray excess 2019arXiv190210045T . Fermi LAT 4FGL lists UGC 11041 as a variable source in the Fermi LAT band, which is confirmed by our following data analysis procedure, so the gamma-ray emission most likely arises from an AGN 2019arXiv190210045T .

gmw_00005888.name: CEERS ID 2919
gmw_00005888.overview: CEERS ID 2919 is a relatively isolated galaxy [arXiv:2307.07599] that hosts an X-ray active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2307.07599, arXiv:2306.08605]. It is classified as a Type 1 optical active galactic nucleus with broad Balmer emission lines [arXiv:2306.08605], though it has also been described as a Type II galaxy [arXiv:2307.07599].

sources.summary: The paper treats ID13098 as an isolated Type II galaxy with X-ray AGN signs and possible blue-nugget wet compaction driving central star formation and outside-in quenching.

quotes.quote: 4 galaxies hosts X-ray AGNs that do not dominate the FIR emissions; 1 is a Type I galaxy (ID30186), 2 are Type II galaxies (ID13098 and ID13776) and the last one is a Type III galaxy (ID23205) ( nandra_aegis-x_2015 ). However, non of them look like a blue nugget except possibly ID13098. We discuss the specific case of ID13098 in Sect. subsec:quenching .

ID13098 is in the correct range of stellar mass and redshift to be in a `blue nugget' phase ( lapiner_wet_2023,dekel_formation_2009,tacchella_evolution_2016 ) where the galaxy undergo a wet compaction caused by gas-rich mergers or smoother gas streams, leading to an episode of high central star-formation and outside-in quenching. The presence of the low-luminosity quiescent disk might indicate that the compaction is not completely done yet. If it is a blue nugget, the outside-in quenching may not be final as when the gas has been consumed at the center and the bulge has grown, a star-forming ring can form in the disk via accretion of new gas-reach material.

The galaxy at lower density is ID13098 that we discuss in Sect. subsec:quenching . The fact that this galaxy is relatively isolated favors the scenario of wet compaction as the origin of its outside-in quenching.

sources.summary: The paper classifies CEERS ID 2919 as a Type 1 optical AGN with broad Balmer emission, an X-ray detection, and lower obscuration than the hidden Compton-thick source.

quotes.quote: Among the optical AGNs, we note that 3 of them also have a broad component detected at S/N >3 in H or H (CEERS ID 2919, 3129, 2904), thus should be considered as Type 1 AGNs, while the remaining systems are likely Type 2 AGNs in the optical (CEERS ID 2754, 5106, 12286, 16406, and 17496). Two of the broad-line AGNs (2919 and 2904) are also detected by Chandra in X-rays nandra15 . Broad-line AGNs show a preference for lower ionization models compared to narrow-line AGNs, which are mostly residing in the upper left part of the BPT diagram.

The high central density and obscuration might be the reason why it does not show broad lines and it is harder to identify compared to the other X-ray AGNs in our sample (ID 2919 and 2904), which have broad lines and a lower N _ H < 10^ 23 cm^ 2 .

gmw_00005889.name: CEERS ID 3129
gmw_00005889.overview: CEERS ID 3129 is a broad-line active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 1.04 [arXiv:2306.08605]. Optical and near-infrared diagnostics consistently place the source in the active galactic nucleus parameter space [arXiv:2306.08605].

sources.summary: The paper uses CEERS ID 3129 as a broad-line z = 1.04 AGN example and finds its optical and near-infrared diagnostics consistently place it in AGN regions.

quotes.quote: In Figure fit_lines_3129 we show as an example of our procedure the fit performed for all the emission lines of the source ID 3129 (a broad-line AGN at z=1.04 ) that are relevant to this work. Among the optical AGNs, we note that 3 of them also have a broad component detected at S/N >3 in H or H (CEERS ID 2919, 3129, 2904), thus should be considered as Type 1 AGNs, while the remaining systems are likely Type 2 AGNs in the optical (CEERS ID 2754, 5106, 12286, 16406, and 17496). In our sample, we have the coverage of \ in 6 galaxies at 1 < z < 1.4 , of which 1 is an optical AGN (ID 3129), 1 is a hidden AGN (ID 9413), and the remaining 4 are normal star-forming galaxies from all our previous classifications.

We can see that the optical AGN and the hidden AGN fall in the AGN parameter space identified by rodriguezardila04,rodriguezardila05 in the first diagram (top panel), and by colina15 in the bottom panel, with the / \ ratios significantly higher than 1 .

gmw_00005890.name: CEERS ID 2904
gmw_00005890.overview: CEERS ID 2904 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a broad-line Type 1 AGN [arXiv:2306.08605]. It is detected in X-rays by Chandra and exhibits a lower level of X-ray obscuration than some other active systems [arXiv:2306.08605].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CEERS ID 2904 as a broad-line Type 1 optical AGN, also detected by Chandra, with lower X-ray obscuration than the hidden AGN ID 2900.

quotes.quote: Among the optical AGNs, we note that 3 of them also have a broad component detected at S/N >3 in H or H (CEERS ID 2919, 3129, 2904), thus should be considered as Type 1 AGNs, while the remaining systems are likely Type 2 AGNs in the optical (CEERS ID 2754, 5106, 12286, 16406, and 17496). Two of the broad-line AGNs (2919 and 2904) are also detected by Chandra in X-rays nandra15 . Broad-line AGNs show a preference for lower ionization models compared to narrow-line AGNs, which are mostly residing in the upper left part of the BPT diagram.

The high central density and obscuration might be the reason why it does not show broad lines and it is harder to identify compared to the other X-ray AGNs in our sample (ID 2919 and 2904), which have broad lines and a lower N _ H < 10^ 23 cm^ 2 .

gmw_00005891.name: CEERS ID 2900
gmw_00005891.overview: CEERS ID 2900 is classified as a hidden near-infrared active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2306.08605]. It is a Compton-thick source with high central density and obscuration that may prevent the detection of broad emission lines [arXiv:2306.08605].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CEERS ID 2900 as a hidden near-infrared AGN and Compton-thick X-ray source whose obscuration may hide broad lines.

quotes.quote: However, we also find that 5 optical star-forming galaxies become AGNs in the near-IR diagnostics (CEERS ID 2900, 8515, 8588, 8710, 9413). We identify them as hidden AGNs, and they are represented as cyan crosses in all the panels in Fig. nearinfrared_highz . All of these hidden AGNs are classified as pure AGNs in the P2S3 diagram.

In particular, we find that the hidden AGN ID 2900 is a Compton thick AGN with a luminosity (L_X/erg/s) = 44.07 0.54 and a hydrogen column density N _ H =10^ 25 cm^ 2 buchner15 . The high central density and obscuration might be the reason why it does not show broad lines and it is harder to identify compared to the other X-ray AGNs in our sample (ID 2919 and 2904), which have broad lines and a lower N _ H < 10^ 23 cm^ 2 .

gmw_00005892.name: CEERS ID 9413
gmw_00005892.overview: CEERS ID 9413 is a galaxy located at a redshift between 1 and 1.4 [arXiv:2306.08605]. While classified as a star-forming galaxy in optical diagnostics, near-infrared diagnostics reveal it to be a hidden active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2306.08605].

sources.summary: The paper classifies CEERS ID 9413 as a hidden near-infrared AGN whose Fe2H2 diagnostic position agrees with the paper’s main near-infrared AGN classification.

quotes.quote: However, we also find that 5 optical star-forming galaxies become AGNs in the near-IR diagnostics (CEERS ID 2900, 8515, 8588, 8710, 9413). We identify them as hidden AGNs, and they are represented as cyan crosses in all the panels in Fig. nearinfrared_highz . All of these hidden AGNs are classified as pure AGNs in the P2S3 diagram.

In our sample, we have the coverage of \ in 6 galaxies at 1 < z < 1.4 , of which 1 is an optical AGN (ID 3129), 1 is a hidden AGN (ID 9413), and the remaining 4 are normal star-forming galaxies from all our previous classifications. We can see that the optical AGN and the hidden AGN fall in the AGN parameter space identified by rodriguezardila04,rodriguezardila05 in the first diagram (top panel), and by colina15 in the bottom panel, with the / \ ratios significantly higher than 1 .

gmw_00005893.name: SDSS J175426.70+471542.3
gmw_00005893.overview: SDSS J175426.70+471542.3 is a starburst galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.1059 [arXiv:2311.13940]. It is identified as the host galaxy of the supernova SN 2019neq and has an estimated star formation rate of 2.6 solar masses per year [arXiv:2311.13940].

sources.summary: The paper associates SN 2019neq with SDSS J175426.70+471542.3 and derives host-galaxy redshift, metallicity, star formation rate, stellar mass, and specific star formation rate.

quotes.quote: At a redshift z 0.1059 (see Sec. sec:spectroscopy ), SN 2019neq is located at RA = 17^ h 54^ m 26 736 , Dec =+47 15 40 62 and it is likely associated with the galaxy SDSS J175426.70+471542.3. Finally, we used the positions of the narrow H , [ O iii ] host-galaxy emission lines measured in the +68, +80 and +231 days spectra to derive a host galaxy redshift of z=0.10592 0.00005 (where the uncertainty is given by the dispersion of the measurements). We also estimated the metallicity at the site of SN 2019neq by means of the narrow emission lines from the host galaxy which show up at late/nebular phases.

Finally, we estimated the star formation rate (SFR) of the host galaxy of SN 2019neq based on the measurements of the flux emitted by the reddening corrected narrow H using equation 2 of kennicutt1998 . The derived SFR is 2.6 M_ yr^ 1 , similar to the SFRs measured by chenetal2017 for a sample of galaxies hosting SLSNe I. To estimate M_ * , we used equation 8 of tayloretal2011 after having measured the g_ host =20.4 0.1 mag and i_ host =19.7 0.1 mag for the host galaxy.

gmw_00005894.name: NGC 7563
gmw_00005894.overview: NGC 7563 is a prototypical barlens galaxy [arXiv:1807.01092]. Its photometric bulge is dominated by a nearly exponential barlens and a surrounding ringlens, both of which are dominated by metal-rich, intermediate-age stars [arXiv:1807.01092].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7563 as a prototypical barlens galaxy whose photometric bulge is dominated by a nearly exponential, metal-rich intermediate-age barlens and surrounding ringlens.

quotes.quote: Typical barlens galaxies NGC 7563 (T = 1) : This is the type of galaxies in which barlenses were originally recognized lauri2011 . The surface brightness profile (Fig. decompositions_1 ) shows a possible separate bulge component, and a nearly exponential barlens, which dominates the photometric bulge. The bar and the barlens are dominated by metal-rich (log _ 10 Z/Z _ = 0.20) intermediate age stars.

There is also a contribution of very old stars ( > 10 Gyrs), but their relative fraction decreases within the barlens, and drops in the galaxy center. The bar is surrounded by a dispersed ringlens, which is also dominated by metal-rich stars, but containing also an increasing fraction of less metal-rich stars. It appears that the density peak in the surface brightness profile is not made of old metal-poor stars early in the history of this galaxy.

gmw_00005895.name: NGC 7321
gmw_00005895.overview: NGC 7321 is a barred spiral galaxy characterized by a high-surface-brightness disk [arXiv:1807.01092]. This disk exhibits stellar migration, and the galaxy features a photometric bulge that is less pronounced than that of NGC 5406 [arXiv:1807.01092].

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 7321 to NGC 5406, noting a less prominent photometric bulge, declining metal-poor stars, and stellar migration in its high-surface-brightness disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 7321 (T = 3) : This galaxy has qualitatively similar stellar age and metallicity distributions as NGC 5406, but the photometric bulge is less prominent. The fraction of the most metal-poor stars (log _ 10 Z/Z _ = -0.7) starts to drop already at 18'', corresponding to the high surface brightness region extending well outside the bar. There is clearly migration of stars inside the high surface brightness disk.

gmw_00005896.name: UGC 10811
gmw_00005896.overview: UGC 10811 is a barred spiral galaxy where the photometric bulge is dominated by a barlens with a nearly exponential surface brightness profile [arXiv:1807.01092]. This barlens is dominated by old, metal-poor stars, whereas the disk outside the bar is dominated by young, metal-rich stars [arXiv:1807.01092].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 10811 as an exceptional barlens galaxy dominated by old metal-poor stars, with a nearly exponential barlens and young metal-rich outer disk.

quotes.quote: UGC 10811 (T = 2) : This galaxy (and NGC 0180) is exceptional in our sample, in a sense that the barlens is dominated by the oldest ( > 10 Gyrs) fairly metal-poor (log _ 10 Z/Z _ = -0.40) stars, which fraction increases toward the galaxy center. The photometric bulge is dominated by the barlens, which has a nearly exponential surface brightness profile ( n = 1.5). The disk outside the bar is dominated by young metal-rich stars (log _ 10 Z/Z _ = 0.20), but has also many other metallicities. Most probably the bar was formed early, but galaxy modeling is needed to interpret how the mass was accumulated to the barlens.

gmw_00005897.name: NGC 0776
gmw_00005897.overview: NGC 0776 is a late-type barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2111.05200, arXiv:1807.01092] featuring a barlens that dominates its bar structure [arXiv:1807.01092] and a weak bar-related bump in its surface brightness profile [arXiv:1712.05354]. While star formation in its central bulge has been extinguished for at least 9 billion years [arXiv:2111.05200], the galaxy hosts a nuclear ring with a young stellar population [arXiv:1807.01092] and an outer disk that continues to grow [arXiv:2111.05200].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 0776 as an iC late-type example where central star formation is long quenched, the outer disk keeps growing, and age-filtered profiles are needed for unbiased bulge-disk decomposition.

quotes.quote: Figure ry1 shows an example of the application of \ to the iC LTG NGC 0776 . From panel b , it is apparent that the H equivalent width \ increases from the center to the periphery, as also reflected in the radial distribution of values for individual spaxels (dots in panel ) for which the spectrum was fit with a mean percentual deviation ADEV < 6 cf. Cid05,GP16-ETGs .

Panel d shows the g band SBPs after suppression of stellar populations youger than 0.03, 0.1, 0.3, 1, 3, 5, 7, and 9 Gyr, as obtained by postprocessing the spectral modeling output from \ with . Removal of stellar populations for all \ has little effect on the bulge, which, taken at face value, implies that SF at the center of the galaxy has been practically extinguished since at least 9 Gyr ago. In contrast, the outer disk ( / 1) has continued building up, as is apparent from the fact that suppression of stellar populations younger than 9 Gyr effects a dimming by 1.5 g mag.

From these considerations it follows that only after removal of stellar populations of age 9 Gyr does the excess emission of the outer disk (aka the \ effect) in NGC 0776 become sufficiently suppressed and its residual older stellar component become roughly compatible with the bulge from the evolutionary point of view (i.e., with regard to its SED and light-weighted age). Only then would the traditional bulge-disk decomposition concept be logically sound and ensure an unbiased determination of the luminosity and color of the bulge (and bar).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 0776 to illustrate how disk fitting and bar light can bias bulge photometry and structural measurements in late-type galaxies.

quotes.quote: This is illustrated on the example of NGC 0776 (Fig. decomposition ): it can be seen that automated fitting of a pure exponential to the down-bending (type ii) profile of the disk would overestimate its central surface brightness and underestimate its scale length . This would then lead to an underestimation of the excess emission from the bulge, and a false determination of its total magnitude, isophotal radius and S ersic parameters. A meaningful approach in this case would be, either to model the disk with a modified exponential distribution for example, the fitting function proposed in P96a , or to interactively select and fit the inner exponential part of the disk (1 / 2.1), as was done here (light-blue line in Fig. decomposition ).

Another salient feature of the SBP of NGC 0776 is a weak bump at 20 ( ) 21 that reflects the emission from a bar, being well visible on the SDSS true-color image composite (lower-left panel of Fig. classes ); its neglect in 1D/2D decomposition could systematically impact S ersic fits to the excess emission above the disk (open blue crosses), which encompasses the more extended lower-surface brightness (LSB) "wings" from the bar. As pointed out in Breda14 , this could lead to an overestimation of and possibly move a PB into the locus of CBs, eventually also increase the scatter in any relation between and other galaxy parameters (for example, bulge _ , M_r , or mean surface brightness).

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 0776 has a dominant barlens, stellar and metallicity properties similar to prototypical barlens galaxies, and a nuclear ring with young stars.

quotes.quote: NGC 0776 (T = 3) : The barlens dominates the bar in such a level that the morphology approaches a non-barred galaxy (i.e. has a barlens classification ``f'' by Laurikainen Salo 2017). However, in spite of that the stellar and metallicity properties are very similar as in such prototypical barlens galaxies as NGC 5406. The unsharp mask image shows a nuclear ring, showing also a significant contribution of the young stellar population.

gmw_00005898.name: NGC 6941
gmw_00005898.overview: NGC 6941 is an SABb spiral galaxy [arXiv:0809.1434] that features a vertically thick inner-bar structure with an X-shape [arXiv:1807.01092]. It is a gas-rich optical giant with a low-surface-brightness disk [arXiv:0809.1434] and a discrepancy between its gas and stellar flat velocities [arXiv:1501.05498].

sources.summary: The paper presents HIPEQ2036 04 as a hydrogen giant containing about 2 x 10^10 solar masses of H I, optically giant, gas-rich, and an SABb spiral with a low-surface-brightness disk.

quotes.quote: HIPEQ2036 04 (NGC6941) contains 2 10 ^ 10 M _ , apparently an upper limit if one excludes other nearby companions - as here, and about 5 times the mass of the Milky Way (Binney Merrifield 1998) [ Fig 12a ] . Their huge hydrogen content means that such galaxies can be detected far away and this one is at 6200 . It is also a giant optically, being 3 L _ * in g but its gas-to-light ratio is M _ H I /L _ B = 0.7 that is 5 times higher than the fiducial value for giants. Fig. 12a shows an SABb (T=3) with a high surface brightness core but a low surface-brightness disc with widely spread spiral arms. The integrated colour is ( g - ) = 0.79, while the fibre spectrum of the core is typical of an early type spiral.

sources.summary: The paper groups NGC 6941 with X-shaped barlens galaxies, noting vertically thick inner-bar structure, metal-rich intermediate-age bar/barlens regions, and a central drop in old stars.
