quotes.quote: Galaxies with X-shapes NGC 6941 (T = 3), UGC 8781 (T = 3) , and NGC 5000 (T = 4): In these galaxies the barlenses show also X-shape features in the unsharp-mask images, which confirms that they are vertically thick inner bar components. Therefore, it is interesting that also in these galaxies the bar/barlens regions have similar age and metallicity distributions as the prototypical barlens galaxies discussed above, i.e. they are dominated by metal-rich (log _ 10 Z/Z _ = 0.20) intermediate age stars, with a significant contribution of the oldest stars (age > 10 Gyrs). In UGC 08781 and NGC 5000 the fraction of the oldest stars is similar in the galaxy center and in the bar/barlens region, whereas in NGC 6941 their fraction drops in the galaxy center. In these galaxies the metallicity starts to drop toward the galaxy center already at the edge of the bar.

sources.summary: NGC6941 is the one galaxy where gas and stellar flat velocities disagree; using gas kinematics would put its R values between 0.8 and 1.5.

quotes.quote: For all galaxies except one, NGC6941, the agreement between the two quantities is within the 1 error bars. This galaxy shows V_ ,flat,gas /V_ ,flat,stellar 1.4 . The values of R for this galaxy would be in the range 0.8 - 1.5 if we use V_ ,flat from gas kinematics.

gmw_00005899.name: NGC 0180
gmw_00005899.overview: NGC 0180 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1710.10320, arXiv:1807.01092] that features a barlens structure dominated by an X-shape feature [arXiv:1807.01092]. This barlens region is dominated by old, metal-poor stars, whereas the galaxy center exhibits a high stellar velocity dispersion [arXiv:1807.01092].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 180's ionized-gas velocity, oxygen abundance, and extinction with CALIFA data, derives spiral-arm geometry, finds a small arm abundance enhancement, and measures kinematic parameters.

quotes.quote: We derive maps of the observed velocity of ionized gas, the oxygen abundance, and the extinction (Balmer decrement) across the area of the four spiral galaxies NGC 36, NGC 180, NGC 6063, and NGC 7653 from integral field spectroscopy obtained by the Calar Alto Legacy Integral Field Area (CALIFA) survey. We searched for spiral arms through Fourier analysis of the spatial distribution of three tracers (non-circular motion, enhancement of the oxygen abundance, and of the extinction) in the discs of our target galaxies. The spiral arms (two-armed logarithmic spirals in the deprojected map) are shown in each target galaxy for each tracer considered. The pitch angles of the spiral arms in a given galaxy obtained with the three different tracers are close to each other. The enhancement of the oxygen abundance in the spiral arms as compared to the abundance in the interarm regions at a given galactocentric distance is small; within a few per cent.

We identified a metallicity gradient in our target galaxies. Both barred galaxies in our sample show flatter gradients than the two galaxies without bars. Galactic inclination, position angle of the major axis, and the rotation curve were also obtained for each target galaxy using the Fourier analysis of the two-dimensional velocity map.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 0180 as an exceptional X-shaped barlens galaxy dominated by old metal-poor stars, with declining central old-star fraction and high central velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: NGC 0180 (T = 3) : The bl/X is dominated by old (age > 10 Gyrs) metal-poor (log _ 10 Z/Z _ = -0.40) stars, in a similar manner as the barlens in UGC 10811. In the galaxy center the fraction of the oldest stars drops. The mean velocity dispersions of the bar, bl/X, and the galaxy center are = 118, 137, and 181 km/sec, respectively. Very old metal-poor stars with high random motions are generally interpreted as manifestations of merger built classical bulges. However, in NGC 0180 the barlens is dominated by an X-shape feature, which challenges that interpretation.

gmw_00005900.name: NGC 7738
gmw_00005900.overview: NGC 7738 is a prototypical barlens galaxy [arXiv:1807.01092] that hosts a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:1511.00431]. Two dust lanes penetrate its barlens to the galactic center [arXiv:1807.01092], where recent gas inflow has triggered central star formation [arXiv:1807.01092].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7738 as a prototypical barlens galaxy with arc-like dust, dust lanes reaching the center, young central stars, and metallicity enrichment in the barlens.

quotes.quote: Barlenses with dust lanes NGC 7738 (T = 3) : This is a prototypical barlens galaxy, similar to NGC 4314 lauri2014 . The arc-like dust features in the unsharp mask image are illustrative, because they hint to the fact that the whole high surface brightness disk surrounding the bar probably form part of the bar structure. Two dust-lanes penetrate through the barlens ending up to the galaxy center, where young stars (age < 1.5 Gyr) appear at 5 arcsec.

This galaxy is enriched in metallicity particularly in the barlens region. Clearly, fresh gas has penetrated through the barlens fairly recently triggering central star formation. Most probably star formation has occurred also in the barlens, leaving behind a metal-rich stellar population, but that star formation has been ceased already a long time ago (lack of young stars in the barlens).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7738 as an optically elusive, Compton-thick AGN candidate with excess soft emission, an added power-law component, and a strong Fe-K line.

quotes.quote: The six objects, NGC 1402, IC 614, 2MASX J11594382 2006579, NGC 6926, 2MASX J23404437 1151178, and NGC 7738, show excess emission around 0.6--1.0 keV suggesting the presence of emission from optically thin plasma. We used an APEC plasma model to represent this feature. An APEC + reflection model describes the continua of IC 614, 2MASX J11594382 2006579, and NGC 6926. An APEC + absorbed power-law model also provided a similar quality of fit to 2MASX J11594382 2006579. 2MASX J23404437 1151178 and NGC 7738 require an additional power-law component, where we assumed a common for the reflection and power-law components since the quality of the data is not sufficient to constrain values for these components separately.

The six objects, NGC 1402, IC 614, 2XMMi J184540.6 630522, NGC 6926, 2MASX J23404437 1151178, and NGC 7738, show an emission line at around 6.4 keV in the source rest frame with an EW in the range of 1.1--4.6 keV.
A signature of the presence of a Compton-thick AGN is clearly seen in our spectrum of NGC 7738. All of these AGNs satisfy the conditions CR _ 0.2-12 /F_ 18 < 1 and CR _ 0.2-12 /F_ 90 < 0.1 .

gmw_00005901.name: PG0117+213
gmw_00005901.overview: PG0117+213 is a radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/9803094] located within a rich galaxy overdensity at a redshift of z=1.50 [arXiv:2006.07384]. The quasar displays C III and C IV emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/9803094], two associated absorption-line systems [arXiv:1307.6799], and prior infrared variability [arXiv:astro-ph/9803094].

sources.summary: The paper says PG 0117 + 213 was the most frequently observed target, showed no significant optical variability, has C III/C IV emission, and prior infrared variability.

quotes.quote: The source with the largest number of observations is PG 0117 + 213 (Table three-quasars ) and some of the differential V-band results are presented in Fig. PG0117+213 . No significant short term variability was observed. Furthermore careful analysis of the remainder of the V-band data and all of the B-band revealed no significant short or long term variability.

A( z<2 ) PG 0117 + 213 was the most frequently observed source in the sample and no significant variability was detected. The spectra of the source reveal the emission lines C iii and C iv at z_ em =1.493 (Hewitt Burbidge 1987). Infrared observations over a period of years show maximum variability of 0.05 and 0.16 magnitudes at 2.2 m and 10.1 m respectively (Neugebauer et al. 1989). Sagar et al. (1996) observed this RQQSO during November 1996 and detected a hint of optical microvariability and suggested that careful monitoring of this source should continue.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PG0117+213 as the richest quasar-field overdensity, with up to 36 members at z=1.50 and a high inferred halo mass.

quotes.quote: One of the overdensities, PG0117+213 at z=1.50 , has potentially 36 spectroscopically confirmed members, consisting of 19 with secure redshifts and 17 with single-line redshifts, within a cylinder of radius 700 kpc. Its halo mass is estimated to be (M/M_ )=14.7 .

Our richest galaxy overdensity is PG0117+213 with 19 unambiguous- z members and a further 17 potential single-line redshift members, 36 in total (see Fig. fig:zhistind ). The additional 17 potential members are single-line galaxies, which would fall into the quasar's z=0.025 redshift bin if the line is confirmed to be H .

The richest galaxy overdensity in our sample is found around the quasar PG0117+213, which has 19 confirmed members and potentially 36 in total and as such, its predicted mass of (M/M_ )=14.7 is likely a lower limit.

sources.summary: PG 0117+213 has two associated absorption-line systems with Lyman-series and doublet detections, apparent line locking, and blends that complicate partial-coverage assessment.

quotes.quote: PG 0117+213 ( =1.4949, 1.5090) -- There are two associated absorption line systems in the sight line toward this quasar at =1.4949 \ and =1.5090 . In Figure fig:pg0117 , we present plots of detected transitions for these two systems. In each of these systems, we detect transitions from the Lyman series, 977, and the 1031.926,1037.617 doublet. The velocity separation of the absorption line systems is such that the doublets are apparently line-locked.

gmw_00005902.name: NGC5669
gmw_00005902.overview: NGC5669 is a galaxy characterized by extended dust emission that has a larger scale length than its optical emission [arXiv:1507.08778]. This dust is associated with extended, low surface brightness optical emission located away from the galaxy's nucleus and bright spiral arms [arXiv:1507.08778].

sources.summary: The paper says NGC5669 has more extended dust emission than optical emission, with submillimetre-to-optical brightness rising to the detected edge.

quotes.quote: Two of the sources (NGC5701 and NGC5669) were observed as part of the Herschel Reference Survey (Boselli et al. 2010), and a third (NGC3049) was observed by the KINGFISH programme (Kennicutt et al. 2010). The SPIRE images were obtained from the Herschel or HerMES data archives. For each of these objects we used an SDSS i band optical image to trace the optical surface brightness profile from stellar emission after smoothing by a gaussian matched to the size of the 250 m SPIRE beam, and then compared this to the 250 m surface brightness profile resulting from dust emission.

The dust emission in NGC5669 has a larger scale length than the optical emission, with the ratio of submm to optical surface brightness rising all the way to the edge of detectable emission in this object. Examination of the optical image suggests that this dust is associated with extended low surface brightness optical emission away from the galaxy's nucleus and bright spiral arms.

gmw_00005903.name: CTD 086
gmw_00005903.overview: CTD 086 is an E2 elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1707.05052] classified as a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy [arXiv:1212.6171, arXiv:1707.05052] or a hybrid FR I/II galaxy [arXiv:0912.3001]. It hosts a narrow-line active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1707.05052] with mild X-ray activity [arXiv:1212.6171] and symmetric east-west radio jets [arXiv:0908.4391].

sources.summary: The paper studies CTD 86 as a nearby FR-I radio galaxy with extended hot gas, mild nuclear X-ray activity, and a likely truncated accretion disk.

quotes.quote: We present an X-ray study of the nuclear and extended emission of a nearby Fanaroff Riley class I (FR-I) radio galaxy CTD 86 based on the observations. Two different components observed are : diffuse thermal emission from hot gas ( kT 0.79 , n_e 10^ 3 cm^ 3 , L_X 5 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 extended over 186 kpc ), and unresolved nuclear emission exhibiting mild activity. The hot gaseous environment of CTD 86 is similar to that found in groups of galaxies or in bright early-type galaxies.

X-ray emission from the nucleus is well constrained by an intrinsically absorbed ( N_H 5.9 10^ 22 cm^ 2 ) power law ( 1.5 ) with 2-10 luminosity L_X 2.1 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 . We have measured the stellar velocity dispersion, =182 8 , for the CTD 86 and estimated a mass M_ BH 9 10^7 M_ with L_ bol /L_ Edd 4 10^ 3 . The low L_ bol /L_ Edd rate and high L_X/L_ [ O III ] ratio suggest that the central engine of CTD 86 consists of a truncated accretion disk lacking a strong ionizing UV radiation and an inner hot flow producing the X-ray emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1422+26 as an extended FR I galaxy with east-west jets, a weak milliarcsecond source, and a distorted 1.6 GHz parsec-scale structure.

quotes.quote: 1422+26 -- An extended FR I galaxy, with two symmetric jets oriented east-west at arcsecond resolution. At 5 GHz, detect a weak (3 mJy) milliarcsecond scale source. The 1.6 GHz observations presented in this work detect a larger flux density ( S_ 1.6 9.3 mJy), distributed in a misaligned and distorted structure (see Fig. f.1422 ). We tentatively identify the peak in the 1.6 GHz image with the nuclear source detected also at 5 GHz. In this scenario the spectral index is 0 and the extended 1.6 GHz structure is a one-sided, distorted jet.

sources.summary: PKS 1422+26 is treated as a hybrid FR I/II radio galaxy with striking wings, warm spots, X-ray cavities, and a ridge shaped by its intragroup medium.

quotes.quote: PKS 1422+26 ( z=0.037 ) : This galaxy has striking wings and ``warm'' spots which occur about 50 of the distance between the lobe edge and the radio core. The radio galaxy defies easy Fanaroff-Riley classification and is here considered a hybrid FR I/II galaxy. canosa99 found radial asymmetry in the ROSAT image of the IGM which the Chandra image confirms. The radio hot spots coincide well with decrements in the X-ray image which must be cavities blown in the gas by the radio lobes. The surface brightness of the ``ridge'' this leaves in the middle is consistent with the hypothesis that the actual orientation of the IGM is elongated in the direction of the radio lobes.

sources.summary: The paper studies CTD 086 as an FR I radio galaxy, finding an E2 elliptical morphology, narrow-line AGN spectrum, and central black-hole mass estimate.

quotes.quote: We present optical imaging and spectroscopic studies of the Fanaroff Riley class I (FR I) radio galaxy CTD 086 based on Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) and Sloan Digital Sky Survey ( SDSS ) observations. We use isophote shape analysis to show that there is no stellar disk component within CTD 086 and further that the morphological class of the galaxy is most likely E2. Optical spectroscopy of this galaxy reveals the presence of narrow emission lines only, and thus it qualifies to be termed as a narrow-line radio galaxy (type 2 AGN).
We also extract stellar kinematics from the absorption-line spectra of CTD 086 using Penalized Pixel-Fitting method and derive the black hole mass M_ BH to be equal to (8.8 2.4) 10^ 7 M .

sources.summary: The paper finds B2 1422+26 is X-ray asymmetric, not an aspect-correction artifact, with an uncertain point-like component and large errors in central-emission estimates.

quotes.quote: B2 1422+26 is due to the ROSAT aspect correction problem. The possible detection of a point-like component is not significant on an F-test at the 95 per cent level (although it passes at the 90 per cent level). We have nevertheless taken the point counts calculated from these model fits as our best estimate of the central emission, though the errors are large.

gmw_00005904.name: PKS B1222+216
gmw_00005904.overview: PKS B1222+216 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar that has been detected at very-high-energy levels above 100 GeV [arXiv:1902.02291]. The galaxy features variable broad-line region emission lines and contains a dust torus with an estimated temperature of 1200 K [arXiv:1902.02291].

sources.summary: The paper includes PKS B1222+216 among bright FSRQs, noting its very-high-energy detection, variable BLR lines, and dust-torus properties used in emission modeling.

quotes.quote: As noted in the Introduction, two of the sources (3C 279 and CTA 102) have already been shown to be variable on extremely short timescales TheFermi-LAT:2016dss,2018ApJ...854L..26S . Here we will introduce a novel objective method to identify a large set of flares in order to conduct a comprehensive search for the short variability in our source sample. Furthermore, PKS B1222+216 2011ApJ...730L...8A , 3C 279 2008Sci...320.1752M , and PKS 1510-089 2011ApJ...730L...8A,2013A&A...554A.107H,2014A&A...569A..46A are among the seven FSRQs also detected above 100 GeV with imaging air Cerenkov Telescopes. For 3C 454.3, the MAGIC and VERITAS telescopes only obtained upper limits on the flux during flaring states in 2007 2009A&A...498...83A and 2010 2016AJ....151..142A .

Furthermore, evidence exists that the the luminosity of BLR emission lines is variable in 3C 454.3, PKS 1510-089, and PKS B1222+216 2013ApJ...763L..36L,2015ApJ...804....7I and correlates with the emission 2013ApJ...763L..36L,2015IAUS..313...43L , which could indicate that are produced through IC scattering with BLR photons (see also Section sec:tcool ).

For PKS B1222+216 and CTA 102 2011ApJ...732..116M found T_ dt = 1200 K and a dust luminosity of 7.9 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 and 7 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 , respectively. 2005ApJ...625L..75H observed silicate emission in 3C 273 from which they deduced a silicate temperature of 140 K and luminosity of 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 but were unable to derive a temperature of the dust see also the discussion in 2011ApJ...732..116M . 2008A&A...486..411S instead found a dust temperature of 1200 K. We adopt the latter value and again set _ dt = 0.1 .

gmw_00005905.name: CTA 102
gmw_00005905.overview: CTA 102 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar that exhibits gamma-ray variability on subhour and minute-scale timeframes [arXiv:1902.02291]. This emission is linked to its millimeter core, and the active galactic nucleus contains dust with an estimated temperature of 1200 K [arXiv:1902.02291].

sources.summary: The paper identifies minute-scale variability in CTA 102, links its gamma-ray emission to the millimeter core, and uses dust and radio-core measurements in modeling.

quotes.quote: Consecutively zooming in on the brightest flares, which we identify in an objective way through Bayesian blocks and a hill-climbing algorithm, we find variability on subhour time scales and as short as minutes for two sources in our sample (3C 279, CTA 102) and weak evidence for variability at time scales less than the Fermi satellite's orbit of 95 minutes for PKS 1510-089 and 3C 454.3. This suggests extremely compact emission regions in the jet. We do not find any signs for ray absorption in the broad-line region (BLR), which indicates that rays are produced at distances greater than hundreds of gravitational radii from the central black hole. This is further supported by a cross-correlation analysis between ray and radio/millimeter light curves, which is consistent with ray production at the same location as the millimeter core for 3C 273, CTA 102, and 3C 454.3. The inferred locations of the ray production zones are still consistent with the observed decay times of the brightest flares if the decay is caused by external Compton scattering with BLR photons. However, the minute-scale variability is challenging to explain in such scenarios.

The following distances d_ core, 15GHz were determined under the assumption that k_r = 1 : for PKS B1222+216 d_ core, 15GHz = 23.41 pc; for 3C 279 d_ core, 15GHz <7.88 pc; for PKS 1510-089 d_ core, 15GHz = 17.71 pc; for CTA 102 d_ core, 15GHz =46.7 pc; and for 3C 454.3 d_ core, 15GHz = 20.36 pc. Dedicated analyses have also been carried out and found for 3C 454.3 k_r = 0.6 0.8 and d_ core, 15GHz 38 pc, and, since d_ core , ^ 1/k_r , d_ core, 43GHz 9 pc 2014MNRAS.437.3396K . For 3C 273 2013ARep...57...34V found k_r = 1.4 and d_ core , = 134 ^ 1/1.4 using radio observations at frequencies between 4.8 and 362 GHz. Lastly, 2015A&A...576A..43F conducted VLBA observations of CTA 102 ranging from 5 to 86 GHz and found k_r = 1.0 as a best-fit value and d_ core, 86GHz 7 pc.

For PKS B1222+216 and CTA 102 2011ApJ...732..116M found T_ dt = 1200 K and a dust luminosity of 7.9 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 and 7 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 , respectively. 2005ApJ...625L..75H observed silicate emission in 3C 273 from which they deduced a silicate temperature of 140 K and luminosity of 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 but were unable to derive a temperature of the dust see also the discussion in 2011ApJ...732..116M . 2008A&A...486..411S instead found a dust temperature of 1200 K. We adopt the latter value and again set _ dt = 0.1 .

gmw_00005906.name: Canes Venatici I
gmw_00005906.overview: Canes Venatici I is a relatively bright ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1110.2882]. It features complex stellar populations with a younger average age of approximately 12.6 billion years and exhibits an irregular, distorted structure [arXiv:1110.2882].

sources.summary: The paper finds CVn I is a relatively bright ultra-faint dwarf with a younger, complex stellar population, a horizontal-branch population gradient, and elongated or distorted structure.

quotes.quote: We take deep images of four ultra faint dwarf (UFD) galaxies, Canes Venatici I (CVn I), Bo otes I (Bo o I), Canes Venatici II (CVn II), and Leo IV using the Suprime-Cam on the Subaru Telescope. The colour-magitude diagrams (CMDs) extend below the main sequence turn-offs (MSTOs) and yield measurements of the ages of stellar populations. The stellar populations of faint three galaxies, Bo o I, CVn II and Leo IV dwarf spheroidal galaxies (dSphs) are estimated as old as a Galactic globular cluster M92. We confirm that Bo o I dSph has no intrinsic colour spread in the MSTO, and no spatial difference in the CMD morphology, which indicates that Bo o I dSph is composed of an old single stellar population. One of the brightest UFDs, CVn I dSph, shows a relatively younger age ( 12.6 Gyr) with respect to Bo o I, CVn II, and Leo IV dSphs, and the distribution of red horizontal branch (HB) stars is more concentrated toward the centre than that of blue HB stars, suggesting that the galaxy contains complex stellar populations.

In CVn I dSph, the peak of stellar density is somewhat offset toward west from the expected centre. The observed regions do not cover the whole area but cover only the area within the half light radius of CVn I and Bo o I dSphs, so it is unclear whether these features really reflect genuine extent of these galaxies or not. Quite irregular shapes of these galaxies, however, indicate that they are suffering from strong tidal effects from the Milky Way.

gmw_00005907.name: Bo otes I
gmw_00005907.overview: Boötes I is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1804.10230, arXiv:1110.2882] that is composed of an old, single stellar population [arXiv:1110.2882]. The galaxy contains fifteen identified RR Lyrae variable stars [arXiv:1804.10230] and shows no intrinsic color spread in its main sequence turn-off [arXiv:1110.2882].

sources.summary: The paper compares Bo o tes I proper motions with Gaia and compiles extensive spectroscopy, abundances, RR Lyrae, and 68 bright members.

quotes.quote: I find good agreement with the proper motions derived by the Gaia collaboration for Bo o tes I and Leo I. Bo o tes I My sample of ultra-faint dwarf galaxies has one object in common with that of gaiadr2helmi : Boo I. Using a photometrically-selected sample as for Leo I, gaiadr2helmi measure proper motions of = -0.459 0.041   , = -1.064 0.029   \ from 115 stars.

Bo o tes I (Boo I) has been the subject of numerous spectroscopic studies at both medium and high spectral resolution. Radial velocities have been measured by WIYN/Hydra munoz06 , Keck/DEIMOS martin07 , AAT/AAOmega norris10 , VLT/FLAMES koposov11 , and Keck/DEIMOS brown14 . The chemical abundances of Boo I stars have been studied by feltzing09 , norris08,norris10,norris10b , lai11 , gilmore13 , ishigaki14 , and frebel16 . Time-series imaging of Boo I has also identified 15 RR Lyrae variables in the galaxy siegel06,dallora06 . As a result of its relatively high luminosity and the extensive follow-up data sets available for Boo I, there are 68 confirmed member stars and 138 confirmed non-members at g < 20 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Bo o I as an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal with an old, single stellar population, no intrinsic MSTO color spread, and an elongated morphology.

quotes.quote: We take deep images of four ultra faint dwarf (UFD) galaxies, Canes Venatici I (CVn I), Bo otes I (Bo o I), Canes Venatici II (CVn II), and Leo IV using the Suprime-Cam on the Subaru Telescope. The colour-magitude diagrams (CMDs) extend below the main sequence turn-offs (MSTOs) and yield measurements of the ages of stellar populations. The stellar populations of faint three galaxies, Bo o I, CVn II and Leo IV dwarf spheroidal galaxies (dSphs) are estimated as old as a Galactic globular cluster M92. We confirm that Bo o I dSph has no intrinsic colour spread in the MSTO, and no spatial difference in the CMD morphology, which indicates that Bo o I dSph is composed of an old single stellar population.

Bo o I dSph has a moderate luminosity ( M_ V 5.8 ) of UFD. Thanks to its small distance, the tight MS is clearly seen in the CMD in Figure fig: pop CMD of BOO1 . Figure fig: pop CMD of BOO1 shows the CMD of whole observed region (Figure fig: pop CMD of BOO1 a), and within 12.5 (corresponding to r_ h ) region (Figures fig: pop CMD of BOO1 b- fig: pop CMD of BOO1 d), with the theoretical isochrones (Figure fig: pop CMD of BOO1 ) and the fiducial sequence of M92 (Figure fig: pop CMD of BOO1 d) in the same manner as the case of CVn I dSph.

gmw_00005908.name: Canes Venatici II
gmw_00005908.overview: Canes Venatici II is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy with an old stellar population similar in age to the Galactic globular cluster M92 [arXiv:1110.2882]. The galaxy exhibits an asymmetric shape, featuring a spherical core and an outer region elongated toward the north and south that suggests ongoing tidal disruption [arXiv:1110.2882].

sources.summary: The paper finds CVn II is an old ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal with a small tidal radius, distorted north-south structure, and possible outer stellar excesses.

quotes.quote: We take deep images of four ultra faint dwarf (UFD) galaxies, Canes Venatici I (CVn I), Bo otes I (Bo o I), Canes Venatici II (CVn II), and Leo IV using the Suprime-Cam on the Subaru Telescope. The colour-magitude diagrams (CMDs) extend below the main sequence turn-offs (MSTOs) and yield measurements of the ages of stellar populations. The stellar populations of faint three galaxies, Bo o I, CVn II and Leo IV dwarf spheroidal galaxies (dSphs) are estimated as old as a Galactic globular cluster M92.

The contour maps of UFDs show various appearances. CVn II dSph has an asymmetric shape and extended structure toward south. The core of CVn II dSph looks spherical shape, but the outer region is elongated toward north and south, which indicate on-going tidal disruption.

gmw_00005909.name: Leo IV
gmw_00005909.overview: Leo IV is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1110.2882]. Its stellar population is estimated to be as old as the Galactic globular cluster M92 [arXiv:1110.2882].

sources.summary: The paper describes Leo IV as an old, faint ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal with a less concentrated spherical shape, blue-straggler candidates, and possible edge overdensities.

quotes.quote: We take deep images of four ultra faint dwarf (UFD) galaxies, Canes Venatici I (CVn I), Bo otes I (Bo o I), Canes Venatici II (CVn II), and Leo IV using the Suprime-Cam on the Subaru Telescope. The colour-magitude diagrams (CMDs) extend below the main sequence turn-offs (MSTOs) and yield measurements of the ages of stellar populations. The stellar populations of faint three galaxies, Bo o I, CVn II and Leo IV dwarf spheroidal galaxies (dSphs) are estimated as old as a Galactic globular cluster M92. We confirm that Bo o I dSph has no intrinsic colour spread in the MSTO, and no spatial difference in the CMD morphology, which indicates that Bo o I dSph is composed of an old single stellar population. One of the brightest UFDs, CVn I dSph, shows a relatively younger age ( 12.6 Gyr) with respect to Bo o I, CVn II, and Leo IV dSphs, and the distribution of red horizontal branch (HB) stars is more concentrated toward the centre than that of blue HB stars, suggesting that the galaxy contains complex stellar populations.

gmw_00005910.name: NGC 3125
gmw_00005910.overview: NGC 3125 is a low-metallicity blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2405.12361] characterized by an amorphous elliptical shape [arXiv:astro-ph/9809015] and a bursty star formation history [arXiv:1311.2227]. Its central starburst region contains two star-forming knots [arXiv:astro-ph/9809015, arXiv:1311.2227] and a bipolar ionization cone [arXiv:1311.2227].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3125 as the strongest local He II 1640 source, with discrepant reddening indicators that may reflect very massive stars and nebular UV continuum.

quotes.quote: These eight selected galaxies are widely studied and well known for their young massive star-forming regions and therefore form an excellent sample to study their He II emission line properties. In Table  table:targets , we present the most important characteristics such as coordinates, redshift, and Galactic extinction values, and the STIS aperture widths for the galaxies. The present work is a pilot program to test the application of He II lines as dust tracers in nearby galaxies, thus, the sources were selected as the strongest He II emitters.
In particular, NGC 3125 is known to be the strongest He II  1640 source in the local Universe chandar_ngc_2004 and therefore provides excellent conditions to test these mechanisms.

Source A in NGC 3125 is the most discrepant data point in Figure  fig:compare_ebv . While the E(B-V)_ Balmer determined from the Balmer lines and from He II agrees reasonably well, the E(B-V)_ UV from the UV slope disagrees. The UV value of E(B-V)_ UV = 0.53 mag significantly exceeds the results obtained via the other two methods. Inspection of thespectrum taken with the Cosmic Origin Spectrograph (COS) confirms a very red UV slope and therefore high reddening.

sources.summary: The paper studies Tol 1004-296 as a blue compact dwarf with strong emission knots, low metallicity, no disk rotation, and a distinctive high-dispersion region between its main knots.

quotes.quote: Tol 1004-296 The dwarf galaxy Tol 1004-296, also known as NGC 3125, ESO 435-G 041, ESO 100419-2941.5, AM 1004-294, MCG 05-24-022, is classified as a BCG with a magnitude of M_V = -17.7 Bergvall1986 and a low metallicity of 12 + Log(O/H) = 8.34 Vacca1992 . It is located at a distance of 11.99 Mpc Local Group distance from Nasa Extragalactic Database . Smith1976 described it as an object with super condensations showing emission lines and a diffuse stellar continuum, from an objective prism survey Smith1975 . Spectroscopic survey by Penston1977 confirmed these objective prism data, exhibiting HeII 4686 emission line in one of the condensation. Reif1982 has detected \ content and Paturel2003 converted it to mass according to Pustilnik2007 , giving an M_ HI = 3.19 10^8 M_ .

Figure fig:VrDispTol1004-296 shows the radial velocity and the velocity dispersion maps of Tol 1004-296. In both maps, we have superimposed the isocontours from the associated H emission. The radial velocity ranges from 1137 5 km s ^ 1 to 1205 6 km s ^ 1 , between the different knots, showing a velocity gradient from the South East to the North West. The velocity amplitude between Knot 1 and Knot 2 is 25 4 km s ^ 1 (with Knot 1 being in the blue side). This velocity amplitude does not imply rotation.

sources.summary: The paper detects WC stars in NGC 3125, describes two bright central starburst knots, and identifies two Wolf-Rayet regions with line strengths relevant to burst modeling.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of WC stars in five Wolf-Rayet (W-R) galaxies: He 2-10, NGC 3049, NGC 3125, NGC 5253 and Tol 89. The faint broad \ line requires sufficiently high S/N ( 40) to be detected explaining the non-detection of this WC feature in previous observations. From the measurement of W-R emission lines ( + , , and ), we conclude that all W-R regions contain a mixed population of WNL, and early WC stars.

The optical appearance of NGC 3125 is an amorphous elliptical shape with a bright central starburst region dominated by two bright knots (regions A and B ) apparently connected by a bridge of fainter intensity.

Given the good signal-to-noise ratio of our observations, it has been possible to detect the W-R lines over a large extension around the maximum intensity. The size of the ``W-R regions'' ranges from 2 \ - 3 \ for Tol 89, NGC 3049, NGC 3125 and NGC 5253 to 6 \ for He 2-10. The W-R lines are detected in two distinct regions in NGC 3125 (separation 10 ) and NGC 5253 (separation 3 ).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3125 has bursty star formation, prominent star-forming knots, and a bipolar ionization cone indicating optically thin escape paths.

quotes.quote: NGC 3125 is a dwarf galaxy that has a bursty star formation history with roughly three recent epochs of star formation b:Vanzi_aap11 . This galaxy has two prominent star forming knots, knot A to the northwest and knot B to the southeast b:Vacca_apj92 ; both contain massive star clusters and are the main hosts for the Wolf-Rayet population in the galaxy. The ionized gas in NGC 3125 is concentrated around the star-forming knots towards the eastern side of the galaxy relative to the overall stellar distribution (Figure f:img_n3125 ). Figure f:ratios_3125 shows the emission-line ratio maps for NGC 3125. We discover a bipolar ionization cone in the emission-line gas extending northeast and southwest of knot B.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3125 as an aperture-effect outlier whose COS spectrum is redder than the IUE spectrum, likely because the IUE aperture captured multiple young star-forming clusters.

quotes.quote: There are a few strong outliers from the _ IUE vs _ COS trend in Figure fig3 , namely (5) IRAS 08339+6517, (8) NGC 3125, (15) NGC 3690, (18) NGC 4214, and (23) NGC 5236.
On the other hand, the COS spectra for (8) NGC 3125 and (18) NGC 4214 seem to have redder slopes than their corresponding IUE spectra.

However, the COS and IUE spectra for NGC 3125 differ by a larger amount. Given that we do not know the true orientation of the IUE aperture, it seems likely that the IUE aperture for NGC 3125 shown in Figure fig1 was actually rotated 45 clockwise such that it captured multiple young star-forming clusters and resulting in a bluer integrated continuum slope.

gmw_00005911.name: Tol 89
gmw_00005911.overview: Tol 89 is a giant H II region located in a spiral arm of the barred spiral galaxy NGC 5398 [arXiv:astro-ph/9809015]. It is a Wolf-Rayet galaxy that exhibits a spatial offset between its stellar continuum and nebular emission peaks [arXiv:astro-ph/9809015].

sources.summary: The paper detects WC stars in Tol 89, describes it as a giant H II region in NGC 5398, and highlights offset nebular and continuum structures plus unusually strong emission.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of WC stars in five Wolf-Rayet (W-R) galaxies: He 2-10, NGC 3049, NGC 3125, NGC 5253 and Tol 89.

A spatial offset between the multiple peaks of the nebular emission and the stellar light in He 2-10 and Tol 89 is observed. These nebular emission structures are likely due to the existence of bubbles and loops, owing to the injection of mechanical energy in the ISM through the W-R winds and/or supernovae.

Tol 89 is a giant \ region located in one of the spiral arms of the barred spiral galaxy NGC 5398, about 34 \ South-West of the nucleus. This object shows the largest shift between the spatial distribution of the continuum and nebular lines. The spatial distribution of the W-R lines is narrower than that of the nebular lines. W-R lines are found at the two regions where the continuum peaks.

gmw_00005912.name: GRB 120422A host galaxy
gmw_00005912.overview: The GRB 120422A host galaxy is a low-mass, star-forming galaxy characterized by a very young starburst and negligible extinction [arXiv:1401.3774]. It features two distinct populations of H II regions in its nucleus and is currently interacting with a neighboring galaxy [arXiv:1401.3774].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 120422A host as a low-mass, barely extinguished star-forming galaxy with young starburst properties, high metallicity, H II-region structure, and interaction evidence.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:Host_SED displays the observed host SED and its best fit. The observed SED is best described by a low-mass, barely-extinguished star-forming galaxy with a very young starburst (see Table  tab:host_prop ). The extracted attenuation and SFR are consistent with results from emission-line diagnostics.

The host galaxy and galaxy environment Our analysis in Sect. sec:environments revealed that i ) the extinction at the explosion site as well as the galaxy nucleus is negligible, ii ) there are two populations of H ii regions in the nucleus, iii ) the GRB appears to have not occurred in a region of enhanced SFR, iv ) the metallicities of the nucleus and the explosion site are high and identical to within errors, v ) the host galaxy is interacting with a galaxy at a projected distance of 23 kpc, and vi ) the host is possibly embedded in a galaxy group.

gmw_00005913.name: G1
gmw_00005913.overview: G1 is a companion galaxy located at a projected distance of 28.7 kpc from a gamma-ray burst host galaxy [arXiv:1401.3774]. The two galaxies are interacting, connected by a blue tidal arm that indicates recent star formation [arXiv:1401.3774].

sources.summary: G1 is identified as a galaxy at the GRB redshift, 28.7 kpc from the host nucleus, connected to the host by a blue tidal arm and likely interacting.

quotes.quote: Our GTC spectrum from 2012 April 25 elucidated that object G1 is at the same redshift as the GRB ( z=0.2831 ; Fig.  fig:GRB120422A_fov , fig:G1 , Table  tab:galaxy_environment ). The angular distance of 7 1 corresponds to a projected distance of 28.7 kpc at z=0.283 from the host galaxy's nucleus. Intriguingly, we detect a curved arm of emission, though not fully recovered, that connects G1 with the GRB host in our deep Gemini and Liverpool Telescope images (Fig.  fig:GRB120422A_fov ). The blue colour of the tidal arm points to recent star formation. Together with G1's blue colour, we have compelling evidence that both galaxies are interacting.

gmw_00005914.name: NGC 7195
gmw_00005914.overview: NGC 7195 is a passive spiral galaxy that features distinct spiral arms but shows no signs of star formation [arXiv:2312.09567]. A nearby stellar stream suggests that its neutral hydrogen gas may have been stripped through interactions with the neighboring galaxy NGC 7194 or other group members [arXiv:2312.09567].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7195 as a passive spiral candidate source for stripped H I, with no significant star formation, distinct arms, a nearby stellar stream, and insufficient expected H I mass.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: AGC320009 centered on R.A.: 22 ^ h 03 ^ m 31. ^ s 7, DEC.: +12 ^ 39 28 (J2000), which is NGC 7195

quotes.quote: Even though the existence of H 1 gas in a PSp galaxy has not been reported to date, NGC 7195 might be the most prime candidate. NGC 7195 is a typical PSp galaxy having no sign of significant star formation and distinct spiral arms. From simulations and observations, spiral arm structures are found to fade over several Gyrs, after gas is stripped ( Bek02 ; Pak19 ; Pak21 ). Since the stellar stream is located at the north-eastern vicinity of the NGC 7195, H 1 gas may be stripped from the NGC 7195 by interaction with the NGC 7194 galaxy and/or other members.

The expected H 1 mass range of NGC 7195 is from 4.3 10 ^8 M _ to 2.1 10 ^ 10 M _ at the stellar mass of log(M _ /M _ ) = 10.53 Cat18 . However, even if we consider the upper limit of the expected H 1 mass, this amount is smaller than the total H 1 mass that we found in this group.

gmw_00005915.name: PGC 67935
gmw_00005915.overview: PGC 67935 is an optically red, weakly star-forming galaxy with weak central H-alpha emission and no FUV detection [arXiv:2312.09567]. It features a clear shell structure and a diffuse stellar stream to its east, indicating past merging or interactions [arXiv:2312.09567].

sources.summary: The paper describes PGC 67935 as a mostly red, weakly star-forming group member with weak central H emission, no FUV detection, a shell structure, and an eastern diffuse stellar stream.

quotes.quote: PGC 67935 is optically red but has a weak H emission line at the center in the SDSS spectrum, which is a weak star-forming galaxy from the BPT diagram Bal81 . Its WISE color of W2 W3 is 2.19, which lies in the boundary between non star forming and weak star-forming galaxies. There is no detection in FUV.
For instance, PGC 67935 also has a clear shell structure and a diffuse stellar stream in the east, which is an evidence of merging or interactions.

gmw_00005916.name: PGC 67927
gmw_00005916.overview: PGC 67927 is a late-type spiral galaxy with a stellar mass of approximately $10^{10.5}$ solar masses [arXiv:2312.09567]. It is a gas-rich system containing substantial neutral hydrogen gas, though its optical morphology reveals an asymmetric spiral arm and a quenched bulge [arXiv:2312.09567].

sources.summary: The paper considers PGC 67927 as a possible flyby intruder, describing it as a nearby late-type galaxy with asymmetric spiral structure, quenched bulge, and substantial H I gas.

quotes.quote: There is a candidate, PGC 67927, which is located in a projected distance of 600 kpc apart from the NGC 7194 to the north-west. PGC 67927 is a late-type galaxy with stellar mass log(M _ /M _ ) 10.5 from the NSA catalog. The morphology of PGC 67927 in optical image shows asymmetry in its spiral arm and quenching in the bulge. PGC 67927 contains H 1 flux of S _ HI = 1.36 Jy km s ^ 1 Spr05 .

Assuming a distance of 118 Mpc from the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database, this corresponds to the H 1 mass of 4.5 10 ^ 9 M _ using the equation (2) in Chu09 , which means PGC 67927 is already a gas-rich system at its stellar mass based on H 1 scaling relation from Cat18 . Therefore, it is unlikely that PGC 67927 left a significant amount of H 1 over the NGC 7194 group.

gmw_00005917.name: RXS J1012.7+4229
gmw_00005917.overview: RXS J1012.7+4229 is a ultra-high-frequency-peaked BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1205.4939]. Radio observations reveal a total flux of approximately 79 mJy at 1.4 GHz and a jet structure that bends toward the northeast direction [arXiv:1205.4939].

sources.summary: The paper describes RXS J1012.7+4229 as a UHBL with 79 mJy 1.4 GHz flux and EVN/MERLIN evidence for a northeast-bending jet with two components.

quotes.quote: The total flux at 1.4 GHz for this source is around 79 mJy from both FIRST and the NRAO VLA Sky Survey (NVSS) . Our EVN, MERLIN and EVN+MERLIN images are shown in Fig. J . The EVN, EVN+MERLIN images show that there is a jet bending to the northeast direction. Consistently, there is a hint of jet structure towards the northeast direction in the MERLIN image. Two jet components are found with P.A. around 6 ^ and 25 ^ , respectively, which is shown in Table table_model .

gmw_00005918.name: RXS J1319+1405
gmw_00005918.overview: RXS J1319+1405 is a blazar with a total radio flux of approximately 65.8 mJy at 1.4 GHz [arXiv:1205.4939]. High-resolution imaging reveals a core-jet structure oriented toward the east, accompanied by weak emission at larger scales in the same direction [arXiv:1205.4939].

sources.summary: The paper reports that RXS J1319+1405 has a FIRST 1.4 GHz flux near 65.8 mJy, an eastward core-jet morphology, and larger-scale weak MERLIN emission.

quotes.quote: The total flux at 1.4 GHz for this source is around 65.8 mJy from FIRST catalogue. Our EVN and MERLIN images are presented in Fig. J . The EVN and EVN+MERLIN images of this source shows a core-jet structure with jet towards east direction with P.A. around 80 ^ (see Table table_model ). In the MERLIN image, the sign of weak emission is found at much larger distance, but at similar direction. The image parameters are shown in Table table_img .

gmw_00005919.name: RXS J1410+6100
gmw_00005919.overview: RXS J1410+6100 is an active galactic nucleus that exhibits a compact core morphology [arXiv:1205.4939]. Observations suggest the source may possess an inverted radio spectrum or experience flux density variations [arXiv:1205.4939].

sources.summary: The paper finds that RXS J1410+6100 has an accurate radio position, compact EVN/MERLIN morphology, and possible inverted spectrum or flux variability.

quotes.quote: Our results show that the accurate position for RXS J1341+3959 and RXS J1410+6100 are significantly different from the positions provided by NED, with position difference larger than 10^ '' . The accurate positions for these two sources can only be derived from our MERLIN data (see Table table_position ), which however, are much closer to that from the Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty Centimeters (FIRST) 1.4-GHz radio catalogue bec95 , with a position difference less than 0^ '' .3 and 0^ '' .5 , respectively.

The total flux at 1.4 GHz for this source is around 5.4 mJy from VLA FIRST. Similar to RXS J1341+3959, a compact core is observed from both the EVN and MERLIN images (Fig. J ), with the image parameters shown in Table table_img . We found that the EVN 5 GHz core flux density is about 7.2 mJy, which however is higher than the FIRST VLA 1.4 GHz total flux. This indicates that either the radio spectrum is invert, or there is variations in the flux at these two radio bands.

gmw_00005920.name: RXS J1458.4+4832
gmw_00005920.overview: RXS J1458.4+4832 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1205.4939] that exhibits a compact core at radio wavelengths [arXiv:1205.4939]. It displays weak, large-scale diffuse emission extending toward the northeast [arXiv:1205.4939] and shows indications of either an inverted spectrum or flux variability [arXiv:1205.4939].

sources.summary: The paper describes RXS J1458.4+4832 as a compact-core UHBL with weak northeast diffuse MERLIN emission, high 5 GHz flux, and possible variability or inverted spectrum.

quotes.quote: The total flux at 1.4 GHz for this source is around 2.5 mJy from VLA NVSS. We show our radio images in Fig. J . The EVN image shows a compact core, while MERLIN image shows weak diffuse emission at a large scale towards northeast direction. Similar to RXS J1410+6100, the EVN 5 GHz total flux 7.6 mJy and the MERLIN peak flux 18.4 mJy/beam are apparently larger than the total NVSS flux, which implies either a invert spectrum or flux variation in RXS J1458.4+4832.

gmw_00005921.name: RXS J2304.6+3705
gmw_00005921.overview: RXS J2304.6+3705 is a BL Lacertae object and blazar [arXiv:1205.4939]. It features a compact core with a peak flux of about 22 mJy/beam and a possible radio jet structure extending toward the northwest [arXiv:1205.4939].

sources.summary: The paper reports that RXS J2304.6+3705 has 22.5 mJy 1.4 GHz flux, a possible northwest EVN jet, and a compact MERLIN core.

quotes.quote: The total flux at 1.4 GHz for this source is around 22.5 mJy from VLA NVSS data. Our EVN image shows a possible jet structure towards northwest direction with P.A. 60^ (see Fig. J and Table table_model ). It has a total radio flux around 11 mJy from EVN image. The MERLIN image shows only a compact core, with peak flux about 22 mJy/beam.

gmw_00005922.name: Serenity-18
gmw_00005922.overview: Serenity-18 is a typical main-sequence galaxy at a redshift of approximately 5.939 [arXiv:1808.01146]. It exhibits active star formation and is associated with a metal-poor damped Lyman-alpha absorber located 40 kiloparsecs away [arXiv:1808.01146].

sources.summary: The paper reports Serenity-18 as a z~5.939 main-sequence galaxy detected in CO, with molecular gas, active star formation, and an associated metal-poor DLA feeding its growth.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of Serenity-18, a galaxy at z 5.939 for which we could measure the content of molecular gas, M( H _2) 5 10^9 M _ , traced by the CO(6-5) emission, together with the metal poor ( [ Fe/H ] =-3.08 0.12 , [ Si/H ] =-2.86 0.14 ) gas clump/filament which is possibly feeding its growth. The galaxy has an estimated star formation rate of 100 M_ yr ^ 1 , implying that it is a typical main sequence galaxy at these redshifts. The metal poor gas is detected through a damped Lyman- absorber (DLA) observed at a spatial separation of 40 kpc and at the same redshift of Serenity-18, along the line of sight to the quasar SDSS J2310+1855 ( z_ em 6.0025 ). The chemical abundances measured for the damped Lyman- system are in very good agreement with those measured for other DLAs discovered at similar redshifts, indicating an enrichment due to massive PopII stars. The galaxy/damped system that we discovered is a direct observational evidence of the assembly of a galaxy at the edge of the reionization epoch.

In this Letter we report the serendipitous discovery of a typical main sequence galaxy at z 5.939 (that we dubbed Serenity-18) detected in CO, associated with a metal poor DLA observed along the sightline to the quasar J231038.88+185519.7 ( z_ em =6.0025 , J2310 hereafter), using ALMA and XSHOOTER/Very Large Telescope (VLT) observations.

gmw_00005923.name: HFLS3
gmw_00005923.overview: HFLS3 is a high-redshift merging system at z ~ 6.34 [arXiv:1404.1378, arXiv:2308.16620] composed of multiple star-forming galaxies [arXiv:2308.16620]. The system is gravitationally lensed by two foreground galaxies, G1 and G2 [arXiv:1404.1378, arXiv:2308.16620], which are located to its north and south [arXiv:1404.1378].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G1 as one of two foreground galaxies near HFLS3, models its lensing and SED properties, and finds it contributes weakly to the lens model.

quotes.quote: The lensing masses are M_ lens = 1.2^ +6.4 _ 0.2 10^9 M _ and 1.2^ +0.2 _ 0.1 10^ 10 M _ for G1 and G2, respectively for the two component model. We find masses consistent within these errors for the case when HFLS3 is described by a single component. The lensing model is mostly sensitive to the mass of G2, while G1, the galaxy fatherst from HFLS3, remains as a minor contribution to the lens model. Therefore the mass of G1 is less constrained in the lens model.

For G1 and G2, we find stellar masses of 8 10^ 8 and 1 10^ 10 M _ , respectively. The ratio of stellar-to-lensing mass for two lensing galaxies at z=2.1 ranges from 0.66 to 0.85. We find that significant dust attenuation is present in the northern galaxy G1, with A_V 3.4 mag. We do not find mm-wave emission from that galaxy in our deep 1.1 mm interferometric continuum emission data, ruling out a sub-mm bright dusty galaxy at this location.

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies G2 as a foreground galaxy rather than HFLS3's rest-frame optical counterpart, and finds it dominates the lensing sensitivity with a larger lensing mass.

quotes.quote: As shown in Figure 1 (left panel), we detect optical emission from more than one galaxy near HFLS3 (galaxies labeled G1 and G2). This is similar to what was previously reported with Keck/NIRC2 LGS-AO imaging data, with the southern component (G2) taken to be the rest-frame optical counterpart to HFLS3 Riechers13 . If this assumption is correct we expect the southern component to be invisible in the shortest wavelength images, as it is a Lyman drop-out at wavelengths shorter than 8900 . Here, however, we have detected both galaxies in Hubble /ACS images, establishing that G2 is a galaxy at z < 5 .

The lensing masses are M_ lens = 1.2^ +6.4 _ 0.2 10^9 M _ and 1.2^ +0.2 _ 0.1 10^ 10 M _ for G1 and G2, respectively for the two component model. We find masses consistent within these errors for the case when HFLS3 is described by a single component. The lensing model is mostly sensitive to the mass of G2, while G1, the galaxy fatherst from HFLS3, remains as a minor contribution to the lens model. Therefore the mass of G1 is less constrained in the lens model.

sources.summary: The paper studies HFLS3 as a z=6.34 dusty starburst, revising its rest-UV counterpart and lensing magnification, and deriving high star-formation, dust, and stellar masses.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: HFLS3 RA & 17:06:47.80 & Riechers et al. 2013 Dec & +58:46:23.51 & Riechers et al.

quotes.quote: We discuss the rest-frame ultraviolet emission from the starbursting galaxy HFLS3 at a redshift of 6.34. The galaxy was discovered in Herschel /SPIRE data due to its red color in the sub-mm wavelengths from 250 to 500 m. The apparent instantaneous star-formation rate of HFLS3 inferred from the total far-IR luminosity measured with over 15 photometric data points between 100 and 1000 m is 2900 M _ yr ^ 1 .

The lack of multiple imaging constrains the lensing magnification to be lower than either 2.7 or 3.5 at the 95 confidence level for the two scenarios, which attribute one or two components to HFLS3 in the source plane. Once accounting for the possibility of gravitational lensing, the instantaneous star-formation rate is 1320 M _ yr ^ 1 with the 95 confidence lower limit around 830 M _ yr ^ 1 . Using models for the rest-frame UV to far-IR spectral energy distribution (SED) we determine the average star-formation rate over the last 100 Myr to be around 660 M _ yr ^ 1 . The dust and stellar masses of HFLS3 from the same SED models are at the level of 3 10^8 M _ and 5 10^ 10 M _ , respectively, with large systematic uncertainties on assumptions related to the SED model.

sources.summary: The paper reinterprets HFLS3 as a lensed, high-redshift merging system rather than a single starburst, resolving multiple z>6 galaxies with JWST/NIRSpec and updated lens modeling.

quotes.quote: One such source is HFLS3 ( z 6.34 ), which was originally identified as an extreme starburst galaxy with mild gravitational magnification ( 2.2 ). Here, we present new observations of HFLS3 with the JWST/NIRSpec IFU in both low (PRISM/CLEAR; R 100 ) and high spectral resolution (G395H/290LP; R 2700 ), with high spatial resolution ( 0.1'' ) and sensitivity. Thanks to the combination of the NIRSpec data and a new lensing model with accurate spectroscopic redshifts, we find that the 3'' 3'' field is crowded, with a lensed arc (C, z=6.3425 0.0002 ), two galaxies to the south (S _1 and S _2 , z=6.3592 0.0001 ), two galaxies to the west (W _1 , z=6.3550 0.0001 ; W _2 , z=6.3628 0.0001 ), and two low-redshift interlopers (G1, z=3.4806 0.0001 ; G2, z=2.00 0.01 ).

We perform gravitational lens modelling, finding evidence for a two-source composition of the lensed central object and a comparable magnification factor ( =2.1-2.4 ) to previous work. The projected distances and velocity offsets of each galaxy suggest that they will merge within the next 1 Gyr. Finally, we examine the dust extinction-corrected SFR _ H of each z>6 source, finding that the total star formation ( 460 90 M _ yr ^ 1 , magnification-corrected) is distributed across the six z 6.34-6.36 objects over a region of diameter 11 kpc. Altogether, this suggests that HFLS3 is not a single starburst galaxy, but instead is a merging system of star-forming galaxies in the Epoch of Reionization.

gmw_00005924.name: UGC 4754
gmw_00005924.overview: UGC 4754 is a nearby, edge-on Sbc spiral galaxy [arXiv:1005.1773, arXiv:1108.5056]. Far-infrared observations of the galaxy reveal emission that is spatially resolved but exceeds the predictions of standard optical radiative-transfer models by a factor of two to three [arXiv:1005.1773, arXiv:1005.3337].

sources.summary: The paper uses Herschel observations and radiative-transfer modeling of UGC 4754, finding the edge-on spiral is resolved but its far-infrared emission exceeds optical-model predictions by two to three times.

quotes.quote: We use Herschel PACS and SPIRE observations of the edge-on spiral galaxy UGC 4754, taken as part of the H-ATLAS SDP observations, to investigate the dust energy balance in this galaxy. We build detailed SKIRT radiative models based on SDSS and UKIDSS maps and use these models to predict the far-infrared emission. We find that our radiative transfer model underestimates the observed FIR emission by a factor of two to three.

In this Letter, we investigate the dust energy balance in the nearby spiral galaxy UGC 4754 as a first case. This Sbc galaxy is the nearest v_r=5662 45 km/s; 2009MNRAS.399..683J and largest edge-on spiral galaxy in the 16 deg ^2 field observed during the H-ATLAS science demonstration phase (SDP). We present detailed radiative transfer simulations of this galaxy that fit optical and near-infrared imaging data, compute synthetic FIR fluxes and images, and compare the model predictions to the Herschel observations at 100, 160, 250, 350, and 500 m.
UGC 4754 was clearly detected and spatially resolved in all the observed bands (Rigby et al. \ 2010, in prep.; Smith et al. \ 2010a, in prep.).

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 4754 as an edge-on Sbc spiral template to test SKIRT radiative-transfer models, refining earlier smooth LTE modeling into clumpy 3D NLTE dust-emission simulations.

quotes.quote: In order to run simulations in a realistic setting, we adopt the Sbc galaxy UGC 4754 as a template model. UGC 4754 is a edge-on spiral galaxy, which has always been a favorite class of galaxies for radiative transfer modellers, as the dust is clearly visible both in absorption and emission e.g. 1997A&A...325..135X, 1998A&A...331..894X, 1999A&A...344..868X, 2000A&A...362..138P, 2011A&A...527A.109P, 2001A&A...372..775M, 2005A&A...437..447D, 2007A&A...471..765B, 2008A&A...490..461B . This galaxy was one of the first large edge-on galaxies to be observed with the Herschel Space Observatory as part of the Herschel Astrophysical Terahertz Large Area Survey H-ATLAS, 2010PASP..122..499E science demonstration phase observations. In 2010A&A...518L..39B , we fitted a radiative model to the observed images of UGC 4754 in the SDSS and UKIDSS bands. We subsequently used the SKIRT code to predict the galaxy's SED and images at FIR wavelengths. While the radiative transfer model used in that paper was sufficient to serve its goal (investigation of the dust energy balance), it suffered from two significant limitations: the assumptions of LTE dust emission and of a smooth interstellar medium.

sources.summary: The paper uses Herschel observations and SKIRT radiative-transfer models of edge-on spiral UGC 4754, finding the model underpredicts far-infrared emission by a factor of two to three.

quotes.quote: We have used Herschel PACS and SPIRE observations of the edge-on spiral galaxy UGC 4754 to investigate its dust energy balance (for more details, see Baes et al. \ 2010). We build detailed SKIRT radiative models based on SDSS and UKIDSS maps, and use these models to predict the far-infrared emission. We find that our radiative transfer model underestimates the observed FIR emission by a factor two to three (see Fig. ).

gmw_00005925.name: NGC 1140
gmw_00005925.overview: NGC 1140 is a nearby, low-metallicity blue compact dwarf [arXiv:1001.0698] and barred irregular galaxy [arXiv:2202.08231] that has likely undergone a merger within the past 1 Gyr [arXiv:0709.3394]. This merger event is thought to have triggered its active starburst [arXiv:0709.3394], which hosts numerous young massive clusters [arXiv:1001.0698] and drives localized superbubble outflows [arXiv:1001.0698].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1140 as a nearby low-metallicity blue compact dwarf starburst, mapping ionized gas kinematics and finding superbubble-driven outflow signatures without a coherent galaxy-scale wind.

quotes.quote: We present deep WIYN H SparsePak and DensePak spatially-resolved optical spectroscopy of the dwarf irregular starburst galaxy NGC 1140. The different spatial resolutions and coverage of the two sets of observations have allowed us to investigate the properties and kinematics of the warm ionized gas within both the central regions of the galaxy and the inner halo.

NGC 1140 is a nearby 20 Mpc; moll07 , low metallicity (LMC-like, 0.4 ; izotov04 ; nagao06 ; moll07 ) blue compact dwarf (BCD) galaxy with a total star formation rate of 0.7 0.3 yr ^ 1 (Hunter, van Woerden Gallagher 1994, hereafter hunter94b ; moll07 ). The current star formation is dominated by an extremely H luminous central concentration that is known to host a number of young massive clusters (YMCs) with ages < 5 Myr and masses > 10^5 \ ( hunter94a ; moll07 ); this extreme youth is supported by the detection of strong WR star signatures moll07 .

Our data clearly show that the ionized gas in the central 20 20 arcsecs ( 2 2 kpc) region of the galaxy is kinematically decoupled from the rest of the system, and rotates at a PA approximately perpendicular to that of the main body of the galaxy at +40^ . This finding of yet another misaligned structural component is not unsurprising, and only adds weight to the interaction/merger scenario proposed by hunter94b . The starburst environment and the properties of the outflowing gas We see no evidence of coherent large-scale outflows within NGC 1140. Instead, the H morphology (Fig fig:SP_finder ), the fact that we find multiple narrow line components with velocity separations of 10--60 \ within a radius of 1--1.5 kpc, and the fact that beyond 2 kpc the [ S ] /H ratios rise significantly implying a strong contribution to the excitation levels by shocks, all suggest that locally intense star formation is driving material outwards in the form of a series of interacting superbubbles/shells (i.e. \ the ``froth'').

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 1140 as a barred irregular, metal-poor star-forming galaxy whose morphology, SSCs, shocks, and unusual CO excitation may reflect merger-driven activity.

quotes.quote: NGC 1140 (Mrk 1063) : NGC 1140 (Figure fig9 ) is a barred irregular galaxy at a distance of 20 Mpc with a metallicity of about 12 + log(O/H) = 8.2 . Its irregular morphology, revealed by H images shows a southwestern chain of H ii regions, the tail, and a northern region, the head, which dominates the emission and star forming activity. This head, with 30 times the H luminosity of the giant H ii region 30 Dor in the LMC , is surrounded by less conspicuous star forming regions. The dominant complex, about 500 pc in size, contains 6--7 blue SSCs , the most luminous of them consisting of several thousand O stars .

There is no galactic scale outflow, but violent shocks from the SSCs are disrupting the ISM on scales of 1--2 pc . Shape and activity of the galaxy may be caused by a merger . As in NGC 5253 (see below), CO is highly excited and not optically thick, with J = 3 2 line flux densities far surpassing those of the J = 1 0 line. Naively, one would therefore expect high kinetic temperatures of the molecular gas giving rise to the observed CO emission. However, model fits including the dust continuum and molecular and atomic abundances relative to hydrogen reveal high densities ( n (H _2 ) > 10 ^5 cm ^ 3 ) but low temperatures ( T_ kin 20 K), CO/C i 0.1, ^ 12 CO/ ^ 13 CO 8--20 and N (CO) 5 10 ^ 16 cm ^ 2 for a realistic gas-to-dust mass ratio of 380 . The low kinetic temperature in combination with a rather low opacity ( ( J = 3 2) 0.2) can be explained if we assume that CO is only existing in spatially highly confined particularly well shielded pockets, where irradiation by UV photons is not as strong as elsewhere in the complex. Overall, the CO fractional abundance turns out to be 8 10 ^ 7 , two orders of magnitude below the commonly encountered value (HCN remains undetected).

sources.summary: The paper presents HST and VLT observations of NGC 1140, describing it as a low-metallicity Wolf-Rayet/star-forming galaxy with central clusters, merger evidence, and high star-formation activity.

quotes.quote: NGC 1140 is a low-metallicity WR galaxy at a distance of 20 Mpc Based on the heliocentric velocity of the galaxy, corrected for the Virgocentric flow, and assuming H_0 = 70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ; adopted from the HyperLeda database at http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ and is a prime example of a nearby analogue of the star-forming galaxies identified at high redshifts. HI and optical observations indicate that the galaxy has undergone a merger within the past 1 Gyr and it is thought that this event is responsible for the plethora of YMCs that the galaxy hosts. Star clusters have been imaged with both the Wide Field/Planetary Camera (WF/PC) and the Wide Field Planetary Camera 2 (WFPC2) on the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) by hunter94_ocon hunter94_ocon Hunter et al. (1994a) and degrijs04_1140 .

The central region of NGC 1140 was observed with UVES on the VLT Kueyen Telescope (UT2) in Chile on 19-20 September 2003. UVES is a cross-dispersed echelle spectrograph, with two arms -- a blue arm comprising a single EEV CCD and a red arm comprising a mosaic of an EEV and a MIT-LL CCD. Using the dichroic beam splitter, red and blue data were taken simultaneously: cross-disperser 4 with 312 l mm ^ 1 was centred on 840 nm and cross-disperser 2 with 660 l mm ^ 1 was centred on 437 nm.

Using the equation: SFR (M _ yr ^ 1 ) &=& 7.9 10^ 42 L(H ) (erg s ^ 1 ) &=& 1.08 10^ 53 Q_0^ Obs ( photons s ^ 1 ) kennicutt98 , we estimate the SFR of the galaxy of 0.7 0.3 M _ yr ^ 1 , in good agreement with the SFR of 0.8 M _ yr ^ 1 , obtained from ground-based continuum subtracted H imaging by hunter94_vanwoerd , after adjustment to our adopted distance.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1140 as a dwarf star-forming galaxy with strong [NeIII], PDR contribution, composite young and older stellar populations, and more PAH emission than NGC 1569 and IIZw40.

quotes.quote: While the UIBs are not obvious in the spectra of IIZw40 and SBS0335-052, and are only very weakly present NGC 1569, they can be distinguished in the spectrum of NGC 1140. In contrast, NGC 1140, which has a very flat continuum, yet very strong [ NeIII ] line, does have a more obvious contribution from PDR regions in its spectra. These galaxies contain evidence for Wolf-Rayet stars and super star clusters have been detected in NGC 1140 , NGC 1569 and SBS0335-052 .

For NGC 1569, NGC 1140 and IIZw40, we construct composite stellar SEDs that require a 75 to 95 mass fraction of an 'older' population ranging in age from about 10 Myr to 30 Myr along with 5 to 30 of a very young population, < 5 Myr. This model gives a PAH/VSG mass ratio for NGC 1569 and IIZw 40 of 2 to 3x10 ^ 4 and 10 times this for NGC 1140. On the contrary, in NGC 1140, where PAHs are more obvious in the MIR spectra (Figure cvfs ), we do not detect [ CII ] .

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 1140's central starburst, finding widespread [FeII], Brackett emission concentrated northward, and evidence for galaxy-wide star formation induced 35-55 Myr ago.

quotes.quote: Moving to lower redshifts, Richard de Grijs has lead a project with the CIRPASS IFU to study the central star-burst region of NGC 1140. Our 1.45-1.67 m wavelength coverage includes the bright [ FeII ] 1.64 m emission line, as well as high-order Brackett hydrogen lines. While strong [ FeII ] emission, thought to originate in the thermal shocks associated with supernova remnants, is found throughout the galaxy, both Br 12--4 and Br 14--4 emission are predominantly associated with the northern starburst region.

The Brackett lines originate from recombination processes occurring on smaller scales in (young) HII regions. The time-scale associated with strong [ FeII ] line emission implies that most of the recent starburst event in NGC 1140 was induced in the past 35--55 Myr. Based on the spatial distributions of the [ FeII ] versus Brackett line emission, we conclude that a galaxy-wide starburst was induced several tens of Myr ago, with more recent starburst activity concentrated around the northern starburst region.

gmw_00005926.name: A68/C0
gmw_00005926.overview: A68/C0 is a triply imaged spiral galaxy gravitationally lensed by the massive galaxy cluster Abell 68 [arXiv:1310.2655, arXiv:1408.0816]. It hosts a young, obscured starburst or post-starburst stellar population [arXiv:1310.2655] and exhibits a high molecular gas fraction with a double-peaked CO emission line indicating rotation [arXiv:1408.0816].

sources.summary: The paper describes A68/C0 as a triply imaged lensed spiral behind Abell 68, with SED fits indicating a young obscured starburst or post-starburst population.

quotes.quote: A68/C0: C0 is a triply-imaged spiral galaxy lying behind the core of the massive galaxy cluster Abell 68. The two most magnified images ( a b ) form a single continuous broad arc close to the BCG. They are shown in Figure C0_image . A third, less magnified and less distorted image ( ) of this galaxy appears further out on the opposite side of the BCG. This third image clearly shows the spiral nature of this galaxy.

The SED fits of A68/C0 performed on the visible to NIR photometry can be seen in Fig. fig_c0_comb , and show the best solution for Calzetti, and the best one for SMC. In terms of 2 the overall best fit was obtained with the Calzetti based, declining SFR ( = 300 Myr) model that includes nebular emission. It interprets C0 as an obscured ( A_ V = 1.7 ) population involving a starburst/post-starburst in a rather young age, t = 180 Myrs, with a well sustained (t)/ of 36 (see Table selected_model_table for error estimates).

sources.summary: The paper reports a secure CO(2-1) detection in lensed spiral A68-C0, unresolved mirrored images, a high gas fraction, and a double-peaked line indicating rotation.

quotes.quote: A68-C0 The CO(2--1) intensity peak is detected at 11 in A68-C0, which makes it a very secure CO detection in this z_ H = 1.5864 galaxy, as shown in Figs. fig:COmaps and fig:COspectra . The CO(2--1) emission is well centred on the HST images to within 0.5'' . The HST images clearly reveal the presence of two counterpart images of the same galaxy with a morphology typical of a spiral galaxy. The spiral structure is strongly stretched and extended by gravitational lensing, but the two counterpart images that are mirrored are easily identified by the two bright bulges observed north and south with the critical line passing in between. The two counterpart images of this galaxy are not resolved in CO, but they both contribute to the detected CO emission given the CO emission intensity precisely peaking on the critical line and the CO elongation in the direction of the stretch.

The derived lensing-corrected molecular gas fraction f_ gas = 0.38 is much higher than the molecular gas fractions of local spirals, but is comparable to the f_ gas distribution of SFGs with CO measurements from the literature in the z_ 1.2 = [ 1,1.6 ] interval which has a mean f_ gas ^ z_ 1.2 = 0.46 0.15 (see Sect. sect:fgas-z ). The observed double-peaked CO emission line profile is a first strong indication of rotation in A68-C0 daddi10a . Two additional observational facts support rotation in this galaxy.

gmw_00005927.name: A68/h7
gmw_00005927.overview: A68/h7 is a gravitationally lensed galaxy system at a redshift of approximately 2.15 [arXiv:1408.0816, arXiv:1310.2655]. It consists of four interacting components [arXiv:1310.2655] that exhibit a clumpy morphology [arXiv:1408.0816] and are characterized by high infrared luminosity and dusty star formation [arXiv:1408.0816, arXiv:1310.2655].

sources.summary: The paper reports A68-h7 as a z=2.1529 lensed galaxy with high infrared luminosity, detected broad CO(3-2), comparable gas fraction, and possible rotating clumpy morphology.

quotes.quote: A68-h7 This z_ CO = 2.1529 galaxy is less magnified with = 3 and characterized by a higher intrinsic IR luminosity L_ IR = 18.3 10^ 11 L_ in comparison to the other galaxies from our sample. It has thus been observed at the IRAM 30 m telescope. The CO(3--2) spectrum shows a broad line detected at 3-4 and well fitted by a single Gaussian function (see Fig. fig:COspectra ). It has a full width half maximum FWHM_ CO = 282 km s^ 1 and an observed integrated flux F_ CO = 1.12 0.22 Jy km s^ 1 . A redshift offset is observed in A68-h7 between the CO redshift and the redshift determined in this case from weak absorption lines in the rest-frame UV, z = (z_ rest-UV z_ CO )= -0.0029 .

The derived lensing-corrected molecular gas fraction f_ gas = 0.33 is comparable to the f_ gas distribution of SFGs with CO measurements from the literature in the z_ 2.2 = [ 2,2.5 ] interval which has a mean f_ gas ^ z_ 2.2 = 0.45 0.22 (see Sect. sect:fgas-z ). The observed double-peaked CO emission line profile, which would require higher signal-to-noise ratio data to be confirmed (in particular for the second component sampled by two channels only given its small full width half maximum FWHM_ CO = 90 km s^ 1 ; see Fig. fig:COspectra ), sustains evidence for rotation in A68-h7 daddi10a . Its morphology in the HST images reveals several components, which probably all contribute to the CO emission signal and may be the analogues of giant star-forming clumps seen in disk-like simulated systems agertz09,genel10,bournaud11 .

sources.summary: The paper presents A68/h7 as four interacting galaxies at z=2.15, confirmed by CO observations, with red photometry and degenerate SED solutions consistent with dusty star formation.

quotes.quote: A68/h7: This source, also located in the field of the cluster Abell 68, consists of a system of four galaxies in interaction (Fig. C0_image ). We have obtained a VLT/FORS2 spectrum of this object from which we have identified faint C II and C IV lines and estimated its redshift to z = 2.15 . We then confirmed the redshift with our CO observations (Dessauges-Zavadsky et al., in prep. ). Although the FORS2 slit was positioned on the brightest (east most) component, the photometry and SED of all of the individual components is consistent with all of them lying at the same redshift.

The CO spectrum further shows only a single line of FWHM = 350 km/s. This strongly suggests that the four components are in some form of interaction, but the exact configuration thereof remains uncertain. It is possible, for example, that the system is made of a weakly interacting pair of two ongoing mergers.

gmw_00005928.name: A68/nn4
gmw_00005928.overview: A68/nn4 is an interacting pair of objects located at a redshift of z = 3.19 [arXiv:1310.2655]. The system is modestly lensed by the galaxy cluster A68, making the highly obscured, far-infrared emitting component intrinsically luminous [arXiv:1310.2655].

sources.summary: The paper studies A68/nn4 as the obscured component of an interacting pair at z=3.19, finding a modestly lensed, intrinsically luminous source.

quotes.quote: A68/nn4: This source consists of a pair of objects in interaction. It has the highest redshift of the sample, that is z = 3.19. Here, we study the most obscured of the two components, which is also the one that appears to be the most related to the FIR emission. It is undetected from the R-band and bluewards. This source lies in the outskirts of A68, so it is modestly lensed ( 2.3 ), and thus intrinsically luminous.

gmw_00005929.name: MACS0451
gmw_00005929.overview: MACS0451 is a highly magnified, elongated giant arc galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.013 [arXiv:1310.2655]. Its infrared emission varies across the arc, containing both a star-forming component and an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1310.2655].

sources.summary: The paper describes MACS0451 as a highly magnified z=2.013 giant arc whose FIR emission varies along the arc and includes separable AGN and star-forming components.

quotes.quote: MACS0451: This is a very elongated arc and highly magnified ( 49 ) source at redshift z=2.013 in the field of the cluster MACSJ0451.9+0006 2010MNRAS.404.1247J . The arc measures 20" in length, and so this source is spatially resolved up to 250 . When examining the FIR SED of this source, we have noticed differences between the northern and the southern parts of the arc, with the first peaking at 250 \ and the latter at 100 , indicating very hot dust. After careful analysis of this object, we came to the conclusion that the infrared emission of this galaxy includes an AGN component.

The northern part of the arc in MACS0451 consists of two mirror images of the same part of the source, and is therefore also crossed by a critical line. The 100 \ emission, in this case also, does not appear to be any brighter near the critical line region. The region further appears bluer than the rest of the galaxy in [ optical - IRAC ] colors, so that, again, the dusty and infrared-bright regions appear to be distributed, just as the optical/NIR light, throughout the whole image.

gmw_00005930.name: MACSJ0916-JFG1
gmw_00005930.overview: MACSJ0916-JFG1 is a blue jellyfish galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.330 [arXiv:2209.07189]. It features a disturbed, asymmetric morphology with a short, five-kiloparsec tail on its eastern side and several star-forming knots in both its disk and tail [arXiv:2209.07189].

sources.summary: The paper studies MACSJ0916-JFG1 as a blue jellyfish galaxy with a short eastern tail, star-forming disk and tail knots, dynamically cold ionized gas, and IFU observations.

quotes.quote: We study the ionized gas properties of five jellyfish galaxies in massive clusters with Gemini GMOS/IFU observations: MACSJ0916-JFG1 ( z=0.330 ), MACSJ1752-JFG2 ( z=0.353 ), A2744-F0083 ( z=0.303 ), MACSJ1258-JFG1 ( z=0.342 ), and MACSJ1720-JFG1 ( z=0.383 ). BPT diagrams show that various mechanisms (star formation, AGN, or mixed effects) are ionizing gas in these galaxies. Radial velocity distributions of ionized gas seem to follow disk rotation of galaxies, with the appearance of a few high-velocity components in the tails as a sign of RPS.

In these HST optical images, all the jellyfish galaxies show disturbed and asymmetric morphology with blue tails and bright star-forming knots. MACSJ0916-JFG1 has a short ( 5 kpc ) tail at the eastern side of the galactic center and a few star-forming knots in the disk and tail. MACSJ1752-JFG2 shows not only an extended ( 25 kpc ) tail towards the southern direction, but also large bright knots with ongoing star formation around the disk. A2744-F0083 is the most spectacular object exhibiting large and bright star-forming knots and multiple stripped tails. MACSJ1258-JFG1 has a bright tail towards the northern direction with a length of 10 kpc and small blue extraplanar knots outside the disk. MACSJ1720-JFG1 is the most distant galaxy in this study. This galaxy shows a star-forming tail in the northern region and a bright compressed region at its head.

gmw_00005931.name: MACSJ1752-JFG2
