gmw_00005931.overview: MACSJ1752-JFG2 is a jellyfish galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.353 [arXiv:2209.07189]. It features a 25 kpc southern tail and disturbed regions with luminous blue knots, which indicate ongoing star formation in these substructures [arXiv:2209.07189].

sources.summary: The paper describes MACSJ1752-JFG2 as a jellyfish galaxy with a 25 kpc southern tail, luminous disturbed knots, and strong disk and tail star formation.

quotes.quote: We study the ionized gas properties of five jellyfish galaxies in massive clusters with Gemini GMOS/IFU observations: MACSJ0916-JFG1 ( z=0.330 ), MACSJ1752-JFG2 ( z=0.353 ), A2744-F0083 ( z=0.303 ), MACSJ1258-JFG1 ( z=0.342 ), and MACSJ1720-JFG1 ( z=0.383 ). BPT diagrams show that various mechanisms (star formation, AGN, or mixed effects) are ionizing gas in these galaxies. Radial velocity distributions of ionized gas seem to follow disk rotation of galaxies, with the appearance of a few high-velocity components in the tails as a sign of RPS.

In blue Figure fig:IFU2 , MACSJ1752-JFG2 shows a long tail extending 25 kpc ( 5 in the angular scale) from the galactic center towards the southern direction. In the flux distribution, our GMOS/IFU data successfully covers the southern tail except for a few faint blue knots in the outer region. This galaxy also shows two disturbed regions at a galactocentric distance of 5 kpc towards the south and east directions, exhibiting luminous blue knots. The flux distribution reproduces these peculiar substructures very well, which is indicative of ongoing star formation in the substructures. In the SFR surface density map, the ``tail'' region outside the 1 boundary (red dashed ellipse) includes the southern tail and some star-forming knots in the east.

gmw_00005932.name: A2744-F0083
gmw_00005932.overview: A2744-F0083 is a jellyfish galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.303 [arXiv:2209.07189]. It features a highly asymmetric flux distribution, multiple non-ordered tails, and numerous bright star-forming knots outside its main disk [arXiv:2209.07189].

sources.summary: The paper presents A2744-F0083 as a spectacular jellyfish galaxy with numerous star-forming knots, multiple stripped tails, asymmetric emission, and strong disk/tail star formation.

quotes.quote: We study the ionized gas properties of five jellyfish galaxies in massive clusters with Gemini GMOS/IFU observations: MACSJ0916-JFG1 ( z=0.330 ), MACSJ1752-JFG2 ( z=0.353 ), A2744-F0083 ( z=0.303 ), MACSJ1258-JFG1 ( z=0.342 ), and MACSJ1720-JFG1 ( z=0.383 ). BPT diagrams show that various mechanisms (star formation, AGN, or mixed effects) are ionizing gas in these galaxies. Radial velocity distributions of ionized gas seem to follow disk rotation of galaxies, with the appearance of a few high-velocity components in the tails as a sign of RPS.

A2744-F0083 is the most spectacular galaxy, exhibiting numerous star-forming knots outside the main disk and non-ordered tails. There are very bright star-forming knots at both the eastern and western sides of the disk. These knots have comparable sizes and luminosities to compact dwarf galaxies owe12 . The flux distribution is highly asymmetric, peaking in the eastern region. There are also long tails and extraplanar knots to 25 kpc ( 5 5 ) towards the southwestern direction from the center, but the S/N of our GMOS/IFU data is very low in these regions.

gmw_00005933.name: MACSJ1258-JFG1
gmw_00005933.overview: MACSJ1258-JFG1 is a jellyfish galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.342 that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2209.07189]. The galaxy features a western tail and outer disk where gas ionization is driven by a combination of star formation and shock-heating mechanisms [arXiv:2209.07189].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MACSJ1258-JFG1 as an AGN-hosting jellyfish galaxy with a 10 kpc northern tail, extraplanar knots, radial line-ratio gradients, and mixed ionization in outer regions.

quotes.quote: We study the ionized gas properties of five jellyfish galaxies in massive clusters with Gemini GMOS/IFU observations: MACSJ0916-JFG1 ( z=0.330 ), MACSJ1752-JFG2 ( z=0.353 ), A2744-F0083 ( z=0.303 ), MACSJ1258-JFG1 ( z=0.342 ), and MACSJ1720-JFG1 ( z=0.383 ). BPT diagrams show that various mechanisms (star formation, AGN, or mixed effects) are ionizing gas in these galaxies. Radial velocity distributions of ionized gas seem to follow disk rotation of galaxies, with the appearance of a few high-velocity components in the tails as a sign of RPS.

MACSJ1258-JFG1 has high flux ratios of log( [ O 3 ] 5007/ H ) 0.5 and log( [ N 2 ] 6584/ H ) 0.25 , indicating that it hosts an AGN. In this figure, several Voronoi bins in the central region and the head region in the east show low S/N of and [ O 3 ] lines. However, the Voronoi bins with S/N >3 show that there is a clear radial gradient of the BPT line ratios from the center to the outer region. The outer disk and tail regions on the western side are classified as composite regions, implying that both star formation and shock-heating mechanisms contribute to gas ionization there.

gmw_00005934.name: MACSJ1720-JFG1
gmw_00005934.overview: MACSJ1720-JFG1 is a jellyfish galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.383 [arXiv:2209.07189]. It features a bright, compressed region at its head and a star-forming tail in the northern region that extends in a direction opposite to the cluster center [arXiv:2209.07189].

sources.summary: The paper describes MACSJ1720-JFG1 as the most distant jellyfish galaxy in the sample, with a northern star-forming tail, compressed head, opposite-cluster tail orientation, and composite ionization.

quotes.quote: We study the ionized gas properties of five jellyfish galaxies in massive clusters with Gemini GMOS/IFU observations: MACSJ0916-JFG1 ( z=0.330 ), MACSJ1752-JFG2 ( z=0.353 ), A2744-F0083 ( z=0.303 ), MACSJ1258-JFG1 ( z=0.342 ), and MACSJ1720-JFG1 ( z=0.383 ). BPT diagrams show that various mechanisms (star formation, AGN, or mixed effects) are ionizing gas in these galaxies. Radial velocity distributions of ionized gas seem to follow disk rotation of galaxies, with the appearance of a few high-velocity components in the tails as a sign of RPS.

MACSJ1720-JFG1 is the most distant galaxy in this study. This galaxy shows a star-forming tail in the northern region and a bright compressed region at its head. The FOV of the GMOS/IFU is wide enough to cover the substructures of the jellyfish galaxies except for a few faint tails at the western side of A2744-F0083. For these jellyfish galaxies, there seems to be no clear trend between the direction of the tails and their direction towards the cluster center. Tails of MACSJ0916-JFG1 and MACSJ1752-JFG2 are extended in a direction nearly tangential to the cluster center. In contrast, tails of MACSJ1258-JFG1 and MACSJ1720-JFG1 are extended towards the opposite direction to the cluster center.

gmw_00005935.name: PEARLSDG
gmw_00005935.overview: PEARLSDG is an isolated, quiescent dwarf galaxy located at a distance of approximately 31 Mpc [arXiv:2309.16028]. It is one of the most isolated quenched dwarf galaxies observed, with no massive companion galaxies within 1 Mpc [arXiv:2309.16028].

sources.summary: The paper reports PEARLSDG as an isolated quiescent dwarf at about 31 Mpc, with resolved RGB stars and no nearby massive companion.

quotes.quote: As a precursor to this potential wealth of discovery, we report the serendipitous discovery of an isolated, quiescent, classical dwarf galaxy at RA =12^ h 12^ m 18^ s , Dec.= +27^ d 35^ m 24^ s , known as throughout, in imaging of the CLG1212 cluster as part of the Prime Extragalactic Areas for Reionization and Lensing Science (PEARLS) program Windhorst2023 . While this galaxy has been photometrically identified in other surveys (DECaLS and SDSS), imaging is able to identify individual red-giant-branch stars constraining its distance to 31 2.2 Mpc. Follow-up optical spectroscopy suggests that it is isolated from nearby massive galaxies and spectral energy distribution fitting confirms that it is quiescent.

Note in Fig. fig:galimg that PEARLSDG is located in a sizeable NIRCam area that is fortuitously devoid of brighter objects, making aperture photometry and sky-subtraction straightforward in all these other images, which have much wider PSFs than .

The closest massive galaxy (SDSS J121156.80+273835.5, or J1227) is 1650 km/s separated from , and there are no massive ( >10^9 ) galaxies within 1000 km/s and 1 Mpc, making it one of the most isolated quiescent dwarf galaxies observed. This is further demonstrated in the top panel of Fig. fig:env ; the Fundamental-plane distance to J1227 is 52 13 Mpc, 1.6 away from . This is in agreement with the predicted distance from COSMOFLOWS-3 https://edd.ifa.hawaii.edu/CF3calculator/ Kourkchi2020 , and would require a +1723 km/s peculiar velocity to be nearby in a region of space where the typical peculiar velocity is 470 km/s, further suggesting that and J1227 are indeed not physically associated.

gmw_00005936.name: 0.3120 galaxy towards J0849+5108
gmw_00005936.overview: The 0.3120 galaxy towards J0849+5108 is a luminous red early-type galaxy [arXiv:1308.4141]. Its outskirts contain a reservoir of cold neutral gas detected via 21-cm and metal absorption lines, which may fuel a low level of star formation activity [arXiv:1308.4141].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the J0849+5108 absorber with a luminous red early-type galaxy whose outskirts contain DLA-level neutral gas and metal absorption, possibly fueling low-level outer star formation.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of 21-cm absorption from foreground galaxies towards quasars, specifically =0.3120 towards SDSS J084957.97+510829.0 ( =0.584; Pair-I) and =0.3714 towards SDSS J144304.53+021419.3 ( =1.82; Pair-II). In both the cases, the integrated 21-cm optical depth is consistent with the absorbing gas being a damped Lyman- (DLA) system. In the case of Pair-I, strong Na i and Ca ii absorption lines are also detected at \ in the QSO spectrum.

We identify an early-type galaxy at an impact parameter of b 14 kpc whose photometric redshift is consistent with that of the detected metal and 21-cm absorption lines. This would be the first example of an early-type galaxy associated with an intervening 21-cm absorber. The gas detected in 21-cm and metal absorption lines on the outskirts of this luminous red galaxy could be associated with the reservoir of cold \ gas with a low level of star formation activity in the outer regions of the galaxy as reported in the literature for z 0.1 early-type galaxies.

gmw_00005937.name: 0.3714 galaxy towards J1443+0214
gmw_00005937.overview: The 0.3714 galaxy towards J1443+0214 is a low-surface-brightness galaxy identified via narrow optical emission lines [arXiv:1308.4141]. It features a modest star formation rate of 0.7 to 0.8 solar masses per year and contains neutral gas consistent with a damped Lyman-alpha system [arXiv:1308.4141].

sources.summary: The paper associates the J1443+0214 absorber with a low-surface-brightness galaxy detected through narrow optical emission, with DLA-level neutral gas, subsolar metallicity, and modest star formation.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of 21-cm absorption from foreground galaxies towards quasars, specifically =0.3120 towards SDSS J084957.97+510829.0 ( =0.584; Pair-I) and =0.3714 towards SDSS J144304.53+021419.3 ( =1.82; Pair-II). In both the cases, the integrated 21-cm optical depth is consistent with the absorbing gas being a damped Lyman- (DLA) system.

In the case of Pair-II, the absorption is associated with a low surface brightness galaxy that, unlike most other known quasar-galaxy pairs (QGPs), i.e. QSO sight lines passing through disks or halos of foreground galaxies, is identified only via narrow optical emission lines detected on top of the QSO spectra. Using SDSS spectra we infer that the emission lines originate within 5 kpc of the QSO sight line, and the gas has metallicity [ 12+O/H ] 8.4 and star formation rate 0.7-0.8 M _ yr ^ 1 . The measured 21-cm optical depth can be reconciled with the N (H i ) we derive from the measured extinction (A _V =0.6) if either the H i gas is warm or the extinction per hydrogen atom in this galaxy is much higher than the mean value of the Small Magellanic Cloud.

gmw_00005938.name: And XXIX
gmw_00005938.overview: And XXIX is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy located within the virial radius of M31 [arXiv:2101.08613]. The galaxy is dominated by an old stellar population formed during an initial active burst, with its star formation quenched over the last 1 to 2 billion years [arXiv:2101.08613].

sources.summary: The paper treats And XXIX as a dwarf spheroidal within M31's virial radius in a homogeneous Local Group SFH comparison, finding old dominant populations, recent quenching, and low metallicity across the studied dwarfs.

quotes.quote: The first column of the table shows the name of the galaxy; (2) -- equatorial coordinates (J2000); (3) -- morphological type; (4) -- absolute magnitude in Johnson B filter; (5) - Holmberg diameter (at the level of _B = 26.5 mag arcsec ^ 2 isophote), in angular minutes; (6) -- the distance in Mpc. The distances to isolated galaxies were measured by us using tip of the red giant branch method (TRGB) in the works mentioned above. The three subsample of the list are indicated in the Table tab:param : highly isolated dwarfs KKR 25, KK 258 and KKs 03; the dSphs within the zero velocity radius, but outside the virial radius Tucana dwarf and And XVIII; the dSphs within the virial radius of M 31 And XXVIII, Cas dSph and And XXIX. Observations of all studied dwarf galaxies (with the exception of KKR 25) were made with HST/ACS.

We have estimated the star formation rate (SFR) and metallicity depending on the age of the stellar populations, as well as the stellar mass formed in each related period of star formation. A number of common trends follows from these results: the star formation has been quenched over the last 1--2 Gyrs which is quite consistent with the morphological type (dSph and dTr) of the studied dwarfs; there is an initial active burst of star formation from about 10--12 to 13.8 Gyrs ago. The fraction of stars formed during this period is different for our objects (we will discuss it below). In addition, there are stars formed about 4--8 Gyrs ago.

gmw_00005939.name: And XXVIII
gmw_00005939.overview: And XXVIII is a dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy located within the virial radius of M31 [arXiv:2101.08613, arXiv:1710.09038]. It features an old stellar population that began forming stars early [arXiv:1606.01207], ceased star formation by 6 Gyr ago [arXiv:1606.01207], and hosts 87 detected RR Lyrae stars [arXiv:1710.09038].

sources.summary: The paper presents HST photometry for And XXVIII, using resolved stellar populations to compare its CMD coverage, horizontal-branch features, and early star-formation history with other M31 dwarf spheroidals.

quotes.quote: The positions of the observed fields are shown in Figure f4 . As can be seen in Figure f4 , for And I and And II the ACS field covers only part of the galaxy within the half light radius and the parallel WFC3 field contains many member stars. Some member stars were detected in the WFC3 field of And III, but not nearly enough to produce a SFH with reasonable uncertainties. For And XV, And XVI, and And XXVIII, the ACS field of view covers most of the galaxy out to the half light radius and the WFC3 field is distant enough that a minimal number of member stars are expected.

Figure f5 shows that the ACS photometry reaches below the oldest main-sequence turn-offs allowing for very strong constraints on the oldest epochs of star formation. These observations of And I, And II, and And III are 2.5 mag fainter than the deepest CMDs previously obtained for these galaxies dacosta1996, dacosta2000, dacosta2002 . There are several notable features that are common to all six ACS CMDs. All six galaxies show the presence of blue horizontal branch stars (BHBs). This is generally taken as evidence of the presence of stars with ages comparable to the Milky Way GC population see discussion in gallart2005 . To date, all galaxies that have been observed with sufficient photometric depth have shown at least some star formation at the earliest times.

Figure f7 shows a variety of SFHs. However, all six SFHs have the common properties of starting their star formation early (there is significant star formation in the earliest time bin for all six galaxies), having at least 50 of their stars formed by a lookback time of 9 Gyr, and then ceasing all star formation by a lookback time of 6 Gyr. And XV and And XXVIII show the oldest mean stellar populations of the sample, but both are clearly not consistent with a single age population and are clearly not consistent with forming all of their stars before the epoch of reionization (as indicated in Figure f7 by the shaded region).

sources.summary: The paper treats And XXVIII as a dwarf spheroidal within M31's virial radius in a homogeneous Local Group SFH comparison, finding old dominant populations, recent quenching, and low metallicity across the studied dwarfs.

sources.summary: The HST census treats And XXVIII as an M31 dSph, detects 87 RRLs, and identifies an unusual Oosterhoff/RRc distribution among nearby galaxies.

quotes.quote: Under the ISLAndS Initial Star formation and Lifetimes of Andromeda Satellites project (based on very deep, multi-epoch HST ACS and WFC3 data), six M31 dSph satellite companion galaxies were observed: And I, And II, And III, And XV, And XVI and And XXVIII.
We detect 870 RR Lyrae (RRL) stars in the fields of And I (296), II (251), III (111), XV (117), XVI (8), XXVIII (87).

And XXVIII is the exception, with a fraction of Oo-I like stars close to 50 . Moreover, the distribution of RRLs in the Bailey diagram is also different from the other And dSphs; the RRab stars show a broad spread and are not concentrated on either Oosterhoff line. And XXVIII is also peculiar for the large fraction of RRcd type stars, which represent 58 of the total. In the LG, if we exclude low-mass galaxies with very small samples of RRLs ( < 15, such as e.g., Bootes I and And XVI, see sec:global_rrl ), And XXVIII is the only galaxy with more RRcd than RRab type stars.

gmw_00005940.name: Cas dSph
gmw_00005940.overview: Cas dSph is a bright dwarf spheroidal galaxy located within the virial radius of M31 [arXiv:2101.08613]. The galaxy is dominated by an old stellar population formed during an initial active burst, with its star formation quenched over the last 1 to 2 Gyr [arXiv:2101.08613].

sources.summary: The paper treats Cas dSph as a bright dwarf spheroidal within M31's virial radius in a homogeneous Local Group SFH comparison, finding old dominant populations, recent quenching, and low metallicity across the studied dwarfs.

gmw_00005941.name: KK 258
gmw_00005941.overview: KK 258 is a highly isolated dwarf spheroidal or transitional-type galaxy [arXiv:2101.08613]. It experienced an initial active burst of star formation 10 to 13.8 billion years ago, followed by quenching over the last 1 to 2 billion years [arXiv:2101.08613].

sources.summary: The paper treats KK 258 as a highly isolated dwarf spheroidal or transitional-type galaxy in a homogeneous Local Group SFH comparison, finding old dominant populations, recent quenching, and low metallicity across the studied dwarfs.

sources.summary: The paper treats KKR 25 as a highly isolated dwarf spheroidal or transitional-type galaxy in a homogeneous Local Group SFH comparison, finding old dominant populations, recent quenching, and low metallicity across the studied dwarfs.

gmw_00005943.name: KKs 03

sources.summary: The paper treats KKs 03 as a highly isolated dwarf spheroidal or transitional-type galaxy in a homogeneous Local Group SFH comparison, finding old dominant populations, recent quenching, and low metallicity across the studied dwarfs.

gmw_00005944.name: Tucana dwarf
gmw_00005944.overview: The Tucana dwarf is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy that may have experienced a close approach to the central body of its group [arXiv:2101.08613]. This potential backsplash interaction, along with encounters with other dwarfs, may have caused a loss of stellar mass and star-forming material [arXiv:2101.08613].

sources.summary: The paper finds Tucana is an outlier: its stellar mass may be underestimated, its SFH agrees with prior HST work, and its quenching resembles backsplash interaction with M31.

quotes.quote: An interesting exception is the Tucana dwarf. Following the relation found above, the stellar mass of this galaxy should be higher. Nevertheless, it is known that in the past the object could experience close approach to the central body of the group, and possibly interactions with other dwarfs before its fall on the Local Group. All these processes could cause the loss of stellar mass and material for star formation monelli2010 .

In this case the total stellar mass of Tucana dSph should be underestimated. One of the recent detailed SFH determination of the Tucana dSph was performed by savino2019 using HST/ACS photometry. The colour-magnitude diagram in this study is deeper than ours, and also the authors put finer scale for their Age-- [ Fe/H ] plane. Nevertheless, the overall star formation periods and the metallicity range of the SFH by savino2019 and our measurements are consistent.

gmw_00005945.name: Andromeda's Parachute
gmw_00005945.overview: Andromeda's Parachute is a quadruply lensed broad absorption-line quasar [arXiv:1707.05873] located at a redshift of 2.341 [arXiv:1801.01851]. The system is gravitationally lensed by a foreground early-type galaxy [arXiv:1911.12123] that has a redshift of approximately 0.57 to 0.678 [arXiv:1911.12123, arXiv:1801.01851].

sources.summary: The paper presents spectra and monitoring of the lensed quasar PS J0147+4630, revising the lens redshift, detecting microlensing in C and D, and estimating the A-B delay.

quotes.quote: Because follow--up observations of quadruple gravitational lens systems are of extraordinary importance for astrophysics and cosmology, we present single--epoch optical spectra and -band light curves of PS J0147+4630. This recently discovered system mainly consists of four images ABCD of a background quasar around a foreground galaxy G that acts as a gravitational lens. First, we use long--slit spectroscopic data in the Gemini Observatory Archive and a multi--component fitting to accurately resolve the spectra of A, D, and G. The spectral profile of G resembles that of an early--type galaxy at a redshift of 0.678 0.001, which is about 20 higher than the previous estimate. Additionally, the stellar velocity dispersion is measured to 5 precision.

Second, our early -band monitoring with the Liverpool Telescope leads to accurate light curves of the four quasar images. Adopting time delays predicted by the lens model, the new lens redshift, and a standard cosmology, we report the detection of microlensing variations in C and D as large as 0.1 mag on timescales of a few hundred days. We also estimate an actual delay between A and B of a few days (B is leading), which demonstrates the big potential of optical monitoring campaigns of PS J0147+4630.

sources.summary: The paper confirms Andromeda's Parachute as a quadruply lensed BAL quasar and uses KCWI spectra of its four images to study intervening absorption coherence.

quotes.quote: We present Keck Cosmic Web Imager spectroscopy of the four putative images of the lensed quasar candidate \ recently discovered by bergheaetal17ph . The data verify the source as a quadruply lensed, broad absorption-line quasar having z_ S = . We detect intervening absorption in the Fe 2 2586, 2600 , Mg 2 2796, 2803 , and/or C 4 1548, 1550 transitions in eight foreground systems, three of which have redshifts consistent with the photometric-redshift estimate reported for the lensing galaxy ( z_ L 0.57 ).

In this Letter, we present spectroscopy from the recently-commissioned Keck Cosmic Web Imager (KCWI; Morrissey, P. et al. \ 2017, in prep.) confirming that this system ( ) is a quadruply lensed quasar at z_ S = . We then analyze intervening metal-line absorption systems for constraints on their spatial coherence. Given the source image configuration and its location on the sky, we refer to this object as `` .''

sources.summary: The paper presents GMOS spectroscopy of PSOJ0147, a quadruply lensed quasar, measuring lens and quasar redshifts and finding microlensing signatures in the quasar images.

quotes.quote: I present timely spectroscopic follow-up of the newly discovered, quadruply lensed quasar PSOJ0147 from the Pan-STARRS 1 survey. The newly acquired optical spectra with GMOS onboard the Gemini north telescope allow us to pin down the redshifts of both the foreground lensing galaxy and the background lensed quasar to be z=0.572 and 2.341, providing firm basis for cosmography with future high cadence photometric monitoring. I also inspect difference spectra from two of the quasar images, revealing the microlensing effect. Long-term spectroscopic follow-ups will shed lights on the structure of the AGN and its environment.

gmw_00005946.name: J0607
gmw_00005946.overview: J0607 is a strongly lensed quasar system containing four resolved images [arXiv:2206.07714]. The system features a lensing galaxy, a small luminous companion galaxy, and extended emission from the lensed source galaxy detected in red filters [arXiv:2206.07714, arXiv:2405.01620].

sources.summary: The paper confirms J0607-2152 as a quad observed at two WHT position angles, with resolved red-arm traces and imaging evidence for a lensing galaxy and fourth image.

quotes.quote: J0607-2152 This quad was confirmed during the 2019 WHT observing run, in which the system was targetted at two position angles: through the brightest image and each of the other two Gaia detections. The traces are resolved in the red arm, however in the blue arm of one observation, the images are not resolved, hence we show only the red arm extraction for the fainter image in Figure lens_spectra1 . The extracted fluxes for the brightest image between the two exposures are in good agreement in the red arm, however in the blue arm they are in disagreement likely due to slit losses and blending. Figure fig:J0607 shows the presence of two further objects between and to the East of the 3 Gaia detections. Including a galaxy and a further PSF fits the data to the noise, so we expect that these are the lensing galaxy and fourth quasar image.

sources.summary: The paper models J0607 as a lensed quasar system with a luminous companion and extended lensed host-galaxy emission measured in red JWST filters.

quotes.quote: For J0607 a small luminous galaxy is also observed in several filters. We include this galaxy in the lens model as a Singular Isothermal Sphere and add a circular S ersic profile to model the light.

With the exception of J1606 and J1042, all the lenses had extended emission from the lensed source galaxy in the bluest filter, F560. J0607, J1537, and J1606 had extended emission from lensed source galaxy in the reddest filters (F2100W for J0607 and J1537, and F2550W for J1606). All of the lenses with source light detected in the reddest filter have sources with redshift less than 2, and the reddest filter corresponds to 8--10 m rest-frame for these systems.

gmw_00005947.name: J1537
gmw_00005947.overview: J1537 is a strongly lensed quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.721 [arXiv:2012.10051, arXiv:1810.04480]. The system exhibits four lensed images in a diamond configuration [arXiv:2012.10051], with extended emission from its host galaxy [arXiv:2405.01620] and offsets in the ultraviolet bands that likely indicate star-forming regions [arXiv:2209.03094].

sources.summary: The paper confirms GraL J153725.3 301017 as a diamond-configuration lensed quasar at z = 1.721, with four blue components and a model predicting the fourth image and lensing galaxy.

quotes.quote: GraL J153725.3 301017 --- This source was presented in as a candidate lensed quasar with three detected lensed images, an ERT probability of P_ ERT = 0.97 , and a wide maximum separation of 3 3. The PanSTARRS image shows all four components in a classic diamond configuration, with hints of a red lensing galaxy in the center. We obtained two Keck spectra at PA=165 , simultaneously observing the eastern and western pair of lensed quasar images in turn. We confirmed that all four blue components correspond to a quasar at z = 1.721 ; Figure 3 presents the spectra of the two western components, which show an AGN with strong narrow-line components superposed on broad emission lines. There was no evidence of the lensing galaxy in these spectra, nor do we detect a foreground absorption line system.

GraL J153725.3 301017 --- This system has a rather large _E 1 5 . While the model is very uncertain since it is based on three lensed images only, it predicts positions of the fourth image and of the lensing galaxy that are qualitatively compatible with the PanSTARRS imaging. An SIE model (without shear) was presented in .

sources.summary: The paper confirms J1537-3010 spectroscopically, places the source at z=1.72, and reports an SIE+shear lens model whose main mismatch is image B flux.

quotes.quote: J1537-3010 Our WHT spectra reveal that the eastern and southern images share a broad emission line at 4212 . A second emission line at 5190 \ has been identified with Subaru (Rusu, private communication). This places the source at a redshift of z=1.72 , with the two lines being CIV and CIII respectively.

The SIE+shear mass model provides a good fit, ^ 2 5 given 4 degrees of freedom. The majority of this derives from image B being half as bright as the model predicts. The shear has a magnitude of 0.07, 34 degrees East of North. This candidate was also independently selected by delchambre2018 as GRAL153725327-301017053 and spectroscopically confirmed (Delchambre et al., private communication).

sources.summary: The paper uses J1537 as a lens-model example, reports extended host-galaxy emission in red filters, and detects hot-dust microlensing for one image.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:ex_im_fit shows an example of the output of our modelling procedure for the F560W filter for J1537.

With the exception of J1606 and J1042, all the lenses had extended emission from the lensed source galaxy in the bluest filter, F560. J0607, J1537, and J1606 had extended emission from lensed source galaxy in the reddest filters (F2100W for J0607 and J1537, and F2550W for J1606). All of the lenses with source light detected in the reddest filter have sources with redshift less than 2, and the reddest filter corresponds to 8--10 m rest-frame for these systems.

At high redshifts, this is better constrained, and we detect differential microlensing of the hot dust relative to the warm dust for J0405 (image B), J0607 (image A), J1537 (image C). The microlensing of the hot dust could be better constrained if more realistic priors were used for, e.g., the relative amount of microlensing allowed for the continuum emission compared to the hot dust, or with microlensing simulations of the accretion disk and hot dust e.g. sluse_mid-infrared_2013 .

sources.summary: J1537-3010 was modeled in all three bands; the paper reports aligned mass and light, UV arc offsets likely from star-forming regions, and prominent arcs useful for cosmography.

quotes.quote: J1537-3010 This system was modeled in all three bands. In each band, we chose five stars from the field to approximate the PSF. The centers of the mass and light distribution align very well within 0.01 . In the model residuals shown in Table tab:results_light we find a small offset between the quasar images and the arc in the bands F475X and F814W. A possible explanation for this offset is that these two bands are in the rest-frame UV of the quasar host galaxy and thus are likely dominated by light from specific star-forming regions in the host galaxy.

gmw_00005948.name: J0748+4712
gmw_00005948.overview: The galaxy J0748+4712 is a tidal disruption event candidate that exhibited a continuum flare in 2004 [arXiv:1605.05145, arXiv:2307.03182]. Its initial spectrum displayed broad helium and Balmer lines, which faded by 2011 as the galaxy returned to a quiescent state [arXiv:2307.03182].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0748+4712 as a TDE candidate with fading coronal lines, declining mid-infrared emission, estimated flare luminosity, and warm circumnuclear dust and gas.

quotes.quote: The continuum flares of J0748+4712 and J1350+2916 occurred within 120 and 700 days prior to the spectroscopic observations, respectively Wang2011,Wang2012 . For J0748+4712, all coronal lines disappeared in the spectra taken at Xinglong and Lijiang station four to five years after the SDSS discovery Wang2012,Yang2013 . In J0748+4712, the high coronal lines ( [ Fe XIV ] , [ Ar XIV ] , [ Fe XI ] and [ Fe X ] ) were detected within 4 months of the continuum peak, consisting with above estimate.

The logarithmic bolometric luminosities are 44.6, 44.0, 44.3, and 44.4 (erg s ^ 1 ), for J0748+4712, J0952+2143, J1342+0530, and J1350+2916, respectively, for graphite grains with a = 0.1 m, and mildly larger for silicate grains. Assuming an average density of =2.5 g cm ^ 3 see section 7.3.5 in Kruegel2003 , the dust mass is 0.14, 0.76, 0.29, and 0.18 M_ for J0748+4712, J0952+2143, J1342+0530, and J1350+2916, respectively, using the values in Table tbflux on A_1 epochs for the graphite grains with a 0.1 m. Assuming a dust to gas ratio of 0.01, the amount of gas is 14, 76, 29, and 18 M_ for J0748+4712, J0952+2143, J1342+0530, and J1350+2916, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper finds SDSS J0748 had broad TDE-like lines in 2004 that faded by 2011 and remained absent in 2019, implying a single flaring event.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0748 The initial 2004 SDSS spectrum of SDSS J0748 displayed \ emission lines along with broad \ and Balmer lines that typify the H+He subclass of optically selected, active TDEs arcavi_2014_CONTINUUMHeRICHTIDAL . All of these features had faded prior to the 2011 MMT spectrum and are also absent in our 2019 MMT spectrum. The spectral shapes of both MMT spectra are consistent. This indicates that the initial flaring event was a single epoch rather than a recurring transient, with the optical spectrum having now most likely returned to a quiescent state.

gmw_00005949.name: J0952+2143
gmw_00005949.overview: The emission line galaxy SDSS J0952+2143 is the first known extreme coronal line emitting galaxy [arXiv:2310.03795, arXiv:2307.03182]. It has exhibited multiwavelength flares [arXiv:2310.03795], transient broad coronal and helium emission lines [arXiv:2310.03795], and a fading mid-infrared echo from its nuclear dust [arXiv:1605.05145].

sources.summary: The paper compares SDSS J0952+2143 as the first ECLE, associated with multiwavelength flares and broad coronal/He II lines resembling the two VLASS TDE hosts.

quotes.quote: The first known ECLE, SDSS J0952+2143, was discovered by in a search for galaxies in SDSS with evolving spectral features. This source was associated with X-ray, optical, IR, and UV flares. There is no radio detection reported, although extensive radio follow-up was not performed. The source showed many transient emission lines, including bright coronal, He II and Balmer features. The coronal lines and He II lines are broad, with FWHM 800 km s ^ 1 .

The emission lines from SDSS J0952+2143 are remarkably similar to those observed from VT J1008 and VT J2012, although we do not detect narrow emission at the location of the Balmer lines and the Oxygen lines detected from our sources are much fainter than those from SDSS J0952+2143. We also do not detect multiwavelength flares analogous to those from SDSS J0952+2143. However, it is plausible that some of the differences between our sources and SDSS J0952+2143 could be reconciled by invoking a TDE in a galaxy with pre-existing accretion disk and/or a different line-of-sight.

sources.summary: The paper uses WISE and Spitzer data for J0952+2143 to trace a fading infrared echo, infer high earlier mid-infrared luminosity, and estimate dust and gas in the nuclear environment.

quotes.quote: To extend the light curve, we estimate the W1 and W2 flux for J0952+2143 during the Spitzer observation. J0952+2143 was observed by Spitzer /IRS with the Short-High (SH) module on 2008 June 5 (MJD: 54624), which is 700 days before its first WISE epoch observation see Komossa2009 . We found that the mid-IR continuum of J0952+2143 is quite flat, and its mid-IR spectrum is almost the same as that of the hot-dust-deficient quasar PG 1121+422, which is composed of its 5-35 m Spitzer mid-IR spectra and 2.5-5 m AKARI near-IR spectra (Figure sed0952 ; Lyu2016 ). Using the 2.5-35 m SED of the latter as a template, we derived the synthetic WISE W1 and W2 photometry of J0952+2143 to be 6.7 and 8.3mJy, which is 6.1 and 5.2 times brighter than the one from the A_1 epoch.

The logarithmic bolometric luminosities are 44.6, 44.0, 44.3, and 44.4 (erg s ^ 1 ), for J0748+4712, J0952+2143, J1342+0530, and J1350+2916, respectively, for graphite grains with a = 0.1 m, and mildly larger for silicate grains. Assuming an average density of =2.5 g cm ^ 3 see section 7.3.5 in Kruegel2003 , the dust mass is 0.14, 0.76, 0.29, and 0.18 M_ for J0748+4712, J0952+2143, J1342+0530, and J1350+2916, respectively, using the values in Table tbflux on A_1 epochs for the graphite grains with a 0.1 m. Assuming a dust to gas ratio of 0.01, the amount of gas is 14, 76, 29, and 18 M_ for J0748+4712, J0952+2143, J1342+0530, and J1350+2916, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J0952 as the first ECLE, with brightness and SED changes, strong highly ionized Fe lines, multipeaked Balmer emission, and UV excess.

quotes.quote: The first ECLE (SDSS J095209.56+214313.3, which we refer to as SDSS J0952) was identified by , who noted that the object changed in brightness and overall spectral energy distribution (SED) between photometric observations by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey SDSS; york_2000_SloanDigitalSky in 2004 and subsequent spectroscopic observations the following year. During this time, the object dimmed to be more consistent with that of near-infrared (NIR) photometry obtained in 1998 by the Two Micron All-Sky Survey 2MASS; skrutskie_2006_TwoMicronAll , whilst displaying a continuum best described by a combination of underlying starlight and an additional power-law component. This spectrum also presented the strong emission lines of highly ionised Fe that subsequently became the hallmark spectral features for the identification of ECLEs. These Fe emission lines had both broad and narrow components and were accompanied by multipeaked Balmer emission lines. Additionally, ultraviolet (UV) observations obtained two months after the SDSS spectrum by the Galaxy Evolution Explorer GALEX ; martin_2005_GalaxyEvolutionExplorer were found to be significantly brighter than would be expected from host-galaxy starlight alone yet consistent with an extrapolation of the power-law component identified in the continuum of the SDSS spectrum.

gmw_00005950.name: J1342+0530
gmw_00005950.overview: J1342+0530 is an emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2307.03182] and a tidal disruption event candidate [arXiv:1605.05145]. It exhibits mid-infrared variability and declining mid-infrared emission [arXiv:1605.05145], alongside evolving coronal-line spectra that include vanished high-ionization lines and a large increase in oxygen line flux [arXiv:2307.03182].

sources.summary: The paper reports evolving coronal-line spectra for SDSS J1342, including vanished high-ionization lines, persistent later features, and an unusually large increase in 4959 and 5007 line flux.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1342 The initial 2002 SDSS spectrum of SDSS J1342 displayed , , and \ but lacked any \ lines. By the time of the MMT spectrum in 2011, the higher ionisation lines were no longer detectable, but \ lines were now clearly observable. The higher-resolution 2022 DESI spectrum reveals a persistence of the \ emission features first seen in the 2011 MMT follow-up spectrum, with no indication of recurrence of the higher ionisation state lines. The NTT spectrum of SDSS J1342 obtained around one month after the DESI spectrum does display some apparent \ coronal emission features, though this spectrum is of too low resolution and S/N for any additional confirmation. This highlights the necessity of high-S/N and high-resolution follow-up spectra to fully capture the evolution of ECLEs.

This object is most interesting for the very large increase in the line flux of 4959, 5007 observed in both the DESI and NTT spectra. Whilst note the increase in \ emission strength in all four of the ECLEs they identify as variable between the intial SDSS spectra and their 2011 MMT spectra, the increase displayed by SDSS J1342 after 2011 is much more extreme, and unique among the current ECLE sample.

sources.summary: J1342+0530 is treated as a TDE candidate with uncertain flare timing, significant WISE variability, declining mid-infrared emission, and inferred warm dust and gas masses.

quotes.quote: Despite the fact that we do not have good constraints on the date for the continuum flare in J1342+0530, there are reasons to believe that the tidal disruption flare happened not too far from the SDSS spectroscopic observation. For J1342+0530, the ALLWISE catalog gives a variability flag of 7 and 9 for W1 and W2 bands, suggesting significant variability between different exposures during the ALLWISE survey. We also detect significant decline within NEOWISE-R survey in three sources (J0748+4712, J1342+0530, and J1350+2916).

The logarithmic bolometric luminosities are 44.6, 44.0, 44.3, and 44.4 (erg s ^ 1 ), for J0748+4712, J0952+2143, J1342+0530, and J1350+2916, respectively, for graphite grains with a = 0.1 m, and mildly larger for silicate grains. Assuming an average density of =2.5 g cm ^ 3 see section 7.3.5 in Kruegel2003 , the dust mass is 0.14, 0.76, 0.29, and 0.18 M_ for J0748+4712, J0952+2143, J1342+0530, and J1350+2916, respectively, using the values in Table tbflux on A_1 epochs for the graphite grains with a 0.1 m. Assuming a dust to gas ratio of 0.01, the amount of gas is 14, 76, 29, and 18 M_ for J0748+4712, J0952+2143, J1342+0530, and J1350+2916, respectively.

gmw_00005951.name: J1350+2916
gmw_00005951.overview: The emission line galaxy J1350+2916 is a tidal disruption event candidate that experienced a continuum flare [arXiv:1605.05145]. Its optical light curve showed a 1.5-magnitude brightening before plateauing, while its coronal emission lines subsequently faded [arXiv:2307.03182].

sources.summary: The paper finds SDSS J1350's coronal lines faded away while its optical light curve showed stronger variability, including a 1.5 mag CRTS brightening before plateauing.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1350 SDSS J1350 initially exhibited - \ emission lines which faded between the 2006 SDSS spectrum and the follow-up 2011 MMT spectrum obtained by , with \ emission lines developing over the same period. Like the higher ionisation state lines before them, these \ lines have now faded; no remaining coronal lines are present in our recent 2021 NTT follow-up spectrum, with the possible exception of a low-S/N \ feature. Given its low S/N and the lack of lower ionisation state lines, we do not claim its detection.

SDSS J1350 In contrast to the previously described objects, SDSS J1350 has shown more significant optical variability. An increase in brightness was observed by to have occurred between the SDSS spectroscopic and photometric observations. A sharp increase in brightness in the CRTS V band by 1.5 mag was observed around MJD 54600 before plateauing for several years.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1350+2916 as a TDE candidate whose flare preceded spectroscopy, whose mid-infrared emission declined, and whose warm dust and gas were estimated from WISE data.

quotes.quote: The continuum flares of J0748+4712 and J1350+2916 occurred within 120 and 700 days prior to the spectroscopic observations, respectively Wang2011,Wang2012 . We also detect significant decline within NEOWISE-R survey in three sources (J0748+4712, J1342+0530, and J1350+2916).

The logarithmic bolometric luminosities are 44.6, 44.0, 44.3, and 44.4 (erg s ^ 1 ), for J0748+4712, J0952+2143, J1342+0530, and J1350+2916, respectively, for graphite grains with a = 0.1 m, and mildly larger for silicate grains. Assuming an average density of =2.5 g cm ^ 3 see section 7.3.5 in Kruegel2003 , the dust mass is 0.14, 0.76, 0.29, and 0.18 M_ for J0748+4712, J0952+2143, J1342+0530, and J1350+2916, respectively, using the values in Table tbflux on A_1 epochs for the graphite grains with a 0.1 m. Assuming a dust to gas ratio of 0.01, the amount of gas is 14, 76, 29, and 18 M_ for J0748+4712, J0952+2143, J1342+0530, and J1350+2916, respectively.

gmw_00005952.name: Kite
gmw_00005952.overview: The Kite is an edge-on S0/a galaxy [arXiv:2305.01335] that exhibits nuclear activity classified spectroscopically as composite [arXiv:2305.01335]. It features a single-sided, 380 kpc star-forming tail [arXiv:2305.01335] and lies near a QSO companion galaxy projected 57 kpc away [arXiv:2305.01335].

sources.summary: The paper presents the Kite as an edge-on S0/a active galaxy with a record-length, narrow, star-forming tail, considering ram pressure, tidal, and three-body ejection origins.

quotes.quote: Upon visual inspection, we identified the candidate located at ( , )= (346.2203^ ,-8.8093^ ) , which is object i in Figure fig:kite , to be near the end of a linear sequence of low surface brightness features leading back to PGC 1000273 (346.183294^ ,-8.703178^ ) , an edge-on S0/a galaxy nair with cz = 13813 25 km s ^ 1 in the CMB frame and M _i = -21.41 AB mag (recessional velocity and magnitude from NED The NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) is funded by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration and operated by the California Institute of Technology. ) that we have named the ``Kite" galaxy given its single-sided tail morphology (Figure fig:kite ). It has a companion galaxy, projected only 57 kpc away at a comparable redshift (z = 0.0470) , that is a QSO (Mrk 0926, or alternately PGC 070409). This pair had attracted previous attention because the Kite galaxy also shows signs of nuclear activity identified spectroscopically as ``composite" by in a study of binary active galaxies liu , although neither liu nor a subsequent study weston identified clear signs of an interaction between the galaxies. The tail structure comprises both well-defined knots of emission and diffuse emission strewn nearly linearly out to a projected separation from the Kite of 7 arcmin (420.0 arcsec from feature j in Figure fig:kite to the center of the Kite galaxy). This separation corresponds to 380 kpc at the galaxy's adopted distance (187 Mpc) .

The Kite shares characteristics common to those formed via ram pressure stripping (``jellyfish" galaxies) and formed via tidal interactions. However, both scenarios face significant challenges that we discuss, leaving open the question of how such an extreme tail formed. We propose that the tail resulted from a three-body interaction from which the lowest-mass galaxy was ejected at high velocity.

gmw_00005953.name: Kite A
gmw_00005953.overview: Kite A is a small galaxy located within the Kite environment [arXiv:2305.01335]. It is likely situated in the system's tail, potentially originating from a gravitational ejection event that occurred approximately 300 million years ago [arXiv:2305.01335].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Kite A as a small galaxy in the Kite environment, likely associated with the tail, and models its high-speed motion in a gravitational ejection scenario.

quotes.quote: Our spectrum of Kite A shows an asymmetric broad absorption line corresponding to H in the observed frame of the Kite. Because of the asymmetry our determination of the line centroid is uncertain at the level of 100 km s ^ 1 . As such we can confidently place Kite A in the Kite environment, but cannot with similar confidence place it in the tail. Nevertheless, it is likely that Kite A is in the tail given its precise projection on the tail and reasonably close redshift.

Our Proposal Given the various reasons to favour a short lifetime for the tail and the presence of Kite A in the tail, we propose a gravitational origin for the tail arising from the ejection of Kite A from the Kite-Mrk 0926 system. A hyperbolic orbit, where Kite A has a velocity significantly larger than the escape speed, not only addresses issues related to the lifetime of the feature but relaxes the orientation constraints because Kite A and its associated detritus would be travelling on near linear trajectories in 3-D.

For a time since pericenter passage of 300 Myr, consistent with the youngest stellar population in Kite A, Kite A would need to have a mean transverse velocity of 460 km s ^ 1 to reach its current projected position, which is almost certainly larger than the escape speed at its current position. The tidal material would both lead and trail Kite A, resulting in a nearly linear feature with Kite A in the middle.

gmw_00005954.name: J1647+21
gmw_00005954.overview: The galaxy J1647+21 is a complex system characterized by two main galaxy knots [arXiv:1401.1985]. These knots exhibit a spatial variation in metallicity, with the brightest knot having a metallicity of approximately one-tenth solar and the second knot being more metal-rich [arXiv:1401.1985].

sources.summary: The paper finds J1647+21 has a significant metallicity drop between its knots, with the brightest knot near one-tenth solar and the other clearly more metal rich.

quotes.quote: Figure  extrange_case is similar to Fig.  first_one in the sense of showing a significant spatial variation of metallicity for J1647+21 . The source is larger and more complex than J2238+14 (Fig.  extrange_case ). The long slit spectrum has not enough signal for metallicity analysis in between the two main galaxy knots (except for a single pixel in between; see Fig.  extrange_case ).

However, the signals in the knots clearly indicate a significant difference of metallicity. The brightest one (at position zero) has a metallicity of the order of 1/10 solar, whereas the second one (at positions between 35 and 40 ) is doubtless metal richer, even though we cannot assess its actual metallicity.

gmw_00005955.name: PSO J011.3899+09.0325
gmw_00005955.overview: PSO J011.3899+09.0325 is a faint quasar located at a high redshift of z=6.42 [arXiv:1710.01251]. It is characterized by a very flat Y-J color and does not exhibit strong emission lines [arXiv:1710.01251].

sources.summary: The paper confirms PSO J011.3899+09.0325 as a faint z=6.42 quasar with flat color, weak emission lines, and a redshift from template comparison.

quotes.quote: PSO J011.3899+09.0325 @ z=6.42 Follow-up imaging data for PSO011+09 were acquired with MPG 2.2m/GROND and du Pont/Retrocam in September 2016; its quasar nature was confirmed with a short 600s low-resolution prism mode spectrum using Magellan/FIRE on 2016 November 20. We then obtained a higher S/N, higher resolution optical spectrum with Keck/LRIS. We consider in this work only the latter spectroscopic observation (see Figure figSpecAll ) because the FIRE spectrum has a very limited S/N and over-exposed H and K bands.

It is a relatively faint object, with J_ G =20.8, and presents a very flat Y_ retro J_ G color of 0.01 (see Table tabPhotFollowUpQSOs ). This quasar does not show strong emission lines. Through a comparison with SDSS quasar templates (see Section subsecRedTempl ), we calculate a redshift of z=6.42 , with an uncertainty of z =0.05.

gmw_00005956.name: PSO J231.6576--20.8335
gmw_00005956.overview: PSO J231.6576--20.8335 is a bright quasar host galaxy at a redshift of approximately 6.59 [arXiv:2105.09958, arXiv:2401.04211]. It features a massive dust reservoir [arXiv:2401.04211], high-excitation molecular gas [arXiv:2105.09958], and a close companion galaxy located less than 10 kiloparsecs away [arXiv:2401.04211].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes PJ231-20 as a z=6.59 quasar host with high-J CO excitation, PDR-dominated molecular mass, XDR-driven CO(10-9), and dense PDR water/OH emission.

quotes.quote: The analysis of the FSLs indicates that the [ CII ] _ 158 m and [ CI ] _ 369 m emission arises predominantly from the neutral medium in photodissociation regions (PDRs). We find that line deficits are in agreement with those of local luminous infrared galaxies. The CO spectral line energy distributions (SLEDs), reveal significant high- J CO excitation in both quasar hosts.

Our CO SLED modeling of the quasar PJ231-20 shows that PDRs dominate the molecular mass and CO luminosities for J_ up 7 , while the J_ up 10 CO emission is likely driven by X-ray dissociation regions produced by the active galactic nucleus (AGN) at the very center of the quasar host. The J_ up >10 lines are undetected in the other galaxies in our study. The H _2 O 3_ 21 3_ 12 line detection in the same quasar places this object on the L_ H_2O L_ TIR relation found for low- z sources, thus suggesting that this water vapor transition is predominantly excited by IR pumping. Models of the H _2 O SLED and of the H _2 O/OH _ 163 m ratio point to PDR contributions with high volume and column density ( n_ H 0.8 10^5 cm^ 3 , N_ H =10^ 24 cm^ 2 ), in an intense radiation field.

sources.summary: The paper describes J231-20 as a bright z>6.5 HYPERION QSO with a close [CII] companion, rich molecular and atomic line detections, and massive dust reservoirs.

quotes.quote: PSO J231.6576-20.8335 (hereafter J231-20) at z=6.587 has been discovered using the Pan-STARRS1 survey mazzucchelli2017 , and it is one of the brightest objects at z>6.5 . decarli2017 detected a [ CII ] bright nearby companion at < 10 kpc separation. Pensabene2021 performed an extensive study of both the QSO and its companion, detecting [ NII ] , CO(7-6), CO(10-9) emission lines, two OH transitions and their underlying continuum in both of them. Additionally, the CO(15-14), CO(16-15) and three transitions of H _2 O emission line have been detected for the central QSO. Analysing the cold dust SED of both the QSO and its companion, they derived T_ dust =54 K, M_ dust =5.1 10^8 \ M_ and T_ dust =35-46 K, M_ dust =(2.3-3.4) 10^8 \ M_ , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper confirms PSO J231.6576--20.8335 spectroscopically and describes it as the brightest newly discovered quasar, among the brightest known at z>6.5.

quotes.quote: PSO J231.6576--20.8335 @ z=6.5864 The imaging follow-up for PSO231 20 was also undertaken with EFOSC2 and SofI at the NTT in February 2015. It was spectroscopically confirmed with Magellan/FIRE on the 2015 March 13, and we acquired a VLT/FORS2 spectrum on the 2015 May 15. With a J- band magnitude of 19.66, this quasar is the brightest newly discovered object, and one of the brightest known at z>6.5 , alongside PSO036+03 and VDESJ0224-4711.

gmw_00005957.name: PSO J247.2970+24.1277
gmw_00005957.overview: PSO J247.2970+24.1277 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z=6.476 [arXiv:1710.01251]. Spectroscopic observations of the active galactic nucleus reveal broad emission lines [arXiv:1710.01251].

sources.summary: The paper describes PSO J247.2970+24.1277 as a z=6.476 quasar confirmed by VLT/FORS2, followed by NIR spectroscopy, with prominent broad emission lines.

quotes.quote: PSO J247.2970+24.1277 @ z=6.476 We acquired follow-up photometric observations of PSO247+24 with CAFOS and Omega2000 at the 2.2m and 3.5m telescope at CAHA, respectively. We confirmed its quasar nature with VLT/FORS2 in March 2016 and we obtained NIR spectroscopy with Magellan/FIRE in the same month. This quasar presents prominent broad emission lines (see Figure figSpecAll ).

gmw_00005958.name: PSO J261.0364+19.0286
gmw_00005958.overview: PSO J261.0364+19.0286 is a relatively faint high-redshift quasar [arXiv:1710.01251]. Spectroscopic observations confirm that the active galactic nucleus is located at a redshift of z=6.44 [arXiv:1710.01251].

sources.summary: The paper confirms PSO J261.0364+19.0286 as a relatively faint quasar at z=6.44 from Palomar spectroscopy and template fitting.

quotes.quote: PSO J261.0364+19.0286 @ z=6.44 We used the 2.2m MPG/GROND and SofI at the NTT in June September 2016 to acquire follow-up photometry for PSO261+19. Spectroscopic observations with the DBSP at the Palomar Observatory in September 2016 confirmed that the object is a quasar at z=6.44 0.05 (redshift from SDSS quasar template fitting; see Section subsecRedTempl ). Similar to PSO11+09, this is a relatively faint quasar, with J_ G =21.09 .

gmw_00005959.name: HSC1205
gmw_00005959.overview: HSC1205 is a faint quasar characterized by a red continuum caused by internal galactic extinction [arXiv:1710.01251]. It hosts a very massive black hole and exhibits a low accretion rate [arXiv:1710.01251].

sources.summary: The paper finds HSC1205 is a faint, internally reddened quasar with a broad Mg II line, very massive black hole, and low accretion rate.

quotes.quote: We notice that HSC1205, the faintest object in the sample, present a very broad Mg II emission line: this leads to a high black hole mass ( 5 10 ^ 9 M _ ) and a low Eddington ratio of 0.06. However, HSC1205 is also characterized by a red J-W1 color of 1.97, suggesting that the quasar has a red continuum, due to internal galactic extinction. This could affect our measurement of the quasar intrinsic luminosity and therefore we could observe a value of the Eddington ratio lower than the intrinsic one.

A ^ 2 minimization routine suggests that this quasar has a large E(B-V) =0.3. The corrected monochromatic luminosity at _ rf =3000 is 1.62 10 ^ 46 erg s ^ 1 , and the resulting black hole mass and Eddington ratio are 7.22 10 ^ 9 M _ and 0.09, respectively. Therefore, even taking into account the high internal extinction, HSC1205 is found to host a very massive black hole and to accrete at the lowest rate in our sample.

gmw_00005960.name: TXS 1206 + 549
gmw_00005960.overview: TXS 1206+549 is a radio- and gamma-ray-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy located at a redshift of 1.344 [arXiv:2312.06917, arXiv:2402.10715]. It exhibits strong multiwavelength variability [arXiv:2103.16521], including a 134-day optical quasi-periodic oscillation [arXiv:2312.06917], and features perpendicular pairs of symmetric radio lobes [arXiv:2402.10715].

sources.summary: The paper reports a 134-day optical QPO in the radio- and gamma-ray loud NLSy1 galaxy TXS 1206+549, with related ATLAS and Fermi-LAT signals.

quotes.quote: Here we report an optical quasi-periodic oscillation (QPO) with a period of 134 day detected in g- and -band light curves of a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy TXS 1206+549 at redshift of 1.34 with the data from the Zwicky Transient Facility (ZTF) observations. After considering the trial factor, the significance levels in the two bands are 3.1 and 2.6 , respectively. The QPO signal presents about 10 cycles ranging from 2018 March to 2021 December lasting 4 year. A near-sinusoidal profile also appears in the folded light curves by a phase-resolved analysis.

TXS 1206+549 is a radio and ray loud NLSy1 galaxy and has a flat radio spectrum rss+21 . As the blazars and other ray NLSy1 galaxies, their broad-band SED shows the typical two-bump structure. They exhibit strong variability in the universal electromagnetic emissions in the optical, infrared, and ray bands. Up to now, TXS 1206+549 is the most distant NLSy1 galaxy at z= 1.344 having ray emissions rss+21 , while other (only about a dozen) ray NLSy1 galaxies were detected at z< 1.

sources.summary: The paper studies TXS 1206+549 as a gamma-ray radio-loud NLS1 galaxy, measuring superluminal jet motion and inferring moderate Lorentz factor and inclination.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: right ascension RA = 12^ h 08^ min (54.256432 0.000014)^ s and declination Dec = 54^ 41^ (58.16329 0.00014)^

quotes.quote: One of the few ray-detected RL-NLS1s that was recently found is TXS 1206 + 549 (also known as J1208+5441) at a redshift of z=1.344 . This is the most distant ray NLS1 found to date. In the Fermi 4LAC catalogue, it is classified as FSRQ .

TXS 1206 + 549 is a well-known, flat-spectrum radio source, with variable ( 120--400 mJy ) total flux density across two orders of magnitude in frequency, from 150  MHz to 15  GHz . Its extended ( 100 kpc scale) structure at low radio frequencies ( 144 and 642  MHz) is composed of two pairs of symmetric lobes that are perpendicular to each other (i.e., roughly east--west and north--south), hinting at a sudden realignment of the jet direction in the past . This extended relic radio structure is rarely seen in RL-NLS1 sources .

The mas-scale radio structure of TXS 1206 + 549 is compact at 8  GHz, as indicated in Figure  fig-map , shown as an example. In all six epochs, from 1994 to 2018, the brightness distribution modelling reveals a compact core (C), which corresponds to the synchrotron, self-absorbed base of the relativistic jet, and a more extended and weaker jet component can be seen south--southeast of the core.

sources.summary: TXS 1206+549 is identified as a z=1.344 gamma-ray NLS1 with narrow H emission, radio-loud flat-spectrum behavior, a blazar-like two-hump SED, and strong multiwavelength variability.

quotes.quote: Here, we report the identification of a new ray emitting NLS1 TXS 1206+549 at z=1.344 . A near-infrared spectrum taken with the Subaru telescope showed H emission line with FWHM of 1194 77 km s ^ 1 and weak [ O III ] emission line but no optical Fe II lines, due to the limited wavelength coverage and poor signal-to-noise ratio. However, UV Fe II lines are present in the SDSS optical spectrum. The source is very radio-loud, unresolved, and has a flat radio spectrum. The broadband SED of the source has the typical two hump structure shown by blazars and other NLS1s.

The source exhibits strong variability at all wavelengths such as the optical, infrared, and ray bands. All these observed characteristics show that TXS 1206+549 is the most distant NLS1 known to date.

gmw_00005961.name: SDSS J133018.65+181032.1
gmw_00005961.overview: SDSS J133018.65+181032.1 is a gravitationally lensed quasar located at a redshift of z=1.393 [arXiv:0809.0913]. The system is a fold-type quadruply lensed quasar that features four lensed images and a bright lensing galaxy [arXiv:0809.0913].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J133018.65+181032.1 as a new fold-type quadruply lensed quasar with a bright lensing galaxy and nearby group affecting the lens potential.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present the discovery of a new gravitationally lensed quasar with four lensed images, SDSS J133018.65+181032.1 (SDSS J1330+1810). It was discovered as part of the Sloan Digital Sky Survey Quasar Lens Search SQLS; oguri06,oguri08,inada08 , which takes advantage of the large spectroscopic quasar catalog see schneider07 of the Sloan Digital Sky Survey SDSS; york00 to locate new lensed quasars. We place particular emphasis on mass modeling and investigation of the structure around the lens.

The gravitational lens SDSS J1330+1810 was selected as a lens candidate by the morphological selection algorithm. The SDSS photometric designation of SDSS J1330+1810 is 5308/40/1/73/88 (run/rerun/camcol/filed/ID), and the spectroscopic designation is 2641/255/54230 (plate/fiber/MJD). Figure fig:fc1330 shows the SDSS i band image of the system (seeing of 1 0 ). The enlarged image clearly indicates that the system, which is classified as a quasar at z=1.393 from the SDSS spectrum (see Figure fig:spec1330 ), is not a point source but consists of multiple components.

We note that the case for gravitational lensing is already very strong from its characteristic configuration of image components, the similarity of their colors, and the presence of a bright lensing galaxy.

gmw_00005962.name: B1713+641
gmw_00005962.overview: B1713+641 is a polarized radio source located away from the center of the Abell 2255 cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0401216]. It exhibits a two-sided radio lobe structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0401216].

sources.summary: The paper treats B1713+641/A2255E as a two-lobed polarized radio source in Abell 2255 and compares standard-fit and Pacman rotation-measure maps for it.

quotes.quote: The polarised radio source B1713+641, which we call hereafter A2255E, has a two sided radio lobe structure and is not directly located in the cluster centre. Polarisation observations were performed using the Very Large Array (VLA) at 4535, 4885, 8085, and 8465 MHz . The data reduction was done with standard AIPS (Astronomical Imaging Processing Software) routines (Govoni et al., in prep.). The polarisation angle maps and their standard error maps for the four frequencies were kindly provided to us by Federica Govoni. A preliminary analysis of an RM map of this source in order to determine the properties of the magnetic field in the intra-cluster gas in Abell 2255 is presented in 2002taiwan.conf .

gmw_00005963.name: SDSS 0050+0113
gmw_00005963.overview: SDSS 0050+0113 is a quasar host galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.14 [arXiv:astro-ph/0209088]. It exhibits clearly detected, extended host emission and represents the most luminous galaxy within its observed sample [arXiv:astro-ph/0209088].

sources.summary: The paper detects extended host emission around SDSS 0050+0113, identifies it as the sample's most luminous host, and finds good agreement between luminosity- and Mg II-based black-hole mass estimates.

quotes.quote: They were SDSS J005008.48+011330.4 ( z=1.14 ; hereafter SDSS 0050+0113), SDSS J172732.39+584634.4 ( z=0.84 ; hereafter SDSS 1727+5846) and SDSS J232351.60+004034.4 ( z=0.76 ; hereafter SDSS 2323+0040). Total integration times were 75min for SDSS 0050+0113, 135 min for SDSS 1727+5846 and 120 min for SDSS 2323+0040.

SDSS 0050+0113 This host is the most luminous galaxy in the sample, so was fairly easily detected despite its high redshift and the relatively short total integration time we obtained. Some PSF residual is visible in Figure 4, but extended host emission is clearly detected. A comparison between the black hole mass estimates from the galaxy luminosities and the Mg ii linewidths shows that for SDSS 1727+5946 and SDSS 0050+0113, the agreement is very good, within a factor of two, but for SDSS 2323+0040 the black hole mass estimated from the linewidth is an order of magnitude less than that derived from the host.

gmw_00005964.name: SDSS 1727+5846
gmw_00005964.overview: SDSS 1727+5846 is a radio-loud quasar located at a redshift of z=0.84 [arXiv:astro-ph/0209088]. The object features a bright quasar nucleus and a relatively faint, poorly detected host galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0209088].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS 1727+5846 as the only radio-loud quasar in the sample, with a faint, poorly detected host and slightly resolved FIRST radio structure.

quotes.quote: They were SDSS J005008.48+011330.4 ( z=1.14 ; hereafter SDSS 0050+0113), SDSS J172732.39+584634.4 ( z=0.84 ; hereafter SDSS 1727+5846) and SDSS J232351.60+004034.4 ( z=0.76 ; hereafter SDSS 2323+0040). Only SDSS 1727+5846 is radio-loud. Total integration times were 75min for SDSS 0050+0113, 135 min for SDSS 1727+5846 and 120 min for SDSS 2323+0040.

SDSS 1727+5846 This quasar is the only radio-loud quasar in our sample. It is also the least well-detected host, with clear PSF residual being visible in Figure 4, due to a combination of a relatively faint host and a bright quasar nucleus. The radio structure is slightly resolved in the Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty-cm (FIRST) survey, with a size of 3 , a PA of 55 deg and a total flux density of 180 mJy; 30 away at PA 43 deg there is an unresolved 22mJy source, which may be associated.

gmw_00005965.name: SDSS 2323+0040
gmw_00005965.overview: SDSS 2323+0040 is a quasar host galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.76 [arXiv:astro-ph/0209088]. The galaxy shows evidence of interaction, with a faint companion galaxy located 1.4 arcseconds to its east and faint emission extending between them [arXiv:astro-ph/0209088].

sources.summary: The paper finds the SDSS 2323+0040 host comfortably detected, relatively well constrained, apparently interacting with nearby companions, and discrepant in black-hole mass estimates.

quotes.quote: They were SDSS J005008.48+011330.4 ( z=1.14 ; hereafter SDSS 0050+0113), SDSS J172732.39+584634.4 ( z=0.84 ; hereafter SDSS 1727+5846) and SDSS J232351.60+004034.4 ( z=0.76 ; hereafter SDSS 2323+0040). In one case (SDSS 2323+0040) the quasar was sufficiently faint that inaccuracies in individual image registrations when constructing the final mosaic add significantly to the width of the effective PSF. Fortunately, the quasar is relatively faint compared to the host galaxy in this case, so the uncertainty in the exact amount by which the PSF was broadened translates to only a small error in the host galaxy magnitude.

SDSS 2323+0040 The host galaxy of this quasar was comfortably detected, largely due to the weakness of the quasar nucleus, and consequently has the best-determined magnitude and scale size. It is the only one of our quasar hosts to show evidence of interaction. There is a faint companion galaxy 1.4 east of the quasar with faint emission between it and the quasar (Figure 4).

gmw_00005966.overview: The radio galaxy 0647+693 is located west of its cluster's X-ray peak [arXiv:astro-ph/0312245]. Its radio structure is intermediate between a narrow-angle and wide-angle tail [arXiv:astro-ph/0312245], showing highly bent inner jets [arXiv:astro-ph/0312245] and evidence of host-galaxy motion [arXiv:astro-ph/0610400].

sources.summary: The paper reports new radio imaging of 0647+693, describing its offset host, WAT plumes, unusually bent inner jets, and likely compact jet-termination features.

quotes.quote: 0647+693 No detailed radio observations of this source had previously been made. Based on a snapshot image, O'Dea Owen (1985) remarked that the source's structure is intermediate between narrow-angle and wide-angle tails, an impression reinforced by our data. G omez \ (1997) showed that the radio source is significantly (30 arcsec) to the west of the cluster X-ray peak.

DSS2 images show that the host galaxy is also 21 arcsec to the west of a second bright elliptical, although the host does appear to be the brighter of the two by around 0.5 mag in R . Our low-resolution maps (Fig. \ 0647l ) show that the plumes, particularly the southern plume, are not abnormal for a WAT. However, the high-resolution maps (Fig. \ 0647h ) show that the inner jets are very much more bent than in any of the other objects in our sample; the southern jet is detected over almost all its length, and (in projection) makes two sharp, almost 90^ bends at the positions labelled SJ1 and SJ2 in Fig. \ 0647h .

sources.summary: The paper models 0647+693 as a radio source whose maps show host-galaxy motion, using hot-spot geometry and opening angle measurements to compare simulation with observed radio structure.

quotes.quote: A radio--map of 0647+693 in the 8.5 GHz band can be found in hardcastle where two radio--images were traced at the resolution of 0.3 0.6 arcsec and 2 arcsec. The influence of the motion of the host galaxy is evident from the maps and can be visualised in the following way A line is traced between the two hot--spots. The perpendicular to the previous line that intersects the nucleus is traced.

The distance along the perpendicular to the previous line turns out to be 36.8 kpc . } From Figure 1 of hardcastle it is also possible to measure the second opening angle , _1 that turns out to be 25^ . The results of the simulation (as outlined in Section twophase ) are reported in Figure 0647_proj (red points) and in Figure 0647_radio_theo_1 , with the radio map superimposed on the simulation ;

gmw_00005967.overview: The radio galaxy 1231+674 is a wide-angle tail source hosted by a dumb-bell galaxy system [arXiv:astro-ph/0312245]. The radio emission is specifically associated with the brighter western component of this host system and features two well-defined jets [arXiv:astro-ph/0312245].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1231+674 as a WAT in a dumb-bell host, with two well-defined jets and distinct northern and southern termination behavior.

quotes.quote: 1231+674 This source has previously been imaged by O'Donoghue \ (1990) and Katz-Stone \ (1999). The image of the large-scale structure (Fig. \ 1231l ) thus shows few new details. The arc appearing perpendicular to the S plume, commented on by O'Donoghue , turns out to have an unresolved radio core identified with an optical galaxy in the field visible in the DSS2 plates.

The host galaxy of 1231+674 itself is a dumb-bell system aligned approximately E-W on the sky, with the radio source being associated with the brighter western component. The high-resolution image (Fig. 1231h ) shows two well-defined jets. The N jet bends smoothly and terminates in the bright region N1; this region contains some compact structure, elongated in the jet direction, whose magnetic field direction is transverse to the jet direction.

gmw_00005968.overview: The galaxy 1333+412 is the primary brightest cluster galaxy of Abell 1763 [arXiv:1812.02645]. It hosts a bent double-lobed radio source shaped by merger-driven intra-cluster medium flows [arXiv:1812.02645], with its northern lobe showing depolarization from this surrounding medium [arXiv:astro-ph/0312245].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BCG1 as Abell 1763's primary brightest cluster galaxy, with a high peculiar velocity and a bent WAT radio source shaped by merger-driven ICM flow.

quotes.quote: The brightest cluster galaxy of Abell 1763 possesses a high line-of-sight peculiar velocity (v _ pec 650 km s ^ 1 ) and hosts a powerful (P _ 1.4 10 ^ 26 W Hz ^ 1 ) bent double-lobed radio source, likely shaped by the relative bulk ICM flow induced in the merger.

In each of the three methods employed to establish the mean of the primary cluster velocity distribution, a high peculiar velocity is observed for BCG1. The peculiar velocity for BCG1 is calculated to be v _ pec = -871 km s ^ 1 , -1048 km s ^ 1 , and -649 km s ^ 1 for the algebraic, single-Gaussian, and double-Gaussian methods, respectively. The velocity offset was detected in the dynamical analysis of Fadda08 as well, with their sample of 100 galaxies.

Serving as indicators of the ICM velocity fields in their vicinities, WATs are hosted by both BCG1 and BCG2. Their bent lobes are oriented in a direction consistent with the described merger configuration.

sources.summary: The paper presents high-resolution observations of 1333+412, detecting its inner jets, lobe polarization asymmetry, and ambiguous northern jet termination.

quotes.quote: 1333+412 This small source has not been observed previously at high resolution; the best previous maps are those of Owen Ledlow (1997). Our low-resolution image (Fig. \ 1333l ) shows much the same features, but gives a clear detection of the inner jets. The small source to the E is associated with one of the many fainter galaxies in the field, and is likely to be a NAT lying in the cluster.

The N lobe is noticeably less strongly polarized than the S lobe. Since the cluster environment of 1333+412 is known to be rich, this is plausibly due to depolarization from the intra-cluster medium (ICM). The high-resolution image (Fig. \ 1333h ) shows the S jet penetrating into the plume for some distance (SJ1) before it terminates in the compact region S1.

gmw_00005969.overview: The radio source 1433+553 is classified as a wide-angle tail radio galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0312245]. Its northern jet leads to a compact, shock-like feature in the northern plume, while its southern jet bends into a compact bright region [arXiv:astro-ph/0312245].

sources.summary: The paper discusses 1433+553 as a WAT whose southern lobe lacks an obvious plume, while its jets bend into compact bright or shock-like features.

quotes.quote: 1433+553 This source was previously observed by O'Donoghue \ (1990) and Katz-Stone \ (1999). The image of the large-scale structure (Fig. \ 1433l ) is similar to others previously presented. The S lobe is unusual in that it shows no obvious evidence, either in total intensity or polarization, for a plume leading away from the source.

It is possible that the plume makes a small angle to the line of sight, so that we are seeing it in projection. The high-resolution image (Fig. \ 1433h ) shows the S jet bending (SJ1) before it enters a reasonably compact bright region, S1. The N jet is not traced all the way into the N plume at this resolution, but there is a clear compact feature (N1) in the plume with an unusual `shock-like' structure.

gmw_00005970.name: 3C 465
gmw_00005970.overview: The wide-angle-tailed radio galaxy 3C 465 is associated with NGC 7720 [arXiv:2005.11403, arXiv:astro-ph/0411148], a multiple-nuclei elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0808.1521] containing a face-on nuclear dust disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0411148]. This active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2005.11403] exhibits core variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0312245] and features a well-collimated jet that transitions smoothly into large-scale radio plumes [arXiv:2005.11403, arXiv:astro-ph/0312245].

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 465 as a WAT radio source associated with NGC7720, mapping its jets, hotspots, plumes, radio core, angular extent, and particle acceleration.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The best position for the core is RA 23h38m29.393s, Dec 27 ^ 01 ' 53. '' 25.

quotes.quote: 3C 465 is associated with NGC7720, the dominant diffuse galaxy in the cluster Abell 2634, and is among the best studied WAT sources in the northern sky due to its proximity and peculiar morphology within this class of radio sources. Detailed imaging studies in the optical (e.g. Colina&Perez90, Capetti05+ ), radio (e.g. Leahy84, Hardcastle&Sakelliou04 ) and X-ray (e.g. Schindler&Prieto97, Hardcastle05+ ) have previously been made. Its jet and hotspots as shown in our total intensity maps (see Section sec:3.3 ) have been extensively studied and large-scale properties of the plumes are also well known.

The total angular extent of 3C 465 estimated from our 1.5 GHz VLA map is 7.6 arcmin. This corresponds to a projected linear size of 275 kpc -- about 30 smaller than that measured by Leahy96+ using shorter VLA baselines. Our radio image shows a narrow, well collimated jet (denoted NW; Fig. fig:emlnvlamap ) emanating from the core of the galaxy; this jet which is presumably pointing towards us exhibit considerably high surface brightness over almost its full length.

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C465/NGC 7720 as an extended radio source in Abell 2634, giving its redshift and RM-map resolution while deriving cluster magnetic-field statistics.

quotes.quote: The radio source 3C465 (or NGC 7720) is located in the Abell cluster 2634 of richness class I. The redshift of the object is 0.0302 1999MNRAS.305..259W .

The details of the radio data reduction of the RM maps for the two sources above can be found in 2002ApJ...567..202E . The typical RM values observed for them ranging between 250 rad m ^ 2 and 250 rad m ^ 2 .
