The beamsize for the map of 3C75 (Abell 400) is 3 arcsec which translates into 1.4 kpc and for 3C465 (Abell 2634) the beamsize is 3.8 arcsec which is equivalent to 2.3 kpc for the cosmology chosen. Furthermore, it was assumed that the source plane is parallel to the observer plane since no indication for the Laing-Garrington effect was found.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C465 as having a systematic extended CCW transverse RM gradient along its southern jet, while its northern jet appears distorted.

quotes.quote: 3C465 .---The southern jet provides a clear example of a systematic, extended transverse RM gradient that extends along most of this jet (Fig. 6 of Eilek Owen 2002). The color scale chosen for the RM distribution is such that the RM map presented by Eilek Owen (2002) is essentially an RM sign map. The orientation of the RM gradient across the southern jet is CCW. The northern jet shows signs that it intrinsically had an overall pattern, but the jet and the RM pattern have become distorted. We do not have access to these data, and so were not able to carry out our own quantitative analysis for this source.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 465 as the prototype wide-angle tailed radio source, embedded in Abell 2634 with a compact host-galaxy core used to motivate the simulated environment.

quotes.quote: The observed head-tail radio sources are classified into two types according to their tail morphology, i.e., the narrow-angle tailed sources NATs; NGC 1265 is known as its prototype, see Figure fig:cartoon-head-tail-sources , right panel; ryle_radio_1968, odea_multifrequency_1986, sun_small_2005 and the wide-angle tailed sources WATs; 3C 465 is known as its prototype, see Figure fig:cartoon-head-tail-sources , left panel; burns_structure_1981, eilek_what_1984, eilek_magnetic_2002, hardcastle_chandra_2005 . As the tails are bent into the same direction, it implies that the intra-cluster weather should be some regular ICM motion relative (probably transverse) to the jet, rather than turbulence. The origin of such intra-cluster weather is subtle and there are several possibilities: (1) it is plausible that NATs are usually field galaxies moving through their host cluster, e.g., NGC 1265, it is falling onto the cluster center with a speed up to 2500 km/s sun_small_2005 ; (2) WATs usually settle in the cluster center, i.e., at the bottom of the potential well, so their relative velocities to the background are thought to be low, e.g., the one of 3C 465 is supposed to be only few hundreds of km/s leahy_3c_1984 ; (3) it is also known that merger between two clusters could induce shocks, the shock passage through the radio galaxies could also affect the jet morphology.

Taking 3C 465 for an example, there is a large X-ray halo with a size of several hundred kilo-parsec surrounding it, which is recognised as the core of its host cluster Abell 2634 hardcastle_chandra_2005 . The observations also show that there is a cold compact core in its host galaxy NGC 7720. The cold compact core is probably a consequence of weak cooling flow which could be supplying accretion fuel to the central supermassive black hole, so it may also be the reason why the galaxy is active schindler_x-ray_1997 .

sources.summary: NGC 7720 is treated as the southern galaxy in an Abell 2634 pair, associated with 3C 465 and showing a face-on nuclear dust disk with centrally peaked gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 7720 --- Fig. 1(i) : This system in Abell 2634 consists of two galaxies separated by 12 . We are interested in the southern galaxy of the pair : it possesses a nearly face-on nuclear dust disk and the radio source 3C 465 appears associated with it (Venturi et al. 1995). An H + [ N II ] image of this galaxy as well as an HST/WFPC2 R band image of the pair are presented in Martel et al. (2000). Our deeper ACS frames show more detail in the structure of the disk. Also, the gas distribution is strongly peaked on the nucleus and does not appear to extend beyond the edges of the disk.

sources.summary: Chandra and XMM-Newton observations detect the nucleus and inner jet of 3C 465, while its plumes lack X-rays and coincide with large-scale X-ray deficits.

quotes.quote: We have observed the prototypical wide-angle tail radio galaxy 3C 465 with Chandra and XMM-Newton . X-ray emission is detected from the active nucleus and the inner radio jet, as well as a small-scale, cool component of thermal emission, a number of the individual galaxies of the host cluster (Abell 2634), and the hotter thermal emission from the cluster itself. The X-ray detection of the jet allows us to argue that synchrotron emission may be an important mechanism in other well-collimated, fast jets, including those of classical double radio sources. The bases of the radio plumes are not detected in the X-ray, which supports the model in which these plumes are physically different from the twin jets of lower-power radio galaxies. The plumes are in fact spatially coincident with deficits of X-ray emission on large scales, which argues that they contain little thermal material at the cluster temperature, although the minimum pressures throughout the source are lower than the external pressures estimated from the observed thermal emission.

sources.summary: The wide-angle-tail radio galaxy 3C 465 has a host-coincident point-like X-ray component plus clear larger-scale extended emission after excluding companion-galaxy contamination.

quotes.quote: 3C 465 Sakelliou Merrifield (1998) present HRI data which show a point-like X-ray component coincident with the host galaxy of this wide-angle tail radio galaxy. After excluding X-ray emission from the northern companion galaxy of the AGN host and realigning the HRI data from the two observation epochs as suggested by Sakelliou Merrifield, we find the radial profile of the central source is moderately well fit by a broadened PRF; we take a 1-arcmin source circle and background between 1.7 and 4 arcmin, excluding confusing sources. Further out, there is clear evidence for extended emission on arcminute scales.

sources.summary: 3C 465 is a wide-angle-tail FR I radio galaxy in cluster gas; the ICM covers most of the chip and is centered on the host.

quotes.quote: 3C 465 ( z=0.031 ) : 3C 465 is a wide-angle tail FR I radio galaxy located in cluster gas that has been well studied both with Chandra and XMM-Newton by hardcastle05 . The ICM covers most of the chip and is centered on the host of 3C 465. The ICM has clear ellipticity and the bright extended emission is comparable to the size of entire radio galaxy We fit ellipses to both the ISM and ``local'' ICM on a scale slightly smaller than half the size of the chip. hardcastle05 also present a radial profile which is in agreement with our average temperature for the cluster gas. The spectrum of the AGN can be fit well either by a power law with < 0.5 and a thermal model or a slightly absorbed power law with = 2.4 and a thermal model.

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C 465 as a WAT in a rich cluster, noting core variability, strong rotation measures, and a smooth jet-plume transition lacking compact hotspots.

quotes.quote: In 3C 465 and 1433+553 there was some evidence of core variability over the epochs of observation, and so the task uvsub was used to bring the core flux densities into agreement. The only direct observational evidence we have on individual sources (except for , which we discuss in more detail below) comes from the observations of 3C 465 by Eilek Owen (2002), which show the magnitude of RM to range up to 250 rad m ^ 2 in small, discrete regions : this would imply polarization corrections of around 20^ . However, 3C 465's host cluster may not be representative.

There are compact (comparable in size to the jet width), relatively bright features associated with the jet termination in 0647+693 (N jet), 1231+674 (N jet), 1333+412 (both jets) and 1433+553 (possibly both jets) but not clearly in 2236 176, 3C 465 or 1610 608, in which the jets simply make a smooth transition into the base of the plumes.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC7720 as a multiple-nuclei galaxy with a massive elliptical companion, published HST imaging, and possible velocity substructure without significant rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC7720: This is a multiple nuclei galaxy and has a massive close companion galaxy, also an elliptical. The surface brightness profile was published by Lauer (1988), and HST imaging by Laine et al. \ (2003). The radial velocity profile reveals no significant rotation but there might be some substructure in this galaxy.

gmw_00005971.name: SDSS J155426.16+193703.0
gmw_00005971.overview: SDSS J155426.16+193703.0 is a highly luminous quasar located at a redshift of z = 4.612 [arXiv:1505.05262]. Observations of its broad [C II] emission line reveal a velocity gradient, indicating the presence of a massive rotating disk of star-forming gas [arXiv:1505.05262].

sources.summary: The paper finds J1554+1937 has broad [C II] with a velocity gradient indicating a massive rotating star-forming disk and a black hole broadly consistent with local scaling relations.

quotes.quote: We present observations and analysis of an unusual [ C II ] emission line in the very luminous QSO SDSS J155426.16+193703.0 at z 4.6 . The line is extremely broad (FWHM 735 km s ^ 1 ) and seems to have a flat-topped or double-peaked line profile. A velocity map of the line shows a gradient across the source that indicates large-scale rotation of star-forming gas. Together, the velocity map and line profile suggest the presence of a massive rotating disc with a dynamical mass M_ dyn 5 10^ 10  M_ .

With a redshift of z=4.612 , the QSO SDSS J155426.16+193703.0 (hereafter J1554+1937) has the highest redshift of these most luminous QSOs from the Krawczyk et al. catalog. J1554+1937 has a bolometric luminosity L_ bol =1.3 10^ 14 L_ . There is no known evidence to suggest that J1554+1937 is lensed (and thus has an artificially brightened apparent luminosity).

gmw_00005972.name: SDSS J121426.52+140258.9
gmw_00005972.overview: SDSS J121426.52+140258.9 is a radio-loud quasar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2211.13407]. Multi-epoch observations reveal that the galaxy transitioned between low and high optical flux states, while exhibiting a soft X-ray excess during its high optical state [arXiv:2211.13407].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes multi-epoch Swift and XMM observations of radio-loud quasar J1214+1402, finding optical-state changes, unusual X-ray behavior, and a soft excess likely tied to a warm corona.

quotes.quote: To understand the X-ray emission of active galactic nuclei (AGNs), we explored the optical-to-X-ray variation correlation of a radio-loud quasar (RLQ) SDSS J121426.52+140258.9 (hereafter J1214+1402) with multi-epoch observations of Swift and XMM-Newton telescopes. With the historical multi-band data, we found that the infrared to X-ray flux of RLQ J1214+1402 should not be dominated by the beamed jet emission. The Swift optical/UV and X-ray light curves showed that J1214+1402 has two optical states with low flux before 2014 April 08 and high flux after 2014 June 11, but has no significant X-ray variations during the time range between 2007 March 09 and 2014 August 04. This result was supported by the XMM-Newton observations in the overlapped time with Swift.

Interestingly, the early XMM-Newton data prior to the Swift time presents two unusual emission epochs when J1214+1402 has relatively low optical fluxes but has the brightest X-ray fluxes. The overall independence of optical-to-X-ray variation seems hard to be described by the disk-corona model. With the X-ray spectral fitting, we find that the soft X-ray excess in J1214+1402 appears only during the high optical state when the X-ray emission is at low state. The soft X-ray excess in J1214+1402 is difficult to be explained by the ionized accretion disk, instead, it may be related to the warm corona.

gmw_00005973.name: B2209 + 236
gmw_00005973.overview: B2209 + 236 is a core-dominated flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:1006.4777]. The source is highly compact and potentially young, exhibiting a radio turnover frequency that exceeds 5 GHz [arXiv:1006.4777].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes B2209 + 236 as a compact core-dominated flat-spectrum radio quasar, possibly young because its radio turnover frequency exceeds 5 GHz.

quotes.quote: B2209 + 236 B2209 + 236 is a core-dominated flat-spectrum radio quasar. The radio turn-over frequency of this source is above 5 GHz dallacasa00 , which indicates that the source is very compact and is possibly young. Here we used the Swift observation obtained in April 2009 with an integration time of 9 ks in the SED study (obsid 00036359002).

gmw_00005974.name: NGC 772
gmw_00005974.overview: NGC 772 is a face-on spiral galaxy located in the blue cloud [arXiv:1907.10706] that features a single spiral arm [arXiv:1907.10706] and an extremely large interaction-driven envelope [arXiv:1907.10706]. It has hosted two simultaneously observable Type II supernovae [arXiv:2302.03112] and exhibits conflicting reports regarding whether its spiral structure is leading or trailing [arXiv:1910.10926].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 772 as a spiral host of two simultaneously observable Type II supernovae, deriving broadly consistent but quality-limited tailored-EPM distances from both events.

quotes.quote: By filtering the catalogue, we found that SN IIP pairs in four host galaxies met all the conditions that we described above: NGC 772, NGC 922, NGC 4303 (M 61), and NGC 6946 (Fig. fig:hosts ). To retrieve the data for the supernovae in these hosts, we made use of the OSC and WISeREP https://www.wiserep.org/ wiserep .

NGC 772 The spiral galaxy NGC 772 (Fig. fig:hosts ) is unique, as it not only had been host to two Type II supernovae, but both objects were also observable simultaneously, as they exploded one and a half months after one another. Both supernovae were followed up by the Carnegie Type II Supernova Program (CATS), and their spectral sequences were previously analysed by . The first supernova, SN 2003hl, was discovered on JD 2452872.0 by .

We obtain D = 33.95 4.49 Mpc and 26.02 1.85 Mpc for the distance of NGC 772 from SN 2003hl and SN 2003iq respectively. Although we could use only one spectrum for SN 2003hl, and hence the resulting distance estimate is fairly uncertain, both the final result and the / v_ ph value around 15 days show broad consistency with those of SN 2003iq.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC772 as a questionable spin-parity case, comparing conflicting approaching-side reports and stating that more observations are needed to decide leading versus trailing structure.

quotes.quote: NGC772 The figures in both Pignatelli2001 and Rhee1996 show that the northwest side is approaching. This makes NGC772 a unique galaxy with leading spiral arms. Heraudeau and Simien1998 , however, indicated that the southeast sides is approaching according to their convention of showing the rotation curve for PA=130 ^ , and this places NGC772 in the category of trailing spirals. For NGC772, additional observational confirmation of which side of the major axis is approaching us is necessary to conclude whether the spiral is leading or trailing.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 772 as a blue-cloud face-on spiral with one arm, three galaxies in a stream, and an extremely large interaction-driven envelope.

quotes.quote: NGC 772 is also identified as being in the blue cloud, but it is a face-on spiral with one spiral arm and 3 galaxies entrained in a stream; they all lie projected on a field of complex infrared cirrus. It is in the blue cloud by virtue of its disk, and hosts an extremely large envelope due to the ongoing interaction. NGC 474, NGC 772, and NGC 5746 are anomalous in their hosting of large envelopes yet not residing on the red sequence.

gmw_00005975.name: G1
gmw_00005975.overview: G1 is a spiral galaxy with an absolute magnitude of -21.1, making it photometrically similar to the Milky Way [arXiv:1912.06659]. It is the nearest galaxy to the 3C 273 sightline, with an impact parameter of 2.5 virial radii [arXiv:1912.06659].

sources.summary: The paper considers G1, the nearest spiral galaxy to the 3C 273 sightline, as a possible but unlikely CGM origin for the observed hot absorption.

quotes.quote: The most likely object to provide sufficient ion columns is G1 (see Fig. fig:filaments ), the nearest galaxy to the sight-line ( b_ impact 500 kpc) at a redshift close to F1. It is a spiral galaxy with M_r = -21.1 , and hence photo-metrically similar to the Milky Way 2015ApJ...809...96L . Using the IllustrisTNG simulations nelson18 for galaxies with halo mass log (M_ halo ) = 12.0 , that is, the same as for the Milky Way, for b_ impact 500 kpc we obtained an average log N_ OVI ( cm^ 2 ) 12.2 with 1 halo-to-halo variation of 9.8-13.0 (D. Nelson priv. comm.). Therefore the O VI column of the z=0.09 absorber, log( N_ OVI )=13.263 0.110 tilton12 , seems consistent with absorption through the halo of G1.

In the case of 3C 273, the sight-line has a 2.5 R_ vir impact parameter with respect to the galaxy G1. Hence, linking the X-ray absorption to the hot halo of G1 would require a substantial CGM, extending far beyond the virial radius. Unfortunately, the available X-ray instrumentation is not capable of providing the information required to determine the mass distribution of hot gas around spirals out to very large radii.

gmw_00005976.name: 4FGL J2146.5-1344
gmw_00005976.overview: 4FGL J2146.5-1344 is a high-redshift blazar classified as a high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2001.05020]. Multiwavelength modeling suggests it likely belongs to the masquerading BL Lac class, which consists of flat-spectrum radio quasars disguised as BL Lac objects [arXiv:2001.05020].

sources.summary: The paper studies 4FGL J2146.5-1344 as a high-redshift HSP BL Lac, modeling its multiwavelength SED and arguing it likely belongs to the masquerading BL Lac class.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we study a high- z BL Lac, 4FGL J2146.5-1344, detected to be at z =1.34 using the photometric dropout technique. We collected multi-wavelength data for this source from optical up to rays, in order to study its spectral energy distribution (SED). In particular, this source was observed for the first time with NuSTAR , which accurately measures the synchrotron emission of this blazar up to 50 keV. Despite being classified as an HSP BL Lac object, the modeling of the SED reveals that this source likely belongs to the ``masquerading BL Lac" class, which comprises of FSRQs appearing as disguised BL Lac objects.

4FGL J2146.5-1344 (J2146 from here on) is an HSP BL Lac first detected in the 1FGL catalog 1fgl and then subsequently reported in all Fermi LAT catalogs 2fgl, 3fgl, 4fgl . Found to be a high- z source ( z= 1.34) by kaur2017 as a part of the photometric campaign for BL Lacs begun by rau2012 , J2146 exhibits a synchrotron peak frequency of 10 ^ 16 Hz, a luminosity in excess of 10 ^ 47 erg s ^ 1 , and a very hard ray spectrum with photon index 1.6.

sources.summary: The paper investigates NVSS J214637-13435, previously featureless with a high photometric redshift, but new SALT/RSS spectra detect no features and cannot confirm the photometric value.

quotes.quote: NVSS J214637-13435 Two featureless medium S/N spectra Shaw13,Mas13 of this source have been reported. Furthermore, a very high photometric redshift, z = 1.34 has been measured by Kau17 . This result is based on the detection of the Lyman break with multicolor Swift /UVOT and GROND photometry.

In order to investigate this interesting source, we performed two observations for a total observing time of 4263 s with SALT/RSS. No feature could be detected in the S/N = 135 spectrum and we could not determine the redshift of this source (Fig. fig_spec4 , bottom panel, on the right). Given that at z = 1.34, Lyman is not detectable from the ground, we cannot confirm or disprove the photometric redshift.

gmw_00005977.name: host of FRB 20190520B
gmw_00005977.overview: The host of FRB 20190520B is a dwarf galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.241 [arXiv:2110.07418, arXiv:2202.13458]. It is characterized by a high specific star-formation rate for its stellar mass [arXiv:2110.07418, arXiv:2204.12334] and contains dust extinction estimated from its Balmer emission lines [arXiv:2204.12334].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J160204.31 111718.5 as the FRB host, measures z=0.241, and characterizes it as a dwarf galaxy with relatively high star formation for its stellar mass.

quotes.quote: The optical image ( R^ band) obtained by CFHT/MegaCam reveals a galaxy (J160204.31 111718.5) at the location of the FRB, the light profile of which peaks at 1 \ south east. Given the measured offset 1.3 of the FRB from the galaxy and the density of galaxies with this magnitude, we estimate a chance coincidence probability of 0.8 (see Methods) , supporting the claim that J160204.31 111718.5 is the host galaxy of . The NIR image ( J band, 1.153-1.354 m) obtained by Subaru/MOIRCS shows the most likely stellar component of J160204.31 111718.5 with an AB magnitude of 22.1 0.1 in J band, with \ and the radio continuum counterpart on the galaxy periphery.

We obtained an optical spectrum at the location of the FRB with the Double Spectrograph on the Palomar 200-inch Hale Telescope which revealed the redshift of the putative host to be z = 0.241 based on a detection of strong H , [ O III ] 4859 , and [ O III ] 5007 \ lines (see Methods). A follow-up observation with the Low Resolution Imaging Spectrometer (LRIS) at the Keck I Telescope covering both the FRB location and the nearby Subaru J band source along the extended R^ band structure indicates the R^ band structure is dominated by the [ O III ] emission at the same redshift of z=0.241 . The H luminosity L_ H = 7.4 0.2 10^ 40 erg sec ^ 1 after extinction correction suggests a star formation rate of 0.41 M _ yr ^ 1 . Based on the J band magnitude, we estimate the stellar mass of the host galaxy to be 6 10^8 M _ . Thus, we characterize J160204.31 111718.5 as a dwarf galaxy with a relatively high star-formation rate for its stellar mass compared with the local SDSS galaxies .

sources.summary: The paper describes the host galaxy of FRB 20190520B as a z=0.24 high-specific-star-formation dwarf with large local DM, Balmer-line extinction, and a 5 kpc FRB offset.

quotes.quote: FRB 20190520B is characterized by its large DM 1200 cm ^ 3 pc compared to the redshift of the host galaxy z = 0.24 , and the association with a compact persistent radio source Niu:2021a . The large DM indicates the DM component that arise from gas in the host galaxy reaches 900 cm ^ 3 pc, which is nearly an order of magnitude higher than that in other FRB host galaxies and overwhelms the IGM component which can be used as a distance indicator.

It is also possible that optical emission from FRB 20190520B is affected by the dust extinction within the host galaxy of the FRB. Although the host galaxy dust extinction along the line-of-sight to the FRB is not known, the averaged extinction in the host galaxy can be estimated from the flux ratio of the emission lines in the Balmer series. The host galaxy of FRB 20190520B is a dwarf galaxy with high specific star formation rate Niu:2021a .

Spectroscopic observation of the host galaxy revealed F_ H_ = (23.9 0.3) 10^ 17 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , and F_ H_ = (6.2 0.3) 10^ 17 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 corrected for the MW dust extinction ( Ocker:2022a Ocker:2022a ; Tsai et al., in prep.). Assuming the intrinsic line ratio of F_ H_ /F_ H_ = 2.86 and the extinction law derived by Calzetti:2000a which is often used for an actively star forming galaxy, the estimated color excess is E_ B-V, host = 0.25 in the rest frame of the host galaxy which corresponds to A_ T, host = 0.94 in the observer frame. However, we note that FRB 20190520B is located 1.3 arcsec offset ( 5 kpc) from the center of the host galaxy, and the spatial structure of the emission lines in the host galaxy is not resolved in the spectroscopic observation.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J160204.31 111718.5 as the dwarf host of FRB 20190520B and argues that gas in this host dominates the burst’s large dispersion and pulse broadening.

quotes.quote: Recently, the repeating FRB 20190520B (hereafter FRB 190520) was discovered by 2021arXiv211007418N with the Five-hundred-meter Aperture Spherical radio Telescope (FAST, nan11,li19 ) and found to have a large = 1205 4 pc cm ^ 3 but a relatively small redshift z = 0.241 for its dwarf host galaxy, J160204.31 111718.5 (hereafter referred to as HG190520). The large implied DM contributed by the host galaxy is an example where the uncertainty in estimating the DM contribution from the IGM is much larger than often assumed. For FRB 190520, naive estimates of the DM budget without the host localization would place the source at a redshift z>1 , demonstrating the significant impact that underestimated host DMs can have on DM-derived distances. These results raise the question of whether the interstellar medium (ISM) of HG190520 contains an anomalously large electron density content, or whether other FRB host galaxy DMs are being systematically underestimated.

We attribute these to scattering in the host galaxy HG190520 and in the Milky Way, respectively, because the pulse broadening is too large to be caused by the IGM or by the halos of either galaxy and the observed frequency structure is consistent with expectations based on scintillation measurements of Galactic pulsars. It therefore appears highly likely that the pulse broadening of FRB 190520 is dominated by gas within HG190520, including material near or within the FRB local environment.

sources.summary: The paper reports no Chandra detection at the FRB 20190520B host center, then identifies the dwarf host as a BPT outlier with a persistent radio source resembling FRB 20121102A.

quotes.quote: For FRB 20190520B, we analyze a single ACIS-S 14.9 ks exposure (Obs ID: 22370; PI: Aggarwal; Sydnor et al. 2023). We do not detect any counts in a 1 \ radius aperture centered on the host center. The 3 upper limit on the count rate is 4.4 10^ 4 cts s ^ 1 . For N_ H,MW = 2.56 10^ 21 \ cm^ 2 , the 3 limit on the X-ray flux is F_X < 3.7 10^ 15 \ erg \ cm^ 2 \ s^ 1 ( 2-10 keV).
Indeed, its location as an outlier on the BPT diagram, coupled with a dwarf host and the presence of a coincident PRS Niu2022 , bear a striking similarity to FRB 20121102A Chatterjee2017 .

gmw_00005978.name: NGC 2336
gmw_00005978.overview: NGC 2336 is a nearly face-on, intermediate-type barred ringed spiral galaxy [arXiv:1803.00506, arXiv:astro-ph/0504393] with a regular morphological structure and an undisturbed disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0504393]. Its multi-armed spiral structure contains star-forming regions associated with its arms [arXiv:1803.00506], though its central region exhibits a deficiency of gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0504393].

sources.summary: NGC 2336 is described as a nearly face-on barred ringed spiral with multi-armed structure, prominent star-forming regions, gas richness, and UV-traced star formation along its arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 2336 (UGC 3809) is a barred ringed spiral galaxy, located in the constellation Camelopardalis (Table ngc2336_para ). The optical and UV images of NGC 2336 clearly show that it is close to face-on and has a multi-armed spiral structure (Figure fig:ngc2336_vis_uv ). It has the Hubble classification of SAB()bc type. It is one of the few galaxies that is thought to strongly resemble our Milky way galaxy efremov2011 .

NGC 2336 is a bright galaxy and has prominent star forming regions associated with its spiral arms. The multi-arm and branched spiral structure is the most striking morphological feature of this galaxy. Young stars, gas, and dust make up the star forming regions along the arms gusev2003structure . The disk has six well developed spiral arms arising from the ring around the small bar boroson1981,biviano1991 .

sources.summary: The paper builds stationary mass and orbit models for barred galaxy NGC 2336, fitting its NIR bulge, bar, and disk to reproduce morphology and HII-gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: For the intermediate-type barred galaxy NGC 2336 stationary models are constructed which reproduce in a consistent manner the observed distribution of the luminous matter and the observed gas kinematics in those regions affected by the bar. We present 2D fits to the observed NIR band luminosity distribution that consist of three components: a bulge, a bar, and a disk. The brightness distribution of each component is converted into an underlying mass distribution by means of a suitable M/L conversion.

In an iterative procedure, the parameters of the NGC 2336-models are optimized by computing and evaluating a large number of parameters. The result is a final model that reproduces the morphological structure of NGC 2336 as well as the observed kinematics of the HII-gas. The parameter values from the morphological decomposition and those needed to fit the HII-rotation curves best are in exellent agreement. The effects of changing single parameter values and possible error sources are discussed in detail. It turns out that the kinematics of the warm HII-gas of NGC 2336 can be explained without considering hydrodynamic effects, even in the central regions.

sources.summary: The paper reports BTA spectroscopy of H II regions in NGC 2336, deriving oxygen and nitrogen abundances, electron temperatures, extinction, and radial abundance information across the galaxy disk.

quotes.quote: Here we report spectra of 63 H ii regions in a sample of six spiral galaxies: NGC 628, NGC 783, NGC 2336, NGC 6217, NGC 7331, and NGC 7678. A first motivation of the spectral observations is the determination of the oxygen and nitrogen abundances and electron temperatures in individual H ii regions using the recent NS- and ON calibration pilyugin2010,pilyuginmattsson2011 . The abundances will be used in the computation of the grid of evolutionary models of star clusters embedded in studied H ii regions.

Spectroscopic observations of 63 H ii regions in six spiral galaxies (NGC 628, NGC 783, NGC 2336, NGC 6217, NGC 7331, and NGC 7678) were carried out with the 6-meter telescope (BTA) of Russian Special Astrophysical Observatory with the Spectral Camera attached to the focal reducer SCORPIO in the multislit mode with a dispersion of 2.1 /pixel and a spectral resolution of 10 . These observations were used to estimate the oxygen and nitrogen abundances and the electron temperatures in H ii regions through the recent variant of the strong line method (NS calibration). The parameters of the radial distribution (the extrapolated central intercept value and the gradient) of the oxygen and nitrogen abundances in the disks of spiral galaxies NGC 628, NGC 783, NGC 2336, NGC 7331, and NGC 7678 have been determined. The abundances in the NGC 783, NGC 2336, NGC 6217, and NGC 7678 are measured for the first time.

sources.summary: The atlas describes NGC 2336 as a small-barred galaxy with two extended radio regions, no detected bar or nuclear emission, no polarization, and likely spurious northern extensions.

quotes.quote: NGC 2336 (Fig. 8) has a small optical bar. Two extended regions of total radio emission are prominent, but they do not coincide well with star-forming regions. No emission has been detected from the bar and the nucleus. No polarization has been detected. The northern extensions at 18 cm and 22 cm are probably not real but result from poor data coverage in the uv plane.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2336 as a ringed barred spiral with a prominent bar, regular morphology, central gas deficiency, and no distinct signs of recent interaction despite a projected companion.

quotes.quote: NGC 2336: NGC 2336 is an intermediate-type ringed barred spiral galaxy with a prominent bar. This galaxy shows a very regular morphological structure with no major asymmetries, except for the central regions where HI maps show a lack of gas in the centre ( 1983A&AS...54...19V 1983A&AS...54...19V ). The monochromatic image suggests the same lack. The NW part of the disc has been cutoff by the wings of the interference filter. Although NGC 2336 belongs to an apparent pair of galaxies (together with IC 467) with a projected linear distance of 135 kpc, its undisturbed disc does not exhibit any distinct sign of recent interactions ( 1999A&A...344..787W 1999A&A...344..787W ).

gmw_00005979.name: NGC 3953
gmw_00005979.overview: NGC 3953 is a massive barred spiral galaxy located in the Ursa Major cluster [arXiv:1605.01638] and associated with the M81 group [arXiv:astro-ph/0504393]. It features a low bulge-to-disc ratio [arXiv:2404.11656], a looping tidal stream [arXiv:2404.11656], and a central deficiency of neutral gas [arXiv:1605.01638, arXiv:astro-ph/0504393].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3953 as a massive, HI-deficient Ursa Major SBbc galaxy with neutral gas inside the stellar disc and incomplete VLA velocity coverage.

quotes.quote: NGC 3953 NGC 3953 is an SBbc galaxy in the Ursa Major cluster. Although, one of the most massive galaxies in the Ursa Major cluster and in the present sample, it is relatively deficient of HI. The neutral gas does not extend much beyond the stellar disc, and the surface density decreases towards the centre where there is a prominent bar visible in the H image in Fig.  fig:n3953sum . The archival VLA observations did not have a bandpass wide enough to include the full velocity range of NGC 3953, as is evident in the HI figures. The receding side of the galaxy is missing about 77 km s ^ 1 , or 15 channels at a velocity resolution of 5.2 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3953 as a barred galaxy with low bulge-to-disc ratio, leftover turbulent gas, a looping tidal stream, and smoother star-formation density.

quotes.quote: On the contrary, there are not many studies concerning NGC 3953 and NGC 4725. The former has a rather low bulge/disc ratio, suggesting that its inner regions are mostly dominated by components other than the bulge, such as the bar and ring Castro-Rodriguez2003 . Besides, the work of seems to show the presence of some leftover turbulent gas in this galaxy, resulting in a newly discovered looping tidal stream.
NGC 3953 is observed in 20 bands; GALEX filters (FUV, NUV) and PACS 70 m photometry are not available for this object.
The only exception is NGC 3953, which shows an overall smoother distribution of SFR density.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3953 as a massive M81-group spiral that is poor in emission, with gas near the bar end, central gas deficiency, and developed arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 3953: One of the most massive spirals of the M81 group, it has been studied in \ by 2001A&A...370..765V ( 2001A&A...370..765V ). It is rather poor in \ and the surface density drops near D_ 25 . Moreover, in CO (Helfer et al. 2003), the gas seems to be located in a ring at the near end of the bar. In , the same lack of gas in the centre can be seen, whereas the arms are well developped.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 3953 for diffuse polarized outer-disc radio emission, absent significant bar and nuclear radio emission, and measurable southern Faraday rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3953 (Fig. 10) is notable for its extended, diffuse, polarized radio emission from the outer disc. No significant radio emission from the bar and the nucleus has been detected. Significant Faraday rotation between 22 cm and 6 cm occurs in the southern part of the galaxy ( +15 ).

gmw_00005980.name: NGC 3059
gmw_00005980.overview: NGC 3059 is a barred spiral galaxy characterized by a small optical bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0207201]. Diffuse, polarized radio emission is detected across its entire disc, indicating a widespread, spiral, regular magnetic field [arXiv:astro-ph/0207201].

sources.summary: NGC 3059 is described as a small-barred galaxy with diffuse polarized emission across the disc, a widespread spiral regular magnetic field, and a possible bar-aligned component unresolved by these observations.

quotes.quote: NGC 3059 (Fig. 22) has a small optical bar. Diffuse, polarized radio emission has been detected in the whole disc, indicating a widespread, spiral, regular magnetic field similar to that of non-barred spiral galaxies. This is not surprising as the bar is small and under-resolved. We cannot exclude that this galaxy has a magnetic field component aligned with the bar.

gmw_00005981.name: GRB 080218B object A
gmw_00005981.overview: GRB 080218B object A is an elongated, extremely red galaxy located within the error circle of the gamma-ray burst GRB 080218B [arXiv:1202.1434]. It is considered the most likely host galaxy and may be a spiral galaxy seen nearly edge-on or a tight pair of galaxies [arXiv:1202.1434].

sources.summary: Object A is treated as the likely GRB 080218B host: an elongated, very red candidate inside the XRT error circle that may be edge-on or a galaxy pair.

quotes.quote: In the FORS image, object A is elongated in the southwest-northeast direction ( 2 5 1 1 ). It could be a spiral galaxy seen nearly edge-on or a tight pair of galaxies. If it is a single galaxy, then its large (R-K)_ AB color (4.2 mag) defines it as an ERO galaxy. The probability of finding a galaxy of this R_C band magnitude within an area of radius 1r_0 on the sky is about 0.1. Given its extremely red color and its position inside the 90 .l. error circle, we consider A as the most likely GRB host galaxy.

gmw_00005982.name: GRB 081204 object F
gmw_00005982.overview: GRB 081204 object F is a compact-core, extremely red object that is considered the most likely host candidate for GRB 081204 [arXiv:1202.1434]. It lies close to nearby galaxies and is possibly interacting with a neighboring galaxy designated as object B [arXiv:1202.1434].

sources.summary: Object F is the preferred GRB 081204 host candidate, a compact-core extremely red object close to nearby galaxies and possibly interacting with object B.

quotes.quote: Within the 90 .l. error circle lie at least three galaxies (A, B, F; Fig. 081204K ), which are all within 5 0 of each other and could represent an interacting group. Objects A and B have similar magnitudes ( R_ AB 23.2 and 23.5, respectively) and sizes. Object F ( R_ AB 24.6 ) lies very close, northeast of object B. In the VIMOS image, it is much fainter than A and B, but in the ISAAC image it is distinctive because of its bright, point-like core ( K_ AB =21.5 ). Within the (not so small) photometric errors, it can be classified as an extremely red object.
Therefore, we consider F, which is possibly interacting with B, as the most likely birthplace of GRB 081204.

gmw_00005983.name: host galaxy of GRB 080810
gmw_00005983.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 080810 is an interacting galaxy system at a redshift of approximately 3.35 [arXiv:1705.01543]. It exhibits an extended morphology with a bright compact region, a fainter secondary component, and Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:1705.01543].

sources.summary: The paper resolves the GRB 080810 host as an interacting high-redshift system with multiple absorption components, extended morphology, Ly-alpha emission, and inferred inflow/outflow structure.

quotes.quote: We reveal multiple components of an interacting galaxy system at z 3.35 through a detailed analysis of the exquisite high-resolution Keck/HIRES spectrum of the afterglow of a gamma-ray burst (GRB). Through Voigt-profile fitting of absorption lines from the Lyman-series, we constrain the neutral hydrogen column density to N_ H I 10^ 18.35 cm ^ 2 for the densest of four distinct systems at the host redshift of GRB 080810, among the lowest N_ H I ever observed in a GRB host, despite the line of sight passing within a projected 5 kpc of the galaxy centres. By detailed analysis of the corresponding metal absorption lines, we derive chemical, ionic and kinematic properties of the individual absorbing systems, and thus build a picture of the host as a whole.

A galaxy is detected at the GRB afterglow location in all of the images we took of the GRB field. The deep VLT image is shown in the left panel of Fig. fig:img ; the host exhibits an extended morphology with a bright compact region accompanied by a a fainter secondary component offset 0.8 to the northeast. A strong drop in the flux in the g and B bands suggests that this source is indeed the host galaxy system. Our spectroscopy confirms this, revealing a strong detection of redshifted Ly- centered at 5305 ( z=3.36 ).

gmw_00005984.name: IC 1262
gmw_00005984.overview: IC 1262 is a central dominant galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.0326 [arXiv:1811.05647] that exhibits very little star formation [arXiv:1811.05647]. It hosts the radio source 4C+43.46 [arXiv:1811.05647], featuring radio jets [arXiv:2312.02283] and multiple X-ray cavities [arXiv:1811.05647, arXiv:2312.02283] that offset the cooling of the surrounding medium [arXiv:1811.05647].

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 1262 as the central galaxy of a group hosting 4C+43.46, with cavities, radio lobes, and AGN power sufficient to offset cooling.

quotes.quote: This group is located at a relatively low redshift of z =0.0326, and its central galaxy hosts the radio source 4C+43.46, whose radio power is given by log P_ 1.4 GHz 22.56 W Hz^ 1 2007A&A...463..153T,2010ApJ...712..883D . A single X-ray cavity was detected by towards the north of the center of the group. It is interesting to note that the central galaxy IC 1262 shows very little star formation ( 4.35 10^ 2 yr^ 1 ) 2016ApJ...818..182V .

This comparison reveals that the radio source hosted by the central dominant galaxy IC 1262 is capable enough to deposit sufficient energy into the IGM to offset the cooling flow. The equality between the heating and cooling at the heat input rates of pV , 4pV and 16pV per cavity are shown by the diagonal lines in this figure. In the same figure we also plot the similar results derived by other authors 2006ApJ...652..216R,2011ApJ...735...11O,2013Ap&SS.345..183P,2016MNRAS.461.1885V,2017MNRAS.466.2054V . The position of the IC 1262 group in this plot, with respect to the others from the literature, is shown by a magenta ``+'', and is found to lie near the 4 pV enthalpy line. This in turn confirms that the total power available within the cavities is sufficient to offset the cooling flow within the cooling radius.

sources.summary: IC 1262 is discussed as a complex X-ray core with a southern arm, eastern discontinuity, northern loop/arm, detected H emission, and a steep central abundance drop.

quotes.quote: IC 1262 In the X-rays, the core of IC 1262 is host to a complex environment. Its main features are a bright arm stretching to the south of the cluster, a sharp discontinuity to the east, and a loop/arm structure to the north Trinchieri07 . detected optical H line emission from IC 1262. From the corresponding radial abundance profile (Fig. fig:abundprofspossible ), it is evident that there is a steep abundance drop in the central 10 kpc of the group, which correspond to the inner spectral bin.
With respect to 0.6 times the Solar abundance, the missing iron mass is calculated to be 9.4 10 ^ 5 M _ . We were unfortunately unable to obtain a value for the dust mass from the literature.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 1262 as a galaxy group with hot-gas substructure, two inner and one outer X-ray cavity, and an extended VLBA feature whose jet position angle agrees with the cavities.

quotes.quote: IC 1262 : galaxy group at z = 0.033. The hot gas in this object shows several substructures (ripples, edges and depressions). There are two inner X-ray cavities (oriented north --south) and an outer, larger X-ray cavity south of the center (see 2019ApJ...870...62P ). The VLBA data reveal an extended feature south of the core, that is recovered only by tapering the visibilities during imaging. The position angle of the jet is in relatively good agreement with that of the inner and outer X-ray cavities.

gmw_00005985.name: UGC 772
gmw_00005985.overview: UGC 772 is a metal-poor dwarf galaxy located in the NGC 428 group [arXiv:0809.1772, arXiv:1810.12944]. Its optical emission consists of two distinct condensations, and its disturbed neutral hydrogen kinematics suggest the system experienced a recent merger [arXiv:0809.1772, arXiv:1810.12944].

sources.summary: The GMRT observations present UGC 772 as a low-metallicity dwarf system in the NGC 428 group, with two optical condensations, disturbed H I, and possible merger signatures.

quotes.quote: UGC 772 The optical emission in UGC 772 (with M _B 16.28 ( )) consists of two well-separated condensations; in the UGC catalog itself, the system is flagged as being `possibly two dwarfs in contact'. It is part of a small group of galaxies of which a SAB(s)m galaxy, NGC 428 is the dominant member (at heliocentric velocity of 1152 \ and with M _ B -19.2). Smoker et al. (1996) present VLA \ observations of the NGC 428 field.

Our low-resolution image (Fig.  fig:ugc772 (Left.)) also shows the tentatively detected south-east \ extension in the VLA map of Smoker et al. (1996). In the channel maps (Fig.  fig:ugc772chanmap ), these extensions can be seen only over relatively narrow velocity ranges (viz., 1186.9--1180.3 and 1147.0--1137.1  ). At higher resolutions (e.g., at 15 10 arcsec ^ 2 , Fig.  fig:ugc772 (Centre.)), the \ emission can be seen to have a central `hole' surrounded by a broken high column density `ring'.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 772 as a metal-poor dwarf member of the NGC 428 group and cites ionized-gas and GMRT studies concluding it had a recent merger event.

quotes.quote: UGC 772 and MCG +00-04-049 are metal-poor dwarf galaxies with 12+ ( O/H ) = 7.15-7.32 (for several knots, Izotov2012 ) and 7.56 0.07 Eridanus , respectively. For UGC 772, Moiseev2010 studied the H line ionized-gas kinematics; Ekta2008 also presented the results of observations with GMRT. In both papers, the authors concluded that this galaxy had experienced a recent merger event.

gmw_00005986.name: UGC 7678
gmw_00005986.overview: UGC 7678 is a dwarf irregular galaxy classified as an Im-type dwarf [arXiv:1804.00469]. It is located in the vicinity of the interacting galaxy pair NGC 4485/4490 at a projected separation of 300 kpc [arXiv:1804.00469].

sources.summary: UGC 7678 is described as an Im-type dwarf near NGC 4485/4490, with outer stars selected for the CMD and TRGB distance measurement.

quotes.quote: UGC 7678. This is a Im-type dwarf situated in vicinity of interacting pair NGC 4485/4490 ( D_ TRGB = 8.91 Mpc, EDD) at a projected separation of 300 kpc. We use in the distance measurement and show in the CMD only stars in the outer part of the galaxy. We selected the stars situated outside of the ellipse with the parameters: X=2286, Y=3138, A=900, B=579, PA=15.

gmw_00005987.name: HLS-2-1
gmw_00005987.overview: HLS-2-1 is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:2111.00090] located at a confirmed redshift of z=5.241 [arXiv:2403.00911, arXiv:2111.00090]. It exhibits low carbon monoxide excitation [arXiv:2403.00911] and is situated 12 arcseconds away from its neighbor, HLS-2-2 [arXiv:2403.00911].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HLS-2-1 as one component of HLS-2, assigns a robust z=5.241 redshift, measures CO excitation, and links it to a possible z=5.2 overdensity.

quotes.quote: Compared to the log-likelihood of redshift with photometric constraints only (see Fig. hls_redshift ), the joint analysis helps us to highlight significant isolated peaks for the redshift of HLS-2-1, HLS-2-2, HLS-3 and HLS-22 at z=5.241, 5.128, 3.123 and 3.036, respectively. The redshift with maximum log-likelihood value is also not sensitive to the choice of IR templates. As shown in Fig  joint_redshift_HLS , the Bethermin+15 template and MMPZ find almost the same redshift where the joint log-likelihood value reaches the maximum, which further confirm their redshift solutions as listed above.

For HLS-2-1, the L' _ CO(8-7) /L' _ CO(5-4) is 0.31 0.11, which is consistent with the values found in typical high-z SMGs with low excitation Bothwell+13 but lower than the reported value of some starburst galaxies and luminous quasars at similar redshift Rawle+14,Li+20 . It is found that HLS 2-1 and HLS 2-2, separated by 12 arcsec on the map, have both a redshift of 5.2.

sources.summary: The paper identifies HLS-2-1 as a high-redshift DSFG, combining SED and spectral-line likelihoods and confirming its z=5.241 redshift with a new 147.7 GHz line.

quotes.quote: shows the log-likelihood of the redshift and spectral lines of HLS-2-1, HLS-3, and HLS-22 from the joint analysis and the SED only, with the maximum log-likelihood normalized to 1. HLS-2-1 has one marginally detected line at 92.3GHz, which matches with the discrete peaks in the normalized log-likelihood maximized at z=5.241. HLS-3 has no line detected in the band1 observation, and thus no significant peaks in log-likelihood. HLS-22 has one significant detection at 85.7 GHz, which also matches with the discrete peaks maximized at z=3.036 and z=4.380 in the log-likelihood.
For HLS-2-1, we confirm the redshift (z=5.241) in the previous analysis with a new line detection at 147.7 GHz.

gmw_00005988.name: HLS-2-2
gmw_00005988.overview: HLS-2-2 is a high-redshift infrared starburst galaxy [arXiv:2403.00911]. Spectroscopic analysis of the galaxy has detected CO(4-3) and [CI](2-1) emission lines, confirming a redshift of z=5.128 [arXiv:2403.00911].

sources.summary: The paper derives a robust z=5.128 solution for HLS-2-2, detects CO/[CI] lines, estimates gas properties, and discusses it within a possible high-redshift DSFG overdensity.

quotes.quote: Compared to the log-likelihood of redshift with photometric constraints only (see Fig. hls_redshift ), the joint analysis helps us to highlight significant isolated peaks for the redshift of HLS-2-1, HLS-2-2, HLS-3 and HLS-22 at z=5.241, 5.128, 3.123 and 3.036, respectively. The redshift with maximum log-likelihood value is also not sensitive to the choice of IR templates. As shown in Fig  joint_redshift_HLS , the Bethermin+15 template and MMPZ find almost the same redshift where the joint log-likelihood value reaches the maximum, which further confirm their redshift solutions as listed above.

We also note that HLS-2-2 could have a secondary redshift solution at z=3.385. However, this redshift could not match with any of the two most significant emission lines found in the spectrum, which correspond to CO(4-3) and [ CI ] (2-1) at z=5.128. Thus, we only adopt the z=5.128 solution in the following analysis.

gmw_00005989.name: HLS-3
gmw_00005989.overview: HLS-3 is a high-redshift dusty star-forming galaxy with a likely redshift of z=3.123 [arXiv:2111.00090, arXiv:2403.00911]. It exhibits a low dust temperature and a long gas depletion time, suggesting it is a massive main-sequence galaxy undergoing secular evolution [arXiv:2403.00911].

sources.summary: The paper treats HLS-3 as a high-redshift DSFG with an ambiguous redshift, double-peaked line profile, large continuum size, low dust temperature, and likely main-sequence character.

quotes.quote: For HLS-3, MMPZ also reveals a secondary redshift solution at z=2.299 with slightly lower log-likelihood in the analysis. This assigns the most significantly detected emission line at 139.746 GHz to be CO(4-3), while the best solution at z=3.123 assigns it to be CO(5-4). If HLS-3 has z=3.123, we also expect to cover the CO(3-2) line in the spectral scan.

The only exception, HLS-3, shows possibly long gas depletion time up to a few Gyrs and being comparable to main sequence galaxies at the same redshift. Remarkably, HLS-3 also has the largest millimeter continuum size (2.8" 0.7", see Table  cont_noema_pos_shape ) and the lowest dust temperature ( 23^ +7 _ 6 K at z=3.123 or 18^ +6 _ 5 K at z=2.299, see Table  MBB_dust_properties ). These atypical properties among SMGs, in addition to its long gas depletion time, suggest that HLS-3 is more likely a massive main sequence galaxy under secular evolution.

sources.summary: The paper treats HLS-3 as a high-redshift DSFG candidate with no band1 line but a new analysis peak at z=3.123 matching a 139.8 GHz detected line.

quotes.quote: shows the log-likelihood of the redshift and spectral lines of HLS-2-1, HLS-3, and HLS-22 from the joint analysis and the SED only, with the maximum log-likelihood normalized to 1. HLS-2-1 has one marginally detected line at 92.3GHz, which matches with the discrete peaks in the normalized log-likelihood maximized at z=5.241. HLS-3 has no line detected in the band1 observation, and thus no significant peaks in log-likelihood. HLS-22 has one significant detection at 85.7 GHz, which also matches with the discrete peaks maximized at z=3.036 and z=4.380 in the log-likelihood.

With a new line detected at 114 GHz, the redshift of HLS-22 is also confirmed to be at z=3.036, matching with one of the prominent peaks in the previous joint log-likelihood. As for HLS-3, the new analysis reveals one prominent peak in log-likelihood at z=3.123 matching to the line detected at 139.8 GHz. This is not contradicting the previous results showing relatively high log-likelihood at this redshift under non-detection.

gmw_00005990.name: HLS-4
gmw_00005990.overview: HLS-4 is a high-redshift dusty star-forming galaxy candidate [arXiv:2403.00911]. Although it lacks a reliable spectroscopic redshift due to the absence of emission line detections, it is inferred to host a massive molecular gas reservoir [arXiv:2403.00911].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HLS-4 as a high-redshift DSFG candidate with accurate photometric redshift but no reliable spectroscopic redshift, while inferring a massive gas reservoir.

quotes.quote: For HLS-4, despite having the most accurate photometric redshift constrained through template fitting, the absence of emission line detection in the band1 spectral-scan observations results in our analysis being unable to identify significant peaks in the joint log-likelihood. Thus no reliable redshift solution is found for this source. For HLS-4, we derive a similarly massive molecular gas reservoir as that of the 4 sources with CO detections.

gmw_00005991.name: HLS-22
gmw_00005991.overview: HLS-22 is a high-redshift dusty star-forming galaxy [arXiv:2111.00090]. Observations of its emission lines have confirmed a redshift of z=3.036 [arXiv:2403.00911, arXiv:2111.00090].

sources.summary: The paper finds a z=3.036 redshift for HLS-22, rejects one narrow candidate as a glitch, and notes redshift robustness depends on line-width assumptions.

quotes.quote: For HLS-3 and HLS-22 with blindly detected candidate emission lines at S/N > 3, our method successfully confirms the candidate lines but for that at 100 GHz in the HLS-22 spectrum. The extremely narrow profile of the candidate detection suggests that this is likely to be a glitch, which is shown and discussed in Appendix  sc:test_linewidth . The only case of significant inconsistency in redshift from the test is HLS-22, where the procedure using 300km/s line width strongly favors a redshift solution at z=2.436 . Given the frequency of the 2 emission lines detected with high SNR, we are confident about the redshift solution at z=3.036 from the analysis with the model spectra of 500km/s line width.

sources.summary: The paper discusses HLS-22 as a high-redshift DSFG whose follow-up lines constrain competing redshift likelihood peaks and confirm z=3.036 with a new 114 GHz line.

quotes.quote: shows the log-likelihood of the redshift and spectral lines of HLS-2-1, HLS-3, and HLS-22 from the joint analysis and the SED only, with the maximum log-likelihood normalized to 1. HLS-2-1 has one marginally detected line at 92.3GHz, which matches with the discrete peaks in the normalized log-likelihood maximized at z=5.241. HLS-3 has no line detected in the band1 observation, and thus no significant peaks in log-likelihood. HLS-22 has one significant detection at 85.7 GHz, which also matches with the discrete peaks maximized at z=3.036 and z=4.380 in the log-likelihood.

For HLS-2-1, we confirm the redshift (z=5.241) in the previous analysis with a new line detection at 147.7 GHz. With a new line detected at 114 GHz, the redshift of HLS-22 is also confirmed to be at z=3.036, matching with one of the prominent peaks in the previous joint log-likelihood.

gmw_00005992.name: PG1407+265
gmw_00005992.overview: PG1407+265 is a luminous weak-line quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.96 [arXiv:2106.05389]. It exhibits long-term X-ray variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0510703, arXiv:2106.05389], weak optical variability [arXiv:2106.05389], and features a weak, relativistic radio jet alongside an inflow or outflow with a mechanical luminosity of order 10^48 erg s^-1 [arXiv:2106.05389].

sources.summary: The paper presents two X-ray observations of the luminous radio-quiet quasar 14, finding distinct flux states and interpreting the variable high state as intermittent jet plus accretion-disc emission.

quotes.quote: We present two \ observations of the luminous ( L_x > 10^ 46 ), radio-quiet quasar, 14, separated by eleven months. The data indicate two distinct states: a highly variable, bright state (first epoch); and a quiescent, low-flux one (second epoch). During the low-flux state the spectrum is consistent with a single, unabsorbed power law.

However, during the brighter state a highly variable, steep component is statistically required. Contemporaneous UV data from the Optical Monitor allow an estimate of the optical-to-X-ray spectral index ( _ ox ), which appears typical of radio-quiet quasars during the low-flux state, but extremely flat during the high-flux state. The \ data can be described as originating from a combination of jet and accretion disc processes, in which the (relativistic) X-ray jet only works intermittently.

sources.summary: The paper presents PG1407+265 as an unusual z=0.96 weak-line quasar with long-term X-ray variability, weak optical variability, powerful inflow or outflow, and a weak relativistic radio jet.

quotes.quote: Gallo Department of Astronomy Physics, Saint Mary's University, 923 Robie Street, Halifax, Nova Scotia, B3H 3C3, Canada We present new observations of the odd z=0.96 weak-line quasar PG1407+265, and report the discovery of CXOU J140927.9+261813, a z=0.68 X-ray cluster. Archival X-ray photometry spanning nearly four decades reveals that PG1407+265 is variable at the 1 dex level on a timescale of years. V-band variability is present with an amplitude less than 0.1 mag. The emission-line properties of PG1407+265 also reveal clear evidence for a powerful inflow or outflow due to near- or super-Eddington accretion, having a mechanical luminosity of order 10^ 48 erg s ^ 1 .

The prototypical X-ray-loud weak-line quasar (WLQ), PG 1407+265, is a luminous z 1 AGN with a probable strong outflow (see Section section:qso ) but only a weak, albeit relativistic, radio jet on parsec scales BBB03 .

gmw_00005993.name: DEIMOS COSMOS 683613
gmw_00005993.overview: DEIMOS COSMOS 683613 is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:2006.09402, arXiv:2402.07982] that shows evidence of a merger with multiple components in both UV/optical and far-infrared emission [arXiv:2402.07982]. The galaxy exhibits a spatial offset of approximately 4 kiloparsecs, indicating that the bulk of its dust is separated from its stars and gas [arXiv:2402.07982].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes HZ3 in paired ALMA datasets, finding lower fluxes in higher-resolution images because surface brightness dimming misses diffuse extended line emission.

quotes.quote: Among the seven galaxies in common with the two ALMA programs, we have analysed only three sources, HZ1, HZ3, and HZ4, since the other targets show a multi-component morphology Carniani:2018 that would lead to a more complex and ambiguous interpretation with respect to current simulations. The two datasets, data calibration, and analysis are presented in App. sec:app_hz . Fig. fig:sim_vs_obs shows the \ fluxes of HZ1, HZ3, and HZ4 inferred from the two individual datasets as a function of the _ beam normalised by the extent of \ emission (D _ source ) estimated from the observations (see App. sec:app_hz for more details).

We notice that the \ measurements obtained from L17, which has lower angular resolution, are systematically higher than those estimated in the higher angular-resolution images of C12. This discrepancy is due to the two different ALMA beams. Indeed in the C12 program the angular resolution of the observations is comparable to the \ size, so increasing the effect of SBD on the flux measurement. Although the significance of the \ detection in C12 is >5 , the sensitivity is not sufficiently high to recover the diffuse and extended emission, whereas in L17 dataset the \ line is barely resolved resulting in a higher flux estimate.

sources.summary: The paper identifies DEIMOS COSMOS 683613 as a high-redshift galaxy with significant FIR-continuum offsets, likely offset dust, and higher-resolution evidence for a merger with multiple components.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:overlaid_contours shows an example of one galaxy (VUDS COSMOS 5101218326) with no significant offset ( s < 3 ) and one (DEIMOS COSMOS 683613) with a significant offset ( s > 3 ). We see that one has all centroids close together (offsets 1.2 kpc), while the other has FIR continuum centroid separated from the other centroids (offset 4 kpc). As the FIR continuum traces dust, it appears that this second galaxy (DEIMOS COSMOS 683613) has the bulk of its dust offset from stars and gas (both atomic and ionised gas as traced by ).

Indeed, Posses et al. (in prep.) have found evidence of such a merger in DEIMOS COSMOS 683613 (Fig. fig:sig_offset ). Higher resolution observations of this galaxy from JWST and the ALMA-CRISTAL project (Herrera-Camus et al. (in prep.)) have revealed multiple components in both UV/optical and FIR emission.

gmw_00005994.name: SWIRE J022513.90-043419.9
gmw_00005994.overview: SWIRE J022513.90-043419.9 is an obscured quasar host galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 3.5 [arXiv:1104.0937]. The system hosts a starburst with a luminosity of 10^13 solar luminosities and a luminous, absorbed X-ray source [arXiv:1104.0937].

sources.summary: The paper presents SW022513 as a z~3.5 obscured quasar host with a powerful starburst, luminous AGN, strong radio emission, broad CO, complex ionized-gas profiles, and likely outflowing molecular gas.

quotes.quote: SWIRE J022513.90-043419.9 and SWIRE J022550.67-042142.2 (SW022513 and SW022550 hereafter) are two obscured quasars at z 3.5 polletta08 and are the two most luminous 24 m emitters at high redshift in the SWIRE survey lonsdale03 . We also include the results of a recent CO(4--3) analysis into our discussion, which has been presented by polletta10 . polletta08 presented a detailed analysis of the multi-wavelength photometric properties of these galaxies. Both galaxies host powerful starbursts ( L 10^ 13 L _ ) and luminous, obscured quasars with bolometric luminosities > 10^ 13 L _ .

SW022513 is well detected in the X-ray pierre07 with a luminosity, L (2-10 keV) = 8.2 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 in the hard X-ray that is 7 times brigher than the soft X-ray luminosity at 0.5-2 keV. It is thus a luminous absorbed X-ray source, e.g. a factor 3 brighter than the archetypal type-2 quasar CDFS-202 at z = 3.700 norman02 .

gmw_00005995.name: SWIRE J022550.67-042142.2
gmw_00005995.overview: SWIRE J022550.67-042142.2 is an obscured quasar host galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 3.5 [arXiv:1104.0937]. The system hosts a starburst, exhibits bright high-ionization UV emission lines, and displays a broad, double-horned CO line profile [arXiv:1104.0937].

sources.summary: The paper describes SW022550 as a z~3.5 obscured quasar host with a powerful starburst, luminous line emission, bright high-ionization UV lines, and broad double-horned CO.

quotes.quote: SWIRE J022513.90-043419.9 and SWIRE J022550.67-042142.2 (SW022513 and SW022550 hereafter) are two obscured quasars at z 3.5 polletta08 and are the two most luminous 24 m emitters at high redshift in the SWIRE survey lonsdale03 . We also include the results of a recent CO(4--3) analysis into our discussion, which has been presented by polletta10 . polletta08 presented a detailed analysis of the multi-wavelength photometric properties of these galaxies. Both galaxies host powerful starbursts ( L 10^ 13 L _ ) and luminous, obscured quasars with bolometric luminosities > 10^ 13 L _ .

The rest-UV spectrum of SW022550 shows bright, high-ionization emission lines, in particular NV 1240, and weak UV continuum emission, which are the typical signatures of luminous, obscured quasars polletta08 . SW022513 is well detected in the X-ray pierre07 with a luminosity, L (2-10 keV) = 8.2 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 in the hard X-ray that is 7 times brigher than the soft X-ray luminosity at 0.5-2 keV. It is thus a luminous absorbed X-ray source, e.g. a factor 3 brighter than the archetypal type-2 quasar CDFS-202 at z = 3.700 norman02 . SW022513 is most probably Compton-thick since its hard X-ray band luminosity is about only 1/50 of that measured around 24 m. polletta08 also present low-resolution ISAAC longslit spectroscopy in the near-infrared for both targets, and rest-frame UV spectroscopy for SW022550, showing that both sources are luminous line emitters. Recent IRAM Plateau de Bure millimeter interferometry of the CO(4--3) line shows that both galaxies are luminous CO line emitters with L _ CO 5 10^ 10 L _ K km s ^ 1 pc ^2 each, corresponding to 4 10^ 10 M _ of cold molecular gas polletta10 . SW022550 has a broad, double-horned CO line profile, whereas the CO(4--3) line in SW022513 is also broad, FWHM = 1000 km s ^ 1 , and featureless, and is not very well fit with a Gaussian profile.

gmw_00005996.name: VCC 0958/NGC 4419
gmw_00005996.overview: VCC 0958/NGC 4419 is a highly inclined spiral galaxy [arXiv:1802.05526] that hosts a likely low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1304.1279, arXiv:1510.02631] and is undergoing ram pressure stripping [arXiv:2205.01695]. The galaxy features a strongly truncated neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:1204.0430, arXiv:1304.1279] and a high nuclear star formation rate [arXiv:1204.0430, arXiv:1304.1279].

sources.summary: The paper revises VCC 0958/NGC 4419 toward the Virgo mean distance, noting poor Ton01 flags, dust-related SBF difficulty, and support from other distance estimates.

quotes.quote: For VCC 0958/NGC 4419, Ton01 reported (m M)=30.65 0.25 mag, which places it in front of Virgo with a significance of 1.8 . The Ton01 quality flags are quite poor, as also indicated by the relatively large quoted error. We find (m M)=31.29 0.18 mag, based on the \ calibration. Using the two-color / \ calibration, we derive (m M)=31.13 0.27 , which is consistent with the calibrated distance, and closer to the Virgo mean. The Ton01 seeing for this galaxy was a relatively good 0 85, but it is a highly inclined spiral with significant dust, and therefore more difficult for the SBF method.

Other recent distance estimates to the galaxy include (m M)=31.06 0.25 from the Tully-Fisher luminosity-linewidth relation by revised to a mean Virgo distance modulus of m M=31.09 gavazzi99 , and (m M)=31.24 from \ reindl05 . This \ distance modulus is based on the the peculiar supernova SN1984A and H_0=60 km/s/Mpc; assuming H_0=70 km/s/Mpc the distance modulus becomes (m M)=30.91 mag. The ``expected random error'' reported by reindl05 is _ m M =0.14 mag, although the distance moduli for single objects could be less accurate. In summary, while the \ distances are not conclusive, based on our and Tully-Fisher distances VCC 0958 appears close to the mean distance of Virgo.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4419 as having a strongly truncated H I disk, asymmetric molecular-dominated gas, a bright nucleus, and star formation proportional to molecular gas with scatter.

quotes.quote: NGC 4419 (Fig. fig:plot_n4419 ): as in NGC 4402 the H i disk is strongly truncated (Chung et al. 2009). Within the disk there is more gas to the northwest of the galaxy center than in the southeast as already pointed out by Kenney et al. (1990). This gas is dominated by the molecular phase. Because of the high infrared surface brightness of the nucleus which lead to PSF artifacts, we had to clip the image of the star formation rate at _ * < 5 10^ 3 M _ kpc ^ 2 yr ^ 1 . The galaxy nucleus has a very high gas surface density and star formation rate.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4419 as H I deficient, gas- and radio-truncated, hosting a likely AGN, and showing a vertically extended radio halo with an X-shaped magnetic field.

quotes.quote: NGC 4419 This highly inclined galaxy is strongly H i deficient and the H i distribution is severely truncated within the stellar disk. It has strong radio continuum emission from a nuclear source, likely an AGN (Decarli et al. 2007). The total gas surface density distribution (CO + H i ) is strongly peaked at the galaxy center (Vollmer et al. 2012b). Due to the high gas surface density, the star formation rate per unit area is also high. As the gas distribution, the radio continuum distribution at 20 and 6 cm is strongly truncated.

sources.summary: The paper considers NGC 4419 a metallicity-matched, ram-pressure-stripped neighbor of BC4 and BC5, but rejects it as their common origin.

quotes.quote: NGC 4419 is in fairly close proximity to both BCs and based on its estimated stellar mass and metallicity, it is likely a close match for the metallicity of these BCs. In addition, there is strong evidence in both \ Chung+2009 and CO Brown+2021 that this galaxy is being ram pressure stripped. However, as ram pressure tails only extend in one general direction (in the wake of a galaxy's motion through the ICM) and BC4 is to the south of NGC 4419 and BC5 is to the north, it is extremely unlikely that this is the point of origin of these BCs. The extension of the molecular gas distribution of NGC 4419 is roughly towards the south Brown+2021 , in the direction of BC4, but away from BC5. If NGC 4419 were simultaneously undergoing ram pressure and tidal stripping then it could plausibly have formed both BCs, but there is no evidence of this in the optical, , or CO images.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4419 as an LLAGN with bright nuclear PAH emission whose 12 micron luminosity may be dominated by star-formation-related emission.

quotes.quote: The only exception was NGC 4419 for which we evaluated them at 10 m to avoid the bad ozone residual in the spectrum at 9.7 m rest-frame (see Fig. fig:Spectra ).

We note that two LLAGN in our sample (NGC 4419 and NGC 4569) show bright PAH emission in their nuclear CanariCam spectra (see Fig. fig:Spectra and also Section sec:PAH ). Thus their 12 m luminosities might be dominated by emission related to star formation activity as found in a few LLAGN see e.g. Mason2012,Asmus2014,GonzalezMartin2015 .
Two of the LLAGN in our sample (NGC 4569 and NGC 4419) show bright nuclear PAH emission, whereas the other two (NGC 4579 and NGC 4258) do not and present the silicate feature in emission.

gmw_00005997.name: BCG1
gmw_00005997.overview: BCG1 is a non-dominant brightest cluster galaxy located in the southwest region of the Abell 1914 cluster [arXiv:1301.5200]. It is associated with a southwestern group of 69 galaxies identified through three-dimensional substructure analysis [arXiv:1301.5200].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BCG1 as the southwest brightest cluster galaxy in Abell 1914, uses it as the cluster center, and compares its local velocity and substructure assignment with BCG2.

quotes.quote: The brightest cluster galaxy is ID. 29 ( ^ Mega =15.87 , hereafter BCG1). It lies at the south-west and is non-dominant (in luminosity) in the cluster. In fact, there is a second brightest cluster galaxy at the north-east (ID. 20, ^ Mega =16.39 , hereafter BCG2).

Furthermore, when considering members within 0.1 from the BCGs (that is two groups of 7 and 8 galaxy members around BCG1 and BCG2, respectively), we measure similar mean velocities. We only find a modest difference, being v _ BCG2 < v _ BCG1 , which is in agreement with the finding that v_ BCG2 <v_ BCG1 (see also section specphoto ).
When applying the 3D-KMM method we still found a bimodal solution with two groups of 69 (SW) and 20 galaxies (NE), with BCG1 and BCG2 assigned to the SW and NE groups, respectively.

gmw_00005998.name: BCG2
gmw_00005998.overview: BCG2 is the second brightest cluster galaxy located in the northeast region of the Abell 1914 cluster [arXiv:1301.5200]. It is separated from the brightest cluster galaxy, BCG1, by 1.7 arcminutes and belongs to a distinct northeast galaxy group [arXiv:1301.5200].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BCG2 as the northeast second brightest cluster galaxy, notes its separation and velocity relative to BCG1, and links it to Abell 1914 substructure analyses.

quotes.quote: In fact, there is a second brightest cluster galaxy at the north-east (ID. 20, ^ Mega =16.39 , hereafter BCG2). The two galaxies are separated by 1.7 , i.e. 0.3 at the cluster distance.

This method found only one significant peak, lying close to BCG2 and elongated toward BCG1. Figure figk2z shows that A1914 presents an elongated profile in the NE-SW direction, suggesting a bimodal structure. To further investigate this point we applied the 2D-KMM method using as seeds the cluster members contained within 0.1 from BCG1 and BCG2 (7 and 8 galaxies, respectively).
When applying the 3D-KMM method we still found a bimodal solution with two groups of 69 (SW) and 20 galaxies (NE), with BCG1 and BCG2 assigned to the SW and NE groups, respectively.

gmw_00005999.overview: 32016857 is a star-forming galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.9392 that exhibits a galactic outflow [arXiv:1304.6405]. It has a low stellar mass, a notably blue color, and an asymmetric morphology that may indicate two merging galaxies [arXiv:1304.6405].

sources.summary: The paper treats 32016857 as the clearest z=0.9392 outflow galaxy with extended scattered emission, blue color, high star formation, low stellar mass, and possible asymmetric or merging morphology.

quotes.quote: Using Keck/LRIS spectroscopy, we have resolved scattered \ emission to the east of 32016857, a star-forming galaxy at z =0.9392 with an outflow. The \ emission from this galaxy exhibits a P-Cygni profile, extends further than both the continuum and [ O 2 ] emission along the eastern side of the slit, and has a constant Doppler shift along the slit which does not follow the velocity gradient of the nebular [ O 2 ] emission.

The R band isophotes of 32016857 indicate a major axis roughly aligned with the position angle of the slit. However, the isophotes clearly show an asymmetry along the major axis which is not consistent with the projection of an inclined, symmetric disk. Higher resolution imaging is required to distinguish whether the major axis reflects the position angle of a disk or some more complicated morphology, such as two merging galaxies.

It has notably blue U-B color. The SFR estimated from the [ O 2 ] luminosity, as described in the notes to Table 1, is approximately 80 . The mass derived from the SED fit, (M / ) = 9.82 Bundy:2006p1663 , places 32016857 in the lowest tertile of the LRIS sample by stellar mass.

gmw_00006000.overview: The galaxy 32010773 is a blue outflow galaxy with a relatively low luminosity [arXiv:1304.6405]. It exhibits spatially extended emission lines, although this emission is not more extended than its blue continuum [arXiv:1304.6405].

sources.summary: The paper describes 32010773 as a blue outflow galaxy with spatially extended line emission that is not more extended than its continuum, making a scattering halo interpretation unnecessary.

quotes.quote: In addition to 32016857, the \ emission in our spectrum of 32010773 is spatially extended. Figure fig:32010773 shows the surface brightness profiles of the \ emission (panel d), blue continuum, [ O 2 ] emission (panel ), and red continuum along the slit crossing 32010773. In contrast to the surface brightness profiles measured across 32016857, the \ emission across 32010773 is no more extended than the blue continuum emission.

Our data do not require (nor do they rule out) scattered \ emission. This galaxy does clearly have an outflow; the large blueshift of the \ resonance lines, V_1 = -198 46 , is quite significant. The color of 32010773, U - B = 0.23 is among the bluest in the sample; but the luminosity of 32010773, M_B - 5 h_ 70 = -20.68 , is much lower than the luminosity of either 32016857 or TKRS 4389.

gmw_00006001.overview: The high-redshift galaxy 12019973 exhibits spatially extended emission and continuum emission [arXiv:1304.6405]. Its line emission surface brightness profile is no more extended than its continuum, and the galaxy shows only marginal evidence for an outflow [arXiv:1304.6405].

sources.summary: The paper reports spatially extended emission and continuum across 12019973, but finds its line profile no more extended than the continuum and only marginal outflow evidence.

quotes.quote: Among these redder \ emitters, we found spatially extended \ and continuum emission across 12019973. Figure fig:12019973 shows the variation in the \ and [ O 2 ] emission along the slit. Across 12019973, much like 32010773, the surface brightness profile of the line emission is no more extended than the continuum emission, neither proving nor ruling out scattering by an outflow.

We note that the Doppler shift of the \ resonance lines, V_1 = -59 28 , provides marginal evidence for an outflow from this galaxy. The color, U - B = 0.539 , and stellar mass, (M/ ) = 10.15 , of 12019973 are typical of the sample. This galaxy has a relatively low luminosity, M_B - 5 h_ 70 = -20.57 , which may contribute to the faintness of any extended halo.

gmw_00006002.overview: The galaxy 22028686 is a star-forming emitter [arXiv:1304.6405]. Its velocity structure, indicated by Doppler shifts, suggests either the projected rotation of a galactic disk or the orbital motion of two merging galaxies [arXiv:1304.6405].

sources.summary: The paper describes 22028686 as a star-forming emitter whose absorption extends beyond the emission region, with velocity structure suggesting complex disk or merger geometry and asymmetric resonance escape.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:22028686 shows the 2D spectra of another \ emitter, 22028686, a star-forming galaxy with U-B > 0.459 . Although the \ emission is not spatially resolved, the resonance absorption troughs extend well beyond the emission region. The \ absorption lines are tilted in the opposite sense of the velocity gradient indicated by the [ O 2 ] Doppler shifts along the slit.

Since the [ O 2 ] emission defines the systemic redshift, the redshifted and blueshifted [ O 2 ] emission to the northeast and southwest of the galactic center reveal either the projected rotation of the galaxy or the orbital motion of two merging galaxies. The centroid of the \ absorption to the southwest of the galaxy is consistent with the velocity of the blueshifted side of the galaxy. It is the even larger blueshift of the \ absorption centroid to the northeast that makes the absorption lines tilt in the opposite direction of the [ O 2 ] emission.

gmw_00006003.name: PG1048 090
