gmw_00006003.overview: PG1048 090 is a radio-loud quasar located within a galaxy cluster [arXiv:2212.07061, arXiv:2308.16780]. On kiloparsec scales, it displays a Fanaroff-Riley type II radio structure with a core and two hotspots [arXiv:2212.07061, arXiv:2308.16780], while its parsec-scale core-jet emission exhibits high brightness temperatures and variability [arXiv:2308.16780].

sources.summary: The paper describes PG1048 090 as a cluster-member steep-spectrum quasar with a core and two hotspots, low but extensive polarization, and hotspot spectral and magnetic-field structure.

quotes.quote: PG1048 090 This SSRQ ( aka 3C246) is a member of a galaxy cluster Yates1989 . It is relatively poorly imaged at radio frequencies compared to the other sources. Previous VLA images show the presence of a core and two hotspots; however the jet and lobe emission is very faint Miller1993, Dennett2000, Kellermann1994 .

We find a similar structure in our images (Figure fig4 ). The Y-shaped ears around the north-western hotspot are due to deconvolution errors that remain in spite of self-calibration and must be regarded as image artefacts. We find that the core and two hotspots show extensive polarization; however their fractional polarization is low.

sources.summary: PG 1048-090 is one of the radio-loud quasars with FR II kiloparsec structure and resolved parsec-scale core-jet emission with high brightness temperature and variability.

quotes.quote: We have also studied the radio-emitting properties of the four RLQs. On kiloparsec scales, all four RLQs display Fanaroff-Riley type II radio structures 1993MNRAS.263..425M,1994AJ....108.1163K . On parsec scales, three RLQs (PG 1004+130, PG1048-090, PG 1425+267) exhibit resolved structures, featuring a bright and compact component located at one end of an elongated structure (Figure fig:RLQ ). This brightest component exhibits a high brightness temperature ( >10^9 K), a flat spectrum ( 0.40< ^ 7.6 _ 1.6 <-0.25 ) (Table table:Spectral_Index ), and shows significant variability (Figure fig:SED-RLQ ).

gmw_00006004.name: PG1100+772
gmw_00006004.overview: PG1100+772 is a radio-loud quasar [arXiv:1203.2941, arXiv:2212.07061] that hosts two associated absorption-line systems [arXiv:1307.6799] and exhibits a hybrid radio morphology [arXiv:2212.07061]. Its far-infrared emission is dominated by cold dust in the host galaxy [arXiv:1203.2941], with only a small potential contribution from starburst activity [arXiv:1508.04343].

sources.summary: The paper fits the radio-loud quasar 3C 249.1 with a clumpy AGN dust-torus model, giving its luminosity, redshift, torus geometry, cloud number, and far-infrared cirrus component.

quotes.quote: Quasar 3C 249.1 The clumpy AGN dust torus model is applied to fit the SED of the quasar 3C249.1. The luminosity of the object is 7 10^ 45 erg/s at redshift of z=0.311 which translates, using standard cosmological parameters, to a luminosity distance of 1600 Mpc. The torus inner edge is set by the sublimation temperature and leads to an inner radius of r_ in =1 pc. In the inner region of the model at 25 r_ in the half opening angle of the torus is _1 = 25^ and in the outer region up to r_ out = 50 r_ in it is 55^ . The torus includes 5500 clouds.
However, the far IR emission of the quasar cannot be explained by the AGN torus. The far IR is dominated by emission of cold dust located furtherout in the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats PG1100+772 as a candidate restarted hybrid radio quasar with asymmetric lobes, complex polarization, bent jets, and several proposed explanations for its morphology.

quotes.quote: PG1100+772 This SSRQ ( aka 3C249.1) is a candidate restarted source Figure fig5 Marecki2012,Fernini2007 . The western lobe is FRII-like while the eastern one does not have a terminal hotspot and is FRI-like. This makes the source a hybrid FRI/FRII radio source.

The inferred B-field is transverse to the jet direction for the optically thick radio core. The polarization features in the inner ``hotspot'' are consistent with a terminal shock in that the B-vectors are transverse to the jet direction here (Figure fig5.1 ) . Further down the eastern lobe, the B-field vectors resemble the bubble-like lobe structures observed in several Seyfert galaxies Kharb2006,Sebastian2020 and is relatively flat in spectral index.

sources.summary: 3C 249.1 harbors two associated absorption-line systems; the first shows Lyman and doublet detections without partial coverage or equivalent-width variation.

quotes.quote: 3C 249.1 ( =0.3080, 0.3136) -- [ AKA PG 1100+772 ] The quasar 3C 249.1 harbors two associated absorption-line systems at =0.3080 \ and =0.3136 . The =0.3080 \ system, previously reported by kpxiii , gan01a , and tripp08 is detected in the Lyman series, and the 1031.926, 1037.617 and 1238.821, 1242.804 doublets. In Figure fig:3c249a , we present velocity-aligned flux profiles of these detected transitions and the velocity-dependent coverage fraction analysis. Neither doublet shows evidence for partial coverage. In addition, comparison to gan01a shows no variation in the equivalent width of the 1031.926 transition.

sources.summary: The paper finds that 3C249.1 can be fit by pure AGN models, with only a small possible starburst contribution and notable near-IR synchrotron emission.

quotes.quote: For 3C249.1 we find that pure AGN models provide a reasonable fit to the SED and its silicate emission feature without the need for SB activity. There are of course AGN+SB models, similar to 3C351, that fit the data. Again, we assume synchrotron emission that makes a significant contribution to the near IR. We find that in this quasar the contribution to the total luminosity from a starburst is 3 (Table para.tab ).

sources.summary: The paper uses XMM-Newton data for 3C 249.1, fitting a second moderately absorbed power law plus a narrow Gaussian line similar to prior results.

quotes.quote: 3C 249.1 Both Chandra and XMM-Newton data exist for this quasar: we use only the XMM data, since there are more counts and no pileup issues to contend with. The best-fitting model includes a second power law with moderate intrinsic absorption, and a narrow Gaussian with rest-frame energy 6.42 _ 0.06 ^ +0.03 keV. The parameters of the double-power-law model are very similar to those fitted by Piconcelli \ (2005) to the same X-ray data.

gmw_00006005.name: PG1704+608
gmw_00006005.overview: PG1704+608 is a radio-loud, lobe-dominated quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/9809114, arXiv:2212.07061] featuring a compact, optically thin radio core [arXiv:2212.07061, arXiv:2308.16780] and a distorted, one-sided radio lobe [arXiv:2212.07061]. Its spectral energy distribution is modeled as a pure active galactic nucleus torus without starburst activity [arXiv:1508.04343].

sources.summary: The paper models ROSAT observations of 3C 351, showing its ionized absorber responds to flux changes consistently with a single-zone photoionization equilibrium model.

quotes.quote: We present two ROSAT PSPC observations of the radio-loud, lobe-dominated quasar 3C 351, which shows an `ionized absorber' in its X-ray spectrum. The factor 1.7 change in flux in the 2 years between the observations allows a test of models for this ionized absorber. The absorption feature at 0.7 keV (quasar frame) is present in both spectra but with a lower optical depth when the source intensity - and hence the ionizing flux at the absorber - is higher, in accordance with a simple, single-zone, equilibrium photoionization model.

The most luminous known AGN with an ionized absorber is the radio-loud lobe-dominated only 0.65 of the flux density is contained in the compact core at 6 cm, (Kellerman et al., 1989). quasar 3C351 (L _ 0.1-2 keV = 2.3 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 , z=0.371, Fiore et al., 1993). Ionized absorbers are common in low redshift, low luminosity Seyfert 1 galaxies (Reynolds, 1997), but rare in higher redshift, higher luminosity quasars. High luminosity AGNs are very likely physically larger and so may exhibit slower time variability and have different physical conditions. 3C 351 has a very high UV to X-ray ratio ( _ OX =1.55 , Tanambaun et al., 1989) and its IR to UV spectrum does not show any evidence of reddening, in contrast to several low redshift Seyfert 1 galaxies thought to host dusty/warm absorbers i.e. IRAS 13349+2438, Brandt et al., 1996, 1997; IRAS 17020+4544, Leighly et al., 1997, Komossa Bade, 1998; MCG-6-30-15, Reynolds et al., 1997. .
Here we show that in 3C 351 at least the simplest photoionization equilibrium, single zone model continues to be sufficient.

sources.summary: The paper presents PG1704+608 as a steep-spectrum quasar with multiple hotspots, a distorted one-sided lobe, complex polarized morphology, optically thin core emission, and a changed jet direction.

quotes.quote: PG1704+608 This SSRQ ( aka 3C351) shows the presence of multiple hotspots and a one-sided distored radio lobe in VLA and GMRT images Bridle1994, Vaddi2019 . A 15 GHz image from the Cambridge 5-km telescope shows that in the northern hotspot, the southern component is compact while the northern component shows extended emission Laing1981 . Our 5 GHz image shows the complex radio morphology in total intensity and polarization (Figure fig9 ).

The inferred B-field is parallel to the jet direction for the optically thin radio core. The southern hotspot shows the characteristic B-field morphology of a hotspot (transverse to the jet direction). The jet direction appears to have suddenly changed in this source as is also visible in the misaligned northern radio lobe.

sources.summary: 3C 351 has associated absorption systems studied in detail, with complex Lyman and doublet absorption split into representative components for comparison.

quotes.quote: 3C 351 (and possibly PKS 0312-77 ). RX J 1230.8+0115 \ and 3C 351 have been examined extensively by gan03b and yuan02 , respectively. The absorption in these two quasars consists of complexes of closely spaced lines, separated by unabsorbed segments of continuum gan01b . In all quasars, the absorption is highly saturated as evidenced by the relative depths of the doublet lines (i.e., similar normalized flux levels for both members of the doublet). The absorption profiles are not black, however, because the coverage fraction is less than unity.

sources.summary: The paper fits 3C351 with a pure AGN torus plus beamed synchrotron component, reproducing its SED, silicate emission, and near-IR bump without significant starburst emission.

quotes.quote: The near IR photometry of 3C351 is fit assuming a contribution of a beamed synchrotron component that we approximate as a power-law ( F_ ^ ) with a slope 0.2 . We show that pure AGN models with parameters are summarized in Table para.tab account for the complete SED without the need of postulating additional starburst activity. The best fit of the AGN+SB models shows that the SED can be explained mainly by a torus and a small (8 , Table paragnsb.tab ) contribution from a starburst. The predicted viewing direction is close to face on ( 33^ o ). The models reproduce the silicate emission feature of this quasar and the near IR bump without the need for an additional hot dust component as suggested by .

sources.summary: PG 1704+608 is one of the radio-loud quasars; unlike the other three, it remains a compact, flat or inverted VLBI core with insignificant variability.

quotes.quote: We have also studied the radio-emitting properties of the four RLQs. On kiloparsec scales, all four RLQs display Fanaroff-Riley type II radio structures 1993MNRAS.263..425M,1994AJ....108.1163K .

PG 1704+608 has only a compact core, with insignificant variability and a flat or inverted spectrum with the turnover likely being between 1.6 and 4.7 GHz. On parsec scales, the radio emission of these RLQs is dominated by the relativistic boosted self-absorbed cores, whereas the radio cores of RQQs are either not dominant or mixed with a significant fraction of the optically-thin jet emission (as discussed above). This difference is also manifested in the brightness temperature: the average T_ B of the VLBI cores of the RLQs, T _ B,RLQ = 2.14 10^ 9 K, is 30 times higher than the average T_ B of the RQQ cores in this sample, T _ B,RQQ = 6.92 10^ 7 K.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 351 as a far-infrared-bright quasar with weak radio-core contamination; radio reductions required care because of strong hot spots.

quotes.quote: SCUBA data could not be obtained for quasar 3C 351, due to its high declination.
3C 351 caused some problems during the reduction of its 6 cm and 2 cm observations, due to the strong hot spots in this source. Using the CLEAN procedure with more fields, this problem was solved.

In the pair consisting of quasar 3C 351 and radio galaxy 3C 42, the former is unusually bright in the far-infrared (as already observed by IRAS), whereas the radio galaxy has no detections. As the radio core in 3C 351 is relatively weak, the contribution of any beamed non-thermal far-infrared component can be ruled out.

sources.summary: J1704+6044 is described as a Seyfert 1.5 in a spiral host with a ring and off-center bulge, plus steep lobe-dominated asymmetric radio emission.

quotes.quote: J1704+6044 This is a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy with a very large H broad component. The host galaxy is a spiral that contains a ring that surrounds an off-center bulge . The radio source is very steep and lobe-dominated; the two lobes are asymmetric, and one of them appears to be stopped by a dense environment .

gmw_00006006.name: MS1358
gmw_00006006.overview: MS1358 is a gravitationally lensed galaxy [arXiv:1606.05309] located at a systemic redshift of z=4.92 [arXiv:1606.05309]. The galaxy contains resolved clumps [arXiv:1606.05309] and has been studied for its star-formation rates and neutral-gas covering fraction [arXiv:1606.05309].

sources.summary: The paper treats MS1358 as a z=4.92 lensed galaxy, measuring its systemic redshift, resolved clumps, star-formation rates, and spatial variation in neutral-gas covering fraction.

quotes.quote: We also discuss the spatially-resolved data for the z=4.92 galaxy lensed by the cluster MS1358+62. We summarize our results in 5. For the present study we selected four gravitationally-lensed galaxies from the literature using the following criteria: (i) spectroscopic redshift z>3 , (ii) total apparent magnitude I_ AB <23 and (iii) the availability of a reasonable lens model to enable the magnification to be determined.

For our sample, a redshift from nebular [ O II ] 3727 \ emission is only available for MS1358 Swinbank09 . However, since our spectra primarily target low-ionization absorption lines and emission, it is more practical to use these to estimate the systemic redshift (which is in reasonable agreement with that derived from nebular emission for MS1358).

Both A1689 and A2219 have low and uniform magnifications and the individual MS1358 clumps are sufficiently small as to have uniform magnifications within each region. When MS1358 is considered as an entirety, we excluded arc B (Figure fig:slit ) which has a high variation in magnification.

gmw_00006007.name: LAB1-ALMA1
gmw_00006007.overview: LAB1-ALMA1 is a highly dust-enshrouded star-forming galaxy located within the Lyman-alpha blob LAB 1 [arXiv:2001.03699]. It lies in close vicinity to the neighboring source LAB1-ALMA2 [arXiv:2001.03699].

sources.summary: The paper describes LAB1-ALMA1 as one of three dust-rich star-forming systems embedded in LAB 1, close to LAB1-ALMA2 and linked to Ly and polarization structure.

quotes.quote: Withstanding difficulties and ambiguities in interpreting Ly observables from LAB 1, advances in sub-mm detector technology and collecting area ultimately lead to a detection of three highly dust-enshrouded star-forming galaxies within LAB 1 Geach2014,Geach2016 . Adopting the nomenclature of , these 850 m detected systems are denoted LAB1-ALMA1 to LAB1-ALMA3, and we list their coordinates in Table tab:sources . LAB1-ALMA1 and LAB1-ALMA2 are in close vicinity to each other. Both sources were previously treated by as a single source with S_ 850 m 1 mJy.

Moreover, hints for an extended low-surface brightness dust-component which is not detected by ALMA are seen in the fact that the single-dish SCUBA-2 observations Geach2014 yield a factor of 2.7 higher flux compared to the interferometric measurement. Interestingly, the positions of LAB1-ALMA1 and LAB1-ALMA2 are at the centre of the circular pattern denoted by the polarisation vectors from . Thus the now converging results from sub-mm campaigns are corroborating the central engine hypothesis.
The highest S/N peaks are found at the position of source C11, followed by a peak at the position LAB1-ALMA1.

gmw_00006008.name: LAB1-ALMA2
gmw_00006008.overview: LAB1-ALMA2 is a dust-enshrouded, star-forming galaxy located in close vicinity to LAB1-ALMA1 [arXiv:2001.03699]. Coincident with the source LAB01-K15c, it is situated near a region of high Lyman-alpha surface brightness [arXiv:2001.03699].

sources.summary: The paper describes LAB1-ALMA2 as a dust-rich star-forming system near LAB1-ALMA1, coincident with LAB01-K15c, and close to high Ly and He II structures.

quotes.quote: Withstanding difficulties and ambiguities in interpreting Ly observables from LAB 1, advances in sub-mm detector technology and collecting area ultimately lead to a detection of three highly dust-enshrouded star-forming galaxies within LAB 1 Geach2014,Geach2016 . Adopting the nomenclature of , these 850 m detected systems are denoted LAB1-ALMA1 to LAB1-ALMA3, and we list their coordinates in Table tab:sources . LAB1-ALMA1 and LAB1-ALMA2 are in close vicinity to each other. Both sources were previously treated by as a single source with S_ 850 m 1 mJy.
Their source LAB01-K15a is spatially coincident with the 850 m and [ C ii ] detected galaxy LAB1-ALMA3 and their source LAB01-K15c is coincident with the 850 m detection LAB1-ALMA2.

Another region of high surface-brightness is found close to the position of the sub-mm detected galaxy pair LAB1-ALMA1 and LAB1-ALMA2. Notably, towards the north- and south- of this pair the Ly flux is lower compared to the surrounding regions.

gmw_00006009.name: LAB1-ALMA3
gmw_00006009.overview: LAB1-ALMA3 is a dust-rich, star-forming galaxy detected in submillimeter and [C II] 158 ΙΙ emission [arXiv:2001.03699]. The galaxy is spatially coincident with a 3GHz radio source and potentially exhibits a weak X-ray signal [arXiv:2001.03699].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LAB1-ALMA3 as a dust-rich star-forming galaxy detected in sub-mm, [C II], radio, and possible X-rays, but not coincident with peak Ly brightness.

quotes.quote: LAB1-ALMA3 is detected in [ C II ] 158 m emission Umehata2017 and spatial coincident with a VLA 3GHz source from . Moreover, find a weak X-Ray signal at the position of this source in the deep (400 ks) Chandra full-band (0.5 - 8 keV) image from . This X-Ray source is, however, below the significance criterion adopted in the blind search by .
Their source LAB01-K15a is spatially coincident with the 850 m and [ C ii ] detected galaxy LAB1-ALMA3 and their source LAB01-K15c is coincident with the 850 m detection LAB1-ALMA2.

Interestingly, the sub-mm source LAB1-ALMA3, which is also detected in [ C II ] and radio emission (and potentially X-Ray, cf. Sect. sec:known-sources ), does not correspond to a peak of high-surface brightness, and neither does the [ O III ] detected galaxy S1 or the K-Band selected system LAB01-K15b.

gmw_00006010.name: SSA22a-C11
gmw_00006010.overview: SSA22a-C11 is a Lyman-break galaxy located in the southwestern region of the Lyman-alpha blob LAB 1 [arXiv:2001.03699]. It has a star formation rate of 10 solar masses per year, which is insufficient to power the surrounding blob [arXiv:2001.03699].

sources.summary: The paper places SSA22a-C11 in southwest LAB 1, revises its coordinates, notes its [O III]-confirmed redshift, and argues its SFR is insufficient to power LAB 1 alone.

quotes.quote: Two of the Lyman break galaxies from are within the combined LAB 1 / LAB 8 structure. Adopting the nomenclature of , these are SSA22a-C11 and SSA22a-C15. We list the coordinates of both galaxies in Table tab:sources We note the coordinates for these Lyman break galaxies provided in - RA = 22 ^ h 17 ^ m 25.67 ^ s /Dec = +00 ^ 12 35.2 for SSA22a-C11, and RA = 22 ^ h 17 ^ m 26.127 ^ s /Dec = +00 ^ 12 55.3 for SSA22a-C15 -- appear to be offset by 1 to the north-west with respect to where these galaxies are located in our data.

SSA22a-C11 is located in the south-west of LAB 1, while SSA22a-C15 is found close to the Ly photometric centre of LAB 8. Both galaxies have their redshift confirmed via near-IR detections of their [ O iii ] 4963,5007 lines McLinden2013 . Interestingly, the systemic redshift determined from [ O iii ] does not differ from the Ly emission line. This is not commonly the case for high- z Ly emitting galaxies where the Ly line is typically found to be offset by 200 km s ^ 1 with respect to the systemic redshift e.g. Rakic2011,Chonis2013,Trainor2015 . Since positive Ly redshift offsets are usually interpreted as signs of outflowing expanding gas, argue that C11 and C15 have no such prominent outflows. Moreover, C11 has an inferred star-formation rate of 10 M _ yr ^ 1 Steidel1998,Steidel2003 , thus falls short by more than a factor of ten in producing the required amount of ionising photons to power LAB 1.
